1866:1.contentEn: The United Romanian Principalities constitute a single indivisible State, under the name of Romania.

content.text: The United Romanian Principalities constitute a single indivisible State, under the name of Romania.

1866:2.contentEn: The territory of Romania is inalienable. The limits of the State cannot be changed or rectified, except by virtue of a law.

content.text: The territory of Romania is inalienable. The limits of the State cannot be changed or rectified, except by virtue of a law.

1866:3.contentEn: The territory of Romania cannot be colonized with populations of foreign origin.

content.text: The territory of Romania cannot be colonized with populations of foreign origin.

1866:4.contentEn: The territory is divided into counties, the counties into districts, the districts into communes. These divisions and subdivisions cannot be changed or rectified except by a law.

content.text: The territory is divided into counties, the counties into districts, the districts into communes. These divisions and subdivisions cannot be changed or rectified except by a law.

1866:5.contentEn: Romanians enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly.

content.text: Romanians enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly.

1866:6.contentEn: The Constitution de facia and the other laws relating to political rights determine, which are, apart from the quality of Romanian, the conditions necessary for the exercise of these rights.

content.text: The Constitution de facia and the other laws relating to political rights determine, which are, apart from the quality of Romanian, the conditions necessary for the exercise of these rights.

1866:7.contentEn: The quality of Romanian is acquired, preserved and lost according to the rules established by the civil laws. Only foreigners of Christian rites can acquire naturalization.

content.text: The quality of Romanian is acquired, preserved and lost according to the rules established by the civil laws. Only foreigners of Christian rites can acquire naturalization.

1866:8.contentEn: Naturalization is granted by the legislative power. Only naturalization assimilates the foreigner with the Romanian for the exercise of political rights.

content.text: Naturalization is granted by the legislative power. Only naturalization assimilates the foreigner with the Romanian for the exercise of political rights.

1866:9.contentEn: Any Romanian from any State, regardless of their place of birth, proving their renunciation of foreign protection, may immediately acquire the exercise of political rights through a vote of the Legislative Bodies.

content.text: Any Romanian from any State, regardless of their place of birth, proving their renunciation of foreign protection, may immediately acquire the exercise of political rights through a vote of the Legislative Bodies.

1866:10.contentEn: There is no class distinction in the State. All Romanians are equal before the law and obliged to contribute without distinction to public taxes and burdens. They alone are eligible for public, civil, and military functions. Special laws shall determine the conditions of eligibility and advancement in the functions of the State. Foreigners may not be admitted to public functions except in exceptional cases specifically established by law.

content.text: There is no class distinction in the State. All Romanians are equal before the law and obliged to contribute without distinction to public taxes and burdens. They alone are eligible for public, civil, and military functions. Special laws shall determine the conditions of eligibility and advancement in the functions of the State. Foreigners may not be admitted to public functions except in exceptional cases specifically established by law.

1866:11.contentEn: All foreigners located on the soil of Romania enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.

content.text: All foreigners located on the soil of Romania enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.

1866:12.contentEn: All privileges, exemptions, and class monopolies are forever prohibited in the Romanian State. Foreign titles of nobility, such as princes, counts, barons, and others of the like, as contrary to the ancient establishment of the land, are and remain inadmissible in the Romanian State. Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the authorization of the Ruler.

content.text: All privileges, exemptions, and class monopolies are forever prohibited in the Romanian State. Foreign titles of nobility, such as princes, counts, barons, and others of the like, as contrary to the ancient establishment of the land, are and remain inadmissible in the Romanian State. Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the authorization of the Ruler.

1866:13.contentEn: Individual liberty is guaranteed. No one may be prosecuted except in the cases provided by law and according to the forms prescribed by it. No one may be detained or arrested, except in the case of flagrant offense, except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant which must be communicated to them at the time of arrest or at the latest within 24 hours after arrest.

content.text: Individual liberty is guaranteed. No one may be prosecuted except in the cases provided by law and according to the forms prescribed by it. No one may be detained or arrested, except in the case of flagrant offense, except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant which must be communicated to them at the time of arrest or at the latest within 24 hours after arrest.

1866:14.contentEn: No one may be removed against their will from the judges that the law gives them. Art. 15. The domicile is inviolable. No search of domicile may be made except in the cases specifically provided by law and according to the forms prescribed by it.

content.text: No one may be removed against their will from the judges that the law gives them. Art. 15. The domicile is inviolable. No search of domicile may be made except in the cases specifically provided by law and according to the forms prescribed by it.

1866:16.contentEn: No penalty may be established or applied except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No penalty may be established or applied except by virtue of a law.

1866:17.contentEn: No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property.

content.text: No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property.

1866:18.contentEn: The death penalty may not be re-established, except in the cases provided for in the Military Penal Code in time of war.

content.text: The death penalty may not be re-established, except in the cases provided for in the Military Penal Code in time of war.

1866:19.contentEn: Property of any nature, as well as all claims against the State, are sacred and inviolable. No one may be expropriated except for reasons of legally established public utility, and after a fair and prior compensation. By reason of public utility shall be understood only communication and public health, as well as works for the defense of the land. The existing laws regarding the alignment and widening of streets in communes, as well as the banks of waters flowing through or alongside them, remain in force. Special laws shall regulate the procedure and manner of expropriation. The free and unimpeded use of navigable and floatable rivers, roads and other means of communication is in the public domain.

content.text: Property of any nature, as well as all claims against the State, are sacred and inviolable. No one may be expropriated except for reasons of legally established public utility, and after a fair and prior compensation. By reason of public utility shall be understood only communication and public health, as well as works for the defense of the land. The existing laws regarding the alignment and widening of streets in communes, as well as the banks of waters flowing through or alongside them, remain in force. Special laws shall regulate the procedure and manner of expropriation. The free and unimpeded use of navigable and floatable rivers, roads and other means of communication is in the public domain.

1866:20.contentEn: The property given to the peasants by the rural law and the compensation guaranteed to the owners by that law may never be affected.

content.text: The property given to the peasants by the rural law and the compensation guaranteed to the owners by that law may never be affected.

1866:21.contentEn: Freedom of conscience is absolute. The freedom of all cults is guaranteed insofar as their celebration does not prejudice public order or good morals. The Orthodox religion of the East is the dominant religion of the Romanian State. The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity with the ecumenical church of the East in matters of dogma. The spiritual, canonical and disciplinary affairs of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be regulated by a single central synodal authority, according to a special law. The metropolitans and diocesan bishops of the Romanian Orthodox Church are elected in the manner determined by a special law.

content.text: Freedom of conscience is absolute. The freedom of all cults is guaranteed insofar as their celebration does not prejudice public order or good morals. The Orthodox religion of the East is the dominant religion of the Romanian State. The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity with the ecumenical church of the East in matters of dogma. The spiritual, canonical and disciplinary affairs of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be regulated by a single central synodal authority, according to a special law. The metropolitans and diocesan bishops of the Romanian Orthodox Church are elected in the manner determined by a special law.

1866:22.contentEn: The acts of the Civil Status are the responsibility of the civil authority. The drawing up of these acts must always precede the religious blessing which for marriages will be obligatory, except in the cases which will be provided for by a specific law.

content.text: The acts of the Civil Status are the responsibility of the civil authority. The drawing up of these acts must always precede the religious blessing which for marriages will be obligatory, except in the cases which will be provided for by a specific law.

1866:23.contentEn: Education is free. Freedom of education is guaranteed insofar as its exercise does not prejudice good morals or public order. The repression of offenses is regulated only by law. Primary schools shall be gradually established in all communes of Romania. Education in state schools is free of charge. Primary education shall be compulsory for young Romanians, wherever primary schools are established. A special law shall regulate all matters relating to public education.

content.text: Education is free. Freedom of education is guaranteed insofar as its exercise does not prejudice good morals or public order. The repression of offenses is regulated only by law. Primary schools shall be gradually established in all communes of Romania. Education in state schools is free of charge. Primary education shall be compulsory for young Romanians, wherever primary schools are established. A special law shall regulate all matters relating to public education.

1866:24.contentEn: The Constitution guarantees to all the freedom to communicate and publish their ideas and opinions by speech, writing and the press, each being responsible for the abuse of these freedoms in the cases determined by the Penal Code, which in this respect shall be revised and completed, without, however, restricting the right itself, or establishing an exceptional law. Press offenses are tried by jury. Neither censorship nor any other preventive measure for the appearance, sale or distribution of any publication may be re-established. For journal publications, no prior authorization from the authority is required. No security shall be required from journalists, writers, editors, printers and lithographers. The press shall never be subjected to the warning regime. No journal or publication may be suspended or suppressed. The author is responsible for his writings, and in the absence of the author, the guarantor or the editor is responsible. Every journal must have a responsible guarantor who enjoys civil and political rights.

content.text: The Constitution guarantees to all the freedom to communicate and publish their ideas and opinions by speech, writing and the press, each being responsible for the abuse of these freedoms in the cases determined by the Penal Code, which in this respect shall be revised and completed, without, however, restricting the right itself, or establishing an exceptional law. Press offenses are tried by jury. Neither censorship nor any other preventive measure for the appearance, sale or distribution of any publication may be re-established. For journal publications, no prior authorization from the authority is required. No security shall be required from journalists, writers, editors, printers and lithographers. The press shall never be subjected to the warning regime. No journal or publication may be suspended or suppressed. The author is responsible for his writings, and in the absence of the author, the guarantor or the editor is responsible. Every journal must have a responsible guarantor who enjoys civil and political rights.

1866:25.contentEn: The secrecy of letters and telegraphic dispatches is inviolable. A law shall determine the responsibility of government agents for violating the secrecy of letters and dispatches entrusted to the post office and telegraph.

content.text: The secrecy of letters and telegraphic dispatches is inviolable. A law shall determine the responsibility of government agents for violating the secrecy of letters and dispatches entrusted to the post office and telegraph.

1866:26.contentEn: Romanians have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms, in accordance with the laws that regulate the exercise of this right, in order to discuss all kinds of matters; for this, no prior authorization is required. This provision shall not apply to gatherings in open places, which are entirely subject to police laws.

content.text: Romanians have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms, in accordance with the laws that regulate the exercise of this right, in order to discuss all kinds of matters; for this, no prior authorization is required. This provision shall not apply to gatherings in open places, which are entirely subject to police laws.

1866:27.contentEn: Romanians have the right to associate, in accordance with the laws that regulate the exercise of this right.

content.text: Romanians have the right to associate, in accordance with the laws that regulate the exercise of this right.

1866:28.contentEn: Everyone has the right to address the public authorities through petitions signed by one or more persons, but may only petition in the name of the signatories. Only constituted authorities have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

content.text: Everyone has the right to address the public authorities through petitions signed by one or more persons, but may only petition in the name of the signatories. Only constituted authorities have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

1866:29.contentEn: No prior authorization is necessary to initiate proceedings against public officials for acts of their administration by the injured parties, remaining unaffected the special rules established regarding ministers. The cases and manner of prosecution shall be regulated by a specific law. Special provisions in the penal code shall determine the penalties for superiors.

content.text: No prior authorization is necessary to initiate proceedings against public officials for acts of their administration by the injured parties, remaining unaffected the special rules established regarding ministers. The cases and manner of prosecution shall be regulated by a specific law. Special provisions in the penal code shall determine the penalties for superiors.

1866:30.contentEn: No Romanian may enter the service of a State without the authorization of the government without thereby losing his nationality. The extradition of political refugees is prohibited.

content.text: No Romanian may enter the service of a State without the authorization of the government without thereby losing his nationality. The extradition of political refugees is prohibited.

1866:31.contentEn: All powers of the State emanate from the nation, which can only exercise them through delegation and according to the principles and rules set forth in the present Constitution.

content.text: All powers of the State emanate from the nation, which can only exercise them through delegation and according to the principles and rules set forth in the present Constitution.

1866:32.contentEn: The legislative power is exercised collectively by the Ruling Prince and the national representation. The national representation is divided into two Assemblies: the Senate and the Assembly of Deputies. Any law requires the consent of all three branches of the legislative power. No law may be submitted to the Ruling Prince for sanction until it has been discussed and freely voted on by the majority of both Assemblies.

content.text: The legislative power is exercised collectively by the Ruling Prince and the national representation. The national representation is divided into two Assemblies: the Senate and the Assembly of Deputies. Any law requires the consent of all three branches of the legislative power. No law may be submitted to the Ruling Prince for sanction until it has been discussed and freely voted on by the majority of both Assemblies.

1866:33.contentEn: The initiative for laws is given to each of the three branches of the legislative power. However, any law relating to the revenues and expenditures of the State or to the army contingent must be voted on first by the Assembly of Deputies.

content.text: The initiative for laws is given to each of the three branches of the legislative power. However, any law relating to the revenues and expenditures of the State or to the army contingent must be voted on first by the Assembly of Deputies.

1866:34.contentEn: The interpretation of laws with the right of authority is done only by the legislative power.

content.text: The interpretation of laws with the right of authority is done only by the legislative power.

1866:35.contentEn: The executive power is entrusted to the Ruling Prince, who exercises it in the manner regulated by the Constitution.

content.text: The executive power is entrusted to the Ruling Prince, who exercises it in the manner regulated by the Constitution.

1866:36.contentEn: The judicial power is exercised by Courts and Tribunals. Their decisions and judgments are pronounced by virtue of the law and are executed in the name of the Ruling Prince.

content.text: The judicial power is exercised by Courts and Tribunals. Their decisions and judgments are pronounced by virtue of the law and are executed in the name of the Ruling Prince.

1866:37.contentEn: The exclusive interests of the counties or communes are regulated by the county or communal councils according to the principles established by the Constitution and by special laws.

content.text: The exclusive interests of the counties or communes are regulated by the county or communal councils according to the principles established by the Constitution and by special laws.

1866:38.contentEn: The members of both Assemblies represent the nation, and not only the county or locality that appointed them.

content.text: The members of both Assemblies represent the nation, and not only the county or locality that appointed them.

1866:39.contentEn: The sessions of the Assemblies are public. Nevertheless, each Assembly forms itself into a secret committee, at the request of the president or of ten members. It subsequently decides by an absolute majority whether the session should be reopened to the public on the same subject.

content.text: The sessions of the Assemblies are public. Nevertheless, each Assembly forms itself into a secret committee, at the request of the president or of ten members. It subsequently decides by an absolute majority whether the session should be reopened to the public on the same subject.

1866:40.contentEn: Each of the Assemblies verifies the titles of its members and adjudicates the disputes that arise in this regard.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies verifies the titles of its members and adjudicates the disputes that arise in this regard.

1866:41.contentEn: No one may be a member of one and the other Assembly at the same time.

content.text: No one may be a member of one and the other Assembly at the same time.

1866:42.contentEn: Members of either Assembly appointed by the Government to a salaried position, which they accept, cease to be deputies and may not resume the exercise of their mandate except by virtue of a new election. These provisions do not apply to ministers. The electoral law determines the incompatibilities.

content.text: Members of either Assembly appointed by the Government to a salaried position, which they accept, cease to be deputies and may not resume the exercise of their mandate except by virtue of a new election. These provisions do not apply to ministers. The electoral law determines the incompatibilities.

1866:43.contentEn: At each session, the Assembly of Deputies shall appoint its president, vice-presidents, and compose its bureau.

content.text: At each session, the Assembly of Deputies shall appoint its president, vice-presidents, and compose its bureau.

1866:44.contentEn: The Senate shall elect from within itself its President and its two Vice-Presidents, as well as the other Members of its Bureau.

content.text: The Senate shall elect from within itself its President and its two Vice-Presidents, as well as the other Members of its Bureau.

1866:45.contentEn: Any resolution is adopted by an absolute majority of votes, except for that which shall be established by the regulations of the Assembly regarding elections and presentations. In case of a tie vote, the proposition under deliberation is rejected. Neither Assembly may take a resolution unless a majority of its Members are present.

content.text: Any resolution is adopted by an absolute majority of votes, except for that which shall be established by the regulations of the Assembly regarding elections and presentations. In case of a tie vote, the proposition under deliberation is rejected. Neither Assembly may take a resolution unless a majority of its Members are present.

1866:46.contentEn: Votes are cast by standing and sitting, by voice vote, or by secret ballot. A bill may not be adopted until it has been voted on article by article.

content.text: Votes are cast by standing and sitting, by voice vote, or by secret ballot. A bill may not be adopted until it has been voted on article by article.

1866:47.contentEn: Each of the Assemblies has the right of inquiry.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies has the right of inquiry.

1866:48.contentEn: The Assemblies have the right to amend and to divide into several parts the articles and amendments proposed.

content.text: The Assemblies have the right to amend and to divide into several parts the articles and amendments proposed.

1866:49.contentEn: Each member of the Assemblies has the right to address interpellations to the Ministers. Art. 50. Anyone has the right to address petitions to the Assemblies through the bureau or one of its members. Each of the Assemblies has the right to transmit to the Ministers the petitions addressed to it. The Ministers are obliged to give explanations on their content whenever the Assemblies request it.

content.text: Each member of the Assemblies has the right to address interpellations to the Ministers. Art. 50. Anyone has the right to address petitions to the Assemblies through the bureau or one of its members. Each of the Assemblies has the right to transmit to the Ministers the petitions addressed to it. The Ministers are obliged to give explanations on their content whenever the Assemblies request it.

1866:51.contentEn: No member of either Assembly may be prosecuted or persecuted for opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising his mandate.

content.text: No member of either Assembly may be prosecuted or persecuted for opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising his mandate.

1866:52.contentEn: No member of either Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested in matters of repression, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrante delicto. The detention or prosecution of a member of either Assembly shall be suspended for the entire duration of the session, if the Assembly so requests.

content.text: No member of either Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested in matters of repression, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrante delicto. The detention or prosecution of a member of either Assembly shall be suspended for the entire duration of the session, if the Assembly so requests.

1866:53.contentEn: Each Assembly shall determine, by its regulations, the manner in which it exercises its attributions.

content.text: Each Assembly shall determine, by its regulations, the manner in which it exercises its attributions.

1866:54.contentEn: Each of the Assemblies shall deliberate and take its resolutions separately, except in the cases specifically specified in the present Constitution.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies shall deliberate and take its resolutions separately, except in the cases specifically specified in the present Constitution.

1866:55.contentEn:
Each of the two Assemblies has the exclusive right to exercise its own police power through its President, who alone, with the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the guard on duty. Art. 56. No armed force may be placed at the doors or around either of the Assemblies without its consent.

#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

content.text: Each of the two Assemblies has the exclusive right to exercise its own police power through its President, who alone, with the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the guard on duty. Art. 56. No armed force may be placed at the doors or around either of the Assemblies without its consent.
#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

1866:57.contentEn: The Assembly of Deputies shall be composed of deputies elected in the manner indicated below:

content.text: The Assembly of Deputies shall be composed of deputies elected in the manner indicated below:

1866:58.contentEn: The electoral body is divided in each county into four colleges.

content.text: The electoral body is divided in each county into four colleges.

1866:59.contentEn: Those who have a land income of 300 guilders or more are part of the first college.

content.text: Those who have a land income of 300 guilders or more are part of the first college.

1866:60.contentEn: Those who have a land income of 300 guilders down to 100 inclusive are part of the second college.

content.text: Those who have a land income of 300 guilders down to 100 inclusive are part of the second college.

1866:61.contentEn: Merchants and industrialists who pay a tax of 80 lei to the State are part of the third college of cities. All liberal professions, retired officers, professors and State pensioners are exempt from census in this college.

content.text: Merchants and industrialists who pay a tax of 80 lei to the State are part of the third college of cities. All liberal professions, retired officers, professors and State pensioners are exempt from census in this college.

1866:62.contentEn: These three colleges elect directly: The first two each elect one deputy, and the third as follows: Bucharest six; Iasi four; Craiova, Galati, Ploesci, Focsani, Barlad, Botosani three each; Pitesci, Bacau, Braila, Roman, Turnu-Severin two each; and the others one each; in total fifty-eight. All the towns of a district form a single college with the town of residence.

content.text: These three colleges elect directly: The first two each elect one deputy, and the third as follows: Bucharest six; Iasi four; Craiova, Galati, Ploesci, Focsani, Barlad, Botosani three each; Pitesci, Bacau, Braila, Roman, Turnu-Severin two each; and the others one each; in total fifty-eight. All the towns of a district form a single college with the town of residence.

1866:63.contentEn: All those who pay a tax to the state, however small, and who do not fall into any of the above categories are part of the fourth college. This college elects a district deputy in the second degree.

content.text: All those who pay a tax to the state, however small, and who do not fall into any of the above categories are part of the fourth college. This college elects a district deputy in the second degree.

1866:64.contentEn: The census can only be proven by the contribution role, receipts or warnings from the tax collectors for the expired year and the current year.

content.text: The census can only be proven by the contribution role, receipts or warnings from the tax collectors for the expired year and the current year.

1866:65.contentEn: The electoral law determines all the other conditions required of voters as well as the course of electoral operations.

content.text: The electoral law determines all the other conditions required of voters as well as the course of electoral operations.

1866:66.contentEn:
In order to be eligible, it is necessary:
- a) To be Romanian by birth, or to have received major naturalization.
- b) To enjoy civil and political rights.
- c) To be twenty-five years of age completed.
- d) To be domiciled in Romania. The electoral law will determine the incapacities.

content.text: In order to be eligible, it is necessary:
a) To be Romanian by birth, or to have received major naturalization.
b) To enjoy civil and political rights.
c) To be twenty-five years of age completed.
d) To be domiciled in Romania. The electoral law will determine the incapacities.

1866:67.contentEn:
The members of the Assembly of Deputies are elected for four years.

#### Section 2. On the Senate

content.text: The members of the Assembly of Deputies are elected for four years.
#### Section 2. On the Senate

1866:68.contentEn: The members of the Senate shall be elected two from each county: one by the primary college composed of the owners of rural estates in the county who have a land income of at least three hundred guilders; the other by the secondary college of the county seats composed of the owners of immovable property having a land income of 300 guilders or less, pursuant to Art. 70. The income shall be proven by the contribution rolls.

content.text: The members of the Senate shall be elected two from each county: one by the primary college composed of the owners of rural estates in the county who have a land income of at least three hundred guilders; the other by the secondary college of the county seats composed of the owners of immovable property having a land income of 300 guilders or less, pursuant to Art. 70. The income shall be proven by the contribution rolls.

1866:69.contentEn: These two colleges shall vote separately and each shall elect one representative to the Senate.

content.text: These two colleges shall vote separately and each shall elect one representative to the Senate.

1866:70.contentEn: In cities where there would not be a number of 100 voters to form the secondary college, this number shall be completed with the owners of the county, possessing a land income between 300 and 100 guilders, always preferring those most heavily taxed and the townspeople over the owners of estates.

content.text: In cities where there would not be a number of 100 voters to form the secondary college, this number shall be completed with the owners of the county, possessing a land income between 300 and 100 guilders, always preferring those most heavily taxed and the townspeople over the owners of estates.

1866:71.contentEn: If among the most heavily taxed there are several with the same income, and if by their number they would exceed the number required to complete the college, the elimination of the surplus shall be done by drawing lots.

content.text: If among the most heavily taxed there are several with the same income, and if by their number they would exceed the number required to complete the college, the elimination of the surplus shall be done by drawing lots.

1866:72.contentEn: The electoral law shall determine the other conditions required of voters, as well as the course of electoral operations.

content.text: The electoral law shall determine the other conditions required of voters, as well as the course of electoral operations.

1866:73.contentEn: The Universities of Iasi and Bucharest shall each send one member to the Senate, elected by the professors of the respective university.

content.text: The Universities of Iasi and Bucharest shall each send one member to the Senate, elected by the professors of the respective university.

1866:74.contentEn:
In order to be elected to the Senate, it is necessary:
1) To be Romanian by birth or naturalized.
2) To enjoy civil and political rights.
3) To be domiciled in Romania.
4) To be 40 years of age.
5) To have an income of any nature of 800 guilders, proven in the manner provided by Art. 64.

content.text: In order to be elected to the Senate, it is necessary:
1) To be Romanian by birth or naturalized.
2) To enjoy civil and political rights.
3) To be domiciled in Romania.
4) To be 40 years of age.
5) To have an income of any nature of 800 guilders, proven in the manner provided by Art. 64.

1866:75.contentEn:
The following are exempt from this census:
- a) The Presidents or Vice-Presidents of any legislative Assembly.
- b) Deputies who have been part of three sessions.
- c) Generals.
- d) Colonels who have been in service for three years.
- e) Those who have been Ministers or diplomatic agents of the country.
- f) Those who have held for one year the positions of President of the Court, Prosecutor General, Counselor at the Court of Cassation.
- g) Those with a doctorate or licentiate degree in any specialty who have practiced their profession for six years.

content.text: The following are exempt from this census:
a) The Presidents or Vice-Presidents of any legislative Assembly.
b) Deputies who have been part of three sessions.
c) Generals.
d) Colonels who have been in service for three years.
e) Those who have been Ministers or diplomatic agents of the country.
f) Those who have held for one year the positions of President of the Court, Prosecutor General, Counselor at the Court of Cassation.
g) Those with a doctorate or licentiate degree in any specialty who have practiced their profession for six years.

1866:76.contentEn:
The following shall be members of the Senate by right:
1) The Heir to the Throne at the age of 18 years, but he shall not have a deliberative vote until the age of 25 years.
2) Metropolitan Bishops and Diocesan Bishops.

content.text: The following shall be members of the Senate by right:
1) The Heir to the Throne at the age of 18 years, but he shall not have a deliberative vote until the age of 25 years.
2) Metropolitan Bishops and Diocesan Bishops.

1866:77.contentEn: The members of the Senate shall not receive any donation or indemnity.

content.text: The members of the Senate shall not receive any donation or indemnity.

1866:78.contentEn: The members of the Senate shall be elected for 8 years and shall be renewed by half every four years by drawing lots of one member from each county.

content.text: The members of the Senate shall be elected for 8 years and shall be renewed by half every four years by drawing lots of one member from each county.
Outgoing members shall be re-eligible.

1866:80.contentEn: In case of dissolution, the Senate shall be renewed in its entirety.

content.text: In case of dissolution, the Senate shall be renewed in its entirety.

1866:81.contentEn: Any meeting of the Senate outside the time of the session of the Assembly of Deputies shall be null and void.

content.text: Any meeting of the Senate outside the time of the session of the Assembly of Deputies shall be null and void.

1866:82.contentEn: The constitutional powers of the Lord shall be hereditary, in the direct and legitimate descending line of Maria Sele, Prince Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants. The descendants of Maria Sele shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East.

content.text: The constitutional powers of the Lord shall be hereditary, in the direct and legitimate descending line of Maria Sele, Prince Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants. The descendants of Maria Sele shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East.

1866:83.contentEn: In the absence of descendants in the male line of Maria Sele Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of his brothers or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding Articolul. If none of his brothers or their descendants are still alive, or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the Ruler may appoint his successor from a sovereign dynasty in Europe with the acceptance of the national representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 84. If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

content.text: In the absence of descendants in the male line of Maria Sele Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of his brothers or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding Articolul. If none of his brothers or their descendants are still alive, or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the Ruler may appoint his successor from a sovereign dynasty in Europe with the acceptance of the national representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 84. If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

1866:84.contentEn: In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall convene immediately in a single Assembly, even without convocation, and at the latest within eight days of their meeting, shall elect a Ruling Prince from a sovereign dynasty of Western Europe. The presence of three-quarters of the members comprising each of the two Assemblies, and a majority of two-thirds of the members present, shall be necessary in order to proceed with this election. In the event that the Assembly has not been formed within the term prescribed above, then on the ninth day from then, the convened Assemblies shall proceed to the election regardless of the number of members present and with an absolute majority of votes. If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following Art. shall be followed. During the vacancy of the Throne, the convened Assemblies shall appoint a Royal Lieutenancy composed of three persons who shall exercise the Ruling Powers until the accession of the Ruling Prince to the Throne. In all cases indicated above, the vote shall be secret.

content.text: In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall convene immediately in a single Assembly, even without convocation, and at the latest within eight days of their meeting, shall elect a Ruling Prince from a sovereign dynasty of Western Europe. The presence of three-quarters of the members comprising each of the two Assemblies, and a majority of two-thirds of the members present, shall be necessary in order to proceed with this election. In the event that the Assembly has not been formed within the term prescribed above, then on the ninth day from then, the convened Assemblies shall proceed to the election regardless of the number of members present and with an absolute majority of votes. If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following Art. shall be followed. During the vacancy of the Throne, the convened Assemblies shall appoint a Royal Lieutenancy composed of three persons who shall exercise the Ruling Powers until the accession of the Ruling Prince to the Throne. In all cases indicated above, the vote shall be secret.

1866:85.contentEn: Upon the death of the Ruling Prince, the Assemblies shall convene even without convocation, at the latest 10 days after the declaration of death. If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand, and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution for a time after those 10 days, then the old Assemblies shall convene until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

content.text: Upon the death of the Ruling Prince, the Assemblies shall convene even without convocation, at the latest 10 days after the declaration of death. If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand, and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution for a time after those 10 days, then the old Assemblies shall convene until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

1866:86.contentEn: From the date of the Ruling Prince's death and until the taking of the oath by his successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the Ruling Prince shall be exercised in the name of the Romanian people by the ministers convened in council and under their responsibility.

content.text: From the date of the Ruling Prince's death and until the taking of the oath by his successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the Ruling Prince shall be exercised in the name of the Romanian people by the ministers convened in council and under their responsibility.

1866:87.contentEn: The Ruling Prince is of age at the age of 18 years completed. Upon His accession to the Throne, He shall first take the following oath before the convened Assemblies: "I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian people, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory."

content.text: The Ruling Prince is of age at the age of 18 years completed. Upon His accession to the Throne, He shall first take the following oath before the convened Assemblies: "I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian people, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory."

1866:88.contentEn: The Ruling Prince, during his lifetime, may appoint a Regency composed of three persons, who, after the death of the Ruling Prince, shall exercise the Ruling Powers during the minority of the successor to the Throne. This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the national representation, given in the form prescribed in Art. 84 of the Constitution herein. The Regency shall also execute the guardianship of the successor to the Throne, during the latter's minority. If, upon the death of the Ruling Prince, the Regency is not found to be appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both convened Assemblies shall appoint a Regency, proceeding according to the forms prescribed in Art. 48 of the Constitution herein. The members of the regency shall not enter into office until they have solemnly taken, before both convened Assemblies, the oath prescribed by Art. 87 of the Constitution herein.

content.text: The Ruling Prince, during his lifetime, may appoint a Regency composed of three persons, who, after the death of the Ruling Prince, shall exercise the Ruling Powers during the minority of the successor to the Throne. This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the national representation, given in the form prescribed in Art. 84 of the Constitution herein. The Regency shall also execute the guardianship of the successor to the Throne, during the latter's minority. If, upon the death of the Ruling Prince, the Regency is not found to be appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both convened Assemblies shall appoint a Regency, proceeding according to the forms prescribed in Art. 48 of the Constitution herein. The members of the regency shall not enter into office until they have solemnly taken, before both convened Assemblies, the oath prescribed by Art. 87 of the Constitution herein.

1866:89.contentEn: If the Ruling Prince is unable to rule, the Ministers, after having legally established this impossibility, shall immediately convene the Assemblies. These shall elect a Regency which shall also form the guardianship.

content.text: If the Ruling Prince is unable to rule, the Ministers, after having legally established this impossibility, shall immediately convene the Assemblies. These shall elect a Regency which shall also form the guardianship.

1866:90.contentEn: No modification may be made to the Constitution during the Regency.

content.text: No modification may be made to the Constitution during the Regency.

1866:91.contentEn: The Ruling Prince may not be at the same time the head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies. Neither of the Assemblies may deliberate on this object unless at least two-thirds of the members comprising them are present, and the decision may only be taken with two-thirds of the votes of the de facto Members.

content.text: The Ruling Prince may not be at the same time the head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies. Neither of the Assemblies may deliberate on this object unless at least two-thirds of the members comprising them are present, and the decision may only be taken with two-thirds of the votes of the de facto Members.

1866:92.contentEn: The Person of the Ruling Prince is inviolable. His Ministers are accountable. No act of the Ruling Prince shall have force unless it is countersigned by a Minister who, by this very act, becomes accountable for that act.

content.text: The Person of the Ruling Prince is inviolable. His Ministers are accountable. No act of the Ruling Prince shall have force unless it is countersigned by a Minister who, by this very act, becomes accountable for that act.

1866:93.contentEn: The Ruling Prince appoints and dismisses his Ministers. He sanctions and promulgates the laws. He may refuse his sanction. He has the right of amnesty in political matters. He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters; except for that which is established regarding the Ministers. He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice. He appoints or confirms all public functions. He may not create a new function without a special law. He makes the regulations necessary for the execution of the laws without ever being able to modify or suspend the laws and may not exempt anyone from their execution. He is the head of the armed forces. He confers military ranks in accordance with the law. He shall confer the Romanian decoration in accordance with a specific law. He has the right to mint coins, in accordance with a special law. He concludes with foreign States the Conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and other such matters; however, in order for these acts to have binding authority, they must first be submitted to the legislative power and approved by it.

content.text: The Ruling Prince appoints and dismisses his Ministers. He sanctions and promulgates the laws. He may refuse his sanction. He has the right of amnesty in political matters. He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters; except for that which is established regarding the Ministers. He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice. He appoints or confirms all public functions. He may not create a new function without a special law. He makes the regulations necessary for the execution of the laws without ever being able to modify or suspend the laws and may not exempt anyone from their execution. He is the head of the armed forces. He confers military ranks in accordance with the law. He shall confer the Romanian decoration in accordance with a specific law. He has the right to mint coins, in accordance with a special law. He concludes with foreign States the Conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and other such matters; however, in order for these acts to have binding authority, they must first be submitted to the legislative power and approved by it.

1866:94.contentEn: The law fixes the civil list for the duration of each Reign.

content.text: The law fixes the civil list for the duration of each Reign.

1866:95.contentEn: On the 15th of November of each year, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate meet without convocation, unless the Ruling Prince has convened them earlier. The duration of each session is three months. At the opening of the session, the Ruling Prince expounds, through a Message, the state of the country, to which the Assemblies make their responses. The Ruling Prince pronounces the closure of the session. He has the right to convene the Assemblies in extraordinary session. He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at once or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors within two months of days and of the Assemblies within three months. The Ruling Prince may adjourn the Assemblies; however, the adjournment may not exceed the term of one month, nor be renewed in the same session without the consent of the Assemblies. Art. 96. The Ruling Prince has no other powers than those given to him by the Constitution.

content.text: On the 15th of November of each year, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate meet without convocation, unless the Ruling Prince has convened them earlier. The duration of each session is three months. At the opening of the session, the Ruling Prince expounds, through a Message, the state of the country, to which the Assemblies make their responses. The Ruling Prince pronounces the closure of the session. He has the right to convene the Assemblies in extraordinary session. He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at once or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors within two months of days and of the Assemblies within three months. The Ruling Prince may adjourn the Assemblies; however, the adjournment may not exceed the term of one month, nor be renewed in the same session without the consent of the Assemblies. Art. 96. The Ruling Prince has no other powers than those given to him by the Constitution.

1866:97.contentEn: Only a person who is Romanian by birth, or who has acquired naturalization, may be a Minister.

content.text: Only a person who is Romanian by birth, or who has acquired naturalization, may be a Minister.

1866:98.contentEn: No member of the Ruling family may be a minister.

content.text: No member of the Ruling family may be a minister.

1866:99.contentEn: If the ministers are not members of the Assemblies, they may take part in the debate of the laws, without, however, having the right to vote. The presence of at least one Minister is necessary at the debates of the Assemblies. The Assemblies may require the presence of the Ministers at their deliberations.

content.text: If the ministers are not members of the Assemblies, they may take part in the debate of the laws, without, however, having the right to vote. The presence of at least one Minister is necessary at the debates of the Assemblies. The Assemblies may require the presence of the Ministers at their deliberations.

1866:100.contentEn: In no case may the verbal or written order of the Ruling Prince protect a Minister from liability.

content.text: In no case may the verbal or written order of the Ruling Prince protect a Minister from liability.

1866:101.contentEn: Each of the two Assemblies, as well as the Domnitor, shall have the right to impeach the Ministers and to send them before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, which alone, in united sections, is entitled to try them, except for what shall be established by laws regarding the exercise of civil action and the offenses committed by the Ministers, apart from the exercise of their function. The impeachment of Ministers may only be pronounced by a majority of two-thirds of the members present. A law presented at the first session shall determine the cases of liability, the penalties applicable to Ministers, and the manner of prosecution against them, both in regard to the impeachment admitted by the national representation and in regard to the prosecution by the injured parties. The impeachment initiated by the national representation against the Ministers shall be sustained by it itself. The prosecution initiated by the Domnitor shall be carried out through the public ministry.

content.text: Each of the two Assemblies, as well as the Domnitor, shall have the right to impeach the Ministers and to send them before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, which alone, in united sections, is entitled to try them, except for what shall be established by laws regarding the exercise of civil action and the offenses committed by the Ministers, apart from the exercise of their function. The impeachment of Ministers may only be pronounced by a majority of two-thirds of the members present. A law presented at the first session shall determine the cases of liability, the penalties applicable to Ministers, and the manner of prosecution against them, both in regard to the impeachment admitted by the national representation and in regard to the prosecution by the injured parties. The impeachment initiated by the national representation against the Ministers shall be sustained by it itself. The prosecution initiated by the Domnitor shall be carried out through the public ministry.

1866:102.contentEn: Until the law provided for in the preceding Art. is made, the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have the power to characterize the offense and to determine the penalty. However, the penalty may not be greater than detention, without prejudice to the cases specifically provided for by the criminal laws.

content.text: Until the law provided for in the preceding Art. is made, the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have the power to characterize the offense and to determine the penalty. However, the penalty may not be greater than detention, without prejudice to the cases specifically provided for by the criminal laws.

1866:103.contentEn: The Domnitor may not pardon or reduce the penalty decided upon for the Ministers by the High Court of Cassation and Justice except only at the request of the Assembly that would have impeached him.

content.text: The Domnitor may not pardon or reduce the penalty decided upon for the Ministers by the High Court of Cassation and Justice except only at the request of the Assembly that would have impeached him.

1866:104.contentEn: No jurisdiction may be established except by virtue of a specific law. Extraordinary commissions and tribunals may not be created under any name or pretext whatsoever. For the entire Romanian State, there is a single Court of Cassation. Art. 105. The jury is established in all criminal matters and for political and press offenses.

content.text: No jurisdiction may be established except by virtue of a specific law. Extraordinary commissions and tribunals may not be created under any name or pretext whatsoever. For the entire Romanian State, there is a single Court of Cassation. Art. 105. The jury is established in all criminal matters and for political and press offenses.

1866:106.contentEn: The county and communal institutions are regulated by laws.

content.text: The county and communal institutions are regulated by laws.

1866:107.contentEn: These laws shall have as their basis a more complete decentralization of the administration and communal independence.

content.text: These laws shall have as their basis a more complete decentralization of the administration and communal independence.

1866:108.contentEn: Any tax is levied only for the benefit of the State, the county, or the commune.

content.text: Any tax is levied only for the benefit of the State, the county, or the commune.

1866:109.contentEn: No state tax may be established and collected except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No state tax may be established and collected except by virtue of a law.

1866:110.contentEn: No county burden or tax may be established except with the consent of the county council. No communal burden or tax may be imposed except with the consent of the communal council. The taxes voted by the county and communal councils must receive the confirmation of the legislative power and the sanction of the Ruling Prince.

content.text: No county burden or tax may be established except with the consent of the county council. No communal burden or tax may be imposed except with the consent of the communal council. The taxes voted by the county and communal councils must receive the confirmation of the legislative power and the sanction of the Ruling Prince.

1866:111.contentEn: Privileges may not be established in matters of taxation. No exemption or reduction of tax may be established except by a law.

content.text: Privileges may not be established in matters of taxation. No exemption or reduction of tax may be established except by a law.

1866:112.contentEn: No funds for pensions or gratuities chargeable to the public treasury may be granted except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No funds for pensions or gratuities chargeable to the public treasury may be granted except by virtue of a law.

1866:113.contentEn: Each year, the Assembly of Deputies closes the accounts and votes on the budget. All revenues or expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and in the accounts. The budget shall always be presented to the Assembly of Deputies one year before its implementation and shall not be final until it has been voted on by it and sanctioned by the Ruling Prince. If the budget is not voted on in a timely manner, the executive power shall provide for public services according to the budget of the previous year, without being able to use that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

content.text: Each year, the Assembly of Deputies closes the accounts and votes on the budget. All revenues or expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and in the accounts. The budget shall always be presented to the Assembly of Deputies one year before its implementation and shall not be final until it has been voted on by it and sanctioned by the Ruling Prince. If the budget is not voted on in a timely manner, the executive power shall provide for public services according to the budget of the previous year, without being able to use that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

1866:114.contentEn: The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Assembly no later than two years after the end of each financial year.

content.text: The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Assembly no later than two years after the end of each financial year.

1866:115.contentEn: Finance laws shall be published in the Official Gazette, as are other laws and regulations of public administration.

content.text: Finance laws shall be published in the Official Gazette, as are other laws and regulations of public administration.

1866:116.contentEn: There is a single Court of Accounts for all of Romania.

content.text: There is a single Court of Accounts for all of Romania.

1866:117.contentEn: The various funds derived hitherto from special funds and which the government disposes of under various titles must be included in the general budget of State revenues.

content.text: The various funds derived hitherto from special funds and which the government disposes of under various titles must be included in the general budget of State revenues.

1866:118.contentEn: Every Romanian is part either of the regular army, or of the militias, or of the civic guard, according to special laws.

content.text: Every Romanian is part either of the regular army, or of the militias, or of the civic guard, according to special laws.

1866:119.contentEn: Military personnel may not be deprived of their ranks, honors, and pensions, except by virtue of a judicial sentence and in the cases determined by law.

content.text: Military personnel may not be deprived of their ranks, honors, and pensions, except by virtue of a judicial sentence and in the cases determined by law.

1866:120.contentEn: The army contingent is voted on each year. The law that fixes this contingent may not be in force for more than one year.

content.text: The army contingent is voted on each year. The law that fixes this contingent may not be in force for more than one year.

1866:121.contentEn: The civic guard is maintained in the Romanian State. Its organization is regulated by a special law.

content.text: The civic guard is maintained in the Romanian State. Its organization is regulated by a special law.

1866:122.contentEn: Only by virtue of a law may the civic guard be mobilized.

content.text: Only by virtue of a law may the civic guard be mobilized.

1866:123.contentEn: No foreign troop may be admitted into the service of the State, nor occupy the territory of Romania, nor pass through it, except by virtue of a specific law.

content.text: No foreign troop may be admitted into the service of the State, nor occupy the territory of Romania, nor pass through it, except by virtue of a specific law.

1866:124.contentEn: The colors of the United Principalities shall be Blue, Yellow, and Red.

content.text: The colors of the United Principalities shall be Blue, Yellow, and Red.

1866:125.contentEn: The city of Bucharest is the capital of the Romanian State and the seat of government.

content.text: The city of Bucharest is the capital of the Romanian State and the seat of government.

1866:126.contentEn: No oath may be imposed on anyone except by virtue of a law that also determines its wording.

content.text: No oath may be imposed on anyone except by virtue of a law that also determines its wording.

1866:127.contentEn: No law, nor any regulation of general, county or communal administration may be binding until it is published in the manner prescribed by law. Art. 128. The current Constitution may not be suspended, either in whole or in part.

content.text: No law, nor any regulation of general, county or communal administration may be binding until it is published in the manner prescribed by law. Art. 128. The current Constitution may not be suspended, either in whole or in part.

1866:129.contentEn: The legislative power has the right to declare that there is a need to submit to revision the provisions of the Constitution specifically indicated. After this declaration, read three times, fifteen days apart, in public session and accepted by both Assemblies, these are dissolved by law and others are convened within the term prescribed by Art. 95. The new Assemblies proceed in agreement with the Ruler to modify the points subject to revision. In this case, the Assemblies may not deliberate unless at least two-thirds of the members of which they are composed are present, and no change may be adopted unless it gathers at least two-thirds of the votes.

content.text: The legislative power has the right to declare that there is a need to submit to revision the provisions of the Constitution specifically indicated. After this declaration, read three times, fifteen days apart, in public session and accepted by both Assemblies, these are dissolved by law and others are convened within the term prescribed by Art. 95. The new Assemblies proceed in agreement with the Ruler to modify the points subject to revision. In this case, the Assemblies may not deliberate unless at least two-thirds of the members of which they are composed are present, and no change may be adopted unless it gathers at least two-thirds of the votes.

1866:130.contentEn: From the day of entry into force of the current Constitution, all provisions of laws, decrees and regulations and other acts contrary to those established by it are repealed.

content.text: From the day of entry into force of the current Constitution, all provisions of laws, decrees and regulations and other acts contrary to those established by it are repealed.

1866:131.contentEn: The Council of State will cease to exist as soon as the law intended to provide for the authority called to replace it in its attributions is voted on. The Court of Cassation will pronounce, as in the past, on conflicts of attributions.

content.text: The Council of State will cease to exist as soon as the law intended to provide for the authority called to replace it in its attributions is voted on. The Court of Cassation will pronounce, as in the past, on conflicts of attributions.

1866:132.contentEn:
Special laws will be made as soon as possible regarding the following objects:
1) On administrative decentralization.
2) On the responsibility of ministers and other Agents of the executive power.
3) On the most appropriate measures to curb the abuse of cumulation.
4) On the modification of the pension law.
5) On the conditions of admissibility and advancement in the functions of public administration.
6) On the development of communication routes.
7) On the exploitation of mines and forests.
8) On navigable or floatable rivers and streams.
9) On the organization of the army, the rights of advancement, retirement and on the different positions of officers.
10) On military justice.
All existing codes and laws will be revised to bring them into harmony with the current Constitution.

content.text: Special laws will be made as soon as possible regarding the following objects:
1) On administrative decentralization.
2) On the responsibility of ministers and other Agents of the executive power.
3) On the most appropriate measures to curb the abuse of cumulation.
4) On the modification of the pension law.
5) On the conditions of admissibility and advancement in the functions of public administration.
6) On the development of communication routes.
7) On the exploitation of mines and forests.
8) On navigable or floatable rivers and streams.
9) On the organization of the army, the rights of advancement, retirement and on the different positions of officers.
10) On military justice.
All existing codes and laws will be revised to bring them into harmony with the current Constitution.

1866:133.contentEn: The inalienability of the lands of the former corvée peasants during the 20-year period provided for by the rural law is maintained.

content.text: The inalienability of the lands of the former corvée peasants during the 20-year period provided for by the rural law is maintained.

1923:1.contentEn: The Kingdom of Romania is a unitary and indivisible national State.

content.text: The Kingdom of Romania is a unitary and indivisible national State.

1923:2.contentEn:
The territory of Romania is inalienable.
The borders of the State may not be changed or rectified except by virtue of a law.

content.text: The territory of Romania is inalienable.
The borders of the State may not be changed or rectified except by virtue of a law.

1923:3.contentEn: The territory of Romania may not be colonized with populations of foreign origin.

content.text: The territory of Romania may not be colonized with populations of foreign origin.

1923:4.contentEn:
From an administrative point of view, the territory of Romania is divided into counties, and the counties into communes.
Their number, extent and territorial subdivisions shall be established according to the forms provided for in the laws on administrative organization.

content.text: From an administrative point of view, the territory of Romania is divided into counties, and the counties into communes.
Their number, extent and territorial subdivisions shall be established according to the forms provided for in the laws on administrative organization.

1923:5.contentEn: Romanians, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, freedom of association and all the freedoms and rights established by law.

content.text: Romanians, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, freedom of association and all the freedoms and rights established by law.

1923:6.contentEn:
The present Constitution and the other laws relating to political rights determine what, apart from the quality of Romanian, are the conditions necessary for the exercise of these rights.
Special laws, passed by a two-thirds majority, shall determine the conditions under which women may have the exercise of political rights. The civil rights of women shall be established on the basis of full equality of the two sexes.

content.text: The present Constitution and the other laws relating to political rights determine what, apart from the quality of Romanian, are the conditions necessary for the exercise of these rights.

Special laws, passed by a two-thirds majority, shall determine the conditions under which women may have the exercise of political rights. The civil rights of women shall be established on the basis of full equality of the two sexes.

1923:7.contentEn:
The difference of religious beliefs and denominations, of ethnic origin and of language, does not constitute in Romania an impediment to acquire civil and political rights and to exercise them.
Only naturalization equates a foreigner with a Romanian for the exercise of political rights.
Naturalization is granted individually by the Council of Ministers, following the finding of a commission, composed of: the first-president and the presidents of the Court of Appeal of the Capital of the Country, that the applicant meets the legal conditions.
A special law shall determine the conditions and procedure by which foreigners acquire naturalization. Naturalization does not have retroactive effect. The wife and minor children benefit, under the conditions provided by law, from the naturalization of the husband or father.

content.text: The difference of religious beliefs and denominations, of ethnic origin and of language, does not constitute in Romania an impediment to acquire civil and political rights and to exercise them.
Only naturalization equates a foreigner with a Romanian for the exercise of political rights.

Naturalization is granted individually by the Council of Ministers, following the finding of a commission, composed of: the first-president and the presidents of the Court of Appeal of the Capital of the Country, that the applicant meets the legal conditions.

A special law shall determine the conditions and procedure by which foreigners acquire naturalization. Naturalization does not have retroactive effect. The wife and minor children benefit, under the conditions provided by law, from the naturalization of the husband or father.

1923:8.contentEn:
No distinction of birth or social class is admitted in the State. All Romanians, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, are equal before the law and obliged to contribute without distinction to public taxes and burdens.
Only they are admissible to public, civil and military functions and dignities. Special laws shall determine the Statute of public officials.
Foreigners may not be admitted to public functions except in exceptional cases and specifically established by law.

content.text: No distinction of birth or social class is admitted in the State. All Romanians, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, are equal before the law and obliged to contribute without distinction to public taxes and burdens.
Only they are admissible to public, civil and military functions and dignities. Special laws shall determine the Statute of public officials.
Foreigners may not be admitted to public functions except in exceptional cases and specifically established by law.

1923:9.contentEn: All foreigners on the territory of Romania enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.

content.text: All foreigners on the territory of Romania enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.

1923:10.contentEn:
All privileges of any nature, exemptions and class monopolies are abolished forever in the Romanian State.
Titles of nobility are and remain inadmissible in the Romanian State. Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the King's authorization.

content.text: All privileges of any nature, exemptions and class monopolies are abolished forever in the Romanian State.
Titles of nobility are and remain inadmissible in the Romanian State. Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the King's authorization.

1923:11.contentEn:
Individual liberty is guaranteed.
No one may be prosecuted or searched except in the cases and according to the forms prescribed by law.
No one may be detained or arrested except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant, which must be communicated to him at the time of arrest or at the latest within 24 hours after detention or arrest.
In case of flagrant offense, the definition or arrest may be made immediately, and the warrant shall be issued within 24 hours and communicated to him in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

content.text: Individual liberty is guaranteed.
No one may be prosecuted or searched except in the cases and according to the forms prescribed by law.
No one may be detained or arrested except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant, which must be communicated to him at the time of arrest or at the latest within 24 hours after detention or arrest.
In case of flagrant offense, the definition or arrest may be made immediately, and the warrant shall be issued within 24 hours and communicated to him in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

1923:12.contentEn: No one may be removed against his will from the judges given to him by law.

content.text: No one may be removed against his will from the judges given to him by law.

1923:13.contentEn:
The domicile is inviolable.
No search of the domicile may be made except by the competent authorities, in the cases specifically provided for by law and in accordance with the forms prescribed by it.

content.text: The domicile is inviolable.
No search of the domicile may be made except by the competent authorities, in the cases specifically provided for by law and in accordance with the forms prescribed by it.

1923:14.contentEn: No penalty may be established or applied except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No penalty may be established or applied except by virtue of a law.

1923:15.contentEn: No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property.

content.text: No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property.

1923:16.contentEn: The death penalty may not be reinstated except in the cases provided for in the Military Penal Code in time of war.

content.text: The death penalty may not be reinstated except in the cases provided for in the Military Penal Code in time of war.

1923:17.contentEn:
Property of any nature, as well as claims against the State, are guaranteed.
The public authority, on the basis of a law, is entitled to use, for the purpose of works of public interest, the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate for the damage caused to the surface, buildings and existing works. In the absence of agreement, the compensation shall be fixed by the courts.
No one may be expropriated except for reasons of public utility and after a fair and prior compensation established by the courts.
A special law shall determine the cases of public utility, the procedure and the manner of expropriation.
Apart from expropriation for communication routes, public health, defense of the country and works of military, cultural interest and those imposed by the direct general interests of the State and public administrations, other cases of public utility shall have to be established by laws passed by a two-thirds majority.
The existing laws regarding the alignment and widening of streets in communes as well as on the banks of waters flowing through or alongside them remain in force throughout the Kingdom.

content.text: Property of any nature, as well as claims against the State, are guaranteed.

The public authority, on the basis of a law, is entitled to use, for the purpose of works of public interest, the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate for the damage caused to the surface, buildings and existing works. In the absence of agreement, the compensation shall be fixed by the courts.
No one may be expropriated except for reasons of public utility and after a fair and prior compensation established by the courts.
A special law shall determine the cases of public utility, the procedure and the manner of expropriation.

Apart from expropriation for communication routes, public health, defense of the country and works of military, cultural interest and those imposed by the direct general interests of the State and public administrations, other cases of public utility shall have to be established by laws passed by a two-thirds majority.
The existing laws regarding the alignment and widening of streets in communes as well as on the banks of waters flowing through or alongside them remain in force throughout the Kingdom.

1923:18.contentEn: Only Romanians and naturalized Romanians may acquire and hold rural properties in Romania under any title. Foreigners shall only be entitled to the value of such properties.

content.text: Only Romanians and naturalized Romanians may acquire and hold rural properties in Romania under any title. Foreigners shall only be entitled to the value of such properties.

1923:19.contentEn:
Mineral deposits, as well as riches of any nature in the subsoil, are the property of the State. Common rock masses, construction material quarries, and peat deposits are excepted, without prejudice to the rights acquired by the State based on prior laws.
A special mining law shall determine the rules and conditions for the exploitation of these assets, shall fix the royalty for the surface owner, and shall simultaneously indicate the possibility and extent to which they shall participate in the exploitation of these riches.
Acquired rights shall be taken into account, insofar as they correspond to a valorization of the subsoil, and according to the distinctions to be made in the special law.
Mining concessions for exploitation, established or granted, in accordance with the laws in force today, shall be respected for the duration for which they were granted, and existing mining operations carried out by the owner only for as long as they exploit them. Perpetual concessions may not be made.
