All concessions and exploitations provided for in the preceding paragraph shall, however, comply with the rules to be established by law, which shall also provide for the maximum duration of such concessions and exploitations, which shall not exceed fifty years from the promulgation of this Constitution.

content.text: Mineral deposits, as well as riches of any nature in the subsoil, are the property of the State. Common rock masses, construction material quarries, and peat deposits are excepted, without prejudice to the rights acquired by the State based on prior laws.

A special mining law shall determine the rules and conditions for the exploitation of these assets, shall fix the royalty for the surface owner, and shall simultaneously indicate the possibility and extent to which they shall participate in the exploitation of these riches.
Acquired rights shall be taken into account, insofar as they correspond to a valorization of the subsoil, and according to the distinctions to be made in the special law.

Mining concessions for exploitation, established or granted, in accordance with the laws in force today, shall be respected for the duration for which they were granted, and existing mining operations carried out by the owner only for as long as they exploit them. Perpetual concessions may not be made.

All concessions and exploitations provided for in the preceding paragraph shall, however, comply with the rules to be established by law, which shall also provide for the maximum duration of such concessions and exploitations, which shall not exceed fifty years from the promulgation of this Constitution.

1923:20.contentEn:
Communication routes, the atmospheric space, and navigable and floatable waters are in the public domain.
The following are public goods: waters that can produce motive power and those that can be used in the public interest.
Acquired rights shall be respected or redeemed through expropriation for reasons of public utility, after fair and prior compensation.
Special laws shall determine the limit to which all the above rights may be left for the use of the owners, the modalities of exploitation, as well as the compensation due for the use of the surface and for the existing installation.

content.text: Communication routes, the atmospheric space, and navigable and floatable waters are in the public domain.
The following are public goods: waters that can produce motive power and those that can be used in the public interest.
Acquired rights shall be respected or redeemed through expropriation for reasons of public utility, after fair and prior compensation.

Special laws shall determine the limit to which all the above rights may be left for the use of the owners, the modalities of exploitation, as well as the compensation due for the use of the surface and for the existing installation.

1923:21.contentEn:
All factors of production enjoy equal protection.
The State may intervene, through laws, in the relations between these factors to prevent economic or social conflicts.
The freedom of labor shall be protected.
The law shall regulate the social insurance of workers in case of illness, accidents, and others.

content.text: All factors of production enjoy equal protection.
The State may intervene, through laws, in the relations between these factors to prevent economic or social conflicts.
The freedom of labor shall be protected.
The law shall regulate the social insurance of workers in case of illness, accidents, and others.

1923:22.contentEn:
Freedom of conscience is absolute.
The State guarantees all denominations equal freedom and protection insofar as their exercise does not infringe public order, good morals, and the laws of organization of the State.
The Romanian Orthodox Church and the Greek-Catholic Church are Romanian churches.
The Romanian Orthodox Church, being the religion of the great majority of Romanians, is the dominant church in the Romanian State; and the Greek-Catholic Church has precedence over the other denominations.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity with the Ecumenical Church of the East regarding dogmas.
Throughout the Kingdom of Romania, the Christian Orthodox Church shall have a unitary organization with the participation of all its constituent elements, clergy and laity.
A special law shall establish the fundamental principles of this unitary organization, as well as the manner in which the Church shall regulate, conduct, and administer, through its own bodies and under the control of the State, its religious, cultural, foundational, and trusteeship matters.
The spiritual and canonical matters of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be regulated by a single central synodal authority.
The metropolitans and bishops of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be elected according to a single special law.
The relations between the different denominations and the State shall be established by law.

content.text: Freedom of conscience is absolute.
The State guarantees all denominations equal freedom and protection insofar as their exercise does not infringe public order, good morals, and the laws of organization of the State.
The Romanian Orthodox Church and the Greek-Catholic Church are Romanian churches.

The Romanian Orthodox Church, being the religion of the great majority of Romanians, is the dominant church in the Romanian State; and the Greek-Catholic Church has precedence over the other denominations.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity with the Ecumenical Church of the East regarding dogmas.
Throughout the Kingdom of Romania, the Christian Orthodox Church shall have a unitary organization with the participation of all its constituent elements, clergy and laity.

A special law shall establish the fundamental principles of this unitary organization, as well as the manner in which the Church shall regulate, conduct, and administer, through its own bodies and under the control of the State, its religious, cultural, foundational, and trusteeship matters.
The spiritual and canonical matters of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be regulated by a single central synodal authority.
The metropolitans and bishops of the Romanian Orthodox Church shall be elected according to a single special law.
The relations between the different denominations and the State shall be established by law.

1923:23.contentEn:
Civil status acts are the responsibility of civil law.
The drawing up of these acts must always precede religious blessing.

content.text: Civil status acts are the responsibility of civil law.
The drawing up of these acts must always precede religious blessing.

1923:24.contentEn:
Education is free under the conditions established by special laws and insofar as it is not contrary to good morals and public order.
Primary education is compulsory. In State schools, this education shall be given free of charge.
The State, counties, and communes shall provide aid and facilities to students lacking means, at all levels of education, to the extent and in the manner provided by law.

content.text: Education is free under the conditions established by special laws and insofar as it is not contrary to good morals and public order.
Primary education is compulsory. In State schools, this education shall be given free of charge.
The State, counties, and communes shall provide aid and facilities to students lacking means, at all levels of education, to the extent and in the manner provided by law.

1923:25.contentEn:
The Constitution guarantees to all the freedom to communicate and publish their ideas and opinions through speech, writing, and the press, each being responsible for the abuse of these freedoms in the cases determined by the Penal Code, which in no case may restrict the right itself.
No exceptional law may be established in this matter.
Neither censorship nor any other preventive measure for the appearance, sale, or distribution of any publication may be established.
No prior authorization from any authority is required for the appearance of any publication.
No security shall be required from journalists, writers, publishers, printers, and lithographers.
The press shall never be placed under the regime of warnings. No newspaper or publication may be suspended or suppressed.
Any periodical publication of any nature must have a responsible director, and in his absence, a responsible editor. The director or editor must enjoy civil and political rights. The name of the director and the name of the editor shall appear visibly and permanently at the head of the publication.
Before the appearance of the periodical publication, its owner is obliged to declare and register his name with the commercial court.
The sanctions for these provisions shall be provided for by special laws.

content.text: The Constitution guarantees to all the freedom to communicate and publish their ideas and opinions through speech, writing, and the press, each being responsible for the abuse of these freedoms in the cases determined by the Penal Code, which in no case may restrict the right itself.
No exceptional law may be established in this matter.
Neither censorship nor any other preventive measure for the appearance, sale, or distribution of any publication may be established.
No prior authorization from any authority is required for the appearance of any publication.
No security shall be required from journalists, writers, publishers, printers, and lithographers.
The press shall never be placed under the regime of warnings. No newspaper or publication may be suspended or suppressed.

Any periodical publication of any nature must have a responsible director, and in his absence, a responsible editor. The director or editor must enjoy civil and political rights. The name of the director and the name of the editor shall appear visibly and permanently at the head of the publication.
Before the appearance of the periodical publication, its owner is obliged to declare and register his name with the commercial court.
The sanctions for these provisions shall be provided for by special laws.

1923:26.contentEn:
With regard to non-periodical publications, the author is responsible for his writings, in his absence the publisher; the owner of the printing house is responsible when the author and publisher have not been discovered.
For periodical publications, responsibility lies with: the author, the director, or the editor in the order of enumeration.
The owner in all cases is jointly and severally liable for the payment of civil damages.
Press offenses shall be tried by juries, except in the cases established herein, which shall be tried by ordinary courts, according to common law:
- a) Offenses that would be committed against the Sovereigns of the country, the Crown Prince, members of the Royal Family and Dynasty, Heads of Foreign States and their representatives;
- b) Direct incitements to murder and rebellion, in cases where they have not been followed by execution;
- c) Slanders, insults, defamations brought to private individuals or public officials, whoever they may be, affected in their private life or in their personal honor.
Preventive arrest in press matters is prohibited.

content.text: With regard to non-periodical publications, the author is responsible for his writings, in his absence the publisher; the owner of the printing house is responsible when the author and publisher have not been discovered.
For periodical publications, responsibility lies with: the author, the director, or the editor in the order of enumeration.
The owner in all cases is jointly and severally liable for the payment of civil damages.
Press offenses shall be tried by juries, except in the cases established herein, which shall be tried by ordinary courts, according to common law:
a) Offenses that would be committed against the Sovereigns of the country, the Crown Prince, members of the Royal Family and Dynasty, Heads of Foreign States and their representatives;
b) Direct incitements to murder and rebellion, in cases where they have not been followed by execution;
c) Slanders, insults, defamations brought to private individuals or public officials, whoever they may be, affected in their private life or in their personal honor.
Preventive arrest in press matters is prohibited.

1923:27.contentEn:
The secrecy of letters, telegrams, and telephone conversations is inviolable.
A special law shall establish the cases in which the judiciary, in the interest of criminal investigation, may make an exception to the present provision.
The same law shall determine the responsibility of State agents and private individuals for the violation of the secrecy of letters, telegrams, and telephone conversations.

content.text: The secrecy of letters, telegrams, and telephone conversations is inviolable.
A special law shall establish the cases in which the judiciary, in the interest of criminal investigation, may make an exception to the present provision.
The same law shall determine the responsibility of State agents and private individuals for the violation of the secrecy of letters, telegrams, and telephone conversations.

1923:28.contentEn:
Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin, language, or religion, have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms, conforming to the laws that regulate the exercise of this right, to discuss all kinds of issues; in this regard, no prior authorization is required.
Meetings in the open air are permitted, except in public squares and roads.
Meetings, processions, and demonstrations on public roads and squares are subject to police laws.

content.text: Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin, language, or religion, have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms, conforming to the laws that regulate the exercise of this right, to discuss all kinds of issues; in this regard, no prior authorization is required.
Meetings in the open air are permitted, except in public squares and roads.
Meetings, processions, and demonstrations on public roads and squares are subject to police laws.

1923:29.contentEn:
Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin, language, or religion, have the right to associate, conforming to the laws that regulate the exercise of this right.
The right of free association does not in itself imply the right to create legal persons.
The conditions under which legal personality is granted shall be established by a special law.

content.text: Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin, language, or religion, have the right to associate, conforming to the laws that regulate the exercise of this right.
The right of free association does not in itself imply the right to create legal persons.
The conditions under which legal personality is granted shall be established by a special law.

1923:30.contentEn:
Everyone has the right to address the public authorities by petitions signed by one or more persons, but may only petition in the name of the signatories.
Only constituted authorities have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

content.text: Everyone has the right to address the public authorities by petitions signed by one or more persons, but may only petition in the name of the signatories.
Only constituted authorities have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

1923:31.contentEn:
No prior authorization is necessary to prosecute public officials for acts of their administration by the injured parties, without prejudice to the special rules established with regard to ministers.
The cases and manner of prosecution shall be regulated by a specific law.
Special provisions in the Penal Code shall determine the penalties for superiors.

content.text: No prior authorization is necessary to prosecute public officials for acts of their administration by the injured parties, without prejudice to the special rules established with regard to ministers.
The cases and manner of prosecution shall be regulated by a specific law.
Special provisions in the Penal Code shall determine the penalties for superiors.

1923:32.contentEn:
No Romanian, without the authorization of the government, may enter the service of a foreign State without thereby losing his citizenship.
The extradition of political refugees is prohibited.

content.text: No Romanian, without the authorization of the government, may enter the service of a foreign State without thereby losing his citizenship.
The extradition of political refugees is prohibited.

1923:33.contentEn: All powers of the State emanate from the nation, which may only exercise them through delegation and according to the principles and rules laid down in the present Constitution.

content.text: All powers of the State emanate from the nation, which may only exercise them through delegation and according to the principles and rules laid down in the present Constitution.

1923:34.contentEn:
Legislative power is exercised collectively by the King and the National Representation.
The National Representation is divided into two Assemblies:
The Senate and the Assembly of Deputies.
Any law requires the consent of all three branches of the legislative power.
No law may be submitted to royal sanction until it has been freely debated and voted on by the majority of both Assemblies.

content.text: Legislative power is exercised collectively by the King and the National Representation.
The National Representation is divided into two Assemblies:
The Senate and the Assembly of Deputies.
Any law requires the consent of all three branches of the legislative power.
No law may be submitted to royal sanction until it has been freely debated and voted on by the majority of both Assemblies.

1923:35.contentEn:
The initiative for laws is given to each of the three branches of the legislative power.
However, any law relating to the revenues and expenditures of the State or to the army contingent must first be voted on by the Assembly of Deputies.

content.text: The initiative for laws is given to each of the three branches of the legislative power.
However, any law relating to the revenues and expenditures of the State or to the army contingent must first be voted on by the Assembly of Deputies.

1923:36.contentEn: The authoritative interpretation of laws is made only by the legislative power.

content.text: The authoritative interpretation of laws is made only by the legislative power.

1923:37.contentEn:
The promulgation of laws, voted by both Assemblies, shall be carried out by the Ministry of Justice, which shall keep one of the originals of the laws voted, and the second original shall be kept by the State Archives.
The Minister of Justice is also the keeper of the Great Seal of the State.
Each year, the Ministry of Justice shall publish the collection of laws and regulations, in which the laws shall be inserted bearing the serial number according to the date of promulgation.

content.text: The promulgation of laws, voted by both Assemblies, shall be carried out by the Ministry of Justice, which shall keep one of the originals of the laws voted, and the second original shall be kept by the State Archives.
The Minister of Justice is also the keeper of the Great Seal of the State.
Each year, the Ministry of Justice shall publish the collection of laws and regulations, in which the laws shall be inserted bearing the serial number according to the date of promulgation.

1923:38.contentEn: No law, nor any regulation of general, county or communal administration may be binding except after it is published in the manner prescribed by law.

content.text: No law, nor any regulation of general, county or communal administration may be binding except after it is published in the manner prescribed by law.

1923:39.contentEn: The executive power is entrusted to the King, who exercises it in the regular manner through the Constitution.

content.text: The executive power is entrusted to the King, who exercises it in the regular manner through the Constitution.

1923:40.contentEn:
The judicial power is exercised by its bodies.
Their decisions are rendered by virtue of the law and are executed in the name of the King.

content.text: The judicial power is exercised by its bodies.
Their decisions are rendered by virtue of the law and are executed in the name of the King.

1923:41.contentEn: The exclusively county or communal interests are regulated by the county or communal councils according to the principles laid down by the Constitution and by special laws.

content.text: The exclusively county or communal interests are regulated by the county or communal councils according to the principles laid down by the Constitution and by special laws.

1923:42.contentEn: The members of the Assemblies represent the nation.

content.text: The members of the Assemblies represent the nation.

1923:43.contentEn: The sessions of the Assemblies are public. Their regulations stipulate the cases and the manner in which these sessions may be declared secret.

content.text: The sessions of the Assemblies are public. Their regulations stipulate the cases and the manner in which these sessions may be declared secret.

1923:44.contentEn:
Each of the Assemblies verifies the titles of its members and adjudicates the disputes that arise in this regard.
No election may be invalidated except by two-thirds of the number of members present.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies verifies the titles of its members and adjudicates the disputes that arise in this regard.
No election may be invalidated except by two-thirds of the number of members present.

1923:45.contentEn: No one may be at the same time a member of one and the other Assembly.

content.text: No one may be at the same time a member of one and the other Assembly.

1923:46.contentEn:
Deputies and senators appointed by the executive power to a salaried position, which they accept, shall fully forfeit their mandate as representatives of the nation.
This provision shall not apply to ministers and undersecretaries of State. The electoral law shall determine the incompatibilities.

content.text: Deputies and senators appointed by the executive power to a salaried position, which they accept, shall fully forfeit their mandate as representatives of the nation.
This provision shall not apply to ministers and undersecretaries of State. The electoral law shall determine the incompatibilities.

1923:47.contentEn: At the beginning of each legislature and each ordinary session, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate shall elect from among their members the president, vice-presidents, and members of the bureaus, according to their internal regulations.

content.text: At the beginning of each legislature and each ordinary session, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate shall elect from among their members the president, vice-presidents, and members of the bureaus, according to their internal regulations.

1923:48.contentEn:
Any decision shall be taken by an absolute majority of votes, except in cases where a higher number of votes is required by the Constitution, laws, or regulations of the Legislative Bodies.
In case of a tie vote, the proposal under deliberation shall be rejected. The Assemblies shall hold sessions with a quorum of one half plus one of the number of members registered in the roll call.

content.text: Any decision shall be taken by an absolute majority of votes, except in cases where a higher number of votes is required by the Constitution, laws, or regulations of the Legislative Bodies.
In case of a tie vote, the proposal under deliberation shall be rejected. The Assemblies shall hold sessions with a quorum of one half plus one of the number of members registered in the roll call.

1923:49.contentEn:
Votes shall be cast by standing and sitting, viva voce, or by secret ballot.
A bill may only be adopted after it has been voted on article by article.

content.text: Votes shall be cast by standing and sitting, viva voce, or by secret ballot.
A bill may only be adopted after it has been voted on article by article.

1923:50.contentEn: Each of the Assemblies shall have the right of inquiry.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies shall have the right of inquiry.

1923:51.contentEn: The Assemblies shall have the right to amend and divide into several parts the articles and amendments proposed.

content.text: The Assemblies shall have the right to amend and divide into several parts the articles and amendments proposed.

1923:52.contentEn: Each member of the Assemblies shall have the right to address interpellations to the ministers, who shall be obliged to answer them within the term provided for by the regulations.

content.text: Each member of the Assemblies shall have the right to address interpellations to the ministers, who shall be obliged to answer them within the term provided for by the regulations.

1923:53.contentEn:
Anyone shall have the right to address petitions to the Assemblies through the bureau or one of its members.
Each of the Assemblies shall have the right to send the petitions addressed to it to the ministers. The ministers shall be obliged to provide explanations on their content whenever the Assembly so requests.

content.text: Anyone shall have the right to address petitions to the Assemblies through the bureau or one of its members.

Each of the Assemblies shall have the right to send the petitions addressed to it to the ministers. The ministers shall be obliged to provide explanations on their content whenever the Assembly so requests.

1923:54.contentEn: None of the members of one or the other Assembly may be prosecuted or persecuted for the opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising his mandate.

content.text: None of the members of one or the other Assembly may be prosecuted or persecuted for the opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising his mandate.

1923:55.contentEn:
No member of one or the other Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested in matters of repression, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrant delicto.
If he has been preventively arrested or prosecuted during the time when the session was closed, the prosecution or arrest must be submitted for the approval of the Assembly of which he is a member, immediately after the opening of the session of the Legislative Bodies.
The detention or prosecution of a member of one or the other Assembly is suspended throughout the session, if the Assembly so requests.

content.text: No member of one or the other Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested in matters of repression, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrant delicto.

If he has been preventively arrested or prosecuted during the time when the session was closed, the prosecution or arrest must be submitted for the approval of the Assembly of which he is a member, immediately after the opening of the session of the Legislative Bodies.
The detention or prosecution of a member of one or the other Assembly is suspended throughout the session, if the Assembly so requests.

1923:56.contentEn: Each Assembly determines, by its regulation, the manner in which it exercises its attributions.

content.text: Each Assembly determines, by its regulation, the manner in which it exercises its attributions.

1923:57.contentEn: Each of the Assemblies deliberates and takes decisions separately, except in the cases specifically specified in the present Constitution.

content.text: Each of the Assemblies deliberates and takes decisions separately, except in the cases specifically specified in the present Constitution.

1923:58.contentEn: Any meeting of one of the Legislative Bodies outside the time of the session of the other is null by law.

content.text: Any meeting of one of the Legislative Bodies outside the time of the session of the other is null by law.

1923:59.contentEn: Each of the two Assemblies has the exclusive right to exercise its own police through its president, who alone, after the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the guard on duty.

content.text: Each of the two Assemblies has the exclusive right to exercise its own police through its president, who alone, after the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the guard on duty.

1923:60.contentEn: No armed force may be placed at the doors or around one or the other of the Assemblies without its consent.

content.text: No armed force may be placed at the doors or around one or the other of the Assemblies without its consent.

1923:61.contentEn: The electoral law establishes all the conditions required to be an elector to the Assembly of Deputies and to the Senate, the incapacities and indignities, as well as the electoral procedure.

content.text: The electoral law establishes all the conditions required to be an elector to the Assembly of Deputies and to the Senate, the incapacities and indignities, as well as the electoral procedure.

1923:62.contentEn: The members of both legislative bodies are elected for four years.

content.text: The members of both legislative bodies are elected for four years.

1923:63.contentEn:
The per diem allowances of deputies and senators shall be established by law.

#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

content.text: The per diem allowances of deputies and senators shall be established by law.
#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

1923:64.contentEn: The Assembly of Deputies shall be composed of deputies elected by adult Romanian citizens, by universal, equal, direct, mandatory, and secret ballot, based on the representation of minorities.

content.text: The Assembly of Deputies shall be composed of deputies elected by adult Romanian citizens, by universal, equal, direct, mandatory, and secret ballot, based on the representation of minorities.

1923:65.contentEn:
The election of deputies shall be conducted in electoral districts. An electoral district may not be larger than a county.
The electoral law shall establish the number of deputies to be elected in each district in proportion to the population.

content.text: The election of deputies shall be conducted in electoral districts. An electoral district may not be larger than a county.
The electoral law shall establish the number of deputies to be elected in each district in proportion to the population.
To be eligible for the Assembly of Deputies, it is required:
a) To be a Romanian citizen;
b) To have the exercise of civil and political rights;
c) To be twenty-five years of age or older;
d) To be domiciled in Romania. The electoral law shall determine the incapacities.
#### Section 2. On the Senate

1923:67.contentEn: The Senate shall be composed of elected senators and senators by right.

content.text: The Senate shall be composed of elected senators and senators by right.

1923:68.contentEn:
All Romanian citizens of 40 years of age or older shall elect, in electoral districts that may not be larger than the county, by mandatory, equal, direct, and secret ballot, a number of senators.
The number of senators to be elected in each district, which may not be larger than a county, shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the population.

content.text: All Romanian citizens of 40 years of age or older shall elect, in electoral districts that may not be larger than the county, by mandatory, equal, direct, and secret ballot, a number of senators.
The number of senators to be elected in each district, which may not be larger than a county, shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the population.

1923:69.contentEn: The members elected to the county councils and the members elected to the urban and rural communal councils, assembled in a single college, shall elect, by mandatory, equal, direct, and secret ballot, one senator from each county.

content.text: The members elected to the county councils and the members elected to the urban and rural communal councils, assembled in a single college, shall elect, by mandatory, equal, direct, and secret ballot, one senator from each county.

1923:70.contentEn: The members of the chambers of commerce, industry, labor, and agriculture, assembled in separate colleges, shall elect from among themselves one senator from each category and for each electoral district. These special electoral districts shall be determined by the electoral law, their number not being able to exceed six.

content.text: The members of the chambers of commerce, industry, labor, and agriculture, assembled in separate colleges, shall elect from among themselves one senator from each category and for each electoral district. These special electoral districts shall be determined by the electoral law, their number not being able to exceed six.

1923:71.contentEn: Each university shall elect, from among its members, by the vote of its professors, one senator.

content.text: Each university shall elect, from among its members, by the vote of its professors, one senator.
The following shall be members of the Senate by right, by virtue of their High position in the State and Church:
a) The Heir to the Throne from the age of 18 years completed; however, he shall not have a deliberative vote until the age of 25 years completed;
b) The Metropolitans of the country;
c) The diocesan Bishops of the Romanian Orthodox and Greek-Catholic Churches, insofar as they are elected in accordance with the laws of the country;

d) The heads of the denominations recognized by the State, one from each denomination, insofar as they are elected or appointed in accordance with the laws of the country and represent a number of over 200,000 believers; as well as the superior religious representative of the Muslims in the Kingdom;
e) The President of the Romanian Academy. The mandate of these senators shall cease with the quality or dignity that attributes the right to them.
The following shall become senators by right:

a) Former Presidents of the Council, insofar as they have four years of service as titular Presidents of the Council, and former Ministers having at least six years of service in one or more governments;
b) Former Presidents of the Legislative Bodies who have exercised this dignity at least during eight ordinary sessions;
c) Former Senators and Deputies elected in at least ten legislatures, regardless of their duration;
d) Former First Presidents of the High Court of Cassation and Justice who have held this function or that of President at the Court of Cassation for five years;
e) Generals in reserve and in retirement:
Who have exercised command of an army in the face of the enemy, as titulars, for at least 3 months.
Who have fulfilled the function of Chief of the General Staff, or of Inspector General of the Army (Commander of the Army), in peacetime, for at least four years.
The number of those in the second category shall not be greater than four, taken in order of seniority, at existing vacancies;
f) Former Presidents of the National Assemblies of Chisinau, Cernauti and Alba-Iulia, who declared the Union.

1923:74.contentEn:
The verification of the fulfillment of the conditions of the senators by right shall be done by a commission composed of the Presidents of the sections of the High Court of Cassation and Justice, under the presidency of the First President of this High Court.
The finding shall be made ex officio, at the request of the President of the Senate or of those who have the right.
The President of the Senate shall register the senators by right on the basis of the Commission's conclusion. The Senate may discuss and signal to the Commission, for rectification, the errors found in the establishment of rights.

content.text: The verification of the fulfillment of the conditions of the senators by right shall be done by a commission composed of the Presidents of the sections of the High Court of Cassation and Justice, under the presidency of the First President of this High Court.
The finding shall be made ex officio, at the request of the President of the Senate or of those who have the right.

The President of the Senate shall register the senators by right on the basis of the Commission's conclusion. The Senate may discuss and signal to the Commission, for rectification, the errors found in the establishment of rights.
In order to be eligible for the Senate, it is required:
a) To be a Romanian citizen;
b) To have the exercise of civil and political rights;
c) To be 40 years of age completed;
d) To have domicile in Romania.
These conditions, apart from age, are also required for senators by right.
#### Section 3. On the Legislative Council

1923:76.contentEn:
A Legislative Council shall be established, whose mission is to assist in an advisory manner in the making and coordination of laws, emanating either from the executive power or from parliamentary initiative, as well as in the drafting of general regulations for the application of laws.
The consultation of the Legislative Council is mandatory for all draft laws, except for those concerning budgetary credits; however, if, within a term fixed by law, the Legislative Council does not give its opinion, the Assemblies may proceed to the discussion and approval of the drafts.
A special law shall determine the organization and mode of operation of the Legislative Council.

content.text: A Legislative Council shall be established, whose mission is to assist in an advisory manner in the making and coordination of laws, emanating either from the executive power or from parliamentary initiative, as well as in the drafting of general regulations for the application of laws.

The consultation of the Legislative Council is mandatory for all draft laws, except for those concerning budgetary credits; however, if, within a term fixed by law, the Legislative Council does not give its opinion, the Assemblies may proceed to the discussion and approval of the drafts.
A special law shall determine the organization and mode of operation of the Legislative Council.

1923:77.contentEn:
The constitutional powers of the King are credited in the direct and legitimate descending line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants.
The Descendants of His Majesty shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East.

content.text: The constitutional powers of the King are credited in the direct and legitimate descending line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants.
The Descendants of His Majesty shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East.

1923:78.contentEn:
In the absence of descendants in the male line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of his brothers or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding article.
If none of the brothers or their descendants are alive or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the King may indicate his successor from a sovereign dynasty in Europe with the Acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 79.
If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

content.text: In the absence of descendants in the male line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of his brothers or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding article.

If none of the brothers or their descendants are alive or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the King may indicate his successor from a sovereign dynasty in Europe with the Acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 79.
If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

1923:79.contentEn:
In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall meet immediately in a single Assembly, even without convocation, and no later than eight days from their meeting shall elect a King from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe.
The presence of three-quarters of the members who compose each of the two Assemblies and a majority of two-thirds of the members present are necessary in order to proceed with this election.
In the event that the Assembly has not been held within the term prescribed above, then on the ninth day, at noon, the united Assemblies shall proceed to the election regardless of the number of members present and by an absolute majority of votes.
If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following article shall be followed.
During the vacancy of the Throne, the united Assemblies shall appoint a royal lieutenancy, composed of three persons, which shall exercise the royal powers until the King ascends the Throne.
In all the cases mentioned above, the vote shall be secret.

content.text: In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall meet immediately in a single Assembly, even without convocation, and no later than eight days from their meeting shall elect a King from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe.
The presence of three-quarters of the members who compose each of the two Assemblies and a majority of two-thirds of the members present are necessary in order to proceed with this election.

In the event that the Assembly has not been held within the term prescribed above, then on the ninth day, at noon, the united Assemblies shall proceed to the election regardless of the number of members present and by an absolute majority of votes.
If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following article shall be followed.
During the vacancy of the Throne, the united Assemblies shall appoint a royal lieutenancy, composed of three persons, which shall exercise the royal powers until the King ascends the Throne.
In all the cases mentioned above, the vote shall be secret.

1923:80.contentEn:
Upon the death of the King, the Assemblies shall meet even without convocation no later than ten days after the declaration of death.
If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution for an epoch after those ten days, then the old Assemblies shall gather until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

content.text: Upon the death of the King, the Assemblies shall meet even without convocation no later than ten days after the declaration of death.

If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution for an epoch after those ten days, then the old Assemblies shall gather until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

1923:81.contentEn: From the date of the King's death until the taking of the oath of his successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the King shall be exercised, in the name of the Romanian people, by the ministers, assembled in council and under their responsibility.

content.text: From the date of the King's death until the taking of the oath of his successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the King shall be exercised, in the name of the Romanian people, by the ministers, assembled in council and under their responsibility.

1923:82.contentEn:
The King is of age at the age of eighteen years completed.
Upon his accession to the Throne, he shall first take the following oath in the bosom of the united Assemblies: "I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian people, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory."

content.text: The King is of age at the age of eighteen years completed.

Upon his accession to the Throne, he shall first take the following oath in the bosom of the united Assemblies: "I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian people, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory."

1923:83.contentEn:
The King, during his lifetime, may appoint a Regency, composed of three persons, who, after the death of the King, shall exercise the royal powers during the minority of the successor to the Throne. This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed in Art. 79 of the present Constitution.
The Regency shall also exercise the guardianship of the successor to the Throne during his minority.
If, upon the death of the King, the Regency has not been appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both united Assemblies shall appoint a Regency, proceeding according to the forms prescribed in Art. 79 of the present Constitution.
The members of the Regency shall not enter into office until they have solemnly taken before both united Assemblies the oath prescribed by Art. 82 of the present Constitution.

content.text: The King, during his lifetime, may appoint a Regency, composed of three persons, who, after the death of the King, shall exercise the royal powers during the minority of the successor to the Throne. This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed in Art. 79 of the present Constitution.
The Regency shall also exercise the guardianship of the successor to the Throne during his minority.

If, upon the death of the King, the Regency has not been appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both united Assemblies shall appoint a Regency, proceeding according to the forms prescribed in Art. 79 of the present Constitution.
The members of the Regency shall not enter into office until they have solemnly taken before both united Assemblies the oath prescribed by Art. 82 of the present Constitution.

1923:84.contentEn:
If the King is unable to reign, the ministers, after having legally established this impossibility, shall immediately convene the Assemblies.
These shall elect the Regency, which shall also form the guardianship.

content.text: If the King is unable to reign, the ministers, after having legally established this impossibility, shall immediately convene the Assemblies.
These shall elect the Regency, which shall also form the guardianship.

1923:85.contentEn: No amendment may be made to the Constitution during a Regency.

content.text: No amendment may be made to the Constitution during a Regency.

1923:86.contentEn:
The King may not at the same time be the Head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies.
Neither of the Assemblies may deliberate on this matter unless at least two-thirds of the members composing them are present, and the decision may only be taken with two-thirds of the votes of the members present.

content.text: The King may not at the same time be the Head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies.

Neither of the Assemblies may deliberate on this matter unless at least two-thirds of the members composing them are present, and the decision may only be taken with two-thirds of the votes of the members present.

1923:87.contentEn:
The Person of the King is inviolable. His ministers are responsible.
No act of the King may have force unless it is countersigned by a minister, who thereby becomes responsible for that act.

content.text: The Person of the King is inviolable. His ministers are responsible.
No act of the King may have force unless it is countersigned by a minister, who thereby becomes responsible for that act.

1923:88.contentEn:
The King appoints and dismisses his ministers.
He sanctions and promulgates the laws.
He may refuse his sanction.
He has the right of amnesty in political matters.
He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters, except as established with regard to ministers.
He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice.
He appoints or confirms public officials in accordance with the laws.
He may not create a new office without a special law.
He makes regulations necessary for the execution of the laws, without ever being able to modify or suspend the laws and may not exempt anyone from their execution.
He is the head of the armed forces.
He confers military ranks in accordance with the law.
He shall confer Romanian decorations in accordance with a specific law.
He has the right to mint coins in accordance with a special law.
He concludes with foreign States the conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and the like; however, in order for these acts to have binding authority, they must first be submitted to the Legislative Power and approved by it.

content.text: The King appoints and dismisses his ministers.
He sanctions and promulgates the laws.
He may refuse his sanction.
He has the right of amnesty in political matters.
He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters, except as established with regard to ministers.
He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice.
He appoints or confirms public officials in accordance with the laws.
He may not create a new office without a special law.
He makes regulations necessary for the execution of the laws, without ever being able to modify or suspend the laws and may not exempt anyone from their execution.
He is the head of the armed forces.
He confers military ranks in accordance with the law.
He shall confer Romanian decorations in accordance with a specific law.
He has the right to mint coins in accordance with a special law.

He concludes with foreign States the conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and the like; however, in order for these acts to have binding authority, they must first be submitted to the Legislative Power and approved by it.

1923:89.contentEn: The law fixes the civil list for the duration of each Reign.

content.text: The law fixes the civil list for the duration of each Reign.

1923:90.contentEn:
On the 15th of October of each year, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate meet without convocation, unless the King has convened them earlier.
The duration of each session is five months.
The King opens the session with a Message, to which the Assemblies make their responses.
The King pronounces the closure of the session.
He has the right to convene the Assemblies in extraordinary session.
He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at the same time or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors within two months and of the Assemblies within three months.
The King may adjourn the Assemblies; however, the adjournment may not exceed the term of one month, nor be renewed in the same session without the consent of the Assemblies.

content.text: On the 15th of October of each year, the Assembly of Deputies and the Senate meet without convocation, unless the King has convened them earlier.
The duration of each session is five months.
The King opens the session with a Message, to which the Assemblies make their responses.
The King pronounces the closure of the session.
He has the right to convene the Assemblies in extraordinary session.

He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at the same time or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors within two months and of the Assemblies within three months.
The King may adjourn the Assemblies; however, the adjournment may not exceed the term of one month, nor be renewed in the same session without the consent of the Assemblies.

1923:91.contentEn: The King has no powers other than those given to him by the Constitution.

content.text: The King has no powers other than those given to him by the Constitution.

1923:92.contentEn: The Government exercises executive power in the name of the King, in the manner established by the Constitution.

content.text: The Government exercises executive power in the name of the King, in the manner established by the Constitution.

1923:93.contentEn:
The assembled Ministers constitute the Council of Ministers, which is presided over, with the title of President of the Council of Ministers, by the one who has been tasked by the King with forming the government.
The ministerial departments and the Under-Secretariats of State may only be established and abolished by law.

content.text: The assembled Ministers constitute the Council of Ministers, which is presided over, with the title of President of the Council of Ministers, by the one who has been tasked by the King with forming the government.
The ministerial departments and the Under-Secretariats of State may only be established and abolished by law.

1923:94.contentEn: Only a person who is Romanian or who has acquired naturalization may be a minister.

content.text: Only a person who is Romanian or who has acquired naturalization may be a minister.

1923:95.contentEn: No member of the Royal Family may be a minister.

content.text: No member of the Royal Family may be a minister.

1923:96.contentEn:
If the ministers are not members of the Assemblies, they may take part in the debate of laws, without, however, having the right to vote.
The presence of at least one minister is necessary at the debates of the Assemblies.
The Assemblies may require the presence of ministers at their deliberations.

content.text: If the ministers are not members of the Assemblies, they may take part in the debate of laws, without, however, having the right to vote.
The presence of at least one minister is necessary at the debates of the Assemblies.
The Assemblies may require the presence of ministers at their deliberations.

1923:97.contentEn:
Under no circumstances may the verbal or written order of the King protect the minister from liability. Art. 98.  Each of the two Assemblies, as well as the King, has the right to request the prosecution of ministers and to send them before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, which alone, in united sections, is entitled to judge them, except for what will be established by laws regarding the exercise of the injured party and regarding crimes and offenses committed by ministers outside the exercise of their function.
The placing under prosecution of ministers by the Legislative Bodies may only be decided by a majority of two-thirds of the members present.
The investigation shall be carried out by a commission of the High Court of Cassation, composed of five members drawn by lot in united sections. This commission also has the power to qualify the facts and to decide whether or not to prosecute.
The prosecution, before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, shall be sustained by the public ministry.
The law on ministerial responsibility determines the cases of liability and the penalties applicable to ministers.

content.text: Under no circumstances may the verbal or written order of the King protect the minister from liability. Art. 98.  Each of the two Assemblies, as well as the King, has the right to request the prosecution of ministers and to send them before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, which alone, in united sections, is entitled to judge them, except for what will be established by laws regarding the exercise of the injured party and regarding crimes and offenses committed by ministers outside the exercise of their function.
The placing under prosecution of ministers by the Legislative Bodies may only be decided by a majority of two-thirds of the members present.

The investigation shall be carried out by a commission of the High Court of Cassation, composed of five members drawn by lot in united sections. This commission also has the power to qualify the facts and to decide whether or not to prosecute.
The prosecution, before the High Court of Cassation and Justice, shall be sustained by the public ministry.
The law on ministerial responsibility determines the cases of liability and the penalties applicable to ministers.

1923:99.contentEn:
Any party injured by a decree or a provision signed or countersigned by a minister that violates an express text of the Constitution or of a law, may request from the State, in accordance with common law, monetary compensation for the damage caused.
Either during the trial or after the pronouncement of the judgment, the minister may be called, at the request of the State, following the vote of one of the Legislative Bodies, before the ordinary courts, to civil liability for the damage claimed or suffered by the State.
The illegal act of the minister does not discharge the official who countersigned from joint liability, except in the case where the latter has drawn the Minister's attention, in writing.

content.text: Any party injured by a decree or a provision signed or countersigned by a minister that violates an express text of the Constitution or of a law, may request from the State, in accordance with common law, monetary compensation for the damage caused.

Either during the trial or after the pronouncement of the judgment, the minister may be called, at the request of the State, following the vote of one of the Legislative Bodies, before the ordinary courts, to civil liability for the damage claimed or suffered by the State.
The illegal act of the minister does not discharge the official who countersigned from joint liability, except in the case where the latter has drawn the Minister's attention, in writing.

1923:100.contentEn:
Under-Secretariats of State may be established.
The Under-Secretaries of State may take part in the debates of the Legislative Bodies under the responsibility of the ministers.

content.text: Under-Secretariats of State may be established.
The Under-Secretaries of State may take part in the debates of the Legislative Bodies under the responsibility of the ministers.

1923:101.contentEn:
No jurisdiction may be established except only by virtue of a specific law.
Extraordinary commissions and tribunals may not be created under any name and under any pretext for specific cases, whether civil or criminal, or for the purpose of judging specific persons.

content.text: No jurisdiction may be established except only by virtue of a specific law.
Extraordinary commissions and tribunals may not be created under any name and under any pretext for specific cases, whether civil or criminal, or for the purpose of judging specific persons.

1923:102.contentEn: For the entire Romanian State, there is only one Court of Cassation and Justice.

content.text: For the entire Romanian State, there is only one Court of Cassation and Justice.

1923:103.contentEn:
Only the Court of Cassation in united sections has the right to judge the constitutionality of laws and to declare inapplicable those that are contrary to the Constitution. The judgment on the unconstitutionality of laws is limited only to the case being judged.
The Court of Cassation shall rule, as in the past, on conflicts of attribution.
The right of appeal in cassation is of constitutional order.

content.text: Only the Court of Cassation in united sections has the right to judge the constitutionality of laws and to declare inapplicable those that are contrary to the Constitution. The judgment on the unconstitutionality of laws is limited only to the case being judged.
The Court of Cassation shall rule, as in the past, on conflicts of attribution.
The right of appeal in cassation is of constitutional order.

1923:104.contentEn: The judges are irremovable under the special conditions that the law will establish.

content.text: The judges are irremovable under the special conditions that the law will establish.

1923:105.contentEn:
The jury is established in all criminal matters and for political and press offenses, except for the cases established by the present Constitution.
The action for damages resulting from press facts and offenses can only be brought before the same jurisdiction of competence as the offense.

content.text: The jury is established in all criminal matters and for political and press offenses, except for the cases established by the present Constitution.
The action for damages resulting from press facts and offenses can only be brought before the same jurisdiction of competence as the offense.

1923:106.contentEn: Military justice is organized by special law.

content.text: Military justice is organized by special law.

1923:107.contentEn:
Special authorities of any kind, with administrative contentious attributions, cannot be established.
The administrative contentious is within the competence of the judicial power, according to the special law.
Anyone injured in their rights, either by an administrative act of authority, or by an act of management made in violation of the laws and regulations, or by bad faith of the administrative authorities to resolve the request relating to a right, may make a request to the judicial courts for the recognition of their right.
The bodies of the judicial power judge whether the act is illegal, may annul it or may pronounce civil damages until the date of restoration of the injured right, also having the competence to judge the claim for damages, either against the administrative authority called to court, or against the guilty official.
The judicial power has no competence to judge acts of government, as well as those of command with a military character.

content.text: Special authorities of any kind, with administrative contentious attributions, cannot be established.
The administrative contentious is within the competence of the judicial power, according to the special law.

Anyone injured in their rights, either by an administrative act of authority, or by an act of management made in violation of the laws and regulations, or by bad faith of the administrative authorities to resolve the request relating to a right, may make a request to the judicial courts for the recognition of their right.

The bodies of the judicial power judge whether the act is illegal, may annul it or may pronounce civil damages until the date of restoration of the injured right, also having the competence to judge the claim for damages, either against the administrative authority called to court, or against the guilty official.
The judicial power has no competence to judge acts of government, as well as those of command with a military character.

1923:108.contentEn:
The county and communal institutions are regulated by laws.
These laws will be based on administrative decentralization.
The members of the county councils and communal councils are elected by the Romanian citizens through universal, equal, direct, secret, mandatory vote and with the representation of the minority, according to the forms provided by law. To these may be added by law also members by right and co-opted members. Among the co-opted members there may also be adult women.

content.text: The county and communal institutions are regulated by laws.
These laws will be based on administrative decentralization.

The members of the county councils and communal councils are elected by the Romanian citizens through universal, equal, direct, secret, mandatory vote and with the representation of the minority, according to the forms provided by law. To these may be added by law also members by right and co-opted members. Among the co-opted members there may also be adult women.

1923:109.contentEn: No tax of any kind can be established and collected except on the basis of a law.

content.text: No tax of any kind can be established and collected except on the basis of a law.

1923:110.contentEn: Taxes may be established by law only for the benefit of the State, counties, communes, and public institutions performing State services.

content.text: Taxes may be established by law only for the benefit of the State, counties, communes, and public institutions performing State services.

1923:111.contentEn: Any county or communal charge or tax may only be levied with the consent of the county or communal councils, within the limits established by law.

content.text: Any county or communal charge or tax may only be levied with the consent of the county or communal councils, within the limits established by law.

1923:112.contentEn:
Privileges in matters of taxes may not be established.
No exception or reduction of tax may be established except by a law.
Monopolies may only be constituted by law and exclusively for the benefit of the State, counties, and communes.

content.text: Privileges in matters of taxes may not be established.
No exception or reduction of tax may be established except by a law.
Monopolies may only be constituted by law and exclusively for the benefit of the State, counties, and communes.

1923:113.contentEn: No fund for pensions or gratuities chargeable to the public treasury may be granted except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No fund for pensions or gratuities chargeable to the public treasury may be granted except by virtue of a law.

1923:114.contentEn:
Each year, the Assembly of Deputies closes the accounts and votes on the budget.
All revenues or expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and in the accounts.
If the budget is not voted on in a timely manner, the executive power shall provide for public services according to the budget of the previous year, without being able to continue with that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

content.text: Each year, the Assembly of Deputies closes the accounts and votes on the budget.
All revenues or expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and in the accounts.

If the budget is not voted on in a timely manner, the executive power shall provide for public services according to the budget of the previous year, without being able to continue with that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

1923:115.contentEn:
The preventive and management control of all revenues and expenditures of the State shall be exercised by the Court of Accounts, which shall submit to the Assembly of Deputies each year the general report summarizing the management accounts of the past budget, while also signaling the irregularities committed by ministers in the application of the budget.
The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Assembly of Deputies no later than two years after the end of each financial year.

content.text: The preventive and management control of all revenues and expenditures of the State shall be exercised by the Court of Accounts, which shall submit to the Assembly of Deputies each year the general report summarizing the management accounts of the past budget, while also signaling the irregularities committed by ministers in the application of the budget.
The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Assembly of Deputies no later than two years after the end of each financial year.

1923:116.contentEn: There is only one Court of Accounts for all of Romania.

content.text: There is only one Court of Accounts for all of Romania.

1923:117.contentEn: All funds originating from special funds, and which the government disposes of under various titles, must be included in the general budget of the State's revenues.

content.text: All funds originating from special funds, and which the government disposes of under various titles, must be included in the general budget of the State's revenues.

1923:118.contentEn: Financial laws shall be published in the Official Gazette as other laws and regulations of public administration.

content.text: Financial laws shall be published in the Official Gazette as other laws and regulations of public administration.

1923:119.contentEn:
Every Romanian, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, is part of one of the elements of the armed forces, according to special laws.
The armed force is composed of: the active army with its permanent cadres, its reserve and the militias.

content.text: Every Romanian, regardless of ethnic origin, language or religion, is part of one of the elements of the armed forces, according to special laws.
The armed force is composed of: the active army with its permanent cadres, its reserve and the militias.

1923:120.contentEn: The ranks, decorations and pensions of the military may only be taken away by virtue of a court sentence and in the cases determined by law.

content.text: The ranks, decorations and pensions of the military may only be taken away by virtue of a court sentence and in the cases determined by law.

1923:121.contentEn: The army contingent shall be voted for each year by both legislative bodies.

content.text: The army contingent shall be voted for each year by both legislative bodies.

1923:122.contentEn: A Superior Council of National Defence shall be established, which shall permanently attend to the measures necessary for the organization of national defence.

content.text: A Superior Council of National Defence shall be established, which shall permanently attend to the measures necessary for the organization of national defence.

1923:123.contentEn: No foreign armed troop may be admitted into the service of the State, nor may it enter or pass through the territory of Romania except by virtue of a specific law.

content.text: No foreign armed troop may be admitted into the service of the State, nor may it enter or pass through the territory of Romania except by virtue of a specific law.

1923:124.contentEn: The colors of the Romanian flag are: Blue, Yellow and Red, placed vertically.

content.text: The colors of the Romanian flag are: Blue, Yellow and Red, placed vertically.

1923:125.contentEn: The seat of the government is in the Capital of the country.

content.text: The seat of the government is in the Capital of the country.

1923:126.contentEn: The Romanian language is the official language of the Romanian State.

content.text: The Romanian language is the official language of the Romanian State.

1923:127.contentEn: No oath may be imposed on anyone except by virtue of a law, which also determines its wording.

content.text: No oath may be imposed on anyone except by virtue of a law, which also determines its wording.

1923:128.contentEn: The present Constitution may not be suspended, either in whole or in part. In case of danger to the State, a state of siege, general or partial, may be instituted by law.

content.text: The present Constitution may not be suspended, either in whole or in part. In case of danger to the State, a state of siege, general or partial, may be instituted by law.

1923:129.contentEn:
The Constitution may be revised in whole or in part on the initiative of the King or either of the legislative Assemblies.
Following this initiative, both Assemblies, meeting separately, shall decide by an absolute majority whether there is reason for the constitutional provisions to be revised.
As soon as the necessity of revision has been admitted, both legislative bodies shall elect from among their members a joint commission, which shall propose the texts of the Constitution to be submitted to revision.
After the report of this commission has been read in each Assembly, twice within an interval of fifteen days, both Assemblies meeting together, under the chairmanship of the oldest of the Presidents, in the presence of at least two-thirds of the total number of members composing them, by a majority of two-thirds, shall definitively determine which articles are to be submitted to revision.
Following this vote, the Assemblies are dissolved by law and the electoral body shall be convened within the term prescribed by the Constitution.

content.text: The Constitution may be revised in whole or in part on the initiative of the King or either of the legislative Assemblies.
Following this initiative, both Assemblies, meeting separately, shall decide by an absolute majority whether there is reason for the constitutional provisions to be revised.

As soon as the necessity of revision has been admitted, both legislative bodies shall elect from among their members a joint commission, which shall propose the texts of the Constitution to be submitted to revision.

After the report of this commission has been read in each Assembly, twice within an interval of fifteen days, both Assemblies meeting together, under the chairmanship of the oldest of the Presidents, in the presence of at least two-thirds of the total number of members composing them, by a majority of two-thirds, shall definitively determine which articles are to be submitted to revision.
Following this vote, the Assemblies are dissolved by law and the electoral body shall be convened within the term prescribed by the Constitution.

1923:130.contentEn:
The new Assemblies shall proceed in agreement with the King to amend the points submitted to revision.
In this case, the Assemblies may not deliberate unless at least two-thirds of the members of which they are composed are present, and no change may be adopted unless it obtains at least two-thirds of the votes.
The Assemblies elected for the revision of the Constitution have an ordinary constitutional duration and, in addition to the amendment of the Constitution, they also function as ordinary legislative Assemblies.
If the Revision Assemblies cannot fulfill their mission, the new Assemblies will have the same character.

content.text: The new Assemblies shall proceed in agreement with the King to amend the points submitted to revision.

In this case, the Assemblies may not deliberate unless at least two-thirds of the members of which they are composed are present, and no change may be adopted unless it obtains at least two-thirds of the votes.

The Assemblies elected for the revision of the Constitution have an ordinary constitutional duration and, in addition to the amendment of the Constitution, they also function as ordinary legislative Assemblies.
If the Revision Assemblies cannot fulfill their mission, the new Assemblies will have the same character.

The provisions of the agrarian laws relating to arable lands, forests and marshes expropriable in whole or in part, to their legal status, to the subsoil, to valuation, to the method of payment, etc., as formulated in the articles below of each of the agrarian laws, are and remain of a constitutional character, and which articles, in their entirety, form an integral part of the present Constitution and as such may only be amended in the forms provided for the revision of the Constitution, namely:

a) Art. 1 (one) para. II, Art. 2 (two), 3 (three) para. I, Art. 4 (four), 6 (six), 7 (seven), 8 (eight), 9 (nine), 10 (ten), 13 (thirteen), 14 (fourteen), 16 (sixteen), 18 (eighteen), 21 (twenty-one), 23 (twenty-three), 32 (thirty-two), 36 (thirty-six) and 69 (sixty-nine) of the law for agrarian reform in Oltenia, Muntenia, Moldova and Dobrogea (the Old Kingdom) of July 17, 1921;

b) Art. 2 (two), 4 (four), 5 (five), 6 (six), 7 (seven), 8 (eight), 9 (nine), 10 (ten), 11 (eleven), 12 (twelve), 13 (thirteen), 16 (sixteen), 45 (forty-five), 46 (forty-six), 47 (forty-seven), 48 (forty-eight), 49 (forty-nine), 50 (fifty) and 53 (fifty-three) of the law for agrarian reform in Bessarabia of March 13, 1920;

c) Art. 3 (three), 4 (four), 5 (five), 6 (six), 7 (seven), 8 (eight), 9 (nine), 10 (ten), 11 (eleven), 12 (twelve), 13 (thirteen), 14 (fourteen), 16 (sixteen), 18 (eighteen), 22 (twenty-two), 24 (twenty-four), 32 (thirty-two), 50 (fifty) and 85 (eighty-five) of the law for agrarian reform in Transylvania, Banat, Crisana and Maramures of July 30, 1921;

d) Art. 2 (two), 3 (three), 4 (four), 5 (five) point a para. I, Art. 6 (six), 7 (seven), 9 (nine), 10 (ten), 12 (twelve), 13 (thirteen), 29 (twenty-nine), 31 (thirty-one) and 55 (fifty-five) of the law for agrarian reform in Bukovina of July 30, 1921.

1923:132.contentEn:
In order to satisfy the normal needs for firewood and construction timber of the rural population of the Old Kingdom, Bessarabia and Bukovina, the State is obliged to allocate the necessary areas for this purpose from its forests in the plains, hills or mountains.
In the Old Kingdom and in Bukovina, where the State would not have forests, within a radius of twenty kilometers from the center of the commune, for the satisfaction of the above-mentioned needs, it, by derogation from Art. 7 letter c and Art. 8 letter a, b and c of the law for agrarian reform in Oltenia, Muntenia, Moldova and Dobrogea of July 17, 1921 and from Art. 5, point a, paragraph IV and from Art. 6 and 7 of the law for agrarian reform in Bukovina of July 30, 1921, may expropriate from the forests of legal persons, whether public or private, which would be in this radius and only in the absence of these, will expropriate proportionally from the forests of all private owners, located in this radius, but only within the limit of these needs and in all cases with respect to an untouchable area of one hundred hectares of each property.
The replanted or being replanted forests are not expropriable, whatever the owner.
The contracts of sale for the exploitation of expropriable forests that will be in force at the time of expropriation will be respected.
The forests thus expropriated remain the property of the State and will be administered and exploited by it to satisfy, according to the law and in the first line, the above needs.
The expropriation of these forests will be done with fair and prior compensation fixed by justice.
The manner of expropriation will be regulated by special law.

content.text: In order to satisfy the normal needs for firewood and construction timber of the rural population of the Old Kingdom, Bessarabia and Bukovina, the State is obliged to allocate the necessary areas for this purpose from its forests in the plains, hills or mountains.

In the Old Kingdom and in Bukovina, where the State would not have forests, within a radius of twenty kilometers from the center of the commune, for the satisfaction of the above-mentioned needs, it, by derogation from Art. 7 letter c and Art. 8 letter a, b and c of the law for agrarian reform in Oltenia, Muntenia, Moldova and Dobrogea of July 17, 1921 and from Art. 5, point a, paragraph IV and from Art. 6 and 7 of the law for agrarian reform in Bukovina of July 30, 1921, may expropriate from the forests of legal persons, whether public or private, which would be in this radius and only in the absence of these, will expropriate proportionally from the forests of all private owners, located in this radius, but only within the limit of these needs and in all cases with respect to an untouchable area of one hundred hectares of each property.
The replanted or being replanted forests are not expropriable, whatever the owner.
The contracts of sale for the exploitation of expropriable forests that will be in force at the time of expropriation will be respected.
The forests thus expropriated remain the property of the State and will be administered and exploited by it to satisfy, according to the law and in the first line, the above needs.
The expropriation of these forests will be done with fair and prior compensation fixed by justice.
The manner of expropriation will be regulated by special law.

1923:133.contentEn:
