The decree-laws are ratified: No. 3.902 of December 29, 1918, published in the Official Gazette No. 223 of December 30, 1918, regarding the granting of citizenship rights; No. 2.085 of May 22, 1919, published in the Official Gazette No. 33 of May 28, 1919, and No. 3.464 of August 12, 1919, published in the Official Gazette No. 93 of August 13, 1919, regarding the naturalization of Jews who lived in the Old Kingdom.
Also, all decree-laws of individual naturalization, which were made before the decrees specified above, are ratified.
The Jews, inhabitants of the Old Kingdom, who will not have regulated their naturalization within the term provided by decree-law No. 3.464 of August 12, 1919, will be able to make the declarations of naturalization according to decree-law No. 2.085 of May 22, 1919, within three months from the promulgation of this Constitution.

content.text: The decree-laws are ratified: No. 3.902 of December 29, 1918, published in the Official Gazette No. 223 of December 30, 1918, regarding the granting of citizenship rights; No. 2.085 of May 22, 1919, published in the Official Gazette No. 33 of May 28, 1919, and No. 3.464 of August 12, 1919, published in the Official Gazette No. 93 of August 13, 1919, regarding the naturalization of Jews who lived in the Old Kingdom.
Also, all decree-laws of individual naturalization, which were made before the decrees specified above, are ratified.

The Jews, inhabitants of the Old Kingdom, who will not have regulated their naturalization within the term provided by decree-law No. 3.464 of August 12, 1919, will be able to make the declarations of naturalization according to decree-law No. 2.085 of May 22, 1919, within three months from the promulgation of this Constitution.

1923:134.contentEn: Until the making of a law on the organization of the legislative Council provided for in this Constitution, the presentation of draft laws before the legislative Bodies will continue to be done in accordance with the laws and regulations in force today.

content.text: Until the making of a law on the organization of the legislative Council provided for in this Constitution, the presentation of draft laws before the legislative Bodies will continue to be done in accordance with the laws and regulations in force today.

1923:135.contentEn: Until the promulgation of the special law based on the principles of the present Constitution, the laws relating to the organization and adjudication of administrative disputes remain in force.

content.text: Until the promulgation of the special law based on the principles of the present Constitution, the laws relating to the organization and adjudication of administrative disputes remain in force.

1923:136.contentEn: A special law will fix the civil list of the First King of Greater Romania.

content.text: A special law will fix the civil list of the First King of Greater Romania.

1923:137.contentEn:
All codes and existing laws in the different parts of the Romanian State will be revised in order to bring them into harmony with the present Constitution and ensure legislative unity.
Until then, they remain in force.
From the day of the promulgation of the Constitution, however, those provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other acts contrary to those inscribed in the present Constitution are repealed.

content.text: All codes and existing laws in the different parts of the Romanian State will be revised in order to bring them into harmony with the present Constitution and ensure legislative unity.
Until then, they remain in force.
From the day of the promulgation of the Constitution, however, those provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other acts contrary to those inscribed in the present Constitution are repealed.

1923:138.contentEn:
The current National Assemblies, after the promulgation of the present Constitution, may function until the expiration of their legal mandate.
During this time, they will have to make, based on the principles of this Constitution, the electoral law.
Until the making of this law, the electoral decree-laws remain in force.

content.text: The current National Assemblies, after the promulgation of the present Constitution, may function until the expiration of their legal mandate.
During this time, they will have to make, based on the principles of this Constitution, the electoral law.
Until the making of this law, the electoral decree-laws remain in force.

1938:1.contentEn: The Kingdom of Romania is a National, unitary and indivisible State.

content.text: The Kingdom of Romania is a National, unitary and indivisible State.

1938:2.contentEn: The territory of Romania is inalienable.

content.text: The territory of Romania is inalienable.

1938:3.contentEn: The territory of Romania may not be colonised with populations of foreign stock.

content.text: The territory of Romania may not be colonised with populations of foreign stock.

1938:4.contentEn: All Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin and religious belief, are bound: to consider the Fatherland as the foremost basis of their purpose in life, to sacrifice themselves for the defence of its integrity, independence and dignity; to contribute through their work to its moral elevation and its economic advancement; to faithfully fulfil the public duties imposed on them by laws and to contribute willingly to the fulfilment of public duties, without which the existence of the State cannot live.

content.text: All Romanians, without distinction as to ethnic origin and religious belief, are bound: to consider the Fatherland as the foremost basis of their purpose in life, to sacrifice themselves for the defence of its integrity, independence and dignity; to contribute through their work to its moral elevation and its economic advancement; to faithfully fulfil the public duties imposed on them by laws and to contribute willingly to the fulfilment of public duties, without which the existence of the State cannot live.

1938:5.contentEn:
All Romanian citizens, without distinction as to ethnic origin and religious belief, are equal before the law, owing it respect and submission.
No one may consider himself released from his civil or military, public or private duties, on the basis of his religious belief or of any other kind.

content.text: All Romanian citizens, without distinction as to ethnic origin and religious belief, are equal before the law, owing it respect and submission.
No one may consider himself released from his civil or military, public or private duties, on the basis of his religious belief or of any other kind.

1938:6.contentEn:
No distinction of social class is admitted in the Romanian State.
Privileges in the establishment of taxes are prohibited. Reductions and increases in taxes may only be general and established by laws.

content.text: No distinction of social class is admitted in the Romanian State.
Privileges in the establishment of taxes are prohibited. Reductions and increases in taxes may only be general and established by laws.

1938:7.contentEn: No Romanian is permitted to advocate, orally or in writing, the change of the form of government of the State, the division or distribution of the property of others, exemption from taxes, or class struggle.

content.text: No Romanian is permitted to advocate, orally or in writing, the change of the form of government of the State, the division or distribution of the property of others, exemption from taxes, or class struggle.

1938:8.contentEn:
Priests, of any rite and religious belief, are prohibited from placing their spiritual authority at the service of political propaganda, both in places intended for worship and official functions, and outside of them.
Political propaganda, in places intended for worship, or on the occasion of religious manifestations, is not permitted to anyone.
Any political association on religious grounds or pretexts is prohibited.
Apart from the persons, conditions and forms provided for in the laws, no one may take or give oaths of allegiance.

content.text: Priests, of any rite and religious belief, are prohibited from placing their spiritual authority at the service of political propaganda, both in places intended for worship and official functions, and outside of them.
Political propaganda, in places intended for worship, or on the occasion of religious manifestations, is not permitted to anyone.
Any political association on religious grounds or pretexts is prohibited.
Apart from the persons, conditions and forms provided for in the laws, no one may take or give oaths of allegiance.

1938:9.contentEn:
A Romanian who, without prior authorisation from the Government, enters into any service of a foreign State, or joins a foreign military corporation, loses Romanian citizenship by full right.
Submission, for any length of time and resulting from any fact, to any foreign protection, entails the loss of Romanian citizenship by full right.
Romanian nationality lost under the conditions indicated here can only be regained through naturalisation.

content.text: A Romanian who, without prior authorisation from the Government, enters into any service of a foreign State, or joins a foreign military corporation, loses Romanian citizenship by full right.
Submission, for any length of time and resulting from any fact, to any foreign protection, entails the loss of Romanian citizenship by full right.
Romanian nationality lost under the conditions indicated here can only be regained through naturalisation.

1938:10.contentEn: Romanians enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of labor, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, freedom of association, and all freedoms from which rights arise under the conditions established by law.

content.text: Romanians enjoy freedom of conscience, freedom of labor, freedom of education, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, freedom of association, and all freedoms from which rights arise under the conditions established by law.

1938:11.contentEn:
Romanian nationality is acquired through marriage, through filiation, through recognition, and through naturalization.
Naturalization is granted by law on an individual basis; it has no retroactive effect. The wife benefits from the naturalization of her husband, and minor children from that of their parents.

content.text: Romanian nationality is acquired through marriage, through filiation, through recognition, and through naturalization.
Naturalization is granted by law on an individual basis; it has no retroactive effect. The wife benefits from the naturalization of her husband, and minor children from that of their parents.

1938:12.contentEn:
Individual liberty is guaranteed.
No one may be prosecuted or searched except in the cases and according to the forms established by law.
No one may be detained or arrested except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant communicated at the time of arrest, or at the latest within 24 hours after arrest.
In case of flagrant offense or urgency, detention or arrest may be made immediately, and the warrant shall be issued and communicated within 24 hours, in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

content.text: Individual liberty is guaranteed.
No one may be prosecuted or searched except in the cases and according to the forms established by law.
No one may be detained or arrested except by virtue of a reasoned judicial warrant communicated at the time of arrest, or at the latest within 24 hours after arrest.
In case of flagrant offense or urgency, detention or arrest may be made immediately, and the warrant shall be issued and communicated within 24 hours, in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

1938:13.contentEn: No one may be removed, against his will, from the judges provided by law.

content.text: No one may be removed, against his will, from the judges provided by law.

1938:14.contentEn:
The domicile is inviolable.
No search of the domicile may be made except by the competent authorities, in the cases and according to the forms established by law.

content.text: The domicile is inviolable.
No search of the domicile may be made except by the competent authorities, in the cases and according to the forms established by law.

1938:15.contentEn:
The death penalty shall be applied in time of war according to the code of military justice.
The Council of Ministers may decide to apply the provisions of the preceding paragraph also in peacetime, for attacks against the Sovereign, Members of the Royal Family, Heads of Foreign States and State dignitaries for reasons related to the exercise of the functions entrusted to them, as well as in cases of robbery with murder and political assassination.

content.text: The death penalty shall be applied in time of war according to the code of military justice.

The Council of Ministers may decide to apply the provisions of the preceding paragraph also in peacetime, for attacks against the Sovereign, Members of the Royal Family, Heads of Foreign States and State dignitaries for reasons related to the exercise of the functions entrusted to them, as well as in cases of robbery with murder and political assassination.

1938:16.contentEn:
Property of any nature, as well as claims against both individuals and the State, are inviolable and guaranteed as such.
Anyone may freely dispose of his property, according to the rules provided for in the laws.
Assets that are part of the public domain are administered and may not be alienated except according to the rules and forms established by law. No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property, except in cases of high treason and embezzlement of public funds.
No one may be expropriated except for reasons of public utility and after a fair and prior compensation established by the courts in accordance with the laws. By reason of public utility can only be understood that which is of such a nature as to benefit at the same time all and each in an actual or eventual manner.
Apart from cases of public utility for national defense, for works of military, sanitary, cultural interest, for land, nautical or air communication routes, for markets and public works provided for in the current laws, no other case may be established except by laws passed by both Assemblies with a majority of two-thirds.

content.text: Property of any nature, as well as claims against both individuals and the State, are inviolable and guaranteed as such.
Anyone may freely dispose of his property, according to the rules provided for in the laws.

Assets that are part of the public domain are administered and may not be alienated except according to the rules and forms established by law. No law may establish the penalty of confiscation of property, except in cases of high treason and embezzlement of public funds.

No one may be expropriated except for reasons of public utility and after a fair and prior compensation established by the courts in accordance with the laws. By reason of public utility can only be understood that which is of such a nature as to benefit at the same time all and each in an actual or eventual manner.

Apart from cases of public utility for national defense, for works of military, sanitary, cultural interest, for land, nautical or air communication routes, for markets and public works provided for in the current laws, no other case may be established except by laws passed by both Assemblies with a majority of two-thirds.

1938:17.contentEn: Mineral deposits as well as riches of any nature of the subsoil are the property of the State. Masses of common rocks, quarries of construction materials and peat deposits are excepted, without prejudice to the rights acquired by the State on the basis of previous laws. A mining law will determine the rules and conditions for the exploitation of these goods, will fix the rights of the owner which will be at least 50% of the royalty and of the price per hectare of the concession and will show, at the same time, the possibility and extent to which they will participate in the exploitation of these riches. The rights acquired in favor of the State on the basis of the concession acts concluded up to the present are respected.

content.text: Mineral deposits as well as riches of any nature of the subsoil are the property of the State. Masses of common rocks, quarries of construction materials and peat deposits are excepted, without prejudice to the rights acquired by the State on the basis of previous laws. A mining law will determine the rules and conditions for the exploitation of these goods, will fix the rights of the owner which will be at least 50% of the royalty and of the price per hectare of the concession and will show, at the same time, the possibility and extent to which they will participate in the exploitation of these riches. The rights acquired in favor of the State on the basis of the concession acts concluded up to the present are respected.

1938:18.contentEn: Major and minor roads, lanes that are the responsibility of the State, Counties, Municipalities and Communes, navigable or floatable rivers and streams, shores, accretions to the shore and places from which the sea water has receded, natural or artificial ports, banks where vessels dock, the atmospheric space, waters producing motive power for public use and generally all goods that are not private property are considered as dependencies of the public domain.

content.text: Major and minor roads, lanes that are the responsibility of the State, Counties, Municipalities and Communes, navigable or floatable rivers and streams, shores, accretions to the shore and places from which the sea water has receded, natural or artificial ports, banks where vessels dock, the atmospheric space, waters producing motive power for public use and generally all goods that are not private property are considered as dependencies of the public domain.

1938:19.contentEn:
Freedom of conscience is absolute.
The State guarantees to all denominations equal freedom and protection, insofar as their exercise does not prejudice public order, good morals and the Security of the State.
The Christian Orthodox Church and the Greek-Catholic Church are Romanian churches. The Christian Orthodox religion being the religion of the great majority of Romanians, the Orthodox Church is the dominant church in the Romanian State, and the Greek-Catholic Church has precedence over the other denominations.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity, with regard to dogmas, with the ecumenical church of the East.
The spiritual and canonical matters of the Romanian Orthodox Church are the responsibility of a single central synodal authority.
The relations between the different denominations and the State are the domain of special laws.

content.text: Freedom of conscience is absolute.
The State guarantees to all denominations equal freedom and protection, insofar as their exercise does not prejudice public order, good morals and the Security of the State.

The Christian Orthodox Church and the Greek-Catholic Church are Romanian churches. The Christian Orthodox religion being the religion of the great majority of Romanians, the Orthodox Church is the dominant church in the Romanian State, and the Greek-Catholic Church has precedence over the other denominations.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is and remains independent of any foreign hierarchy, while maintaining its unity, with regard to dogmas, with the ecumenical church of the East.
The spiritual and canonical matters of the Romanian Orthodox Church are the responsibility of a single central synodal authority.
The relations between the different denominations and the State are the domain of special laws.

1938:20.contentEn:
Civil status records are the responsibility of civil law.
The drawing up of these acts must always precede the religious blessing, which is obligatory for all members of the denominations.

content.text: Civil status records are the responsibility of civil law.
The drawing up of these acts must always precede the religious blessing, which is obligatory for all members of the denominations.

1938:21.contentEn:
Education is free under the conditions established by special laws and insofar as it is not contrary to good morals, public order and State interests.
Primary education is compulsory. In State schools this education will be free.

content.text: Education is free under the conditions established by special laws and insofar as it is not contrary to good morals, public order and State interests.
Primary education is compulsory. In State schools this education will be free.

1938:22.contentEn: The Constitution guarantees to everyone, within the limits and conditions of the law, the freedom to communicate and publish his ideas and opinions, by speech, by writing, by images, by sounds or by any other means.

content.text: The Constitution guarantees to everyone, within the limits and conditions of the law, the freedom to communicate and publish his ideas and opinions, by speech, by writing, by images, by sounds or by any other means.

1938:23.contentEn: The secrecy of letters, telegrams and telephone conversations is inviolable. Exceptions are the cases in which justice is obliged to obtain information according to the law.

content.text: The secrecy of letters, telegrams and telephone conversations is inviolable. Exceptions are the cases in which justice is obliged to obtain information according to the law.

1938:24.contentEn:
Romanian citizens have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms to discuss all kinds of matters, in accordance with the laws governing the exercise of this right.
Meetings, processions and demonstrations on public roads or in the open air are subject to the laws and police regulations.

content.text: Romanian citizens have the right to assemble peacefully and without arms to discuss all kinds of matters, in accordance with the laws governing the exercise of this right.
Meetings, processions and demonstrations on public roads or in the open air are subject to the laws and police regulations.

1938:25.contentEn:
Anyone has the right to address petitions, subscribed by one or more persons, to the public authorities, but only in the name of those subscribed.
The constituted authorities alone have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

content.text: Anyone has the right to address petitions, subscribed by one or more persons, to the public authorities, but only in the name of those subscribed.
The constituted authorities alone have the right to address petitions in a collective name.

1938:26.contentEn:
Romanian citizens have the right to associate, in compliance with the laws.
The right of free association does not imply the right to create legal persons. The conditions under which legal personality is granted shall be established by law.

content.text: Romanian citizens have the right to associate, in compliance with the laws.
The right of free association does not imply the right to create legal persons. The conditions under which legal personality is granted shall be established by law.

1938:27.contentEn:
Only Romanian citizens are admissible to public, civil and military functions and dignities, taking into account the majority and State-creating character of the Romanian Nation.
Foreign subjects may not hold such functions, except in the cases established by law.
Foreigners on Romanian soil enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.
Only Romanians and naturalized Romanians may acquire by any title and own rural real estate in Romania. Foreigners shall only be entitled to the value of such real estate.

content.text: Only Romanian citizens are admissible to public, civil and military functions and dignities, taking into account the majority and State-creating character of the Romanian Nation.
Foreign subjects may not hold such functions, except in the cases established by law.
Foreigners on Romanian soil enjoy the protection given by law to persons and property in general.
Only Romanians and naturalized Romanians may acquire by any title and own rural real estate in Romania. Foreigners shall only be entitled to the value of such real estate.

1938:28.contentEn:
Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the King's authorization.
Insignia, emblems, medals, as well as uniforms of any kind, may not be created, attributed, or worn without lawful approval.

content.text: Foreign decorations shall be worn by Romanians only with the King's authorization.
Insignia, emblems, medals, as well as uniforms of any kind, may not be created, attributed, or worn without lawful approval.

1938:29.contentEn:
All powers of the State emanate from the Romanian Nation.
However, they may only be exercised by delegation and only according to the principles and rules laid down in this Constitution.

content.text: All powers of the State emanate from the Romanian Nation.
However, they may only be exercised by delegation and only according to the principles and rules laid down in this Constitution.

1938:30.contentEn: The King is the Head of State.

content.text: The King is the Head of State.

1938:31.contentEn:
Legislative power is exercised by the King through the National Representation, which is divided into two Assemblies: the Senate and the Assembly of Deputies.
The King sanctions and promulgates the laws.
Before the royal sanction is given, the law is not valid.
The King may refuse sanction.
No law may be submitted to royal sanction until it has been discussed and voted on by a majority of both Assemblies.
The promulgation of laws voted by both Assemblies shall be carried out by the Minister of Justice, who is also the keeper of the Great Seal of the State.
The initiative for laws is given to the King. Each of the two Assemblies may propose, on its own initiative, only laws in the public interest of the State.
The interpretation of laws with the right of authority is done only by the legislative power.
No law or regulation of general or communal administration may be binding until it is published in the manner prescribed therein.

content.text: Legislative power is exercised by the King through the National Representation, which is divided into two Assemblies: the Senate and the Assembly of Deputies.
The King sanctions and promulgates the laws.
Before the royal sanction is given, the law is not valid.
The King may refuse sanction.
No law may be submitted to royal sanction until it has been discussed and voted on by a majority of both Assemblies.
The promulgation of laws voted by both Assemblies shall be carried out by the Minister of Justice, who is also the keeper of the Great Seal of the State.
The initiative for laws is given to the King. Each of the two Assemblies may propose, on its own initiative, only laws in the public interest of the State.
The interpretation of laws with the right of authority is done only by the legislative power.
No law or regulation of general or communal administration may be binding until it is published in the manner prescribed therein.

1938:32.contentEn: Executive power is entrusted to the King, who exercises it through His Government in the manner established by the Constitution.

content.text: Executive power is entrusted to the King, who exercises it through His Government in the manner established by the Constitution.

1938:33.contentEn:
Judicial power is exercised by its bodies.
Judicial decisions are pronounced by virtue of the law.
They are executed in the name of the King.

content.text: Judicial power is exercised by its bodies.
Judicial decisions are pronounced by virtue of the law.
They are executed in the name of the King.

1938:34.contentEn:
The constitutional powers of the King are hereditary in the direct and legitimate descending line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants.
The descendants of His Majesty shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East. Art. 35. -In the absence of descendants in the male line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of His brothers, or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding article.
If none of the brothers or their descendants are alive, or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the King may indicate His successor from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe, with the acceptance of the National Representation given in the form prescribed by Art. 36.
If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

content.text: The constitutional powers of the King are hereditary in the direct and legitimate descending line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, from male to male by order of primogeniture and with the perpetual exclusion of women and their descendants.

The descendants of His Majesty shall be raised in the Orthodox religion of the East. Art. 35. -In the absence of descendants in the male line of His Majesty King Carol I of Hohenzollern Sigmaringen, the succession to the Throne shall be vested in the eldest of His brothers, or their descendants, according to the rules established in the preceding article.

If none of the brothers or their descendants are alive, or declare in advance that they do not accept the Throne, then the King may indicate His successor from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe, with the acceptance of the National Representation given in the form prescribed by Art. 36.
If neither one nor the other takes place, the Throne is vacant.

1938:36.contentEn:
In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall meet immediately in a single Assembly even without convocation and, no later than eight days from their meeting, shall elect a King from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe.
The presence of three-quarters of the members composing each of the two Assemblies and a majority of two-thirds of the members present are necessary in order to proceed with this election. In the event that the Assembly has not been held within the term described above, then on the ninth day at noon the Assemblies meeting shall proceed to the election, whatever the number of members present and with the absolute majority of votes.
If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following article shall be followed.
During the vacancy of the Throne, the Assemblies meeting shall appoint a Royal Lieutenancy composed of three persons, which shall exercise the Royal powers until the King's accession to the Throne.
In all the above cases the vote shall be secret.

content.text: In the event of a vacancy of the Throne, both Assemblies shall meet immediately in a single Assembly even without convocation and, no later than eight days from their meeting, shall elect a King from a sovereign dynasty in Western Europe.

The presence of three-quarters of the members composing each of the two Assemblies and a majority of two-thirds of the members present are necessary in order to proceed with this election. In the event that the Assembly has not been held within the term described above, then on the ninth day at noon the Assemblies meeting shall proceed to the election, whatever the number of members present and with the absolute majority of votes.
If the Assemblies are dissolved at the time of the vacancy of the Throne, the procedure prescribed in the following article shall be followed.
During the vacancy of the Throne, the Assemblies meeting shall appoint a Royal Lieutenancy composed of three persons, which shall exercise the Royal powers until the King's accession to the Throne.
In all the above cases the vote shall be secret.

1938:37.contentEn:
Upon the death of the King, the Assemblies shall meet even without convocation, no later than ten days after the declaration of death.
If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution, for an epoch after those ten days, then the old Assemblies shall meet until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

content.text: Upon the death of the King, the Assemblies shall meet even without convocation, no later than ten days after the declaration of death.

If, by chance, they have been dissolved beforehand and their convocation has been decided in the act of dissolution, for an epoch after those ten days, then the old Assemblies shall meet until the meeting of those who are to replace them.

1938:38.contentEn:
From the date of the King's death until the taking of the Oath by His successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the King shall be exercised in the name of the Romanian Nation by the Ministers meeting in council and under their responsibility. Art. 39. -The King is of age at the age of 18 years completed.
Upon His accession to the Throne He shall first take the following Oath before the Assemblies meeting: «I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian Nation, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory».

content.text: From the date of the King's death until the taking of the Oath by His successor to the Throne, the constitutional powers of the King shall be exercised in the name of the Romanian Nation by the Ministers meeting in council and under their responsibility. Art. 39. -The King is of age at the age of 18 years completed.

Upon His accession to the Throne He shall first take the following Oath before the Assemblies meeting: «I swear to uphold the Constitution and laws of the Romanian Nation, to maintain its national rights and the integrity of the territory».

1938:40.contentEn:
The King may appoint a Regent and two Alternates who, after His death, shall exercise the Royal powers during the minority of His successor.
This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 36 of the present Constitution.
The Regent shall also exercise the guardianship of the minor King.
If, upon the death of the King, the Regent has not been appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both Assemblies meeting shall appoint the Regent and the two Alternates, proceeding according to the forms prescribed by Art. 36 of the present Constitution.
The Regent and His alternates shall not enter into office until they have taken before both Assemblies meeting, the oath prescribed by Art. 39 of the present Constitution.
In the event of the death of the Regent, the eldest Alternate shall by right take his place, proceeding in accordance with the provisions of the preceding paragraph for the election of a new Alternate.

content.text: The King may appoint a Regent and two Alternates who, after His death, shall exercise the Royal powers during the minority of His successor.
This appointment shall be made with the acceptance of the National Representation, given in the form prescribed by Art. 36 of the present Constitution.
The Regent shall also exercise the guardianship of the minor King.

If, upon the death of the King, the Regent has not been appointed and the successor to the Throne is a minor, both Assemblies meeting shall appoint the Regent and the two Alternates, proceeding according to the forms prescribed by Art. 36 of the present Constitution.
The Regent and His alternates shall not enter into office until they have taken before both Assemblies meeting, the oath prescribed by Art. 39 of the present Constitution.

In the event of the death of the Regent, the eldest Alternate shall by right take his place, proceeding in accordance with the provisions of the preceding paragraph for the election of a new Alternate.

1938:41.contentEn: If the King is unable to reign, the Heir Apparent, if of age, shall by right take the Regency. If the Heir Apparent is a minor, the Council of Ministers, after legally establishing the impossibility of reigning, shall immediately convene the Legislative Assemblies reunited to decide.

content.text: If the King is unable to reign, the Heir Apparent, if of age, shall by right take the Regency. If the Heir Apparent is a minor, the Council of Ministers, after legally establishing the impossibility of reigning, shall immediately convene the Legislative Assemblies reunited to decide.

1938:42.contentEn: No amendment may be made to the Constitution during a Regency.

content.text: No amendment may be made to the Constitution during a Regency.

1938:43.contentEn:
The King may not at the same time be the Head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies.
Neither Assembly may deliberate on this matter unless at least two-thirds of its members are present, and the decision may only be taken by two-thirds of the votes of the members present.

content.text: The King may not at the same time be the Head of another State without the consent of the Assemblies.
Neither Assembly may deliberate on this matter unless at least two-thirds of its members are present, and the decision may only be taken by two-thirds of the votes of the members present.

1938:44.contentEn:
The Person of the King is inviolable. His Ministers are accountable.
The King's State acts shall be countersigned by a Minister who, by that very act, becomes accountable for them.
The appointment of the Prime Minister is excepted, which shall not be countersigned.

content.text: The Person of the King is inviolable. His Ministers are accountable.
The King's State acts shall be countersigned by a Minister who, by that very act, becomes accountable for them.
The appointment of the Prime Minister is excepted, which shall not be countersigned.

1938:45.contentEn:
The King convenes the Legislative Assemblies at least once a year, opening the session by message, to which the Assemblies present their response.
The King pronounces the closure of the session.
He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at once or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors and of the new Assemblies.
The King may adjourn the Assemblies. They shall meet as of right upon the expiry of one year from the date of the adjournment, if they have not been convened in the meantime.

content.text: The King convenes the Legislative Assemblies at least once a year, opening the session by message, to which the Assemblies present their response.
The King pronounces the closure of the session.
He has the right to dissolve both Assemblies at once or only one of them. The act of dissolution must contain the convocation of the electors and of the new Assemblies.
The King may adjourn the Assemblies. They shall meet as of right upon the expiry of one year from the date of the adjournment, if they have not been convened in the meantime.

1938:46.contentEn:
The King appoints and dismisses His Ministers.
He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters, except as established with regard to Ministers.
He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice.
He appoints or confirms public officials in accordance with the laws.
He may not create a new office without a special law.
He makes the regulations necessary for the execution of the laws, without being able to modify the laws or exempt anyone from their execution.
He may, while the Legislative Assemblies are dissolved and in the interval between sessions, make Decrees with the force of law in any respect, which shall be submitted to the Assemblies for ratification at their nearest session.
He is the Head of the Army.
He has the right to declare war and conclude peace.
He confers military ranks in accordance with the law.
He confers Romanian decorations.
He accredits Ambassadors and Ministers Plenipotentiary to the Heads of Foreign States.
He has the right to mint coins, according to a special law.
He concludes political and military treaties with Foreign States. However, the conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and the like concluded by Him, in order to have the force of law internally, must be submitted to the Legislative Assemblies and approved by them.

content.text: The King appoints and dismisses His Ministers.
He has the right to pardon or reduce sentences in criminal matters, except as established with regard to Ministers.
He may not suspend the course of prosecution or judgment, nor intervene in any way in the administration of justice.
He appoints or confirms public officials in accordance with the laws.
He may not create a new office without a special law.
He makes the regulations necessary for the execution of the laws, without being able to modify the laws or exempt anyone from their execution.

He may, while the Legislative Assemblies are dissolved and in the interval between sessions, make Decrees with the force of law in any respect, which shall be submitted to the Assemblies for ratification at their nearest session.
He is the Head of the Army.
He has the right to declare war and conclude peace.
He confers military ranks in accordance with the law.
He confers Romanian decorations.
He accredits Ambassadors and Ministers Plenipotentiary to the Heads of Foreign States.
He has the right to mint coins, according to a special law.

He concludes political and military treaties with Foreign States. However, the conventions necessary for commerce, navigation and the like concluded by Him, in order to have the force of law internally, must be submitted to the Legislative Assemblies and approved by them.

1938:47.contentEn: The civil list shall be determined by law.

content.text: The civil list shall be determined by law.

1938:48.contentEn: The members of the Legislative Assemblies represent the Nation and shall not exercise their mandate until after taking the lawful oath.

content.text: The members of the Legislative Assemblies represent the Nation and shall not exercise their mandate until after taking the lawful oath.

1938:49.contentEn: Each Assembly shall determine by its regulations the manner in which it is constituted and exercises its attributions.

content.text: Each Assembly shall determine by its regulations the manner in which it is constituted and exercises its attributions.

1938:50.contentEn: The validation of the elections for both Assemblies and the verification of the titles of the members belonging to each of them shall be carried out by the High Court of Cassation and Justice, before the date set for the meeting of the Assemblies.

content.text: The validation of the elections for both Assemblies and the verification of the titles of the members belonging to each of them shall be carried out by the High Court of Cassation and Justice, before the date set for the meeting of the Assemblies.

1938:51.contentEn: No one may be a member of one and the other Assembly at the same time.

content.text: No one may be a member of one and the other Assembly at the same time.

1938:52.contentEn:
Deputies and Senators, appointed by the executive power to a salaried position that they receive, shall lose, by the force of law, their mandate as representatives of the Nation.
This provision shall not apply to Ministers and Undersecretaries of State.

content.text: Deputies and Senators, appointed by the executive power to a salaried position that they receive, shall lose, by the force of law, their mandate as representatives of the Nation.
This provision shall not apply to Ministers and Undersecretaries of State.

1938:53.contentEn: The members of the Legislative Assemblies may not defend private interests against the State; they may not be part of the Boards of Directors of enterprises that have contracted with the State, Counties or Communes.

content.text: The members of the Legislative Assemblies may not defend private interests against the State; they may not be part of the Boards of Directors of enterprises that have contracted with the State, Counties or Communes.

1938:54.contentEn:
Any decision shall be taken by an absolute majority of votes, except in the cases otherwise established by the Constitution. In case of parity of votes, the proposal under deliberation is rejected.
The Assemblies shall hold sessions with one half plus one of the number of members registered in the roll call.

content.text: Any decision shall be taken by an absolute majority of votes, except in the cases otherwise established by the Constitution. In case of parity of votes, the proposal under deliberation is rejected.
The Assemblies shall hold sessions with one half plus one of the number of members registered in the roll call.

1938:55.contentEn: Each member of the Assemblies has the right to address questions to the Ministers, to which they are obliged to answer within the term provided by the regulations.

content.text: Each member of the Assemblies has the right to address questions to the Ministers, to which they are obliged to answer within the term provided by the regulations.

1938:56.contentEn: None of the members of one or the other Assembly may be prosecuted for the opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising the mandate.

content.text: None of the members of one or the other Assembly may be prosecuted for the opinions and votes expressed by him in the course of exercising the mandate.

1938:57.contentEn:
No member of one or the other Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested for criminal offences, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrante delicto.
The detention or prosecution of any of the members of the Assemblies shall be suspended throughout the session, if the Assembly so requests.

content.text: No member of one or the other Assembly may, during the session, be prosecuted or arrested for criminal offences, except with the authorization of the Assembly of which he is a member, except in the case of flagrante delicto.
The detention or prosecution of any of the members of the Assemblies shall be suspended throughout the session, if the Assembly so requests.

1938:58.contentEn: Each Assembly deliberates and decides separately, except in the cases specifically indicated in the present Constitution.

content.text: Each Assembly deliberates and decides separately, except in the cases specifically indicated in the present Constitution.

1938:59.contentEn: The police of the Assemblies is exercised by the President of each of them who, alone, after the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the respective guard.

content.text: The police of the Assemblies is exercised by the President of each of them who, alone, after the approval of the Assembly, may give orders to the respective guard.

1938:60.contentEn:
The per diems of the Deputies and Senators are established by law.

#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

content.text: The per diems of the Deputies and Senators are established by law.
#### Section 1. On the Assembly of Deputies

1938:61.contentEn:
The Assembly of Deputies is composed of Deputies elected by the Romanian citizens, who are 30 years of age and effectively practice an occupation falling into one of the following three categories:
1. Agriculture and manual labor;
2. Commerce and industry;
3. Intellectual occupations.
The election is made by secret, mandatory vote expressed through a single-member ballot, in constituencies that ensure the representation of the type of occupation of the voters.
The electoral law will fix the constituencies and will establish, according to the rules imposed above, the conditions required to be a voter, for men and women, the incapacities, forfeitures, incompatibilities, the voting procedure and the guarantees of the freedom of elections, as well as the number of deputies.
The duration of the mandate is six years.

content.text: The Assembly of Deputies is composed of Deputies elected by the Romanian citizens, who are 30 years of age and effectively practice an occupation falling into one of the following three categories:
Agriculture and manual labor;
Commerce and industry;
Intellectual occupations.
The election is made by secret, mandatory vote expressed through a single-member ballot, in constituencies that ensure the representation of the type of occupation of the voters.

The electoral law will fix the constituencies and will establish, according to the rules imposed above, the conditions required to be a voter, for men and women, the incapacities, forfeitures, incompatibilities, the voting procedure and the guarantees of the freedom of elections, as well as the number of deputies.
The duration of the mandate is six years.
In order to be eligible in the Assembly of Deputies, it is required:
a) To be a Romanian citizen;

b) To have the exercise of civil and political rights and to effectively practice the respective occupation of one of the three categories indicated in the previous article, whose voters they are to represent;
c) To be 30 years of age;
d) To be domiciled in Romania.
The incapacities, temporary or definitive forfeitures and incompatibilities shall be established by the electoral law.
#### Section 2. On the Senate

1938:63.contentEn: The Senate is composed of Senators appointed by the King, Senators by right and Senators elected by mandatory, secret vote expressed through a single-member ballot by the members of the bodies constituted in the State, in the number and conditions, for the voters and eligible persons, men and women, that will be established by the electoral law. The proportion of appointed Senators in relation to the elected ones is half.

content.text: The Senate is composed of Senators appointed by the King, Senators by right and Senators elected by mandatory, secret vote expressed through a single-member ballot by the members of the bodies constituted in the State, in the number and conditions, for the voters and eligible persons, men and women, that will be established by the electoral law. The proportion of appointed Senators in relation to the elected ones is half.
The following are members of the Senate by right, by virtue of their high position in the State and the Church:
a) The Heir to the Throne from the age of 18 years;
b) All Princes of the Royal Family, of age;
c) The Patriarch and the Metropolitans of the Country;
d) The diocesan Bishops of the Romanian Orthodox and Greek-Catholic churches, insofar as they are elected in accordance with the laws of the Country;

e) The heads of the denominations recognized by the State, one from each denomination, insofar as they are elected or appointed in accordance with the laws of the Country and represent a number of over 200,000 believers;
f) The current Senators by right recognized until the date of promulgation of the present Constitution.
The mandate of the Senators by right, enumerated in paragraphs a - e inclusive, ceases with the quality or dignity by virtue of which they hold it.

The duration of the mandates of the appointed and elected Senators will be nine years. The mandates of the elected Senators are renewed every three years from the date of taking the oath provided for in Art. 48 of the Constitution, in a proportion of one third. The last third not drawn for renewal retains the mandates until the expiration of the nine-year term.
For the mandates drawn, new elections will be held in accordance with the electoral law.

1938:65.contentEn:
The Government is composed of Ministers and Undersecretaries of State.
The Ministers exercise the executive power in the name of the King, under the conditions established by the Constitution and on their responsibility.
The Ministers have political responsibility only to the King.

content.text: The Government is composed of Ministers and Undersecretaries of State.
The Ministers exercise the executive power in the name of the King, under the conditions established by the Constitution and on their responsibility.
The Ministers have political responsibility only to the King.

1938:66.contentEn:
The assembled Ministers constitute the Council of Ministers, which is presided over by the one who was entrusted by the King with the formation of the Government and who bears the title of President of the Council of Ministers.
Ministerial Departments and Undersecretariats of State may only be established and dissolved by law.

content.text: The assembled Ministers constitute the Council of Ministers, which is presided over by the one who was entrusted by the King with the formation of the Government and who bears the title of President of the Council of Ministers.
Ministerial Departments and Undersecretariats of State may only be established and dissolved by law.

1938:67.contentEn: Only a person who has been Romanian for at least three generations may be a Minister. Those who have been Ministers until now are excepted.

content.text: Only a person who has been Romanian for at least three generations may be a Minister. Those who have been Ministers until now are excepted.

1938:68.contentEn: Members of the Royal Family may not be Ministers.

content.text: Members of the Royal Family may not be Ministers.

1938:69.contentEn:
Ministers and Undersecretaries of State who are not Members of the Assemblies may take part in the debate of laws but may not vote; the Assemblies may request the presence of Ministers at their deliberations.
Debates may not begin without the presence of a Minister.

content.text: Ministers and Undersecretaries of State who are not Members of the Assemblies may take part in the debate of laws but may not vote; the Assemblies may request the presence of Ministers at their deliberations.
Debates may not begin without the presence of a Minister.

1938:70.contentEn:
The King and each of the Assemblies may request the prosecution of Ministers and their referral for judgment to the High Court of Cassation and Justice which, alone, in united sections, is entitled to judge them. With regard to the exercise of the civil action of the injured party and with regard to the crimes and offenses committed by them outside the exercise of their function, they are subject to the rules of common law.
The placing under prosecution of Ministers by the Legislative Bodies shall be decided by a majority of two-thirds of the members present.
The investigation shall be carried out by a commission of the High Court of Cassation and Justice composed of five members drawn by lot in united sections. This commission also has the power to qualify the facts and to decide whether or not to prosecute.
The prosecution before the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall be sustained by the Public Ministry.
The law on Ministerial responsibility determines the cases of liability and the penalties applicable to Ministers.
Ministers of Justice who have left office may not practice the profession of lawyer for one year from the date of leaving office.
Ministers who have left office may not be part of the boards of directors of the company with which they have concluded contracts in the following three years.

content.text: The King and each of the Assemblies may request the prosecution of Ministers and their referral for judgment to the High Court of Cassation and Justice which, alone, in united sections, is entitled to judge them. With regard to the exercise of the civil action of the injured party and with regard to the crimes and offenses committed by them outside the exercise of their function, they are subject to the rules of common law.
The placing under prosecution of Ministers by the Legislative Bodies shall be decided by a majority of two-thirds of the members present.

The investigation shall be carried out by a commission of the High Court of Cassation and Justice composed of five members drawn by lot in united sections. This commission also has the power to qualify the facts and to decide whether or not to prosecute.
The prosecution before the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall be sustained by the Public Ministry.
The law on Ministerial responsibility determines the cases of liability and the penalties applicable to Ministers.
Ministers of Justice who have left office may not practice the profession of lawyer for one year from the date of leaving office.
Ministers who have left office may not be part of the boards of directors of the company with which they have concluded contracts in the following three years.

1938:71.contentEn: Any party injured in its rights by a decree or provision signed by a Minister, in violation of an express text of the Constitution or laws in force, may claim from the State pecuniary compensation for the damage suffered, according to common law.

content.text: Any party injured in its rights by a decree or provision signed by a Minister, in violation of an express text of the Constitution or laws in force, may claim from the State pecuniary compensation for the damage suffered, according to common law.

1938:72.contentEn:
The Legislative Council operates on the basis of its organic law.
The consultation of the Legislative Council is mandatory for all draft laws, both before and after their amendment in committees, except for those concerning budgetary credits.
No regulation for the application of laws may be made without the prior consultation of the Legislative Council, except in the case provided for in the following paragraph for draft laws.
The Legislative Assemblies may proceed to discuss draft laws without waiting for the approval of the Legislative Council, if it does not give it within the term fixed by its organic law.

content.text: The Legislative Council operates on the basis of its organic law.
The consultation of the Legislative Council is mandatory for all draft laws, both before and after their amendment in committees, except for those concerning budgetary credits.
No regulation for the application of laws may be made without the prior consultation of the Legislative Council, except in the case provided for in the following paragraph for draft laws.
The Legislative Assemblies may proceed to discuss draft laws without waiting for the approval of the Legislative Council, if it does not give it within the term fixed by its organic law.

1938:73.contentEn:
No jurisdiction may be established except by virtue of a law.
Extraordinary Commissions and Tribunals may not be created, under any name or word, for the purpose of specific proceedings, whether civil or criminal, or for the purpose of judging specific persons.
The jury is abolished.

content.text: No jurisdiction may be established except by virtue of a law.
Extraordinary Commissions and Tribunals may not be created, under any name or word, for the purpose of specific proceedings, whether civil or criminal, or for the purpose of judging specific persons.
The jury is abolished.

1938:74.contentEn: For the entire Romanian State, there is only one High Court of Cassation and Justice.

content.text: For the entire Romanian State, there is only one High Court of Cassation and Justice.

1938:75.contentEn:
Only the High Court of Cassation and Justice in united sections has the right to judge the constitutionality of laws and to declare inapplicable those that are contrary to the Constitution. The judgment of the unconstitutionality of laws is limited only to the case being judged.
The High Court of Cassation and Justice shall rule on conflicts of attribution.
The right of appeal in cassation is of constitutional order.

content.text: Only the High Court of Cassation and Justice in united sections has the right to judge the constitutionality of laws and to declare inapplicable those that are contrary to the Constitution. The judgment of the unconstitutionality of laws is limited only to the case being judged.
The High Court of Cassation and Justice shall rule on conflicts of attribution.
The right of appeal in cassation is of constitutional order.

1938:76.contentEn: Judges are irremovable. Irremovability shall be established by a special law that will intervene no later than six months from the promulgation of the present Constitution. During this period, disciplinary sanctions shall be applied by Royal Decree.

content.text: Judges are irremovable. Irremovability shall be established by a special law that will intervene no later than six months from the promulgation of the present Constitution. During this period, disciplinary sanctions shall be applied by Royal Decree.

1938:77.contentEn: Military justice is organized by law.

content.text: Military justice is organized by law.

1938:78.contentEn:
Administrative litigation is within the competence of the judicial power, according to special law.
The judicial power has no competence to judge acts of government as well as acts of command with a military character.

content.text: Administrative litigation is within the competence of the judicial power, according to special law.
The judicial power has no competence to judge acts of government as well as acts of command with a military character.

1938:79.contentEn: Administrative institutions are established by laws.

content.text: Administrative institutions are established by laws.

1938:80.contentEn:
No tax of any nature may be established and levied except on the basis of a law.
By law, taxes may be created only for the benefit of the State, Counties, Communes and Public Institutions performing a State service.

content.text: No tax of any nature may be established and levied except on the basis of a law.
By law, taxes may be created only for the benefit of the State, Counties, Communes and Public Institutions performing a State service.

1938:81.contentEn: Monopolies may not be established except by law and only for the benefit of the State, Counties and Communes.

content.text: Monopolies may not be established except by law and only for the benefit of the State, Counties and Communes.

1938:82.contentEn: Pension or gratuity funds chargeable to the public treasury may only be established by virtue of a law.

content.text: Pension or gratuity funds chargeable to the public treasury may only be established by virtue of a law.

1938:83.contentEn:
Each year, the Legislative Assemblies shall close the accounts and vote on the budget without being able to increase the proposed expenditures.
All revenues and expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and accounts.
If the budget is not voted on in due time, the executive power shall meet the needs of public services by applying the budget of the previous year, without being able to use that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

content.text: Each year, the Legislative Assemblies shall close the accounts and vote on the budget without being able to increase the proposed expenditures.
All revenues and expenditures of the State must be included in the budget and accounts.

If the budget is not voted on in due time, the executive power shall meet the needs of public services by applying the budget of the previous year, without being able to use that budget for more than one year beyond the year for which it was voted.

1938:84.contentEn:
The preventive and management control of all revenues and expenditures of the State shall be exercised by the High Court of Accounts, which shall submit each year to the Legislative Assemblies a general report on the management accounts of the past budget, pointing out the irregularities that may have been committed by the Ministers in its application.
The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Legislative Assemblies no later than two years after the end of each financial year.
A superior control body shall have the mission of supervising and examining the legality and correctness of the execution of management in all public services.
This body shall function under the Presidency of the Council of Ministers and shall work according to the orders of the Head of Government.

content.text: The preventive and management control of all revenues and expenditures of the State shall be exercised by the High Court of Accounts, which shall submit each year to the Legislative Assemblies a general report on the management accounts of the past budget, pointing out the irregularities that may have been committed by the Ministers in its application.
The final settlement of accounts must be presented to the Legislative Assemblies no later than two years after the end of each financial year.
A superior control body shall have the mission of supervising and examining the legality and correctness of the execution of management in all public services.
This body shall function under the Presidency of the Council of Ministers and shall work according to the orders of the Head of Government.

1938:85.contentEn: All funds originating from Special Houses, which the Government disposes of under various titles, must be included in the general budget of State revenues.

content.text: All funds originating from Special Houses, which the Government disposes of under various titles, must be included in the general budget of State revenues.

1938:86.contentEn: There is only one Court of Accounts for the whole of Romania.

content.text: There is only one Court of Accounts for the whole of Romania.

1938:87.contentEn: The embezzlement of public funds is qualified as a crime and is punished as such.

content.text: The embezzlement of public funds is qualified as a crime and is punished as such.

1938:88.contentEn: All Romanian citizens are obliged to be part of one of the elements of the military, according to the laws.

content.text: All Romanian citizens are obliged to be part of one of the elements of the military, according to the laws.

1938:89.contentEn: Military ranks, decorations and pensions may only be withdrawn by virtue of a court sentence.

content.text: Military ranks, decorations and pensions may only be withdrawn by virtue of a court sentence.

1938:90.contentEn: The army contingent shall be voted for each year by the Legislative Assemblies.

content.text: The army contingent shall be voted for each year by the Legislative Assemblies.

1938:91.contentEn: No foreign armed troop may be admitted into the service of the State and may not enter or pass through the territory of Romania except by virtue of a law.

content.text: No foreign armed troop may be admitted into the service of the State and may not enter or pass through the territory of Romania except by virtue of a law.

1938:92.contentEn: The colors of the Romanian flag are: Blue, Yellow, Red, placed vertically.

content.text: The colors of the Romanian flag are: Blue, Yellow, Red, placed vertically.

1938:93.contentEn: The seat of the Government is in the Capital of the Country.

content.text: The seat of the Government is in the Capital of the Country.

1938:94.contentEn: The Romanian language is the official language of the State.

content.text: The Romanian language is the official language of the State.

1938:95.contentEn: No oath binds and can be imposed except by the power of a law, which also determines its formula.

content.text: No oath binds and can be imposed except by the power of a law, which also determines its formula.

1938:96.contentEn:
The present Constitution may not be suspended, neither in whole nor in part.
In case of danger to the State, a general or partial state of siege may be instituted.

content.text: The present Constitution may not be suspended, neither in whole nor in part.
In case of danger to the State, a general or partial state of siege may be instituted.

1938:97.contentEn:
The present Constitution may not be revised in whole or in part except on the initiative of the King and with the prior consultation of the Legislative Bodies, which shall also indicate the texts to be revised.
The consultation of the Legislative Assemblies shall be done by Royal message and shall be expressed by a majority of two-thirds of the Assemblies convened in one single body under the chairmanship of the President of the Senate. The result of the consultation shall be brought to the attention of the King by the presidents of the two Assemblies accompanied by a special Commission.
The new texts, to replace the revised ones, shall be voted by a majority of two-thirds, by each Assembly separately.

content.text: The present Constitution may not be revised in whole or in part except on the initiative of the King and with the prior consultation of the Legislative Bodies, which shall also indicate the texts to be revised.

The consultation of the Legislative Assemblies shall be done by Royal message and shall be expressed by a majority of two-thirds of the Assemblies convened in one single body under the chairmanship of the President of the Senate. The result of the consultation shall be brought to the attention of the King by the presidents of the two Assemblies accompanied by a special Commission.
The new texts, to replace the revised ones, shall be voted by a majority of two-thirds, by each Assembly separately.

1938:98.contentEn:
All lands expropriated and distributed based on the law for the agrarian reform of the Old Kingdom of July 17, 1921; the law for the agrarian reform of Bessarabia of March 13, 1920; the law for the agrarian reform of Transylvania, Banat, Crisana and Maramures of June 13, 1921 and the law for the agrarian reform of Bukovina of July 30, 1921, are respected as vested rights and shall be governed, for any disputes relating to them at present and in the future, by those laws.
The provisions of these laws introduced in Art. 131 of the Constitution of 1923 remain of a constitutional nature.
All political rights acquired by virtue of the decree-laws ratified by Art. 133 of the Constitution of 1923, are respected.
All codes and laws in force shall be revised in order to unify the legislation, bringing them into harmony with the principles of the current Constitution. Until then, they remain in force. From the day of promulgation of this Constitution, those provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other acts that are contrary to those enshrined in this Constitution are repealed.
The Constitution promulgated by Royal Decree No. 1,360 of March 28, 1923 is also repealed from the day of promulgation of this Constitution.
The jury in criminal matters shall continue to function until the Penal Code Carol al II-lea and the Criminal Procedure are brought into line with the principle established in Art. 73 of this Constitution.
Until the convocation of the Legislative Assemblies, all decrees have the power of law without the need for their ratification.
The Statute of the Royal Family has a Constitutional character and can only be amended by the procedure for the revision of the Constitution.

content.text: All lands expropriated and distributed based on the law for the agrarian reform of the Old Kingdom of July 17, 1921; the law for the agrarian reform of Bessarabia of March 13, 1920; the law for the agrarian reform of Transylvania, Banat, Crisana and Maramures of June 13, 1921 and the law for the agrarian reform of Bukovina of July 30, 1921, are respected as vested rights and shall be governed, for any disputes relating to them at present and in the future, by those laws.
The provisions of these laws introduced in Art. 131 of the Constitution of 1923 remain of a constitutional nature.
All political rights acquired by virtue of the decree-laws ratified by Art. 133 of the Constitution of 1923, are respected.

All codes and laws in force shall be revised in order to unify the legislation, bringing them into harmony with the principles of the current Constitution. Until then, they remain in force. From the day of promulgation of this Constitution, those provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other acts that are contrary to those enshrined in this Constitution are repealed.
The Constitution promulgated by Royal Decree No. 1,360 of March 28, 1923 is also repealed from the day of promulgation of this Constitution.

The jury in criminal matters shall continue to function until the Penal Code Carol al II-lea and the Criminal Procedure are brought into line with the principle established in Art. 73 of this Constitution.
Until the convocation of the Legislative Assemblies, all decrees have the power of law without the need for their ratification.
The Statute of the Royal Family has a Constitutional character and can only be amended by the procedure for the revision of the Constitution.

1938:99.contentEn:
For the election of the Legislative Assemblies to be established on the basis of this Constitution, a Royal Decree with the power of law, under the conditions of Art. 98, shall determine the conditions required to be an elector and eligible, in the Chamber and Senate, the electoral districts, the number of Deputies and Senators, the rules according to which the election is to be made.
This decree has a Constitutional character and can only be amended by a two-thirds majority.

content.text: For the election of the Legislative Assemblies to be established on the basis of this Constitution, a Royal Decree with the power of law, under the conditions of Art. 98, shall determine the conditions required to be an elector and eligible, in the Chamber and Senate, the electoral districts, the number of Deputies and Senators, the rules according to which the election is to be made.
This decree has a Constitutional character and can only be amended by a two-thirds majority.

1938:100.contentEn:
This Constitution, after it has been enacted by the King, shall be submitted to the Romanian Nation for «good knowledge and consent».
A Royal Decree shall determine the procedure to be followed for this purpose.
After the completion of this operation and the bringing of its result to the King's attention by the President of the Council of Ministers, this Constitution shall be promulgated and shall enter into force.

content.text: This Constitution, after it has been enacted by the King, shall be submitted to the Romanian Nation for «good knowledge and consent».
A Royal Decree shall determine the procedure to be followed for this purpose.

After the completion of this operation and the bringing of its result to the King's attention by the President of the Council of Ministers, this Constitution shall be promulgated and shall enter into force.

1948:1.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic is a popular, unitary, independent and sovereign State.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic is a popular, unitary, independent and sovereign State.

1948:2.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic was established through the struggle waged by the people, led by the working class, against fascism, reaction and imperialism.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic was established through the struggle waged by the people, led by the working class, against fascism, reaction and imperialism.

1948:3.contentEn:
In the Romanian People's Republic, all state power emanates from the people and belongs to the people.
The people exercise their power through representative bodies, elected by universal, equal, direct and secret suffrage.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, all state power emanates from the people and belongs to the people.
The people exercise their power through representative bodies, elected by universal, equal, direct and secret suffrage.

1948:4.contentEn: The representatives of the people in all bodies of state power are accountable to the people and may be recalled by the will of the voters, under the conditions established by law.

content.text: The representatives of the people in all bodies of state power are accountable to the people and may be recalled by the will of the voters, under the conditions established by law.

1948:5.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, the means of production belong either to the State, as property of the entire people, or to cooperative organizations, or to private individuals, natural or legal persons.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, the means of production belong either to the State, as property of the entire people, or to cooperative organizations, or to private individuals, natural or legal persons.

1948:6.contentEn:
Resources of any nature of the subsoil, mineral deposits, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, railway, road, water and air communication routes, post, telegraph, telephone and radio belong to the State, as common property of the people.
The modalities of transferring into the ownership of the State the goods enumerated in the preceding paragraph, which, on the date of entry into force of the present Constitution, were in private hands, shall be established by law.

content.text: Resources of any nature of the subsoil, mineral deposits, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, railway, road, water and air communication routes, post, telegraph, telephone and radio belong to the State, as common property of the people.

The modalities of transferring into the ownership of the State the goods enumerated in the preceding paragraph, which, on the date of entry into force of the present Constitution, were in private hands, shall be established by law.

1948:7.contentEn:
The common property of the people constitutes the material foundation of the economic prosperity and national independence of the Romanian People's Republic.
The defence and development of the common property of the people are a duty of every citizen.

content.text: The common property of the people constitutes the material foundation of the economic prosperity and national independence of the Romanian People's Republic.
The defence and development of the common property of the people are a duty of every citizen.

1948:8.contentEn:
Private property and the right of inheritance are recognised and guaranteed by law.
Private property, acquired through labour and saving, enjoys special protection.

content.text: Private property and the right of inheritance are recognised and guaranteed by law.
Private property, acquired through labour and saving, enjoys special protection.

1948:9.contentEn:
The land belongs to those who cultivate it.
The State protects peasant labour property. The State encourages and supports village co-operation.
In order to stimulate the raising of agriculture, the State may create agricultural enterprises, the property of the State.

content.text: The land belongs to those who cultivate it.
The State protects peasant labour property. The State encourages and supports village co-operation.
In order to stimulate the raising of agriculture, the State may create agricultural enterprises, the property of the State.

1948:10.contentEn: Expropriations may be made for reasons of public utility on the basis of a law and with a fair compensation established by justice.

content.text: Expropriations may be made for reasons of public utility on the basis of a law and with a fair compensation established by justice.

1948:11.contentEn: When the general interest requires, the means of production, banks and insurance companies, which are private property of natural or legal persons, may become the property of the State, that is, property of the people, under the conditions provided for by law.

content.text: When the general interest requires, the means of production, banks and insurance companies, which are private property of natural or legal persons, may become the property of the State, that is, property of the people, under the conditions provided for by law.
