1948:12.contentEn: Labour is the basic factor of the economic life of the State. It is a duty of every citizen. The State grants support to all those who work, in order to protect them against exploitation and to raise their standard of living.

content.text: Labour is the basic factor of the economic life of the State. It is a duty of every citizen. The State grants support to all those who work, in order to protect them against exploitation and to raise their standard of living.

1948:13.contentEn: The State grants protection to private initiative put in the service of the general interests.

content.text: The State grants protection to private initiative put in the service of the general interests.

1948:14.contentEn: Domestic and foreign trade is regulated and controlled by the State and is exercised by state, private and cooperative commercial enterprises.

content.text: Domestic and foreign trade is regulated and controlled by the State and is exercised by state, private and cooperative commercial enterprises.

1948:15.contentEn: The State guides and plans the national economy in order to develop the economic power of the country, ensure the well-being of the people and guarantee national independence.

content.text: The State guides and plans the national economy in order to develop the economic power of the country, ensure the well-being of the people and guarantee national independence.

1948:16.contentEn: All citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, without distinction as to sex, nationality, race, religion or level of culture, are equal before the law.

content.text: All citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, without distinction as to sex, nationality, race, religion or level of culture, are equal before the law.

1948:17.contentEn: Any advocacy or manifestation of racial or national hatred shall be punished by law.

content.text: Any advocacy or manifestation of racial or national hatred shall be punished by law.

1948:18.contentEn:
All citizens, without distinction as to sex, nationality, race, religion, level of culture, profession, including military personnel, magistrates and public officials, have the right to elect and to be elected to all State bodies.
All citizens who have reached the age of 18 have the right to vote, and those who have reached the age of 23 have the right to be elected.
Persons interdicted, deprived of civil and political rights and unworthy, declared as such by the competent bodies, according to the law, shall not enjoy the right to vote.

content.text: All citizens, without distinction as to sex, nationality, race, religion, level of culture, profession, including military personnel, magistrates and public officials, have the right to elect and to be elected to all State bodies.
All citizens who have reached the age of 18 have the right to vote, and those who have reached the age of 23 have the right to be elected.
Persons interdicted, deprived of civil and political rights and unworthy, declared as such by the competent bodies, according to the law, shall not enjoy the right to vote.

1948:19.contentEn: Citizens have the right to work. The State shall gradually ensure this right through the planned organisation and development of the national economy.

content.text: Citizens have the right to work. The State shall gradually ensure this right through the planned organisation and development of the national economy.

1948:20.contentEn: Citizens have the right to rest. The right to rest is ensured by the regulation of working hours, by paid leave, in accordance with the law, by the organisation of rest homes, sanatoriums, clubs, parks, sports grounds and specially designed establishments.

content.text: Citizens have the right to rest. The right to rest is ensured by the regulation of working hours, by paid leave, in accordance with the law, by the organisation of rest homes, sanatoriums, clubs, parks, sports grounds and specially designed establishments.

1948:21.contentEn:
Women have equal rights with men in all areas of State, economic, social, cultural, political and private law.
For equal work, women have the right to equal pay with men. Art. 22.- In the Romanian People's Republic, all citizens have the right to education.
The State shall ensure the fulfilment of this right through the organisation and development of compulsory and free primary education, through State scholarships granted to meritorious pupils and students and through the organisation and development of vocational and technical education.

content.text: Women have equal rights with men in all areas of State, economic, social, cultural, political and private law.
For equal work, women have the right to equal pay with men. Art. 22.- In the Romanian People's Republic, all citizens have the right to education.

The State shall ensure the fulfilment of this right through the organisation and development of compulsory and free primary education, through State scholarships granted to meritorious pupils and students and through the organisation and development of vocational and technical education.

1948:23.contentEn: The State encourages and supports the development of science and art and organises research institutes, libraries, publishing houses, theatres, museums, conservatories.

content.text: The State encourages and supports the development of science and art and organises research institutes, libraries, publishing houses, theatres, museums, conservatories.

1948:24.contentEn:
In the Romanian People's Republic, the co-inhabiting nationalities are ensured the right to use their mother tongue and to organise education of all levels in their mother tongue. The administration and justice, in the districts inhabited also by populations of another nationality than the Romanian one, shall use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationality and shall make appointments of officials from within the respective nationality or from another nationality, who know the language of the local population.
The teaching of the Romanian language and literature is compulsory in schools of any level.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, the co-inhabiting nationalities are ensured the right to use their mother tongue and to organise education of all levels in their mother tongue. The administration and justice, in the districts inhabited also by populations of another nationality than the Romanian one, shall use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationality and shall make appointments of officials from within the respective nationality or from another nationality, who know the language of the local population.
The teaching of the Romanian language and literature is compulsory in schools of any level.

1948:25.contentEn:
The State takes care of public health by establishing and developing sanitary services and by encouraging and supporting physical education.
The State ensures social protection and medical assistance for illness, accidents and disability, resulting from work, during work or in the service of defending the homeland, as well as for old age, both for its employees and for those of private enterprises, whose contribution and rights are established by law.

content.text: The State takes care of public health by establishing and developing sanitary services and by encouraging and supporting physical education.

The State ensures social protection and medical assistance for illness, accidents and disability, resulting from work, during work or in the service of defending the homeland, as well as for old age, both for its employees and for those of private enterprises, whose contribution and rights are established by law.

1948:26.contentEn:
Marriage and the family enjoy the protection of the State.
The mother, as well as children up to the age of 18, enjoy special protection, established by law.
Parents have the same duties towards children born out of wedlock as for those born in wedlock.
Only civil status documents concluded by the State bodies are valid.

content.text: Marriage and the family enjoy the protection of the State.
The mother, as well as children up to the age of 18, enjoy special protection, established by law.
Parents have the same duties towards children born out of wedlock as for those born in wedlock.
Only civil status documents concluded by the State bodies are valid.

1948:27.contentEn:
Freedom of conscience and religious freedom are guaranteed by the State.
Religious denominations are free to organise themselves and may function freely if their ritual and practice are not contrary to the Constitution, public security or good morals.
No religious confession, congregation or community may open or maintain general education institutions, but only special schools for the training of cult personnel under the control of the State.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is autocephalous and unitary in its organisation.
The manner of organisation and functioning of religious denominations shall be regulated by law.

content.text: Freedom of conscience and religious freedom are guaranteed by the State.
Religious denominations are free to organise themselves and may function freely if their ritual and practice are not contrary to the Constitution, public security or good morals.
No religious confession, congregation or community may open or maintain general education institutions, but only special schools for the training of cult personnel under the control of the State.
The Romanian Orthodox Church is autocephalous and unitary in its organisation.
The manner of organisation and functioning of religious denominations shall be regulated by law.

1948:28.contentEn:
The individual freedom of citizens is guaranteed.
No one may be arrested and detained for more than 48 hours, without a warrant from the prosecutor's office, the investigation bodies, established by law, or the authorisation of the courts, in accordance with the provisions of the law.

content.text: The individual freedom of citizens is guaranteed.

No one may be arrested and detained for more than 48 hours, without a warrant from the prosecutor's office, the investigation bodies, established by law, or the authorisation of the courts, in accordance with the provisions of the law.

1948:29.contentEn: The domicile is inviolable. No one may enter the domicile or residence of citizens without their consent, except in their presence and on the basis of a written order from the competent authority or in case of flagrant offence.

content.text: The domicile is inviolable. No one may enter the domicile or residence of citizens without their consent, except in their presence and on the basis of a written order from the competent authority or in case of flagrant offence.

1948:30.contentEn: No one may be convicted and held to serve a sentence except on the basis of a court decision, pronounced in accordance with the law.

content.text: No one may be convicted and held to serve a sentence except on the basis of a court decision, pronounced in accordance with the law.

1948:31.contentEn:
Freedom of the press, of speech, of assembly, meetings, processions and demonstrations is guaranteed.
The exercise of these rights is ensured by the fact that printing facilities, paper and meeting places are made available to those who work.

content.text: Freedom of the press, of speech, of assembly, meetings, processions and demonstrations is guaranteed.
The exercise of these rights is ensured by the fact that printing facilities, paper and meeting places are made available to those who work.

1948:32.contentEn:
Citizens have the right to associate and organize, if the purpose pursued is not directed against the democratic order established by the Constitution.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited and punished by law.

content.text: Citizens have the right to associate and organize, if the purpose pursued is not directed against the democratic order established by the Constitution.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited and punished by law.

1948:33.contentEn: The secrecy of correspondence is guaranteed. Only in case of criminal investigation, under a state of siege, or in case of mobilization, correspondence may be controlled.

content.text: The secrecy of correspondence is guaranteed. Only in case of criminal investigation, under a state of siege, or in case of mobilization, correspondence may be controlled.

1948:34.contentEn: Any citizen has the right to petition, as well as the right to request the bodies provided for by law to bring to trial any public official for offenses committed during the performance of their service.

content.text: Any citizen has the right to petition, as well as the right to request the bodies provided for by law to bring to trial any public official for offenses committed during the performance of their service.

1948:35.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic grants the right of refuge to all foreigners persecuted for their democratic activity, for the struggle for national liberation, for scientific or cultural activity.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic grants the right of refuge to all foreigners persecuted for their democratic activity, for the struggle for national liberation, for scientific or cultural activity.

1948:36.contentEn:
The defense of the homeland is a duty of honor for all citizens.
Military service is compulsory for all citizens, in accordance with the law.
Treason against the homeland - violation of the oath, entering the service of the enemy, causing damage to the military power of the State - constitutes the most serious crime against the people and is punished with the full severity of the law.

content.text: The defense of the homeland is a duty of honor for all citizens.
Military service is compulsory for all citizens, in accordance with the law.

Treason against the homeland - violation of the oath, entering the service of the enemy, causing damage to the military power of the State - constitutes the most serious crime against the people and is punished with the full severity of the law.

1948:37.contentEn: The supreme body of State power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

content.text: The supreme body of State power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

1948:38.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic has the following direct competence:
1) Election of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic;
2) Formation of the Government of the R. P. R.;
3) Amendment of the Constitution;
4) Establishing the number, attributions and denomination of the ministries and the abolition, merger or new denomination of the existing ones;
5) Voting on the State budget, the conclusion of budget exercises, fixing taxes and the manner of their perception;
6) Issues of war and peace;
7) To decide on consulting the people by referendum;
8) Granting amnesty.

1948:40.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects from among its members the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Presidium is elected with one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects from among its members the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Presidium is elected with one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

1948:41.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic is composed of a president, three vice-presidents, a secretary and 14 members elected directly by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic is composed of a president, three vice-presidents, a secretary and 14 members elected directly by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

1948:42.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., in its entirety, or any of its members, are revocable at any time by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic with the majority provided for in Art. 40.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., in its entirety, or any of its members, are revocable at any time by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic with the majority provided for in Art. 40.

1948:43.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic is responsible for its entire activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic is responsible for its entire activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1948:44.contentEn:
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
  1. Convenes the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic in ordinary and extraordinary sessions;
  2. Issues decrees;
  3. Interprets the laws voted by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic;
  4. Exercises the right of pardon and commutes sentences;
  5. Confers the decorations and medals of the Romanian People's Republic;
  6. Represents the Romanian People's Republic in international relations;
  7. Accredits and recalls, at the proposal of the government, the diplomatic representatives of the Romanian People's Republic;
  8. Receives the letters of accreditation and recall of the diplomatic representatives of foreign States, accredited to it;
  9. In the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, appoints and revokes ministers, at the proposal of the President of the Council of Ministers;
  10. Establishes military ranks, diplomatic ranks and honorary titles, at the proposal of the government;
  11. Makes appointments and confirmations in public functions, at the proposal of the ministers of the relevant department or of the government, according to the law;
  12. In the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, at the proposal of the government, declares the state of war and partial or general mobilization, in case of aggression against the Romanian People's Republic, or against another State, towards which it has obligations of mutual defense arising from international treaties;
  13. Ratifies or denounces international treaties, at the proposal of the government;
  14. Resolves any issue with which it is charged by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic and exercises any attribution given to it by law.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
<ol>
• Convenes the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic in ordinary and extraordinary sessions;
• Issues decrees;
• Interprets the laws voted by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic;
• Exercises the right of pardon and commutes sentences;
• Confers the decorations and medals of the Romanian People's Republic;
• Represents the Romanian People's Republic in international relations;
• Accredits and recalls, at the proposal of the government, the diplomatic representatives of the Romanian People's Republic;
• Receives the letters of accreditation and recall of the diplomatic representatives of foreign States, accredited to it;
• In the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, appoints and revokes ministers, at the proposal of the President of the Council of Ministers;
• Establishes military ranks, diplomatic ranks and honorary titles, at the proposal of the government;
• Makes appointments and confirmations in public functions, at the proposal of the ministers of the relevant department or of the government, according to the law;

• In the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, at the proposal of the government, declares the state of war and partial or general mobilization, in case of aggression against the Romanian People's Republic, or against another State, towards which it has obligations of mutual defense arising from international treaties;
• Ratifies or denounces international treaties, at the proposal of the government;
• Resolves any issue with which it is charged by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic and exercises any attribution given to it by law.

1948:45.contentEn:
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic takes valid decisions with a simple majority of its members.
The decrees shall be signed by the president and the secretary of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.
In case of impediment of these, the president will be replaced by one of the vice-presidents, and the secretary, by one of the members designated by the Presidium, from among its members.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic takes valid decisions with a simple majority of its members.
The decrees shall be signed by the president and the secretary of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.
In case of impediment of these, the president will be replaced by one of the vice-presidents, and the secretary, by one of the members designated by the Presidium, from among its members.

1948:46.contentEn: After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., or in case of dissolution before the term, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. continues its functions until the election of the new Presidium.

content.text: After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., or in case of dissolution before the term, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. continues its functions until the election of the new Presidium.

1948:47.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is elected for a term of 4 years. It is composed of the representatives of the people (deputies), elected according to the norms established by the electoral law.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is elected for a term of 4 years. It is composed of the representatives of the people (deputies), elected according to the norms established by the electoral law.

1948:48.contentEn:
The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. take place at least twice a year and last until the completion of the works.
The convocation of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is done by decree, by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

content.text: The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. take place at least twice a year and last until the completion of the works.
The convocation of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is done by decree, by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

1948:49.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may be convened in extraordinary sessions by a decree of the Presidium, at the request of at least one third of the number of deputies.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may be convened in extraordinary sessions by a decree of the Presidium, at the request of at least one third of the number of deputies.

1948:50.contentEn:
After the validation of the deputies, the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. elects a bureau to conduct the debates, composed of a president, 3 vice-presidents and secretaries. The Bureau of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is elected for each session of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.
The debates of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. shall be presided over by the president, or by one of the vice-presidents of the bureau, according to the regulations elaborated by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

content.text: After the validation of the deputies, the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. elects a bureau to conduct the debates, composed of a president, 3 vice-presidents and secretaries. The Bureau of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. is elected for each session of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

The debates of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. shall be presided over by the president, or by one of the vice-presidents of the bureau, according to the regulations elaborated by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.

1948:51.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. validly works with one half plus one of the total number of deputies and takes valid decisions with a simple majority of the deputies present, unless the Constitution or the regulations provide for another number.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. validly works with one half plus one of the total number of deputies and takes valid decisions with a simple majority of the deputies present, unless the Constitution or the regulations provide for another number.

1948:52.contentEn: Voting may be done by secret ballot, by raising hands or acclamations, as the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. will decide.

content.text: Voting may be done by secret ballot, by raising hands or acclamations, as the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. will decide.

1948:53.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. validates the election of the deputies. The vote of the deputies is valid even before validation.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. validates the election of the deputies. The vote of the deputies is valid even before validation.

1948:54.contentEn:
The validated deputies take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.:
"I swear that I will serve the people and the Romanian People's Republic with all my devotion and working power, that I will keep and respect the Constitution and the laws of the country;
That I will keep state secrets and defend the interests of the people and the State, democratic freedoms and the independence of the homeland".
The same oath shall be taken by the members of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. and by the members of the government upon entering office.

content.text: The validated deputies take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R.:
"I swear that I will serve the people and the Romanian People's Republic with all my devotion and working power, that I will keep and respect the Constitution and the laws of the country;
That I will keep state secrets and defend the interests of the people and the State, democratic freedoms and the independence of the homeland".
The same oath shall be taken by the members of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. and by the members of the government upon entering office.

1948:55.contentEn: Legislative initiative belongs to the Government. Likewise, deputies, in a number of at least one-fifth of the total number, may take the initiative for any law.

content.text: Legislative initiative belongs to the Government. Likewise, deputies, in a number of at least one-fifth of the total number, may take the initiative for any law.

1948:56.contentEn: After the laws are voted on by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., they shall be signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Gazette. The law shall enter into force on the date indicated in its content or on the third day after publication in the Official Gazette.

content.text: After the laws are voted on by the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., they shall be signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Gazette. The law shall enter into force on the date indicated in its content or on the third day after publication in the Official Gazette.

1948:57.contentEn: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. are public, unless the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. decides on a secret session.

content.text: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. are public, unless the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. decides on a secret session.

1948:58.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. has the right to conduct investigations and inquiries in any field, through commissions to be elected from within it.
All state authorities and bodies, as well as private individuals, are obliged to provide any information and make available any documents requested by the investigation committees.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. has the right to conduct investigations and inquiries in any field, through commissions to be elected from within it.
All state authorities and bodies, as well as private individuals, are obliged to provide any information and make available any documents requested by the investigation committees.

1948:59.contentEn: No deputy may be detained, arrested or prosecuted, without the authorization of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., during sessions, or of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., between sessions, for any criminal acts, except in cases of flagrant delicto, when the approval of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. or of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. shall be requested immediately.

content.text: No deputy may be detained, arrested or prosecuted, without the authorization of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., during sessions, or of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R., between sessions, for any criminal acts, except in cases of flagrant delicto, when the approval of the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. or of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. shall be requested immediately.

1948:60.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. shall be considered dissolved upon the expiration of the mandate for which it was elected. The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may dissolve itself before this date.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. shall be considered dissolved upon the expiration of the mandate for which it was elected. The Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may dissolve itself before this date.

1948:61.contentEn: In case of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

content.text: In case of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly of the R. P. R. may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

1948:62.contentEn: In the event that war breaks out or another exceptional circumstance occurs while the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. is dissolved, the Presidium of the dissolved Grand National Assembly shall convene it again and the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., thus convened, may extend its mandate, according to Art. 61.

content.text: In the event that war breaks out or another exceptional circumstance occurs while the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. is dissolved, the Presidium of the dissolved Grand National Assembly shall convene it again and the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., thus convened, may extend its mandate, according to Art. 61.

1948:63.contentEn: Elections for a new Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. shall be held no later than 3 months from the dissolution of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: Elections for a new Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. shall be held no later than 3 months from the dissolution of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:64.contentEn: The deputies shall receive an allowance which shall be fixed by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: The deputies shall receive an allowance which shall be fixed by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:65.contentEn: Any deputy shall have the right to put questions to or interpellate the government or the ministers individually. The Prime Minister or the minister questioned and interpellated shall be obliged to answer, in the same sitting or in another sitting, which shall be fixed by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: Any deputy shall have the right to put questions to or interpellate the government or the ministers individually. The Prime Minister or the minister questioned and interpellated shall be obliged to answer, in the same sitting or in another sitting, which shall be fixed by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:66.contentEn: The supreme executive and administrative body of the R.P.R. shall be the government. The government shall be composed of: the Chairman of the Council of Ministers (Prime Minister), one or more vice-chairmen and ministers, who together shall form the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The supreme executive and administrative body of the R.P.R. shall be the government. The government shall be composed of: the Chairman of the Council of Ministers (Prime Minister), one or more vice-chairmen and ministers, who together shall form the Council of Ministers.

1948:67.contentEn: The ministries and their attributions shall be established by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., pursuant to Articolul 39, para. 4.

content.text: The ministries and their attributions shall be established by the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., pursuant to Articolul 39, para. 4.

1948:68.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may appoint, upon the proposal of the Council of Ministers, deputy ministers to any ministries.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may appoint, upon the proposal of the Council of Ministers, deputy ministers to any ministries.

1948:69.contentEn: The government shall be responsible for its activity and shall give account thereof to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., and in the interval between sessions, to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: The government shall be responsible for its activity and shall give account thereof to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., and in the interval between sessions, to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:70.contentEn: The members of the government shall take the oath before the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: The members of the government shall take the oath before the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:71.contentEn: Ministers shall be appointed from among the deputies or from among persons not belonging to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. Ministers who do not belong to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may participate in any deliberation of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., but without the right to vote.

content.text: Ministers shall be appointed from among the deputies or from among persons not belonging to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. Ministers who do not belong to the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may participate in any deliberation of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., but without the right to vote.

1948:72.contentEn:
The Government is responsible for the administrative management of the State.
It coordinates and gives general directives to the relevant ministries, directs and plans the national economy, executes the State budget, ensures public order and State security.
The Government conducts the general policy of the State in the field of international relations.
It organizes and equips the armed forces.
For certain fields of activity, the government may organize and manage special services of any kind, which will depend directly on the Council of Ministers.
The Council of Ministers may annul ministerial decisions that do not comply with the Constitution or the laws.
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may annul the decisions of the Council of Ministers that do not comply with the Constitution or the laws.
The decisions of the Council of Ministers are binding throughout the territory of the R.P.R.

content.text: The Government is responsible for the administrative management of the State.
It coordinates and gives general directives to the relevant ministries, directs and plans the national economy, executes the State budget, ensures public order and State security.
The Government conducts the general policy of the State in the field of international relations.
It organizes and equips the armed forces.
For certain fields of activity, the government may organize and manage special services of any kind, which will depend directly on the Council of Ministers.
The Council of Ministers may annul ministerial decisions that do not comply with the Constitution or the laws.
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R. may annul the decisions of the Council of Ministers that do not comply with the Constitution or the laws.
The decisions of the Council of Ministers are binding throughout the territory of the R.P.R.

1948:73.contentEn:
Ministers are liable for their criminal acts committed in the exercise of their functions.
A special law shall establish the manner of prosecution and trial of ministers.

content.text: Ministers are liable for their criminal acts committed in the exercise of their functions.
A special law shall establish the manner of prosecution and trial of ministers.

1948:74.contentEn: Ministers manage their respective departments on the basis of general directives given by the Council of Ministers. They issue, in accordance with the law, decisions binding on all citizens.

content.text: Ministers manage their respective departments on the basis of general directives given by the Council of Ministers. They issue, in accordance with the law, decisions binding on all citizens.

1948:75.contentEn: The territory of the Romanian People's Republic is divided administratively into: communes, districts, counties and regions. The law may make changes to these divisions.

content.text: The territory of the Romanian People's Republic is divided administratively into: communes, districts, counties and regions. The law may make changes to these divisions.

1948:76.contentEn: The local bodies of State power are the local people's councils.

content.text: The local bodies of State power are the local people's councils.

1948:77.contentEn: The local people's councils are representative bodies, elected for 4 years, by universal, direct, equal and secret vote.

content.text: The local people's councils are representative bodies, elected for 4 years, by universal, direct, equal and secret vote.

1948:78.contentEn:
The local people's councils guide and manage the local economic, social and cultural activity, according to the laws and provisions of the superior administrative bodies.
They elaborate and execute the economic plan and the local budget, taking into account the general national plan and the general budget of the State, take care of the good administration of local goods and enterprises, of the maintenance of public order, of the defense of the rights of the inhabitants, of the respect and application of the laws, as well as of taking the necessary measures for the good functioning of the local household.

content.text: The local people's councils guide and manage the local economic, social and cultural activity, according to the laws and provisions of the superior administrative bodies.

They elaborate and execute the economic plan and the local budget, taking into account the general national plan and the general budget of the State, take care of the good administration of local goods and enterprises, of the maintenance of public order, of the defense of the rights of the inhabitants, of the respect and application of the laws, as well as of taking the necessary measures for the good functioning of the local household.

1948:79.contentEn: In the performance of their duties, the people's councils rely on the initiative and broad participation of the popular masses.

content.text: In the performance of their duties, the people's councils rely on the initiative and broad participation of the popular masses.

1948:80.contentEn: The local people's councils render accounts to the people.

content.text: The local people's councils render accounts to the people.

1948:81.contentEn: The local people's councils meet in ordinary and extraordinary working sessions.

content.text: The local people's councils meet in ordinary and extraordinary working sessions.

1948:82.contentEn:
The management and executive bodies of the local people's councils are the executive committees.
They are elected from within the respective local people's councils and are formed and operate in the manner established by law.

content.text: The management and executive bodies of the local people's councils are the executive committees.
They are elected from within the respective local people's councils and are formed and operate in the manner established by law.

1948:83.contentEn: The executive committees are accountable to the respective local people's councils.

content.text: The executive committees are accountable to the respective local people's councils.

1948:84.contentEn: The people's councils and local executive committees carry out their activity in accordance with the laws and are subordinate to the higher people's councils and executive committees, as well as to the central State administrative bodies.

content.text: The people's councils and local executive committees carry out their activity in accordance with the laws and are subordinate to the higher people's councils and executive committees, as well as to the central State administrative bodies.

1948:85.contentEn: Sections of the people's councils may be established by branches of activity, these being subordinate in their activity of any kind to the people's councils and executive committees, alongside which they operate, and with regard to specialized technical guidance, to the corresponding sections alongside the higher people's councils, as well as to the competent central State administrative bodies.

content.text: Sections of the people's councils may be established by branches of activity, these being subordinate in their activity of any kind to the people's councils and executive committees, alongside which they operate, and with regard to specialized technical guidance, to the corresponding sections alongside the higher people's councils, as well as to the competent central State administrative bodies.

1948:86.contentEn: The courts of law are: the Supreme Court, one for the entire country, the Courts, tribunals and people's courts.

content.text: The courts of law are: the Supreme Court, one for the entire country, the Courts, tribunals and people's courts.

1948:87.contentEn: Special courts for certain branches of activity may be established by law.

content.text: Special courts for certain branches of activity may be established by law.

1948:88.contentEn: In all courts, with the exception of the Supreme Court, the trial takes place with people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

content.text: In all courts, with the exception of the Supreme Court, the trial takes place with people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

1948:89.contentEn: The first president, the presidents and the members of the Supreme Court are appointed by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., upon the proposal of the government.

content.text: The first president, the presidents and the members of the Supreme Court are appointed by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., upon the proposal of the government.

1948:90.contentEn: The Supreme Court supervises the judicial activity of the courts and judicial bodies, under the conditions of the law.

content.text: The Supreme Court supervises the judicial activity of the courts and judicial bodies, under the conditions of the law.

1948:91.contentEn: In all courts of law, the debates are public, except in the cases and conditions provided by law.

content.text: In all courts of law, the debates are public, except in the cases and conditions provided by law.

1948:92.contentEn: The right of defense before all courts is guaranteed.

content.text: The right of defense before all courts is guaranteed.

1948:93.contentEn: In the exercise of their duties, judges of any rank are subject only to the law and apply the laws equally to all citizens.

content.text: In the exercise of their duties, judges of any rank are subject only to the law and apply the laws equally to all citizens.

1948:94.contentEn: A law shall determine the organization and functioning of the courts, as well as the manner of appointment and removal of judges of any rank.

content.text: A law shall determine the organization and functioning of the courts, as well as the manner of appointment and removal of judges of any rank.

1948:95.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, the Prosecutor's Office supervises the observance of criminal laws, both by public officials and by other citizens.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, the Prosecutor's Office supervises the observance of criminal laws, both by public officials and by other citizens.

1948:96.contentEn: The Prosecutor's Office especially ensures the prosecution and punishment of crimes against the democratic order and freedom, economic interests, national independence and sovereignty of the Romanian State.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office especially ensures the prosecution and punishment of crimes against the democratic order and freedom, economic interests, national independence and sovereignty of the Romanian State.

1948:97.contentEn: The Prosecutor's Office is composed of a Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic and several prosecutors. The organization, attributions and functioning of the Prosecutor's Office shall be determined by law.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office is composed of a Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic and several prosecutors. The organization, attributions and functioning of the Prosecutor's Office shall be determined by law.

1948:98.contentEn: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall be appointed by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., upon the proposal of the Government.

content.text: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall be appointed by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R., upon the proposal of the Government.

1948:99.contentEn: The Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. In the middle there is a drilling rig, and around the coat of arms a crown of wheat ears.

content.text: The Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. In the middle there is a drilling rig, and around the coat of arms a crown of wheat ears.

1948:100.contentEn: The Seal of the State represents the coat of arms of the country.

content.text: The Seal of the State represents the coat of arms of the country.

1948:101.contentEn:
The flag of the Romanian People's Republic consists of the colors: blue, yellow and red, placed vertically.
In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the country.

content.text: The flag of the Romanian People's Republic consists of the colors: blue, yellow and red, placed vertically.
In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the country.

1948:102.contentEn: The capital of the Romanian People's Republic is the city of Bucharest.

content.text: The capital of the Romanian People's Republic is the city of Bucharest.

1948:103.contentEn: The Constitution of the Romanian People's Republic may be amended, in part or in whole, upon the proposal of the government or one third of the members of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: The Constitution of the Romanian People's Republic may be amended, in part or in whole, upon the proposal of the government or one third of the members of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:104.contentEn: The draft law on the amendment of the Constitution shall be considered adopted if it has been voted by two thirds of the total number of members of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

content.text: The draft law on the amendment of the Constitution shall be considered adopted if it has been voted by two thirds of the total number of members of the Grand National Assembly of the R.P.R.

1948:105.contentEn:
All existing codes and laws shall be revised in order to bring them into agreement with the Constitution.
From the date of entry into force of the Constitution, by its publication in the Official Gazette, all provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other provisions contrary to the provisions of the Constitution shall be repealed.
This Constitution was voted by the Grand National Assembly, in the session of April 13, 1948, and was approved unanimously by 401 votes.

content.text: All existing codes and laws shall be revised in order to bring them into agreement with the Constitution.

From the date of entry into force of the Constitution, by its publication in the Official Gazette, all provisions of laws, decrees, regulations and any other provisions contrary to the provisions of the Constitution shall be repealed.
This Constitution was voted by the Grand National Assembly, in the session of April 13, 1948, and was approved unanimously by 401 votes.

1952:1.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic is a state of the working people of towns and villages.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic is a state of the working people of towns and villages.

1952:2.contentEn: The basis of popular power in the Romanian People's Republic is the alliance of the working class with the working peasantry, in which the leading role belongs to the working class.

content.text: The basis of popular power in the Romanian People's Republic is the alliance of the working class with the working peasantry, in which the leading role belongs to the working class.

1952:3.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic was born and strengthened as a result of the liberation of the country by the armed forces of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics from under the yoke of fascism and from under imperialist domination, as a result of the overthrow of the power of the landlords and capitalists by the masses of towns and villages led by the working class, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic was born and strengthened as a result of the liberation of the country by the armed forces of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics from under the yoke of fascism and from under imperialist domination, as a result of the overthrow of the power of the landlords and capitalists by the masses of towns and villages led by the working class, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party.

1952:4.contentEn:
In the Romanian People's Republic, power belongs to the working people of towns and villages, who exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and the People's Councils.
The People's Councils constitute the political basis of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, power belongs to the working people of towns and villages, who exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and the People's Councils.
The People's Councils constitute the political basis of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:5.contentEn: The national economy of the Romanian People's Republic comprises three socio-economic formations: the socialist formation, small-scale commodity production, and the private-capitalist formation.

content.text: The national economy of the Romanian People's Republic comprises three socio-economic formations: the socialist formation, small-scale commodity production, and the private-capitalist formation.

1952:6.contentEn:
The foundation of the socialist socio-economic formation is socialist ownership of the means of production, which takes either the form of state property (common property of the people) or the form of cooperative-collectivist property (property of collective agricultural holdings or cooperative organizations).
In the socialist formation of the national economy, the exploitation of man by man is liquidated.
The socialist formation, to which the leading role in the national economy of the Romanian People's Republic belongs, constitutes the basis for the country's development on the path of socialism. The state of people's democracy, proclaiming that its main task is the construction of socialism, strengthens and expands the socialist formation unceasingly, ensures the uninterrupted increase of the material well-being and cultural level of the working people.

content.text: The foundation of the socialist socio-economic formation is socialist ownership of the means of production, which takes either the form of state property (common property of the people) or the form of cooperative-collectivist property (property of collective agricultural holdings or cooperative organizations).
In the socialist formation of the national economy, the exploitation of man by man is liquidated.

The socialist formation, to which the leading role in the national economy of the Romanian People's Republic belongs, constitutes the basis for the country's development on the path of socialism. The state of people's democracy, proclaiming that its main task is the construction of socialism, strengthens and expands the socialist formation unceasingly, ensures the uninterrupted increase of the material well-being and cultural level of the working people.

1952:7.contentEn: The riches of any nature of the subsoil, factories, plants and mines, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, means of communication of any kind, railway, river, maritime and air transport, banks, post office, telegraph, telephone, radio, printing facilities, cinematography and theatre, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations, communal enterprises and the nationalized part of the housing stock in towns, constitute state property, common property of the people.

content.text: The riches of any nature of the subsoil, factories, plants and mines, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, means of communication of any kind, railway, river, maritime and air transport, banks, post office, telegraph, telephone, radio, printing facilities, cinematography and theatre, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations, communal enterprises and the nationalized part of the housing stock in towns, constitute state property, common property of the people.

1952:8.contentEn: The land in the Romanian People's Republic belongs to those who cultivate it.

content.text: The land in the Romanian People's Republic belongs to those who cultivate it.

1952:9.contentEn:
The live and dead inventory of collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives, the production achieved, as well as all enterprises or constructions belonging to them, represent the common property of the collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives.
The peasants who are members of the collective agricultural holdings have for their personal use a plot of land next to the house and in personal ownership the household on this plot, the dwelling house, productive animals, poultry, small agricultural inventory – in accordance with the statute of the collective agricultural holding.

content.text: The live and dead inventory of collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives, the production achieved, as well as all enterprises or constructions belonging to them, represent the common property of the collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives.

The peasants who are members of the collective agricultural holdings have for their personal use a plot of land next to the house and in personal ownership the household on this plot, the dwelling house, productive animals, poultry, small agricultural inventory – in accordance with the statute of the collective agricultural holding.

1952:10.contentEn:
Small commodity production in the Romanian People's Republic includes small and medium-sized peasant households, which possess private property over the land based on the producer's own labor, as well as the workshops of craftsmen who do not exploit the labor of others. The State protects the right of private property over the land of peasants with small and medium-sized households, based on the laws in force.
The people's democratic State supports the peasants with small and medium-sized households and the craftsmen, with the aim of protecting them from capitalist exploitation, of increasing the production achieved by them and of raising their welfare.

content.text: Small commodity production in the Romanian People's Republic includes small and medium-sized peasant households, which possess private property over the land based on the producer's own labor, as well as the workshops of craftsmen who do not exploit the labor of others. The State protects the right of private property over the land of peasants with small and medium-sized households, based on the laws in force.

The people's democratic State supports the peasants with small and medium-sized households and the craftsmen, with the aim of protecting them from capitalist exploitation, of increasing the production achieved by them and of raising their welfare.

1952:11.contentEn:
The private-capitalist formation in the Romanian People's Republic includes kulak households, private commercial enterprises, small non-nationalized industrial enterprises, based on the exploitation of wage labor.
The people's democratic State consistently implements the policy of restricting and eliminating capitalist elements.

content.text: The private-capitalist formation in the Romanian People's Republic includes kulak households, private commercial enterprises, small non-nationalized industrial enterprises, based on the exploitation of wage labor.
The people's democratic State consistently implements the policy of restricting and eliminating capitalist elements.

1952:12.contentEn: The right of personal property of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic over income and savings derived from labor, over the dwelling house and auxiliary household next to the house, over household and personal use items, as well as the right of inheritance over the personal property of citizens are protected by law.

content.text: The right of personal property of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic over income and savings derived from labor, over the dwelling house and auxiliary household next to the house, over household and personal use items, as well as the right of inheritance over the personal property of citizens are protected by law.

1952:13.contentEn: The economic life of the Romanian People's Republic develops on the basis of the state plan of the national economy, in the interest of building socialism, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the working people, the strengthening of the country's national independence and its defense capacity.

content.text: The economic life of the Romanian People's Republic develops on the basis of the state plan of the national economy, in the interest of building socialism, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the working people, the strengthening of the country's national independence and its defense capacity.

1952:14.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, foreign trade is a state monopoly.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, foreign trade is a state monopoly.

1952:15.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, work is a duty and a matter of honor for every citizen capable of work, according to the principle “whoever does not work, does not eat”. In the Romanian People's Republic, the principle of socialism is being implemented more and more widely: “From each according to his abilities, to each according to his work”.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, work is a duty and a matter of honor for every citizen capable of work, according to the principle “whoever does not work, does not eat”. In the Romanian People's Republic, the principle of socialism is being implemented more and more widely: “From each according to his abilities, to each according to his work”.

1952:16.contentEn: The state regime of the Romanian People's Republic is the regime of people's democracy, which represents the power of the working people.

content.text: The state regime of the Romanian People's Republic is the regime of people's democracy, which represents the power of the working people.
The Romanian People's Democratic State – a unitary, sovereign, and independent state:

a) defends the independence and sovereignty of the Romanian people, the gains of the working people in towns and villages, the rights, freedoms, and power of the working people, against the enemies of the working people;

b) ensures the strengthening and development of the country's productive forces through its socialist industrialization, through the elimination of economic, technical, and cultural backwardness, through the gradual socialist transformation of agriculture based on the free consent of the working peasants;
c) organizes and develops the planned economy, relying on state and cooperative enterprises;
d) organizes the defense of the Republic against external enemies and commands the country's armed forces;
e) ensures the internal security of citizens, renders harmless and represses the enemies of the people;
f) manages the monetary and credit system, elaborates and implements the state budget, establishes the taxes, fees, and revenues necessary for the needs of the state;
g) administers the state industrial, agricultural, and commercial banks, enterprises, and institutions;
h) manages public education at all levels;
i) ensures the continuous improvement of the well-being and health of the popular masses in towns and villages;
j) ensures the development of the Romanian people's culture and the culture of national minorities, socialist in content, national in form;
k) ensures the application and observance of the Constitution and laws of the Romanian People's Republic, as they are the expression of the will and interests of the working people.

The observance and strict application of the Constitution and laws of the country is the main duty of every state institution and every citizen, as they are binding throughout the territory of the Republic.

1952:18.contentEn:
The Romanian People's Republic has the following administrative-territorial division:
The Regions: Arad, Bacău, Baia Mare, Bîrlad, Bucharest, Cluj, Constanța, Craiova, Galați, Hunedoara, Iași, Oradea, Pitești, Ploiești, Stalin, Suceava, Timișoara, the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic has the following administrative-territorial division:
The Regions: Arad, Bacău, Baia Mare, Bîrlad, Bucharest, Cluj, Constanța, Craiova, Galați, Hunedoara, Iași, Oradea, Pitești, Ploiești, Stalin, Suceava, Timișoara, the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

1952:19.contentEn:
The Hungarian Autonomous Region of the Romanian People's Republic is formed from the territory inhabited by the compact Hungarian Szekler population and has autonomous administrative leadership, elected by the population of the Autonomous Region.
The Hungarian Autonomous Region includes the districts: Ciuc, Gheorgheni, Odorhei, Reghin, Sîngeorgiu de Pădure, Sf. Gheorghe, Tîrgu-Mureș, Tîrgu-Secuiesc, Toplița.
The administrative center of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is the city of Tîrgu-Mureș.

content.text: The Hungarian Autonomous Region of the Romanian People's Republic is formed from the territory inhabited by the compact Hungarian Szekler population and has autonomous administrative leadership, elected by the population of the Autonomous Region.
The Hungarian Autonomous Region includes the districts: Ciuc, Gheorgheni, Odorhei, Reghin, Sîngeorgiu de Pădure, Sf. Gheorghe, Tîrgu-Mureș, Tîrgu-Secuiesc, Toplița.
The administrative center of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is the city of Tîrgu-Mureș.

1952:20.contentEn: The laws of the Romanian People's Republic, the decisions and provisions of the central state bodies are binding on the territory of the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

content.text: The laws of the Romanian People's Republic, the decisions and provisions of the central state bodies are binding on the territory of the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

1952:21.contentEn: The Regulation of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is elaborated by the People's Council of the Autonomous Region and submitted for approval to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Regulation of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is elaborated by the People's Council of the Autonomous Region and submitted for approval to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:22.contentEn: The supreme body of state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The supreme body of state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:23.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Grand National Assembly has in its direct competence:
a) the election of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic;
b) the formation of the Government of the Romanian People's Republic;
c) the amendment of the Constitution;
d) matters of war and peace;
e) the establishment of national economy plans;
f) the approval of the state budget, the conclusion of budget exercises, and the establishment of taxes and revenues intended for the state budget;
g) the establishment of the number of ministries, the naming, merging, and dissolution of ministries;
h) the modification of the division of the territory of the Romanian People's Republic into regions;
i) the granting of amnesty;
j) general control over the application of the Constitution.

1952:25.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly is elected by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, in electoral districts, one deputy for every 40,000 inhabitants.
The Grand National Assembly is elected for a term of 4 years.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly is elected by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, in electoral districts, one deputy for every 40,000 inhabitants.
The Grand National Assembly is elected for a term of 4 years.

1952:26.contentEn: A law is deemed adopted if it is voted for by a simple majority of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: A law is deemed adopted if it is voted for by a simple majority of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:27.contentEn:
After the adoption of laws by the Grand National Assembly, they are signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Bulletin of the Grand National Assembly.
The fulfillment of the laws adopted by the Grand National Assembly is mandatory for all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: After the adoption of laws by the Grand National Assembly, they are signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Bulletin of the Grand National Assembly.
The fulfillment of the laws adopted by the Grand National Assembly is mandatory for all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:28.contentEn: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly are held twice a year. The convocation of the Grand National Assembly is done by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly are held twice a year. The convocation of the Grand National Assembly is done by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:29.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly may be convened in extraordinary sessions by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, or at the request of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly may be convened in extraordinary sessions by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, or at the request of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

1952:30.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly elects in each legislature a President and two Vice-Presidents, who conduct the meetings of the Grand National Assembly in accordance with the internal rules of procedure.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly elects in each legislature a President and two Vice-Presidents, who conduct the meetings of the Grand National Assembly in accordance with the internal rules of procedure.

1952:31.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly validates the mandates of the elected deputies.
In order to verify the conditions under which the election of each deputy took place, the Grand National Assembly elects a validation committee. This committee submits its report for the approval of the Grand National Assembly, which decides on the validation or annulment of the election of each deputy.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly validates the mandates of the elected deputies.

In order to verify the conditions under which the election of each deputy took place, the Grand National Assembly elects a validation committee. This committee submits its report for the approval of the Grand National Assembly, which decides on the validation or annulment of the election of each deputy.

1952:32.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly may appoint committees of inquiry and review on various issues.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to provide these committees with the necessary information and documents.
The Grand National Assembly establishes for each committee separately its powers and mode of activity.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly may appoint committees of inquiry and review on various issues.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to provide these committees with the necessary information and documents.
The Grand National Assembly establishes for each committee separately its powers and mode of activity.

1952:33.contentEn: Each deputy has the right to ask questions or to interpellate the Government or individual ministers. The Government or the minister questioned has the obligation to respond verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days.

content.text: Each deputy has the right to ask questions or to interpellate the Government or individual ministers. The Government or the minister questioned has the obligation to respond verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days.

1952:34.contentEn: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be brought to trial or arrested without the approval of the Grand National Assembly during sessions, and between sessions – of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be brought to trial or arrested without the approval of the Grand National Assembly during sessions, and between sessions – of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:35.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, composed of a President, two Vice-Presidents, a Secretary and thirteen members.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, composed of a President, two Vice-Presidents, a Secretary and thirteen members.

1952:36.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly is responsible for all its activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly is responsible for all its activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
a) convenes the Grand National Assembly in sessions;
b) issues decrees;
c) interprets the laws in force in the Romanian People's Republic;
d) decides on consulting the people (referendum);
e) annuls the decisions and provisions of the Council of Ministers when they do not conform to the laws;

f) in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, dismisses and appoints ministers to the Government at the proposal of the President of the Council of Ministers and with the subsequent approval of the Grand National Assembly;
g) establishes the decorations, medals and honorary titles of the Romanian People's Republic;
h) confers the decorations, medals and honorary titles of the Romanian People's Republic; establishes military ranks, diplomatic ranks and other special titles;

i) in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, at the proposal of the Government, declares a state of war in the event of armed aggression directed against the Romanian People's Republic or against another state, in respect of which the Romanian People's Republic has obligations of mutual defense arising from international treaties;
j) appoints and dismisses the supreme commander of the armed forces of the Romanian People's Republic;
k) declares partial or general mobilization;
l) exercises the right of pardon and commutation of sentences;
m) ratifies and denounces the international treaties of the Romanian People's Republic;
n) accredits and recalls the plenipotentiary representatives of the Romanian People's Republic to foreign states;
o) receives the letters of accreditation and recall of the diplomatic representatives of foreign states, accredited to the Presidium;

p) in the interest of defending the Romanian People's Republic or ensuring public order and state security, proclaims a state of emergency in some localities or throughout the territory of the country.

1952:38.contentEn: The mandate of the Grand National Assembly ceases upon the expiration of the term for which it was elected.

content.text: The mandate of the Grand National Assembly ceases upon the expiration of the term for which it was elected.

1952:39.contentEn:
After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly sets new elections within a maximum of three months, counted from the day of the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly.
The Presidium in office retains its powers until the election of the new Presidium by the new Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly sets new elections within a maximum of three months, counted from the day of the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly.
The Presidium in office retains its powers until the election of the new Presidium by the new Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:40.contentEn: In the event of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

content.text: In the event of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

1952:41.contentEn: The newly elected Grand National Assembly shall be convened by the acting Presidium within a maximum of three months after the elections.

content.text: The newly elected Grand National Assembly shall be convened by the acting Presidium within a maximum of three months after the elections.

1952:42.contentEn: The supreme executive and dispositive body of the state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The supreme executive and dispositive body of the state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Council of Ministers is constituted by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic and is composed of:
– The President of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The Vice-Presidents of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The President of the State Planning Committee;
– The President of the State Control Commission;
– The Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The President of the State Supply Committee;
– The President of the State Committee for the Collection of Agricultural Products;
– The President of the Higher Education Committee;
– The President of the Cinematography Committee;
– The President of the Art Committee.

1952:44.contentEn: The Council of Ministers is responsible and accountable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is responsible and accountable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:45.contentEn: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic issues decisions and orders based on the laws in force or for the purpose of their application and controls their execution.

content.text: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic issues decisions and orders based on the laws in force or for the purpose of their application and controls their execution.

1952:46.contentEn: The application of the decisions and orders of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic is mandatory throughout the entire territory of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The application of the decisions and orders of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic is mandatory throughout the entire territory of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
a) coordinates and guides the activity of the ministries and other institutions subordinated to it;
b) takes measures for the realization of the national economy plan, the state budget and for the consolidation of the monetary and credit system;
