The provisions of Art. 52 shall apply, correspondingly, to the constitutional and legal committee.

content.text: In exercising the control of the constitutionality of laws, as well as for the preparation of works regarding the adoption of laws, the Grand National Assembly shall elect, for the duration of the legislature, a constitutional and legal committee.
Specialists who are not deputies may be elected to the constitutional and legal committee - without exceeding one third of the total number of members of the committee.

The constitutional and legal committee shall present to the Grand National Assembly reports or opinions regarding the constitutionality of laws. It shall also examine the constitutionality of decrees containing norms with the power of law and of the decisions of the Council of Ministers, according to the Rules of Procedure of the Grand National Assembly.
The provisions of Art. 52 shall apply, correspondingly, to the constitutional and legal committee.

1965:54.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly shall work in sessions.
The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly shall be convened twice a year, upon the proposal of the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.
The Grand National Assembly shall be convened, whenever necessary, in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the State Council, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly or at least one third of the total number of deputies.
The convening of the Grand National Assembly in sessions shall be done by decree of the State Council.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall work in sessions.
The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly shall be convened twice a year, upon the proposal of the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.

The Grand National Assembly shall be convened, whenever necessary, in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the State Council, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly or at least one third of the total number of deputies.
The convening of the Grand National Assembly in sessions shall be done by decree of the State Council.

1965:55.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly shall work only if at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies are present.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall work only if at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies are present.

1965:56.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly shall adopt laws and decisions.
Laws and decisions shall be adopted if they obtain the vote of the majority of the deputies of the Grand National Assembly.
The Constitution shall be adopted and amended with the vote of at least two thirds of the total number of deputies of the Grand National Assembly.
The laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly shall be signed by the president or vice-president of the Grand National Assembly who chaired the sitting.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall adopt laws and decisions.
Laws and decisions shall be adopted if they obtain the vote of the majority of the deputies of the Grand National Assembly.
The Constitution shall be adopted and amended with the vote of at least two thirds of the total number of deputies of the Grand National Assembly.
The laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly shall be signed by the president or vice-president of the Grand National Assembly who chaired the sitting.

1965:57.contentEn: After their adoption by the Grand National Assembly, the laws shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania, within a maximum of 10 days, under the signature of the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: After their adoption by the Grand National Assembly, the laws shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania, within a maximum of 10 days, under the signature of the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:58.contentEn:
Each deputy of the Grand National Assembly shall have the right to ask questions and address interpellations to the Council of Ministers or to any of its members.
The deputy, within the framework of the control exercised by the Grand National Assembly, may ask questions and address interpellations to the president of the Supreme Tribunal and the general prosecutor.
The person questioned or interpellated shall have the obligation to answer verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days and in any case in the same session.

content.text: Each deputy of the Grand National Assembly shall have the right to ask questions and address interpellations to the Council of Ministers or to any of its members.

The deputy, within the framework of the control exercised by the Grand National Assembly, may ask questions and address interpellations to the president of the Supreme Tribunal and the general prosecutor.
The person questioned or interpellated shall have the obligation to answer verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days and in any case in the same session.

1965:59.contentEn: In order to prepare the debates of the Grand National Assembly or the interpellations, the deputy shall have the right to request the necessary information from any state body, addressing for this purpose the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: In order to prepare the debates of the Grand National Assembly or the interpellations, the deputy shall have the right to request the necessary information from any state body, addressing for this purpose the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.

1965:60.contentEn: Each deputy shall be obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his/her activity and on the activity of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: Each deputy shall be obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his/her activity and on the activity of the Grand National Assembly.

1965:61.contentEn:
No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be detained, arrested, or brought to criminal trial without the prior approval of the Grand National Assembly during the session, and between sessions, of the State Council.
Only in the case of a flagrant offense may the deputy be detained without this approval.
The State Council

content.text: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be detained, arrested, or brought to criminal trial without the prior approval of the Grand National Assembly during the session, and between sessions, of the State Council.
Only in the case of a flagrant offense may the deputy be detained without this approval.
The State Council

1965:62.contentEn: The State Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme body of state power with permanent activity, subordinate to the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The State Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme body of state power with permanent activity, subordinate to the Grand National Assembly.

1965:63.contentEn:
The State Council permanently exercises the following main attributions:
  1. Sets the date of elections for the Grand National Assembly and the people's councils;
  2. Establishes the manner of organizing and conducting the referendum;
  3. Organizes the ministries and other central state bodies;
  4. Ratifies and denounces international treaties, with the exception of those whose ratification is the competence of the Grand National Assembly;
  5. Establishes military ranks;
  6. Institutes decorations and honorary titles.

content.text: The State Council permanently exercises the following main attributions:
<ol>

• Sets the date of elections for the Grand National Assembly and the people's councils;
  • Establishes the manner of organizing and conducting the referendum;
  • Organizes the ministries and other central state bodies;
  • Ratifies and denounces international treaties, with the exception of those whose ratification is the competence of the Grand National Assembly;
  • Establishes military ranks;
  • Institutes decorations and honorary titles.

1965:64.contentEn:
The State Council exercises, in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, the following main attributions:
  1. Establishes, without being able to modify the Constitution, norms with the force of law. The norms with the force of law are submitted, at the first session, to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws. The single national plan for economic and social development, the state budget, as well as the general account of the closing of the budget year, may be adopted by the State Council only when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
  2. Appoints and revokes the Prime Minister;
  3. Appoints and revokes the Council of Ministers and the Supreme Tribunal, when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
  4. Gives the laws in force a generally binding interpretation;
  5. Grants amnesty;
  6. Controls the application of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly, the activity of the Council of Ministers, the ministries and other central bodies of state administration, as well as the activity of the Prosecutor's Office; listens to reports from the Supreme Tribunal and controls its guiding decisions; controls the decisions of the people's councils;
  7. Declares, in case of emergency, partial or general mobilization;
  8. Declares, in case of emergency, a state of war. The state of war may be declared only in the case of armed aggression against the Socialist Republic of Romania or against another state towards which the Socialist Republic of Romania has obligations of mutual defense assumed through international treaties, if the situation for which the obligation to declare a state of war is established has occurred.
The attributions provided for in this Article may be exercised by the State Council also during the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, if economic and social needs require the immediate adoption of measures, and the Grand National Assembly is not meeting in its plenum; the norms with the force of law adopted are submitted to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws, upon the resumption of work in the plenum.

content.text: The State Council exercises, in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, the following main attributions:
<ol>

• Establishes, without being able to modify the Constitution, norms with the force of law. The norms with the force of law are submitted, at the first session, to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws. The single national plan for economic and social development, the state budget, as well as the general account of the closing of the budget year, may be adopted by the State Council only when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
  • Appoints and revokes the Prime Minister;
  • Appoints and revokes the Council of Ministers and the Supreme Tribunal, when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
  • Gives the laws in force a generally binding interpretation;
  • Grants amnesty;
  • Controls the application of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly, the activity of the Council of Ministers, the ministries and other central bodies of state administration, as well as the activity of the Prosecutor's Office; listens to reports from the Supreme Tribunal and controls its guiding decisions; controls the decisions of the people's councils;
  • Declares, in case of emergency, partial or general mobilization;
  • Declares, in case of emergency, a state of war. The state of war may be declared only in the case of armed aggression against the Socialist Republic of Romania or against another state towards which the Socialist Republic of Romania has obligations of mutual defense assumed through international treaties, if the situation for which the obligation to declare a state of war is established has occurred.

The attributions provided for in this Article may be exercised by the State Council also during the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, if economic and social needs require the immediate adoption of measures, and the Grand National Assembly is not meeting in its plenum; the norms with the force of law adopted are submitted to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws, upon the resumption of work in the plenum.

1965:65.contentEn: The State Council is elected by the Grand National Assembly from among its members, for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof. The State Council functions until the election of the new State Council in the next legislature.

content.text: The State Council is elected by the Grand National Assembly from among its members, for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof. The State Council functions until the election of the new State Council in the next legislature.

1965:66.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the President of the State Council.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the President of the State Council.

1965:67.contentEn: The State Council is composed of a president, vice-presidents and members.

content.text: The State Council is composed of a president, vice-presidents and members.

1965:68.contentEn: The State Council carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

content.text: The State Council carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

1965:69.contentEn:
The Council of State issues decrees and adopts decisions.
The decrees and decisions shall be signed by the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania. The normative decrees shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The Council of State issues decrees and adopts decisions.

The decrees and decisions shall be signed by the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania. The normative decrees shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:70.contentEn:
The Council of State shall present to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of its attributions, as well as on the observance and execution, in the state activity, of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly.
The Council of State, in its entirety, and each of its members shall be liable before the Grand National Assembly for the entire activity of the Council of State.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania

content.text: The Council of State shall present to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of its attributions, as well as on the observance and execution, in the state activity, of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly.
The Council of State, in its entirety, and each of its members shall be liable before the Grand National Assembly for the entire activity of the Council of State.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania

1965:71.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the head of state and represents the state power in the internal and international relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the head of state and represents the state power in the internal and international relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:72.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and remains in office until the election of the president in the next legislature.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and remains in office until the election of the president in the next legislature.

1965:73.contentEn:
Upon his election, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly:
"I swear to serve the homeland with faith, to act firmly for the defense of the country's independence, sovereignty and integrity, for the well-being and happiness of the entire people, for the building of socialism and communism in the Socialist Republic of Romania!
I swear to respect and defend the Constitution and the laws of the country, to do everything for the consistent application of the principles of socialist democracy, for the affirmation in the life of society of the norms of socialist ethics and equity!
I swear to unswervingly promote the foreign policy of friendship and alliance with all socialist countries, of collaboration with all the nations of the world, regardless of social order, on the basis of full equality of rights, of solidarity with the revolutionary, progressive forces everywhere, of peace and friendship between peoples!
I swear that I will always do my duty with honor and devotion for the brilliance and greatness of our socialist nation, of the Socialist Republic of Romania!"

content.text: Upon his election, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly:

"I swear to serve the homeland with faith, to act firmly for the defense of the country's independence, sovereignty and integrity, for the well-being and happiness of the entire people, for the building of socialism and communism in the Socialist Republic of Romania!

I swear to respect and defend the Constitution and the laws of the country, to do everything for the consistent application of the principles of socialist democracy, for the affirmation in the life of society of the norms of socialist ethics and equity!

I swear to unswervingly promote the foreign policy of friendship and alliance with all socialist countries, of collaboration with all the nations of the world, regardless of social order, on the basis of full equality of rights, of solidarity with the revolutionary, progressive forces everywhere, of peace and friendship between peoples!
I swear that I will always do my duty with honor and devotion for the brilliance and greatness of our socialist nation, of the Socialist Republic of Romania!"

1965:74.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme commander of the armed forces and the president of the Defence Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme commander of the armed forces and the president of the Defence Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:75.contentEn:
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall perform, in accordance with the Constitution and the laws, the following main duties:
  1. Presides over the State Council;
  2. Presides over the meetings of the Council of Ministers when it appears necessary;
  3. Establishes the measures of particular importance concerning the supreme interests of the country, which are to be submitted by the Grand National Assembly for consultation with the people, by referendum;
  4. Appoints and dismisses, upon the proposal of the Prime Minister, the Deputy Prime Ministers, Ministers and Presidents of other central bodies of state administration, who are part of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the heads of central state bodies, who are not part of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the members of the Supreme Tribunal;
  5. During the time when the Grand National Assembly is not convened in its plenum, appoints and dismisses the President of the Supreme Tribunal and the Prosecutor General;
  6. Grants the ranks of general, admiral and marshal;
  7. Confers decorations and honorary titles; authorizes the wearing of decorations conferred by other states;
  8. Grants pardon;
  9. Grants citizenship, approves renunciation of citizenship and withdraws Romanian citizenship; approves the establishment of domicile in Romania for citizens of other states;
  10. Grants the right of asylum;
  11. Establishes the ranks of diplomatic missions, accredits and recalls the diplomatic representatives of the Socialist Republic of Romania;
  12. Receives the letters of credence and recall of the diplomatic representatives of other states;
  13. Concludes international treaties on behalf of the Socialist Republic of Romania; may empower, for this purpose, the Prime Minister or members of the Council of Ministers or diplomatic representatives;
  14. In the interest of the defense of the Socialist Republic of Romania, of ensuring public order or state security, proclaims, in case of emergency, in some localities or throughout the territory of the country, the state of necessity.
In the performance of his duties, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania issues presidential decrees and decisions.

content.text:
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall perform, in accordance with the Constitution and the laws, the following main duties:
  1. Presides over the State Council;
  2. Presides over the meetings of the Council of Ministers when it appears necessary;
  3. Establishes the measures of particular importance concerning the supreme interests of the country, which are to be submitted by the Grand National Assembly for consultation with the people, by referendum;
  4. Appoints and dismisses, upon the proposal of the Prime Minister, the Deputy Prime Ministers, Ministers and Presidents of other central bodies of state administration, who are part of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the heads of central state bodies, who are not part of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the members of the Supreme Tribunal;
  5. During the time when the Grand National Assembly is not convened in its plenum, appoints and dismisses the President of the Supreme Tribunal and the Prosecutor General;
  6. Grants the ranks of general, admiral and marshal;
  7. Confers decorations and honorary titles; authorizes the wearing of decorations conferred by other states;
  8. Grants pardon;
  9. Grants citizenship, approves renunciation of citizenship and withdraws Romanian citizenship; approves the establishment of domicile in Romania for citizens of other states;
  10. Grants the right of asylum;
  11. Establishes the ranks of diplomatic missions, accredits and recalls the diplomatic representatives of the Socialist Republic of Romania;
  12. Receives the letters of credence and recall of the diplomatic representatives of other states;
  13. Concludes international treaties on behalf of the Socialist Republic of Romania; may empower, for this purpose, the Prime Minister or members of the Council of Ministers or diplomatic representatives;
  14. In the interest of the defense of the Socialist Republic of Romania, of ensuring public order or state security, proclaims, in case of emergency, in some localities or throughout the territory of the country, the state of necessity.
In the performance of his duties, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania issues presidential decrees and decisions.

1965:76.contentEn:
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is responsible to the Grand National Assembly for his entire activity.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania periodically presents to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of his duties and on the development of the state.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is responsible to the Grand National Assembly for his entire activity.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania periodically presents to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of his duties and on the development of the state.

1965:77.contentEn:
The Council of Ministers is the supreme body of state administration. The Council of Ministers exercises general leadership of executive activity throughout the country, having the following main attributions:
  1. Establishes general measures for the implementation of internal and external policy;
  2. Decides on the measures necessary for the organization and ensuring the execution of laws;
  3. Leads, coordinates and controls the activity of ministries and other central bodies of state administration;
  4. Elaborates the draft of the single national plan for economic and social development and the draft state budget, as well as any other draft laws; elaborates draft decrees;
  5. Establishes measures for the implementation of the single national plan for economic and social development and the state budget; draws up the general report on the implementation of the single national plan for economic and social development and the general account of the budget year;
  6. Establishes economic organizations, state enterprises and institutions of republican interest;
  7. Takes measures to ensure public order, defend the interests of the state and protect the rights of citizens;
  8. Takes measures, according to the decisions of the Defence Council, for the general organization of the armed forces and the fixing of annual contingents of citizens to be called to perform military service;
  9. Exercises general leadership in the field of relations with other states and takes measures for the conclusion of international agreements;
  10. Supports the activity of mass and public organizations;
  11. Exercises, under the conditions provided by law, its leadership and control attributions over the activity of the executive committees and executive bureaus of the people's councils.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is the supreme body of state administration.

The Council of Ministers exercises general leadership of executive activity throughout the country, having the following main attributions:
  1. Establishes general measures for the implementation of internal and external policy;
  2. Decides on the measures necessary for the organization and ensuring the execution of laws;
  3. Leads, coordinates and controls the activity of ministries and other central bodies of state administration;
  4. Elaborates the draft of the single national plan for economic and social development and the draft state budget, as well as any other draft laws; elaborates draft decrees;
  5. Establishes measures for the implementation of the single national plan for economic and social development and the state budget; draws up the general report on the implementation of the single national plan for economic and social development and the general account of the budget year;
  6. Establishes economic organizations, state enterprises and institutions of republican interest;
  7. Takes measures to ensure public order, defend the interests of the state and protect the rights of citizens;
  8. Takes measures, according to the decisions of the Defence Council, for the general organization of the armed forces and the fixing of annual contingents of citizens to be called to perform military service;
  9. Exercises general leadership in the field of relations with other states and takes measures for the conclusion of international agreements;
  10. Supports the activity of mass and public organizations;
  11. Exercises, under the conditions provided by law, its leadership and control attributions over the activity of the executive committees and executive bureaus of the people's councils.

1965:78.contentEn: The Council of Ministers is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session. The Council of Ministers functions until the election of the new Council of Ministers in the next legislature.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session. The Council of Ministers functions until the election of the new Council of Ministers in the next legislature.

1965:79.contentEn:
In the fulfillment of its attributions, the Council of Ministers adopts decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the laws.
Decisions of a normative character are published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: In the fulfillment of its attributions, the Council of Ministers adopts decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the laws.
Decisions of a normative character are published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:80.contentEn:
The Council of Ministers is composed of: the prime minister, the deputy prime ministers, the ministers, the state secretary ministers, as well as the presidents of other central bodies of state administration provided by law.
The Council of Ministers also includes, as members, the president of the Central Council of the General Trade Union Confederation, the president of the National Union of Agricultural Production Cooperatives, the president of the National Women's Council and the first secretary of the Central Committee of the Union of Communist Youth.
The Council of Ministers may establish an Executive Bureau for the operative resolution of current problems and the monitoring of the implementation of the Council of Ministers' decisions.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is composed of: the prime minister, the deputy prime ministers, the ministers, the state secretary ministers, as well as the presidents of other central bodies of state administration provided by law.

The Council of Ministers also includes, as members, the president of the Central Council of the General Trade Union Confederation, the president of the National Union of Agricultural Production Cooperatives, the president of the National Women's Council and the first secretary of the Central Committee of the Union of Communist Youth.
The Council of Ministers may establish an Executive Bureau for the operative resolution of current problems and the monitoring of the implementation of the Council of Ministers' decisions.

1965:81.contentEn: The Council of Ministers carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership, ensuring the unity of political and administrative action of the ministries and other central bodies of state administration.

content.text: The Council of Ministers carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership, ensuring the unity of political and administrative action of the ministries and other central bodies of state administration.

1965:82.contentEn: The Council of Ministers, as a whole, and each of its members are responsible to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council. Each member of the Council of Ministers is responsible both for his/her own activity and for the entire activity of the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The Council of Ministers, as a whole, and each of its members are responsible to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council. Each member of the Council of Ministers is responsible both for his/her own activity and for the entire activity of the Council of Ministers.

1965:83.contentEn:
The ministries and other central bodies of state administration implement the policy of the state in the branches or fields of activity for which they were established.
They lead, guide and control the enterprises, economic organizations and state institutions under their subordination.

content.text: The ministries and other central bodies of state administration implement the policy of the state in the branches or fields of activity for which they were established.
They lead, guide and control the enterprises, economic organizations and state institutions under their subordination.

1965:84.contentEn: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall issue, on the basis and for the purpose of the execution of the laws and of the decisions of the Council of Ministers, instructions and orders, as well as other acts provided by law; their normative acts shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall issue, on the basis and for the purpose of the execution of the laws and of the decisions of the Council of Ministers, instructions and orders, as well as other acts provided by law; their normative acts shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:85.contentEn: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall be responsible for the activity of the bodies they head before the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall be responsible for the activity of the bodies they head before the Council of Ministers.

1965:86.contentEn:
The people's councils are the local bodies of state power in the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected.
The people's councils shall manage the local activity, ensuring the economic, socio-cultural and urban-administrative development of the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected, the defence of socialist property, the protection of the rights of citizens, socialist legality and the maintenance of public order.
The people's councils shall organise the participation of citizens in the local settlement of state and public affairs.

content.text: The people's councils are the local bodies of state power in the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected.

The people's councils shall manage the local activity, ensuring the economic, socio-cultural and urban-administrative development of the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected, the defence of socialist property, the protection of the rights of citizens, socialist legality and the maintenance of public order.
The people's councils shall organise the participation of citizens in the local settlement of state and public affairs.

1965:87.contentEn:
The people's council shall exercise the following main attributions:
  1. Adopts the economic plan and the local budget, approves the account of the closing of the budgetary exercise;
  2. Elects and revokes the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau;
  3. Establishes economic organisations, state enterprises and institutions of local interest;
  4. Manages, guides and controls the activity of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, of the local specialised bodies of state administration, of the economic organisations, enterprises and subordinate institutions;
  5. Controls the decisions of the hierarchically inferior people's councils;
  6. Elects and revokes, according to the law, the judges, the people's assessors and the chief prosecutor of the county or of the municipality of Bucharest.

content.text: The people's council shall exercise the following main attributions:
<ol>
• Adopts the economic plan and the local budget, approves the account of the closing of the budgetary exercise;
• Elects and revokes the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau;
• Establishes economic organisations, state enterprises and institutions of local interest;

• Manages, guides and controls the activity of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, of the local specialised bodies of state administration, of the economic organisations, enterprises and subordinate institutions;
• Controls the decisions of the hierarchically inferior people's councils;
• Elects and revokes, according to the law, the judges, the people's assessors and the chief prosecutor of the county or of the municipality of Bucharest.

1965:88.contentEn:
The people's councils shall be composed of deputies elected in electoral districts, one deputy for each district.
The electoral districts formed for the election of deputies to a people's council shall have the same number of inhabitants.
The duration of the mandate of the county people's councils and of the municipality of Bucharest shall be 5 years, and that of the municipal, Bucharest municipality sectors, town and commune people's councils shall be 2 and a half years.
The date of elections for the people's councils shall be established according to the law.

content.text: The people's councils shall be composed of deputies elected in electoral districts, one deputy for each district.
The electoral districts formed for the election of deputies to a people's council shall have the same number of inhabitants.

The duration of the mandate of the county people's councils and of the municipality of Bucharest shall be 5 years, and that of the municipal, Bucharest municipality sectors, town and commune people's councils shall be 2 and a half years.
The date of elections for the people's councils shall be established according to the law.

1965:89.contentEn: The people's councils shall elect, from among the deputies, permanent commissions, which shall support them in the fulfilment of their tasks.

content.text: The people's councils shall elect, from among the deputies, permanent commissions, which shall support them in the fulfilment of their tasks.

1965:90.contentEn:
The people's councils shall work in sessions; the convening in sessions shall be done by the executive committee or, as the case may be, by the executive bureau of the people's council.
The people's councils shall be convened whenever necessary in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, or of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The people's councils shall work in sessions; the convening in sessions shall be done by the executive committee or, as the case may be, by the executive bureau of the people's council.

The people's councils shall be convened whenever necessary in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, or of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

1965:91.contentEn: The people's councils shall work in the presence of at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The people's councils shall work in the presence of at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

1965:92.contentEn: Each deputy is obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his activity and on the people's council in which he was elected.

content.text: Each deputy is obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his activity and on the people's council in which he was elected.

1965:93.contentEn:
The people's councils adopt decisions.
A decision is adopted if it obtains the vote of the majority of the deputies of the people's council.
Decisions of a normative nature shall be brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided for by law.

content.text: The people's councils adopt decisions.
A decision is adopted if it obtains the vote of the majority of the deputies of the people's council.
Decisions of a normative nature shall be brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided for by law.

1965:94.contentEn: The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council are local bodies of state administration with general competence in the administrative-territorial unit in which the people's council was elected.

content.text: The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council are local bodies of state administration with general competence in the administrative-territorial unit in which the people's council was elected.

1965:95.contentEn:
The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council have the following main attributions:
  1. They implement the laws, decrees, as well as the decisions of the Council of Ministers and the other acts of the superior bodies;
  2. They execute the decisions of the people's councils that elected them;
  3. They elaborate the draft economic plan and local budget;
  4. They execute the economic plan and the local budget, draw up the report on the fulfillment of the local economic plan, as well as the closing account of the budgetary exercise;
  5. They lead, guide and control the activity of the local specialized bodies of the state administration;
  6. They lead, guide, coordinate and control the activity of the economic organizations, enterprises and subordinate institutions;
  7. They lead, guide and control the activity of the executive committees or, as the case may be, of the executive bureaus of the people's councils hierarchically inferior to the people's councils that elected them.
In the interval between the sessions of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau also fulfills the attributions thereof, with the exception of those provided for in Art. 87 items 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, submitting the adopted decisions for ratification to the people's council at its first session.

content.text:
The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council have the following main attributions:
  1. They implement the laws, decrees, as well as the decisions of the Council of Ministers and the other acts of the superior bodies;
  2. They execute the decisions of the people's councils that elected them;
  3. They elaborate the draft economic plan and local budget;
  4. They execute the economic plan and the local budget, draw up the report on the fulfillment of the local economic plan, as well as the closing account of the budgetary exercise;
  5. They lead, guide and control the activity of the local specialized bodies of the state administration;
  6. They lead, guide, coordinate and control the activity of the economic organizations, enterprises and subordinate institutions;
  7. They lead, guide and control the activity of the executive committees or, as the case may be, of the executive bureaus of the people's councils hierarchically inferior to the people's councils that elected them.

In the interval between the sessions of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau also fulfills the attributions thereof, with the exception of those provided for in Art. 87 items 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, submitting the adopted decisions for ratification to the people's council at its first session.

1965:96.contentEn:
The county people's councils, of the municipality of Bucharest, of its sectors, as well as those of the municipalities, elect executive committees, and the people's councils of the cities and communes elect executive bureaus.
The executive committee or the executive bureau is elected from among the deputies of the people's council at the first session after the elections, for the duration of the mandate of that council.
After the expiration of the mandate of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau continues to function until the election of the new executive committee or bureau.

content.text: The county people's councils, of the municipality of Bucharest, of its sectors, as well as those of the municipalities, elect executive committees, and the people's councils of the cities and communes elect executive bureaus.
The executive committee or the executive bureau is elected from among the deputies of the people's council at the first session after the elections, for the duration of the mandate of that council.

After the expiration of the mandate of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau continues to function until the election of the new executive committee or bureau.

1965:97.contentEn: The executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council is composed, according to the law, of a president, one or more vice-presidents and other members.

content.text: The executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council is composed, according to the law, of a president, one or more vice-presidents and other members.

1965:98.contentEn:
In the exercise of its attributions, the executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council issues decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the law.
Decisions of a normative nature shall be brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided for by law.

content.text: In the exercise of its attributions, the executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council issues decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the law.
Decisions of a normative nature shall be brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided for by law.

1965:99.contentEn:
The executive committee and the executive bureau carry out their activity according to the principle of collective leadership.
The executive committee or the executive bureau as a whole and each of its members are responsible to the people's council that elected them, as well as to the executive committee or the executive bureau of the hierarchically superior people's council and to the Council of Ministers.
Each member of the executive committee or of the executive bureau is responsible both for his own activity and for the entire activity of the body of which he is a part.

content.text: The executive committee and the executive bureau carry out their activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

The executive committee or the executive bureau as a whole and each of its members are responsible to the people's council that elected them, as well as to the executive committee or the executive bureau of the hierarchically superior people's council and to the Council of Ministers.
Each member of the executive committee or of the executive bureau is responsible both for his own activity and for the entire activity of the body of which he is a part.

1965:100.contentEn: The people's councils organize, according to the law, alongside their executive committees or, as the case may be, the executive bureaus, local specialized bodies of the state administration. The local specialized bodies of the state administration are subordinated both to the people's council and the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau, and to the hierarchically superior local and central bodies of the state administration.

content.text: The people's councils organize, according to the law, alongside their executive committees or, as the case may be, the executive bureaus, local specialized bodies of the state administration. The local specialized bodies of the state administration are subordinated both to the people's council and the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau, and to the hierarchically superior local and central bodies of the state administration.

1965:101.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, justice is administered, according to the law, by the Supreme Tribunal, the county tribunals, the courts of law, as well as by the military tribunals.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, justice is administered, according to the law, by the Supreme Tribunal, the county tribunals, the courts of law, as well as by the military tribunals.

1965:102.contentEn:
Through judicial activity, the tribunals and courts of law defend the socialist order and the rights of individuals, educating citizens in the spirit of respecting the laws.
The tribunals and courts of law, in applying penal sanctions, aim at the correction and re-education of offenders, as well as the prevention of the commission of new offenses.

content.text: Through judicial activity, the tribunals and courts of law defend the socialist order and the rights of individuals, educating citizens in the spirit of respecting the laws.
The tribunals and courts of law, in applying penal sanctions, aim at the correction and re-education of offenders, as well as the prevention of the commission of new offenses.

1965:103.contentEn:
The tribunals and courts of law adjudicate civil, criminal, and any other cases falling within their competence.
In the cases provided for by law, the tribunals and courts of law exercise control over the decisions of administrative or public bodies with jurisdictional activity.
The tribunals and courts of law adjudicate the claims of those injured in their rights by administrative acts, being able to rule, under the conditions of the law, also on the legality of these acts.

content.text: The tribunals and courts of law adjudicate civil, criminal, and any other cases falling within their competence.
In the cases provided for by law, the tribunals and courts of law exercise control over the decisions of administrative or public bodies with jurisdictional activity.
The tribunals and courts of law adjudicate the claims of those injured in their rights by administrative acts, being able to rule, under the conditions of the law, also on the legality of these acts.

1965:104.contentEn:
The Supreme Tribunal exercises general control over the judicial activity of all tribunals and courts of law. The manner of exercising this control is established by law.
In order to ensure the uniform application of the laws in judicial activity, the Supreme Tribunal issues, in its plenary session, guiding decisions.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal exercises general control over the judicial activity of all tribunals and courts of law. The manner of exercising this control is established by law.
In order to ensure the uniform application of the laws in judicial activity, the Supreme Tribunal issues, in its plenary session, guiding decisions.

1965:105.contentEn:
The Supreme Tribunal is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session.
The Supreme Tribunal functions until the election of the new Supreme Tribunal in the following legislature.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session.
The Supreme Tribunal functions until the election of the new Supreme Tribunal in the following legislature.

1965:106.contentEn: The Supreme Tribunal is responsible for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and between sessions, to the State Council.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal is responsible for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and between sessions, to the State Council.

1965:107.contentEn:
The organization of the courts of law and tribunals, their competence, and the procedure of adjudication are established by law.
The adjudication of cases in the first instance in the courts of law, in the county tribunals, and in the military tribunals is done with the participation of people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

content.text: The organization of the courts of law and tribunals, their competence, and the procedure of adjudication are established by law.

The adjudication of cases in the first instance in the courts of law, in the county tribunals, and in the military tribunals is done with the participation of people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

1965:108.contentEn: Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.

content.text: Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.

1965:109.contentEn:
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, judicial procedure is conducted in the Romanian language, ensuring, in the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of a nationality other than the Romanian one, the use of the mother tongue of that population.
The parties who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted are ensured the possibility to become acquainted, through an interpreter, with the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, judicial procedure is conducted in the Romanian language, ensuring, in the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of a nationality other than the Romanian one, the use of the mother tongue of that population.

The parties who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted are ensured the possibility to become acquainted, through an interpreter, with the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

1965:110.contentEn: The trial takes place in public session, with the exception of the cases provided for by law.

content.text: The trial takes place in public session, with the exception of the cases provided for by law.

1965:111.contentEn: In their judicial activity, judges and people's assessors are independent and subject only to the law.

content.text: In their judicial activity, judges and people's assessors are independent and subject only to the law.

1965:112.contentEn: The Prosecutor's Office of the Socialist Republic of Romania supervises the activity of the criminal investigation bodies and the bodies for the execution of punishments and ensures, under the conditions of the law, the observance of legality, the defense of the socialist order, of the legitimate rights and interests of socialist organizations, of other legal persons, as well as of citizens.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office of the Socialist Republic of Romania supervises the activity of the criminal investigation bodies and the bodies for the execution of punishments and ensures, under the conditions of the law, the observance of legality, the defense of the socialist order, of the legitimate rights and interests of socialist organizations, of other legal persons, as well as of citizens.

1965:113.contentEn:
The Prosecutor's Office is headed by the Prosecutor General. The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are: the General Prosecutor's Office, the county prosecutor's offices, the local prosecutor's offices and the military prosecutor's offices.
The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are hierarchically subordinated.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office is headed by the Prosecutor General. The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are: the General Prosecutor's Office, the county prosecutor's offices, the local prosecutor's offices and the military prosecutor's offices.
The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are hierarchically subordinated.

1965:114.contentEn:
The Prosecutor General is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and functions until the election of the new Prosecutor General in the first session of the following legislature.
Prosecutors are appointed according to the law, with the exception of those provided for in Art. 87 pct. 6.

content.text: The Prosecutor General is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and functions until the election of the new Prosecutor General in the first session of the following legislature.
Prosecutors are appointed according to the law, with the exception of those provided for in Art. 87 pct. 6.

1965:115.contentEn: The Prosecutor General is accountable to the Grand National Assembly for the activity of the Prosecutor's Office, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council.

content.text: The Prosecutor General is accountable to the Grand National Assembly for the activity of the Prosecutor's Office, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council.

1965:116.contentEn: The coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the coat of arms there is a derrick. The coat of arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the coat of arms there is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the coat of arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which is written "REPUBLICA SOCIALISTA ROMANIA".

content.text: The coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the coat of arms there is a derrick. The coat of arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the coat of arms there is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the coat of arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which is written "REPUBLICA SOCIALISTA ROMANIA".

1965:117.contentEn: The state seal depicts the country's coat of arms, around which is written "REPUBLICA SOCIALISTA ROMANIA".

content.text: The state seal depicts the country's coat of arms, around which is written "REPUBLICA SOCIALISTA ROMANIA".

1965:118.contentEn: The flag of the Socialist Republic of Romania bears the colors red, yellow and blue, placed vertically, with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The flag of the Socialist Republic of Romania bears the colors red, yellow and blue, placed vertically, with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1965:119.contentEn: The state anthem of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall be approved by the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The state anthem of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall be approved by the Grand National Assembly.

1965:120.contentEn: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its adoption.

content.text: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its adoption.

1965:121.contentEn: The Constitution of September 24, 1952, as well as any provisions of laws, decrees and other normative acts contrary to the provisions of the present Constitution, are repealed on the same date.

content.text: The Constitution of September 24, 1952, as well as any provisions of laws, decrees and other normative acts contrary to the provisions of the present Constitution, are repealed on the same date.

1986:1.contentEn: Romania is a socialist republic. The Socialist Republic of Romania is a state of the working people from cities and villages, sovereign, independent and unitary. Its territory is inalienable and indivisible.

content.text: Romania is a socialist republic. The Socialist Republic of Romania is a state of the working people from cities and villages, sovereign, independent and unitary. Its territory is inalienable and indivisible.

1986:2.contentEn:
All power in the Socialist Republic of Romania belongs to the people, free and in control of their destiny.
People's power is based on the worker-peasant alliance. In close unity, the working class – the leading class in society -, the peasantry, the intelligentsia, the other categories of working people, regardless of nationality, build the socialist order, creating the conditions for the transition to communism.

content.text: All power in the Socialist Republic of Romania belongs to the people, free and in control of their destiny.

People's power is based on the worker-peasant alliance. In close unity, the working class – the leading class in society -, the peasantry, the intelligentsia, the other categories of working people, regardless of nationality, build the socialist order, creating the conditions for the transition to communism.

1986:3.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the leading political force of the entire society is the Romanian Communist Party.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the leading political force of the entire society is the Romanian Communist Party.

1986:4.contentEn:
As the sovereign holder of power, the people exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and through the people's councils, bodies elected by universal, equal, direct and secret vote.
The Grand National Assembly and the people's councils constitute the basis of the entire system of state bodies.
The Grand National Assembly is the supreme body of state power, under whose leadership and control all other state bodies carry out their activity.

content.text: As the sovereign holder of power, the people exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and through the people's councils, bodies elected by universal, equal, direct and secret vote.
The Grand National Assembly and the people's councils constitute the basis of the entire system of state bodies.
The Grand National Assembly is the supreme body of state power, under whose leadership and control all other state bodies carry out their activity.

1986:5.contentEn:
The national economy of Romania is a socialist economy, based on socialist ownership of the means of production.
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the exploitation of man by man is forever abolished, and the socialist principle of distribution according to the quantity and quality of work is implemented.
Work is a duty of honor for every citizen of the country.

content.text: The national economy of Romania is a socialist economy, based on socialist ownership of the means of production.
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the exploitation of man by man is forever abolished, and the socialist principle of distribution according to the quantity and quality of work is implemented.
Work is a duty of honor for every citizen of the country.

1986:6.contentEn: Socialist ownership of the means of production is either state property – over goods belonging to the entire people, or cooperative property – over goods belonging to each cooperative organization.

content.text: Socialist ownership of the means of production is either state property – over goods belonging to the entire people, or cooperative property – over goods belonging to each cooperative organization.

1986:7.contentEn: Resources of any nature in the subsoil, mines, lands from the state land fund, forests, waters, natural energy sources, factories and plants, banks, state agricultural enterprises, stations for the mechanization of agriculture, communication routes, state means of transport and telecommunications, the state fund of buildings and dwellings, the material basis of state socio-cultural institutions, belong to the entire people, are state property.

content.text: Resources of any nature in the subsoil, mines, lands from the state land fund, forests, waters, natural energy sources, factories and plants, banks, state agricultural enterprises, stations for the mechanization of agriculture, communication routes, state means of transport and telecommunications, the state fund of buildings and dwellings, the material basis of state socio-cultural institutions, belong to the entire people, are state property.

1986:8.contentEn: Foreign trade is a state monopoly.

content.text: Foreign trade is a state monopoly.

1986:9.contentEn:
The land of agricultural production cooperatives, the animals, tools, installations and constructions belonging to them are cooperative property.
The plot of land which, according to the statute of the agricultural production cooperatives, is used by the family households of the cooperative peasants, constitutes cooperative property.
The dwelling house and the household constructions, the land on which they are located, as well as, according to the statute of the agricultural cooperatives, the production animals and the small agricultural inventory, constitute the personal property of the cooperative peasants. The tools, machines, installations and constructions of handicraft cooperatives and of cooperatives for the production, acquisition and sale of goods are cooperative property.

content.text: The land of agricultural production cooperatives, the animals, tools, installations and constructions belonging to them are cooperative property.
The plot of land which, according to the statute of the agricultural production cooperatives, is used by the family households of the cooperative peasants, constitutes cooperative property.

The dwelling house and the household constructions, the land on which they are located, as well as, according to the statute of the agricultural cooperatives, the production animals and the small agricultural inventory, constitute the personal property of the cooperative peasants. The tools, machines, installations and constructions of handicraft cooperatives and of cooperatives for the production, acquisition and sale of goods are cooperative property.

1986:10.contentEn:
Agricultural production cooperatives, a socialist form of organization of agriculture, ensure conditions for the intensive cultivation of the land and the application of advanced science, contribute, through increased production, to the development of the national economy, to the continuous raising of the living standards of the peasantry and of the entire people.
The state supports agricultural production cooperatives and protects their property. The state also supports other cooperative organizations and protects their property.

content.text: Agricultural production cooperatives, a socialist form of organization of agriculture, ensure conditions for the intensive cultivation of the land and the application of advanced science, contribute, through increased production, to the development of the national economy, to the continuous raising of the living standards of the peasantry and of the entire people.
The state supports agricultural production cooperatives and protects their property. The state also supports other cooperative organizations and protects their property.

1986:11.contentEn:
Under the conditions of cooperativized agriculture, the state guarantees to the peasants who cannot associate in agricultural production cooperatives the ownership of the land they work themselves and their families, of the tools used for this purpose, as well as the ownership of working and production animals.
The ownership of craftsmen over their own workshops is also guaranteed.

content.text: Under the conditions of cooperativized agriculture, the state guarantees to the peasants who cannot associate in agricultural production cooperatives the ownership of the land they work themselves and their families, of the tools used for this purpose, as well as the ownership of working and production animals.
The ownership of craftsmen over their own workshops is also guaranteed.

1986:12.contentEn: Land and buildings may be expropriated only for works of public interest and with the payment of fair compensation.

content.text: Land and buildings may be expropriated only for works of public interest and with the payment of fair compensation.

In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the entire state activity is aimed at developing the order and flourishing of the socialist nation, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the people, ensuring the freedom and dignity of man, the multilateral affirmation of the human personality.
For this, the Romanian socialist state:
Organizes, plans and manages the national economy;
Protects socialist property;
Guarantees the full exercise of the rights of citizens, ensures socialist legality and defends the rule of law;
Develops education of all levels, ensures the conditions for the development of science, art and culture, carries out health protection;
Ensures the defense of the country and organizes its armed forces;
Organizes relations with other states.

1986:14.contentEn:
The Socialist Republic of Romania maintains and develops relations of friendship and collaboration with the socialist countries in the spirit of socialist internationalism, promotes relations of collaboration with countries having a different social-political order, is active in international organizations in order to ensure peace and understanding between peoples.
The external relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania are based on the principles of respect for national sovereignty and independence, equality of rights and mutual advantage, non-interference in internal affairs.

content.text: The Socialist Republic of Romania maintains and develops relations of friendship and collaboration with the socialist countries in the spirit of socialist internationalism, promotes relations of collaboration with countries having a different social-political order, is active in international organizations in order to ensure peace and understanding between peoples.

The external relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania are based on the principles of respect for national sovereignty and independence, equality of rights and mutual advantage, non-interference in internal affairs.

1986:15.contentEn:
The territory of the Socialist Republic of Romania is organised into administrative-territorial units: the county, the city and the commune. The capital of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the municipality of Bucharest, which is organised into sectors.
The more important cities may be organised as municipalities.

content.text: The territory of the Socialist Republic of Romania is organised into administrative-territorial units: the county, the city and the commune. The capital of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the municipality of Bucharest, which is organised into sectors.
The more important cities may be organised as municipalities.

1986:16.contentEn: Romanian citizenship is acquired and lost according to the law.

content.text: Romanian citizenship is acquired and lost according to the law.

1986:17.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania, without distinction as to nationality, race, sex or religion, are equal in rights in all fields of economic, political, legal, social and cultural life.
The State guarantees the equality of rights of citizens. No restriction of these rights and no distinction in their exercise on the grounds of nationality, race, sex or religion shall be permitted.
Any manifestation aimed at establishing such restrictions, nationalist-chauvinist propaganda, incitement to racial or national hatred, shall be punished by law.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania, without distinction as to nationality, race, sex or religion, are equal in rights in all fields of economic, political, legal, social and cultural life.
The State guarantees the equality of rights of citizens. No restriction of these rights and no distinction in their exercise on the grounds of nationality, race, sex or religion shall be permitted.
Any manifestation aimed at establishing such restrictions, nationalist-chauvinist propaganda, incitement to racial or national hatred, shall be punished by law.

1986:18.contentEn:
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, citizens have the right to work. Every citizen is ensured the possibility to carry out, according to his/her training, an activity in the economic, administrative, social or cultural field, remunerated according to its quantity and quality. Equal pay for equal work.
The law shall establish measures for the protection and safety of labour, as well as special measures for the protection of the labour of women and youth.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, citizens have the right to work. Every citizen is ensured the possibility to carry out, according to his/her training, an activity in the economic, administrative, social or cultural field, remunerated according to its quantity and quality. Equal pay for equal work.
The law shall establish measures for the protection and safety of labour, as well as special measures for the protection of the labour of women and youth.

1986:19.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to rest.
The right to rest is guaranteed to those who work by establishing the maximum duration of the working day at 8 hours, a weekly rest and paid annual leave.
In heavy and very heavy work sectors, the duration of the working day is reduced below 8 hours, without reducing remuneration.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to rest.
The right to rest is guaranteed to those who work by establishing the maximum duration of the working day at 8 hours, a weekly rest and paid annual leave.
In heavy and very heavy work sectors, the duration of the working day is reduced below 8 hours, without reducing remuneration.

1986:20.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to material security in old age, illness or incapacity for work.
The right to material security is realised for workers and civil servants through pensions and sickness benefits granted within the state social insurance system, and for members of cooperative organisations or other public organisations, through the forms of insurance organised by them. The State provides medical assistance through its health institutions.
Paid maternity leave is guaranteed.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to material security in old age, illness or incapacity for work.

The right to material security is realised for workers and civil servants through pensions and sickness benefits granted within the state social insurance system, and for members of cooperative organisations or other public organisations, through the forms of insurance organised by them. The State provides medical assistance through its health institutions.
Paid maternity leave is guaranteed.

1986:21.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to education.
The right to education is ensured through compulsory general education, through free education of all grades, as well as through the system of state scholarships.
Education in the Socialist Republic of Romania is state education.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to education.
The right to education is ensured through compulsory general education, through free education of all grades, as well as through the system of state scholarships.
Education in the Socialist Republic of Romania is state education.

1986:22.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, co-inhabiting nationalities are ensured the free use of their mother tongue, as well as books, newspapers, magazines, theatres, education of all grades, in their own language. In the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of another nationality than the Romanian one, all bodies and institutions use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationality and make appointments of officials from among it or other citizens who know the language and way of life of the local population.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, co-inhabiting nationalities are ensured the free use of their mother tongue, as well as books, newspapers, magazines, theatres, education of all grades, in their own language. In the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of another nationality than the Romanian one, all bodies and institutions use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationality and make appointments of officials from among it or other citizens who know the language and way of life of the local population.

1986:23.contentEn:
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, women have equal rights with men.
The State protects marriage and the family and defends the interests of the mother and child.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, women have equal rights with men.
The State protects marriage and the family and defends the interests of the mother and child.

1986:24.contentEn: The Socialist Republic of Romania ensures to young people the necessary conditions for the development of their physical and intellectual aptitudes.

content.text: The Socialist Republic of Romania ensures to young people the necessary conditions for the development of their physical and intellectual aptitudes.

1986:25.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to elect and to be elected to the Grand National Assembly and to the people's councils.
The vote is universal, equal, direct and secret. All citizens who have reached the age of 18 have the right to vote.
Citizens with the right to vote who have reached the age of 23 may be elected deputies to the Grand National Assembly and to the people's councils.
The right to submit candidacies belongs to the Front of Socialist Democracy and Unity, the broadest permanent, revolutionary, democratic political body, with a representative character, which constitutes the organizational framework for uniting, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party, the political and social forces of our socialist nation, of all mass and public organizations, for the participation of the entire people in the implementation of the internal and external policy of the party and the state, in the management of all fields of activity.
The voters have the right to recall the deputy at any time according to the procedure provided by law.
The alienated and the mentally deficient, as well as persons deprived of these rights for the duration established by a court decision of conviction, do not have the right to elect and to be elected.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to elect and to be elected to the Grand National Assembly and to the people's councils.
The vote is universal, equal, direct and secret. All citizens who have reached the age of 18 have the right to vote.
Citizens with the right to vote who have reached the age of 23 may be elected deputies to the Grand National Assembly and to the people's councils.

The right to submit candidacies belongs to the Front of Socialist Democracy and Unity, the broadest permanent, revolutionary, democratic political body, with a representative character, which constitutes the organizational framework for uniting, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party, the political and social forces of our socialist nation, of all mass and public organizations, for the participation of the entire people in the implementation of the internal and external policy of the party and the state, in the management of all fields of activity.
The voters have the right to recall the deputy at any time according to the procedure provided by law.
The alienated and the mentally deficient, as well as persons deprived of these rights for the duration established by a court decision of conviction, do not have the right to elect and to be elected.

1986:26.contentEn:
The most advanced and conscious citizens from among the workers, peasants, intellectuals and other categories of working people unite in the Romanian Communist Party, the highest form of organization of the working class, its vanguard detachment.
The Romanian Communist Party expresses and faithfully serves the aspirations and vital interests of the people, fulfills the leading role in all fields of socialist construction, guides the activity of mass and public organizations, as well as of state bodies.

content.text: The most advanced and conscious citizens from among the workers, peasants, intellectuals and other categories of working people unite in the Romanian Communist Party, the highest form of organization of the working class, its vanguard detachment.
