The Romanian Communist Party expresses and faithfully serves the aspirations and vital interests of the people, fulfills the leading role in all fields of socialist construction, guides the activity of mass and public organizations, as well as of state bodies.

1986:27.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to associate in trade union, cooperative, youth, women's, socio-cultural organizations, in creative unions, scientific, technical, sports associations, as well as in other public organizations. The State supports the activity of mass and public organizations, creates conditions for the development of the material base of these organizations and protects their patrimony.
Mass and public organizations ensure the broad participation of the popular masses in the political, economic, social, cultural life of the Socialist Republic of Romania and in the exercise of public control – an expression of the democratism of the socialist order. Through mass and public organizations, the Romanian Communist Party carries out an organized link with the working class, the peasantry, the intelligentsia and other categories of working people, mobilizes them in the struggle for the completion of the construction of socialism.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to associate in trade union, cooperative, youth, women's, socio-cultural organizations, in creative unions, scientific, technical, sports associations, as well as in other public organizations. The State supports the activity of mass and public organizations, creates conditions for the development of the material base of these organizations and protects their patrimony.

Mass and public organizations ensure the broad participation of the popular masses in the political, economic, social, cultural life of the Socialist Republic of Romania and in the exercise of public control – an expression of the democratism of the socialist order. Through mass and public organizations, the Romanian Communist Party carries out an organized link with the working class, the peasantry, the intelligentsia and other categories of working people, mobilizes them in the struggle for the completion of the construction of socialism.

1986:28.contentEn: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania are guaranteed freedom of speech, of the press, of assembly, of meetings and of demonstrations.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania are guaranteed freedom of speech, of the press, of assembly, of meetings and of demonstrations.

1986:29.contentEn:
Freedom of speech, of the press, of assembly, of meetings and of demonstrations may not be used for purposes contrary to the socialist order and the interests of those who work.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited. Participation in such associations and propaganda of a fascist or anti-democratic nature are punished by law.

content.text: Freedom of speech, of the press, of assembly, of meetings and of demonstrations may not be used for purposes contrary to the socialist order and the interests of those who work.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited. Participation in such associations and propaganda of a fascist or anti-democratic nature are punished by law.

1986:30.contentEn:
Freedom of conscience is guaranteed to all citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.
Everyone is free to share or not a religious belief. The freedom to exercise religious worship is guaranteed. Religious denominations are organized and function freely. The manner of organization and functioning of religious denominations is regulated by law.
The school is separated from the church. No religious confession, congregation or community may open or maintain other educational institutions than special schools for the training of cult personnel.

content.text: Freedom of conscience is guaranteed to all citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

Everyone is free to share or not a religious belief. The freedom to exercise religious worship is guaranteed. Religious denominations are organized and function freely. The manner of organization and functioning of religious denominations is regulated by law.

The school is separated from the church. No religious confession, congregation or community may open or maintain other educational institutions than special schools for the training of cult personnel.

1986:31.contentEn:
The inviolability of the person is guaranteed to the citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.
No person may be detained or arrested unless there is evidence or well-grounded indications that he has committed an act provided for and punished by law. The investigation bodies may order the detention of a person for a maximum period of 24 hours.
No one may be arrested except on the basis of an arrest warrant issued by a court or prosecutor.
The right to defence is guaranteed throughout the course of the proceedings.

content.text: The inviolability of the person is guaranteed to the citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

No person may be detained or arrested unless there is evidence or well-grounded indications that he has committed an act provided for and punished by law. The investigation bodies may order the detention of a person for a maximum period of 24 hours.
No one may be arrested except on the basis of an arrest warrant issued by a court or prosecutor.
The right to defence is guaranteed throughout the course of the proceedings.

1986:32.contentEn: The domicile is inviolable. No one may enter a person's dwelling without his consent, except in the cases and under the conditions specifically provided for by law.

content.text: The domicile is inviolable. No one may enter a person's dwelling without his consent, except in the cases and under the conditions specifically provided for by law.

1986:33.contentEn: The secrecy of correspondence and telephone conversations is guaranteed.

content.text: The secrecy of correspondence and telephone conversations is guaranteed.

1986:34.contentEn: The right to petition is guaranteed. State bodies have the obligation to resolve citizens' petitions regarding personal or public rights and interests.

content.text: The right to petition is guaranteed. State bodies have the obligation to resolve citizens' petitions regarding personal or public rights and interests.

1986:35.contentEn: Anyone injured in a right of his by an illegal act of a state body may request the competent bodies, under the conditions provided for by law, the annulment of the act and the reparation of the damage.

content.text: Anyone injured in a right of his by an illegal act of a state body may request the competent bodies, under the conditions provided for by law, the annulment of the act and the reparation of the damage.

1986:36.contentEn:
The right of personal property is protected by law.
The object of the right of personal property may be the income and savings from work, the dwelling house, the household next to it and the land on which they are located, as well as goods for personal use and comfort.

content.text: The right of personal property is protected by law.

The object of the right of personal property may be the income and savings from work, the dwelling house, the household next to it and the land on which they are located, as well as goods for personal use and comfort.

1986:37.contentEn: The right of inheritance is protected by law.

content.text: The right of inheritance is protected by law.

1986:38.contentEn: The Socialist Republic of Romania grants the right of asylum to foreign citizens persecuted for their activity in defense of the interests of the working people, for participation in the struggle for national liberation or for the defense of peace.

content.text: The Socialist Republic of Romania grants the right of asylum to foreign citizens persecuted for their activity in defense of the interests of the working people, for participation in the struggle for national liberation or for the defense of peace.

1986:39.contentEn: Every citizen of the Socialist Republic of Romania is obliged to respect the Constitution and the laws, to defend socialist property, to contribute to the strengthening and development of the socialist order.

content.text: Every citizen of the Socialist Republic of Romania is obliged to respect the Constitution and the laws, to defend socialist property, to contribute to the strengthening and development of the socialist order.

1986:40.contentEn: Military service in the ranks of the armed forces of the Socialist Republic of Romania is compulsory and constitutes an honorable duty of the citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: Military service in the ranks of the armed forces of the Socialist Republic of Romania is compulsory and constitutes an honorable duty of the citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:41.contentEn: The defense of the homeland is the sacred duty of every citizen of the Socialist Republic of Romania. Violation of the military oath, treason, defection to the enemy, causing damage to the state's defense capability, constitute the most serious crimes against the people and are punished by law with all severity.

content.text: The defense of the homeland is the sacred duty of every citizen of the Socialist Republic of Romania. Violation of the military oath, treason, defection to the enemy, causing damage to the state's defense capability, constitute the most serious crimes against the people and are punished by law with all severity.

1986:42.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly, the supreme body of state power, is the sole legislative body of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly, the supreme body of state power, is the sole legislative body of the Socialist Republic of Romania.
The Grand National Assembly has the following main attributions:
Adopts and amends the Constitution of the Socialist Republic of Romania;
Regulates the electoral system;
Decides on consulting the people, by referendum, on measures of particular importance concerning the supreme interests of the country;
Adopts the single national plan for economic and social development, the state budget and the general account of the budget year's closure;
Organizes the Council of Ministers; establishes the general rules for the organization and functioning of ministries and other central state bodies;
Regulates the judicial organization and the Prosecutor's Office;
Establishes the rules for the organization and functioning of the people's councils;
Establishes the administrative organization of the territory;
Grants amnesty;
Ratifies and denounces international treaties that involve the amendment of laws;
Elects and recalls the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania;
Elects and recalls the State Council;
Elects and recalls the Council of Ministers;
Elects and recalls the Supreme Tribunal and the Prosecutor General;
Exercises general control over the application of the Constitution. Only the Grand National Assembly decides on the constitutionality of laws;
Controls the activity of the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania and of the State Council;
Controls the activity of the Council of Ministers, of the ministries and of the other central bodies of the state administration;
Listens to reports on the activity of the Supreme Tribunal and controls its guiding decisions;
Controls the activity of the Prosecutor's Office;
Exercises general control over the activity of the people's councils;
Establishes the general line of foreign policy;
Proclaims, in the interest of defending the country, public order or state security, a state of necessity, in some localities or throughout the entire territory of the country;
Declares partial or general mobilization;

Declares a state of war. A state of war may be declared only in the event of armed aggression against the Socialist Republic of Romania or against another state towards which the Socialist Republic of Romania has mutual defense obligations assumed through international treaties, if the situation for which the obligation to declare a state of war is established has occurred.

1986:44.contentEn:
The deputies of the Grand National Assembly are elected in electoral districts having the same number of inhabitants.
The standard of representation for the election of deputies and the delimitation of electoral districts are established by decree of the State Council.
In each electoral district, one deputy is elected.

content.text: The deputies of the Grand National Assembly are elected in electoral districts having the same number of inhabitants.
The standard of representation for the election of deputies and the delimitation of electoral districts are established by decree of the State Council.
In each electoral district, one deputy is elected.

1986:45.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly is elected for a legislature of 5 years.
The mandate of the Grand National Assembly may not cease before the end of the legislature for which it was elected. The mandate ceases on the date of the elections for the new Grand National Assembly.
In the event that it ascertains the existence of circumstances that make the holding of elections impossible, the Grand National Assembly may decide to extend its mandate for the duration of these circumstances.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly is elected for a legislature of 5 years.

The mandate of the Grand National Assembly may not cease before the end of the legislature for which it was elected. The mandate ceases on the date of the elections for the new Grand National Assembly.

In the event that it ascertains the existence of circumstances that make the holding of elections impossible, the Grand National Assembly may decide to extend its mandate for the duration of these circumstances.

1986:46.contentEn:
Elections for the Grand National Assembly take place on one of the non-working days of the month of March of the year in which the previous legislature ends. In the case provided for in [Art. 45 para. 3](#articolul-45), the elections take place within 2 months from the expiration of the term for which the mandate of the Grand National Assembly was extended.
The newly elected Grand National Assembly is convened within the following 3 months from the cessation of the mandate of the previous Grand National Assembly.

content.text: Elections for the Grand National Assembly take place on one of the non-working days of the month of March of the year in which the previous legislature ends. In the case provided for in [Art. 45 para. 3](#articolul-45), the elections take place within 2 months from the expiration of the term for which the mandate of the Grand National Assembly was extended.
The newly elected Grand National Assembly is convened within the following 3 months from the cessation of the mandate of the previous Grand National Assembly.

1986:47.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly verifies the legality of the election of each deputy, deciding on the validation or annulment of the election.
In the event of annulment of an election, the rights and duties of the deputy cease from the moment of annulment.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly verifies the legality of the election of each deputy, deciding on the validation or annulment of the election.
In the event of annulment of an election, the rights and duties of the deputy cease from the moment of annulment.

1986:48.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly adopts its rules of procedure.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly adopts its rules of procedure.

1986:49.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly establishes its annual budget, which is included in the state budget.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly establishes its annual budget, which is included in the state budget.

1986:50.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly elects, for the duration of the legislature, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly, composed of the President of the Grand National Assembly and four vice-presidents.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly elects, for the duration of the legislature, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly, composed of the President of the Grand National Assembly and four vice-presidents.

1986:51.contentEn:
The President of the Grand National Assembly conducts the proceedings of the sessions of the Grand National Assembly.
The President of the Grand National Assembly may designate any of the vice-presidents to perform some of his duties.

content.text: The President of the Grand National Assembly conducts the proceedings of the sessions of the Grand National Assembly.
The President of the Grand National Assembly may designate any of the vice-presidents to perform some of his duties.

1986:52.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly elects, from among the deputies, standing committees.
The standing committees examine and debate, on the assignment of the Grand National Assembly or of the State Council, draft laws, decrees, decisions or other acts to be adopted, as well as any other issues.
Also, on the assignment of the Grand National Assembly or of the State Council, the standing committees listen, periodically or on issues, each according to its competence, to reports of the heads of any bodies of state administration, of the Supreme Tribunal and of the Prosecutor's Office, on the activity of these bodies, as well as of the presidents of the executive committees or of the executive bureaus of the people's councils on the activity of these councils, and analyze the manner in which the bodies mentioned implement the policy of the Romanian Communist Party and ensure the application of the law.
The committees draw up reports, opinions or proposals in connection with all the issues mentioned in paragraphs 2 and 3, which they submit to the Grand National Assembly or the State Council.
The Grand National Assembly may elect temporary committees for any issues or fields of activity, establishing the powers and mode of activity for each of these committees.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to make available to the committees of the Grand National Assembly the information and documents requested.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly elects, from among the deputies, standing committees.

The standing committees examine and debate, on the assignment of the Grand National Assembly or of the State Council, draft laws, decrees, decisions or other acts to be adopted, as well as any other issues.

Also, on the assignment of the Grand National Assembly or of the State Council, the standing committees listen, periodically or on issues, each according to its competence, to reports of the heads of any bodies of state administration, of the Supreme Tribunal and of the Prosecutor's Office, on the activity of these bodies, as well as of the presidents of the executive committees or of the executive bureaus of the people's councils on the activity of these councils, and analyze the manner in which the bodies mentioned implement the policy of the Romanian Communist Party and ensure the application of the law.
The committees draw up reports, opinions or proposals in connection with all the issues mentioned in paragraphs 2 and 3, which they submit to the Grand National Assembly or the State Council.
The Grand National Assembly may elect temporary committees for any issues or fields of activity, establishing the powers and mode of activity for each of these committees.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to make available to the committees of the Grand National Assembly the information and documents requested.

1986:53.contentEn:
In exercising the control of the constitutionality of laws, as well as for the preparation of works regarding the adoption of laws, the Grand National Assembly elects, for the duration of the legislature, a constitutional and legal committee.
Specialists who are not deputies may be elected to the constitutional and legal committee – without exceeding one third of the total number of members of the committee.
The constitutional and legal committee shall submit to the Grand National Assembly reports or opinions regarding the constitutionality of laws. It also examines the constitutionality of decrees containing norms with the force of law and of the decisions of the Council of Ministers, according to the Rules of Procedure of the Grand National Assembly. The provisions of [Art. 52](#articolul-52) shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the constitutional and legal committee.

content.text: In exercising the control of the constitutionality of laws, as well as for the preparation of works regarding the adoption of laws, the Grand National Assembly elects, for the duration of the legislature, a constitutional and legal committee.
Specialists who are not deputies may be elected to the constitutional and legal committee – without exceeding one third of the total number of members of the committee.

The constitutional and legal committee shall submit to the Grand National Assembly reports or opinions regarding the constitutionality of laws. It also examines the constitutionality of decrees containing norms with the force of law and of the decisions of the Council of Ministers, according to the Rules of Procedure of the Grand National Assembly. The provisions of [Art. 52](#articolul-52) shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the constitutional and legal committee.

1986:54.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly shall work in sessions.
The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly shall be convened twice a year, at the proposal of the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly. The Grand National Assembly shall be convened, whenever necessary, in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the State Council, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly or at least one third of the total number of deputies.
The convening of the Grand National Assembly in sessions shall be done by decree of the State Council.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall work in sessions.

The ordinary sessions of the Grand National Assembly shall be convened twice a year, at the proposal of the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly. The Grand National Assembly shall be convened, whenever necessary, in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the State Council, the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly or at least one third of the total number of deputies.
The convening of the Grand National Assembly in sessions shall be done by decree of the State Council.

1986:55.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly shall work only if at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies are present.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall work only if at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies are present.

1986:56.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly shall adopt laws and decisions.
Laws and decisions shall be adopted if they obtain the vote of the majority of the deputies of the Grand National Assembly.
The Constitution shall be adopted and amended with the vote of at least two thirds of the total number of deputies of the Grand National Assembly. The laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly shall be signed by the president or vice-president of the Grand National Assembly who chaired the meeting.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly shall adopt laws and decisions.
Laws and decisions shall be adopted if they obtain the vote of the majority of the deputies of the Grand National Assembly.

The Constitution shall be adopted and amended with the vote of at least two thirds of the total number of deputies of the Grand National Assembly. The laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly shall be signed by the president or vice-president of the Grand National Assembly who chaired the meeting.

1986:57.contentEn: After their adoption by the Grand National Assembly, the laws shall be published in the Official Gazette of the Socialist Republic of Romania, within a maximum of 10 days, under the signature of the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: After their adoption by the Grand National Assembly, the laws shall be published in the Official Gazette of the Socialist Republic of Romania, within a maximum of 10 days, under the signature of the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:58.contentEn:
Each deputy of the Grand National Assembly has the right to ask questions and address interpellations to the Council of Ministers or to any of its members.
The deputy, within the control exercised by the Grand National Assembly, may ask questions and address interpellations to the president of the Supreme Court and the general prosecutor.
The person questioned or interpellated is obliged to answer verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days and in any case in the same session.

content.text: Each deputy of the Grand National Assembly has the right to ask questions and address interpellations to the Council of Ministers or to any of its members.
The deputy, within the control exercised by the Grand National Assembly, may ask questions and address interpellations to the president of the Supreme Court and the general prosecutor.
The person questioned or interpellated is obliged to answer verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days and in any case in the same session.

1986:59.contentEn: In order to prepare the debates of the Grand National Assembly or the interpellations, the deputy has the right to request the necessary information from any state body, addressing for this purpose the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: In order to prepare the debates of the Grand National Assembly or the interpellations, the deputy has the right to request the necessary information from any state body, addressing for this purpose the Bureau of the Grand National Assembly.

1986:60.contentEn: Each deputy is obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his/her activity and on the activity of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: Each deputy is obliged to periodically present to the voters reports on his/her activity and on the activity of the Grand National Assembly.

1986:61.contentEn:
No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be detained, arrested, or brought to criminal trial without the prior approval of the Grand National Assembly during the session, and between sessions, of the State Council.
Only in the case of a flagrant offense may the deputy be detained without this approval.
The State Council

content.text: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be detained, arrested, or brought to criminal trial without the prior approval of the Grand National Assembly during the session, and between sessions, of the State Council.
Only in the case of a flagrant offense may the deputy be detained without this approval.
The State Council

1986:62.contentEn: The State Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme body of state power with permanent activity, subordinate to the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The State Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme body of state power with permanent activity, subordinate to the Grand National Assembly.
The State Council permanently exercises the following main attributions:
Establishes the date of elections for the Grand National Assembly and the people's councils;
Establishes the manner of organizing and conducting the referendum;
Organizes the ministries and other central state bodies;
Ratifies and denounces international treaties, with the exception of those whose ratification is the competence of the Grand National Assembly;
Establishes military ranks;
Institutes decorations and honorary titles.
In the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, the State Council exercises the following main attributions:

Establishes, without being able to amend the Constitution, norms with the power of law. The norms with the power of law are submitted, at the first session, to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws. The single national plan for economic and social development, the state budget, as well as the general account of the closing of the budget year, may be adopted by the State Council only when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
Appoints and dismisses the Prime Minister;
Appoints and dismisses the Council of Ministers and the Supreme Tribunal, when the Grand National Assembly cannot meet due to exceptional circumstances;
Gives the generally binding interpretation to the laws in force;
Grants amnesty;

Controls the application of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly, the activity of the Council of Ministers, of the ministries and of the other central bodies of the state administration, as well as the activity of the Prosecutor's Office; hears reports from the Supreme Tribunal and controls its guiding decisions; controls the decisions of the people's councils;
Declares, in case of emergency, partial or general mobilization;

Declares, in case of emergency, the state of war. The state of war may be declared only in the case of an armed aggression against the Socialist Republic of Romania or against another state towards which the Socialist Republic of Romania has mutual defense obligations assumed through international treaties, if the situation for which the obligation to declare the state of war is established has occurred.

The attributions provided for in this Articolul may be exercised by the State Council also during the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, if the economic and social needs require the immediate adoption of measures, and the Grand National Assembly is not meeting in its plenum; the norms with the power of law adopted are submitted to the debate of the Grand National Assembly, according to the procedure for adopting laws, upon the resumption of work in the plenum.

1986:65.contentEn: The State Council is elected by the Grand National Assembly from among its members, for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof. The State Council functions until the election of the new State Council in the following legislature.

content.text: The State Council is elected by the Grand National Assembly from among its members, for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof. The State Council functions until the election of the new State Council in the following legislature.

1986:66.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the President of the State Council.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the President of the State Council.

1986:67.contentEn: The State Council is composed of a president, vice-presidents, and members.

content.text: The State Council is composed of a president, vice-presidents, and members.

1986:68.contentEn: The State Council carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

content.text: The State Council carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

1986:69.contentEn:
The State Council issues decrees and adopts decisions.
The decrees and decisions shall be signed by the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania. The normative decrees shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The State Council issues decrees and adopts decisions.

The decrees and decisions shall be signed by the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania. The normative decrees shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:70.contentEn:
The State Council shall present to the Grand National Assembly reports regarding the exercise of its attributions, as well as regarding the observance and execution, in the state activity, of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly.
The State Council, in its entirety, and each of its members are liable before the Grand National Assembly for the entire activity of the State Council.

**The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania**

content.text: The State Council shall present to the Grand National Assembly reports regarding the exercise of its attributions, as well as regarding the observance and execution, in the state activity, of the laws and decisions of the Grand National Assembly.
The State Council, in its entirety, and each of its members are liable before the Grand National Assembly for the entire activity of the State Council.
**The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania**

1986:71.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the head of state and represents the state power in the internal and international relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the head of state and represents the state power in the internal and international relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:72.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and remains in office until the election of the president in the next legislature.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and remains in office until the election of the president in the next legislature.

1986:73.contentEn:
Upon his election, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly:
“I swear to serve the homeland with faith, to act firmly for the defense of the country's independence, sovereignty and integrity, for the well-being and happiness of the entire people, for the building of socialism and communism in the Socialist Republic of Romania!
I swear to respect and defend the Constitution and the laws of the country, to do everything for the consistent application of the principles of socialist democracy, for the affirmation in the life of society of the norms of socialist ethics and equity!
I swear to unswervingly promote the foreign policy of friendship and alliance with all socialist countries, of collaboration with all the nations of the world, regardless of social order, on the basis of full equality of rights, of solidarity with the revolutionary, progressive forces everywhere, of peace and friendship between peoples!
I swear that I will always do my duty with honor and devotion for the brilliance and greatness of our socialist nation, of the Socialist Republic of Romania!”

content.text: Upon his election, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall take the following oath before the Grand National Assembly:

“I swear to serve the homeland with faith, to act firmly for the defense of the country's independence, sovereignty and integrity, for the well-being and happiness of the entire people, for the building of socialism and communism in the Socialist Republic of Romania!

I swear to respect and defend the Constitution and the laws of the country, to do everything for the consistent application of the principles of socialist democracy, for the affirmation in the life of society of the norms of socialist ethics and equity!

I swear to unswervingly promote the foreign policy of friendship and alliance with all socialist countries, of collaboration with all the nations of the world, regardless of social order, on the basis of full equality of rights, of solidarity with the revolutionary, progressive forces everywhere, of peace and friendship between peoples!
I swear that I will always do my duty with honor and devotion for the brilliance and greatness of our socialist nation, of the Socialist Republic of Romania!”

1986:74.contentEn: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme commander of the armed forces and the president of the Defense Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the supreme commander of the armed forces and the president of the Defense Council of the Socialist Republic of Romania.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall, in accordance with the Constitution and the laws, perform the following main duties:
Presides over the State Council;
Presides over the meetings of the Council of Ministers when necessary;

Establishes the measures of particular importance concerning the supreme interests of the country, which are to be submitted by the Grand National Assembly for consultation with the people, by referendum;

Appoints and dismisses, upon the proposal of the Prime Minister, the Deputy Prime Ministers, Ministers and Presidents of other central bodies of the state administration, who are members of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the heads of the central state bodies, who are not members of the Council of Ministers; appoints and dismisses the members of the Supreme Tribunal;
During the time when the Grand National Assembly is not convened in its plenum, appoints and dismisses the President of the Supreme Tribunal and the Prosecutor General;
Grants the ranks of general, admiral and marshal;
Confers decorations and honorary titles; authorises the wearing of decorations conferred by other states;
Grants pardon;
Grants citizenship, approves renunciation of citizenship and withdraws Romanian citizenship; approves the establishment of domicile in Romania for citizens of other states;
Grants the right of asylum;
Establishes the ranks of diplomatic missions, accredits and recalls the diplomatic representatives of the Socialist Republic of Romania;
Receives the letters of accreditation and recall of the diplomatic representatives of other states;

Concludes international treaties on behalf of the Socialist Republic of Romania; may empower, for this purpose, the Prime Minister or members of the Council of Ministers or diplomatic representatives;

In the interest of the defence of the Socialist Republic of Romania, of ensuring public order or state security, proclaims, in case of emergency, in some localities or throughout the territory of the country, the state of necessity.
In the performance of his/her duties, the President of the Socialist Republic of Romania issues presidential decrees and decisions.

1986:76.contentEn:
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is accountable to the Grand National Assembly for all his/her activity.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall periodically present to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of his/her duties and on the development of the state.

content.text: The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania is accountable to the Grand National Assembly for all his/her activity.
The President of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall periodically present to the Grand National Assembly reports on the exercise of his/her duties and on the development of the state.
The Council of Ministers is the supreme body of state administration.
The Council of Ministers exercises general leadership of executive activity throughout the entire territory of the country, having the following main attributions:
Establishes general measures for the implementation of internal and external policy;
Decides on the necessary measures regarding the organization and ensuring the execution of laws;
Leads, coordinates, and controls the activity of ministries and other central bodies of state administration;
Elaborates the draft of the single national plan for economic and social development and the draft state budget, as well as any other draft laws; elaborates draft decrees;

Establishes measures for the implementation of the single national plan for economic and social development and the state budget; prepares the general report regarding the fulfillment of the single national plan for economic and social development and the general account of the budget year closure;
Establishes economic organizations, enterprises, and state institutions of republican interest;
Takes measures to ensure public order, defend the interests of the state, and protect the rights of citizens;

Takes measures, according to the decisions of the Defence Council, for the general organization of the armed forces and the fixing of annual contingents of citizens who are to be called to fulfill military service;
Exercises general leadership in the field of relations with other states and takes measures for the conclusion of international agreements;
Supports the activity of mass and public organizations;
Exercises, under the conditions provided by law, its leadership and control attributions over the activity of the executive committees and executive bureaus of the people's councils.

1986:78.contentEn:
The Council of Ministers is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session.
The Council of Ministers functions until the election of the new Council of Ministers in the following legislature.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session.
The Council of Ministers functions until the election of the new Council of Ministers in the following legislature.

1986:79.contentEn:
In fulfilling its attributions, the Council of Ministers adopts decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the laws.
Decisions of a normative character are published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: In fulfilling its attributions, the Council of Ministers adopts decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the laws.
Decisions of a normative character are published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:80.contentEn:
The Council of Ministers is composed of: the prime minister, the deputy prime ministers, the ministers, the state secretary ministers, as well as the presidents of other central bodies of state administration provided by law.
The Council of Ministers also includes, as members, the president of the Central Council of the General Trade Union Confederation, the president of the National Union of Agricultural Production Cooperatives, the president of the National Women's Council, and the first secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Youth Union.
The Council of Ministers may establish an Executive Bureau for the prompt resolution of current issues and the monitoring of the execution of the Council of Ministers' decisions.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is composed of: the prime minister, the deputy prime ministers, the ministers, the state secretary ministers, as well as the presidents of other central bodies of state administration provided by law.

The Council of Ministers also includes, as members, the president of the Central Council of the General Trade Union Confederation, the president of the National Union of Agricultural Production Cooperatives, the president of the National Women's Council, and the first secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Youth Union.
The Council of Ministers may establish an Executive Bureau for the prompt resolution of current issues and the monitoring of the execution of the Council of Ministers' decisions.

1986:81.contentEn: The Council of Ministers carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership, ensuring the unity of political and administrative action of the ministries and other central bodies of state administration.

content.text: The Council of Ministers carries out its activity according to the principle of collective leadership, ensuring the unity of political and administrative action of the ministries and other central bodies of state administration.

1986:82.contentEn: The Council of Ministers, as a whole, and each of its members are responsible to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council. Each member of the Council of Ministers is responsible both for his/her own activity and for the entire activity of the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The Council of Ministers, as a whole, and each of its members are responsible to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council. Each member of the Council of Ministers is responsible both for his/her own activity and for the entire activity of the Council of Ministers.

1986:83.contentEn:
The ministries and other central bodies of state administration implement the policy of the state in the branches or fields of activity for which they were established.
They lead, guide, and control the enterprises, economic organizations, and state institutions under their subordination.

content.text: The ministries and other central bodies of state administration implement the policy of the state in the branches or fields of activity for which they were established.
They lead, guide, and control the enterprises, economic organizations, and state institutions under their subordination.

1986:84.contentEn: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall issue, on the basis and for the execution of the laws and decisions of the Council of Ministers, instructions and orders, as well as other acts provided for by law; their normative acts shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall issue, on the basis and for the execution of the laws and decisions of the Council of Ministers, instructions and orders, as well as other acts provided for by law; their normative acts shall be published in the Official Bulletin of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:85.contentEn: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall be responsible for the activity of the bodies they lead before the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The ministers and the heads of the other central bodies of state administration shall be responsible for the activity of the bodies they lead before the Council of Ministers.

1986:86.contentEn:
The people's councils are the local bodies of state power in the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected.
The people's councils shall manage local activity, ensuring the economic, socio-cultural and urban-administrative development of the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected, the defense of socialist property, the protection of citizens' rights, socialist legality and the maintenance of public order.
The people's councils shall organize the participation of citizens in solving state and public affairs at the local level.

content.text: The people's councils are the local bodies of state power in the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected.

The people's councils shall manage local activity, ensuring the economic, socio-cultural and urban-administrative development of the administrative-territorial units in which they were elected, the defense of socialist property, the protection of citizens' rights, socialist legality and the maintenance of public order.
The people's councils shall organize the participation of citizens in solving state and public affairs at the local level.
The people's council shall exercise the following main attributions:
Adopts the economic plan and the local budget, approves the account of the closing of the budgetary exercise;
Elects and revokes the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau;
Establishes economic organizations, state enterprises and institutions of local interest;

Manages, guides and controls the activity of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, of the local specialized bodies of the state administration, of the economic organizations, enterprises and subordinate institutions;
Controls the decisions of hierarchically inferior people's councils;
Elects and revokes, according to the law, the judges, the people's assessors and the chief prosecutor of the county or of the municipality of Bucharest.

1986:88.contentEn:
The people's councils are composed of deputies elected in electoral districts, one deputy for each district.
The electoral districts formed for the election of the deputies of a people's council have the same number of inhabitants.
The duration of the mandate of the county people's councils and of the municipality of Bucharest is 5 years, and that of the municipal, Bucharest municipality sectors, town and commune people's councils is 2 and a half years.
The date of the elections for the people's councils shall be established according to the law.

content.text: The people's councils are composed of deputies elected in electoral districts, one deputy for each district.
The electoral districts formed for the election of the deputies of a people's council have the same number of inhabitants.

The duration of the mandate of the county people's councils and of the municipality of Bucharest is 5 years, and that of the municipal, Bucharest municipality sectors, town and commune people's councils is 2 and a half years.
The date of the elections for the people's councils shall be established according to the law.

1986:89.contentEn: The people's councils shall elect, from among the deputies, permanent commissions, which support them in the fulfillment of their tasks.

content.text: The people's councils shall elect, from among the deputies, permanent commissions, which support them in the fulfillment of their tasks.

1986:90.contentEn:
The people's councils work in sessions; the convening in sessions is done by the executive committee or, as the case may be, by the executive bureau of the people's council.
The people's councils shall be convened whenever necessary in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, or of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The people's councils work in sessions; the convening in sessions is done by the executive committee or, as the case may be, by the executive bureau of the people's council.

The people's councils shall be convened whenever necessary in extraordinary sessions, on the initiative of the executive committee or, as the case may be, of the executive bureau, or of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

1986:91.contentEn: The people's councils work in the presence of at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The people's councils work in the presence of at least one half plus one of the total number of deputies.

1986:92.contentEn: Each deputy is obligated to periodically present to the voters reports on his activity and on the people's council in which he was elected.

content.text: Each deputy is obligated to periodically present to the voters reports on his activity and on the people's council in which he was elected.

1986:93.contentEn:
The people's councils adopt resolutions.
A resolution is adopted if it obtains the vote of the majority of the deputies of the people's council.
Resolutions of a normative character are brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided by law.

content.text: The people's councils adopt resolutions.
A resolution is adopted if it obtains the vote of the majority of the deputies of the people's council.
Resolutions of a normative character are brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided by law.

1986:94.contentEn: The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council are local bodies of state administration with general competence in the administrative-territorial unit in which the people's council was elected.

content.text: The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council are local bodies of state administration with general competence in the administrative-territorial unit in which the people's council was elected.
The executive committee of the people's council and the executive bureau of the people's council have the following main attributions:
To implement the laws, decrees, as well as the decisions of the Council of Ministers and the other acts of the superior bodies;
To execute the resolutions of the people's councils that elected them;
To elaborate the drafts of the economic plan and of the local budget;
To execute the economic plan and the local budget, to draw up the report regarding the fulfillment of the local economic plan, as well as the closing account of the budgetary exercise;
To lead, guide and control the activity of the local specialized bodies of the state administration;
To lead, guide, coordinate and control the activity of the economic organizations, of the enterprises and subordinate institutions;

To lead, guide and control the activity of the executive committees or, as the case may be, of the executive bureaus of the people's councils hierarchically inferior to the people's councils that elected them.

In the interval between the sessions of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau also fulfills the attributions thereof, with the exception of those provided at [Art. 87 pct. 1, 2, 4, 5 și 6](#articolul-87), submitting the adopted decisions to the ratification of the people's council at its first session.

1986:96.contentEn:
The county people's councils, of the municipality of Bucharest, of its sectors, as well as those of the municipalities, elect executive committees, and the people's councils of the cities and communes elect executive bureaus.
The executive committee or the executive bureau is elected from among the deputies of the people's council at the first session after the elections, for the duration of the mandate of that council.
After the expiration of the mandate of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau continues to function until the election of the new executive committee or bureau.

content.text: The county people's councils, of the municipality of Bucharest, of its sectors, as well as those of the municipalities, elect executive committees, and the people's councils of the cities and communes elect executive bureaus.
The executive committee or the executive bureau is elected from among the deputies of the people's council at the first session after the elections, for the duration of the mandate of that council.

After the expiration of the mandate of the people's council, the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau continues to function until the election of the new executive committee or bureau.

1986:97.contentEn: The executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council is composed, according to the law, of a president, one or more vice-presidents and other members.

content.text: The executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council is composed, according to the law, of a president, one or more vice-presidents and other members.

1986:98.contentEn:
In the exercise of its attributions, the executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council issues decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the law.
Decisions of a normative character are brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided by law.

content.text: In the exercise of its attributions, the executive committee or the executive bureau of the people's council issues decisions on the basis and for the purpose of executing the law.
Decisions of a normative character are brought to the attention of citizens in the forms provided by law.

1986:99.contentEn:
The executive committee and the executive bureau carry out their activity according to the principle of collective leadership.
The executive committee or the executive bureau in its entirety and each of its members are responsible before the people's council that elected them, as well as before the executive committee or the executive bureau of the hierarchically superior people's council and the Council of Ministers.
Each member of the executive committee or of the executive bureau is responsible both for his own activity and for the entire activity of the body of which he is a part.

content.text: The executive committee and the executive bureau carry out their activity according to the principle of collective leadership.

The executive committee or the executive bureau in its entirety and each of its members are responsible before the people's council that elected them, as well as before the executive committee or the executive bureau of the hierarchically superior people's council and the Council of Ministers.
Each member of the executive committee or of the executive bureau is responsible both for his own activity and for the entire activity of the body of which he is a part.

1986:100.contentEn: The people's councils shall organize, according to the law, alongside their executive committees or, as the case may be, the executive bureaus, local specialized bodies of state administration. The local specialized bodies of state administration shall be subordinate both to the people's council and the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau, and to the hierarchically superior local and central bodies of state administration.

content.text: The people's councils shall organize, according to the law, alongside their executive committees or, as the case may be, the executive bureaus, local specialized bodies of state administration. The local specialized bodies of state administration shall be subordinate both to the people's council and the executive committee or, as the case may be, the executive bureau, and to the hierarchically superior local and central bodies of state administration.

1986:101.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, justice shall be administered, according to the law, by the Supreme Tribunal, the county tribunals, the courts of law, as well as by the military tribunals.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, justice shall be administered, according to the law, by the Supreme Tribunal, the county tribunals, the courts of law, as well as by the military tribunals.

1986:102.contentEn:
Through their judicial activity, the tribunals and courts of law shall defend the socialist order and the rights of persons, educating citizens in the spirit of respect for the laws.
By applying penal sanctions, the tribunals and courts of law shall pursue the correction and re-education of offenders, as well as the prevention of the commission of new offences.

content.text: Through their judicial activity, the tribunals and courts of law shall defend the socialist order and the rights of persons, educating citizens in the spirit of respect for the laws.
By applying penal sanctions, the tribunals and courts of law shall pursue the correction and re-education of offenders, as well as the prevention of the commission of new offences.

1986:103.contentEn:
The tribunals and courts of law shall adjudicate civil, criminal and any other cases falling within their competence.
In the cases provided for by law, the tribunals and courts of law shall exercise control over the decisions of administrative or public bodies with jurisdictional activity.
The tribunals and courts of law shall adjudicate the claims of those injured in their rights by administrative acts, being able to rule, under the conditions of the law, also on the legality of these acts.

content.text: The tribunals and courts of law shall adjudicate civil, criminal and any other cases falling within their competence.
In the cases provided for by law, the tribunals and courts of law shall exercise control over the decisions of administrative or public bodies with jurisdictional activity.

The tribunals and courts of law shall adjudicate the claims of those injured in their rights by administrative acts, being able to rule, under the conditions of the law, also on the legality of these acts.

1986:104.contentEn:
The Supreme Tribunal shall exercise general control over the judicial activity of all tribunals and courts of law.
The manner of exercising this control shall be established by law.
With a view to the uniform application of the laws in judicial activity, the Supreme Tribunal shall issue, in its plenary session, guiding decisions.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal shall exercise general control over the judicial activity of all tribunals and courts of law.
The manner of exercising this control shall be established by law.
With a view to the uniform application of the laws in judicial activity, the Supreme Tribunal shall issue, in its plenary session, guiding decisions.

1986:105.contentEn:
The Supreme Tribunal shall be elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof.
The Supreme Tribunal shall function until the election of the new Supreme Tribunal in the following legislature.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal shall be elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in the first session thereof.
The Supreme Tribunal shall function until the election of the new Supreme Tribunal in the following legislature.

1986:106.contentEn: The Supreme Tribunal shall be answerable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and between sessions, to the Council of State.

content.text: The Supreme Tribunal shall be answerable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and between sessions, to the Council of State.

1986:107.contentEn:
The organization of the courts of law and tribunals, their competence and the procedure of adjudication shall be established by law.
The adjudication of cases in the first instance in the courts of law, the county tribunals and the military tribunals shall be done with the participation of people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

content.text: The organization of the courts of law and tribunals, their competence and the procedure of adjudication shall be established by law.

The adjudication of cases in the first instance in the courts of law, the county tribunals and the military tribunals shall be done with the participation of people's assessors, except in cases where the law provides otherwise.

1986:108.contentEn: Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.

content.text: Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.

1986:109.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, judicial procedure is conducted in the Romanian language, ensuring, in the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of a nationality other than Romanian, the use of the mother tongue of that population. Parties who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted are ensured the possibility to take cognizance, through an interpreter, of the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, judicial procedure is conducted in the Romanian language, ensuring, in the administrative-territorial units inhabited also by a population of a nationality other than Romanian, the use of the mother tongue of that population. Parties who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted are ensured the possibility to take cognizance, through an interpreter, of the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

1986:110.contentEn: The trial takes place in public session, with the exception of the cases provided for by law.

content.text: The trial takes place in public session, with the exception of the cases provided for by law.

1986:111.contentEn: In their judicial activity, judges and people's assessors are independent and subject only to the law.

content.text: In their judicial activity, judges and people's assessors are independent and subject only to the law.

1986:112.contentEn: The Prosecutor's Office of the Socialist Republic of Romania supervises the activity of the criminal investigation bodies and the bodies for the execution of punishments and ensures, under the conditions of the law, the observance of legality, the defense of the socialist order, of the rights and legitimate interests of socialist organizations, of other legal persons, as well as of citizens.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office of the Socialist Republic of Romania supervises the activity of the criminal investigation bodies and the bodies for the execution of punishments and ensures, under the conditions of the law, the observance of legality, the defense of the socialist order, of the rights and legitimate interests of socialist organizations, of other legal persons, as well as of citizens.

1986:113.contentEn:
The Prosecutor's Office is headed by the Prosecutor General. The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are: the General Prosecutor's Office, the county prosecutor's offices, the local prosecutor's offices and the military prosecutor's offices.
The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are hierarchically subordinated.

content.text: The Prosecutor's Office is headed by the Prosecutor General. The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are: the General Prosecutor's Office, the county prosecutor's offices, the local prosecutor's offices and the military prosecutor's offices.
The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office are hierarchically subordinated.

1986:114.contentEn:
The Prosecutor General is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and functions until the election of the new Prosecutor General in the first session of the following legislature.
Prosecutors are appointed according to the law, with the exception of those provided for in [Art. 87 pct. 6](#articolul-87).

content.text: The Prosecutor General is elected by the Grand National Assembly for the duration of the legislature, in its first session, and functions until the election of the new Prosecutor General in the first session of the following legislature.
Prosecutors are appointed according to the law, with the exception of those provided for in [Art. 87 pct. 6](#articolul-87).

1986:115.contentEn: The Prosecutor General is responsible to the Grand National Assembly for the activity of the Prosecutor's Office, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council.

content.text: The Prosecutor General is responsible to the Grand National Assembly for the activity of the Prosecutor's Office, and in the interval between sessions, to the State Council.

1986:116.contentEn: The coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the coat of arms is a derrick. The coat of arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the coat of arms is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the coat of arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which is written "Republica Socialistă România"

content.text: The coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the coat of arms is a derrick. The coat of arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the coat of arms is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the coat of arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which is written "Republica Socialistă România"

1986:117.contentEn: The state seal depicts the country's coat of arms, around which is written "Republica Socialistă România"

content.text: The state seal depicts the country's coat of arms, around which is written "Republica Socialistă România"

1986:118.contentEn: The flag of the Socialist Republic of Romania bears the colors red, yellow, and blue, placed vertically, with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

content.text: The flag of the Socialist Republic of Romania bears the colors red, yellow, and blue, placed vertically, with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the coat of arms of the Socialist Republic of Romania.

1986:119.contentEn: The state anthem of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall be approved by the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The state anthem of the Socialist Republic of Romania shall be approved by the Grand National Assembly.

1986:120.contentEn: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its adoption.

content.text: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its adoption.

1986:121.contentEn: The Constitution of September 24, 1952, as well as any provisions of laws, decrees, and other normative acts contrary to the provisions of the present Constitution, are repealed on the same date.

content.text: The Constitution of September 24, 1952, as well as any provisions of laws, decrees, and other normative acts contrary to the provisions of the present Constitution, are repealed on the same date.

1991:1.contentEn:
1. Romania is a national, sovereign and independent, unitary and indivisible State.
2. The form of government of the Romanian State is the republic.
3. Romania is a rule of law, democratic and social state, in which human dignity, the rights and freedoms of citizens, the free development of human personality, justice and political pluralism represent supreme values and are guaranteed.

content.text: Romania is a national, sovereign and independent, unitary and indivisible State.
The form of government of the Romanian State is the republic.

Romania is a rule of law, democratic and social state, in which human dignity, the rights and freedoms of citizens, the free development of human personality, justice and political pluralism represent supreme values and are guaranteed.

1991:2.contentEn:
1. National sovereignty belongs to the Romanian people, who exercise it through their representative bodies and by referendum.
2. No group and no person may exercise sovereignty in their own name.

content.text: National sovereignty belongs to the Romanian people, who exercise it through their representative bodies and by referendum.
No group and no person may exercise sovereignty in their own name.

1991:3.contentEn:
1. The territory of Romania is inalienable.
2. The country's borders are enshrined by organic law, in compliance with the principles and other generally accepted norms of international law.
3. The territory is organised, from an administrative point of view, into communes, cities and counties. Under the conditions provided by law, some cities are declared municipalities.
4. Foreign populations may not be displaced or colonised on the territory of the Romanian State.

content.text: The territory of Romania is inalienable.
The country's borders are enshrined by organic law, in compliance with the principles and other generally accepted norms of international law.
The territory is organised, from an administrative point of view, into communes, cities and counties. Under the conditions provided by law, some cities are declared municipalities.
Foreign populations may not be displaced or colonised on the territory of the Romanian State.

1991:4.titleEn: The Unity of the People and Equality Among Citizens
1991:4.contentEn:
1. The State has as its foundation the unity of the Romanian people.
2. Romania is the common and indivisible homeland of all its citizens, without distinction as to race, nationality, ethnic origin, language, religion, sex, opinion, political affiliation, wealth or social origin.

content.text: The State has as its foundation the unity of the Romanian people.

Romania is the common and indivisible homeland of all its citizens, without distinction as to race, nationality, ethnic origin, language, religion, sex, opinion, political affiliation, wealth or social origin.

1991:5.contentEn:
1. Romanian citizenship is acquired, retained or lost under the conditions provided for by organic law.
2. Romanian citizenship may not be withdrawn from a person who acquired it by birth.

content.text: Romanian citizenship is acquired, retained or lost under the conditions provided for by organic law.
Romanian citizenship may not be withdrawn from a person who acquired it by birth.

1991:6.contentEn:
1. The State recognises and guarantees to persons belonging to national minorities the right to preserve, develop and express their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity.
2. The protection measures taken by the State to preserve, develop and express the identity of persons belonging to national minorities must be in accordance with the principles of equality and non-discrimination in relation to other Romanian citizens.

content.text: The State recognises and guarantees to persons belonging to national minorities the right to preserve, develop and express their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity.

The protection measures taken by the State to preserve, develop and express the identity of persons belonging to national minorities must be in accordance with the principles of equality and non-discrimination in relation to other Romanian citizens.

1991:7.contentEn: The State supports the strengthening of links with Romanians living outside the country's borders and acts to preserve, develop and express their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity, in compliance with the legislation of the State of which they are citizens.

content.text: The State supports the strengthening of links with Romanians living outside the country's borders and acts to preserve, develop and express their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity, in compliance with the legislation of the State of which they are citizens.

1991:8.contentEn:
1. Pluralism in Romanian society is a condition and a guarantee of constitutional democracy.
2. Political parties are established and carry out their activities under the conditions provided by law. They contribute to the definition and expression of the political will of the citizens, respecting national sovereignty, territorial integrity, the rule of law and the principles of democracy.

content.text: Pluralism in Romanian society is a condition and a guarantee of constitutional democracy.

Political parties are established and carry out their activities under the conditions provided by law. They contribute to the definition and expression of the political will of the citizens, respecting national sovereignty, territorial integrity, the rule of law and the principles of democracy.

1991:9.contentEn: Trade unions are established and carry out their activities in accordance with their statutes, under the conditions provided by law. They contribute to the defence of the rights and the promotion of the professional, economic and social interests of employees.

content.text: Trade unions are established and carry out their activities in accordance with their statutes, under the conditions provided by law. They contribute to the defence of the rights and the promotion of the professional, economic and social interests of employees.

1991:10.contentEn: Romania maintains and develops peaceful relations with all states and, within this framework, good neighbourly relations, based on the principles and other generally accepted norms of international law.

content.text: Romania maintains and develops peaceful relations with all states and, within this framework, good neighbourly relations, based on the principles and other generally accepted norms of international law.

1991:11.contentEn:
1. The Romanian State undertakes to fulfil in good faith its obligations deriving from the treaties to which it is a party.
2. Treaties ratified by the Parliament, according to the law, are part of domestic law.

content.text: The Romanian State undertakes to fulfil in good faith its obligations deriving from the treaties to which it is a party.
Treaties ratified by the Parliament, according to the law, are part of domestic law.

1991:12.contentEn:
1. The flag of Romania is tricoloured; the colours are arranged vertically in the following order, starting from the hoist: blue, yellow, red.
2. The National Day of Romania is 1 December.
3. The National Anthem of Romania is "Deșteaptă-te române".
4. The coat of arms of the country and the state seal shall be established by organic laws.

content.text: The flag of Romania is tricoloured; the colours are arranged vertically in the following order, starting from the hoist: blue, yellow, red.
The National Day of Romania is 1 December.
The National Anthem of Romania is "Deșteaptă-te române".
The coat of arms of the country and the state seal shall be established by organic laws.

1991:13.contentEn: In Romania, the official language is Romanian.

content.text: In Romania, the official language is Romanian.

1991:14.contentEn: The capital of Romania is the Municipality of Bucharest.

content.text: The capital of Romania is the Municipality of Bucharest.

1991:15.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall enjoy the rights and freedoms enshrined in the Constitution and other laws, and shall have the duties laid down therein.
2. The law shall only have effect for the future, with the exception of the more favourable criminal law.

content.text: Citizens shall enjoy the rights and freedoms enshrined in the Constitution and other laws, and shall have the duties laid down therein.
The law shall only have effect for the future, with the exception of the more favourable criminal law.

1991:16.contentEn:
1. Citizens are equal before the law and public authorities, without any privilege or discrimination.
2. No one is above the law.
3. Public offices and dignities, civil or military, may be held by persons who have only Romanian citizenship and domicile in the country.

content.text: Citizens are equal before the law and public authorities, without any privilege or discrimination.
No one is above the law.
Public offices and dignities, civil or military, may be held by persons who have only Romanian citizenship and domicile in the country.

1991:17.contentEn: Romanian citizens shall enjoy the protection of the Romanian State when abroad, and shall fulfil their duties, with the exception of those incompatible with their absence from the country.

content.text: Romanian citizens shall enjoy the protection of the Romanian State when abroad, and shall fulfil their duties, with the exception of those incompatible with their absence from the country.

1991:18.contentEn:
1. Foreign citizens and stateless persons residing in Romania shall enjoy the general protection of persons and property, guaranteed by the Constitution and other laws.
2. The right of asylum shall be granted and withdrawn under the conditions laid down by law, in compliance with the international treaties and conventions to which Romania is a party.

content.text: Foreign citizens and stateless persons residing in Romania shall enjoy the general protection of persons and property, guaranteed by the Constitution and other laws.
The right of asylum shall be granted and withdrawn under the conditions laid down by law, in compliance with the international treaties and conventions to which Romania is a party.

1991:19.contentEn:
