1. A Romanian citizen may not be extradited or expelled from Romania.
2. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may be extradited only on the basis of an international convention or under conditions of reciprocity.
3. Expulsion or extradition shall be decided by the courts.

content.text: A Romanian citizen may not be extradited or expelled from Romania.
Foreign citizens and stateless persons may be extradited only on the basis of an international convention or under conditions of reciprocity.
Expulsion or extradition shall be decided by the courts.

1991:20.contentEn:
1. The constitutional provisions concerning the rights and freedoms of citizens shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, with the covenants and with the other treaties to which Romania is a party.
2. Where inconsistencies exist between the covenants and treaties on fundamental human rights to which Romania is a party and domestic laws, international regulations shall take precedence.

content.text: The constitutional provisions concerning the rights and freedoms of citizens shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, with the covenants and with the other treaties to which Romania is a party.
Where inconsistencies exist between the covenants and treaties on fundamental human rights to which Romania is a party and domestic laws, international regulations shall take precedence.

1991:21.contentEn:
1. Any person shall be entitled to apply to the courts for the defence of his/her legitimate rights, freedoms and interests.
2. No law may restrict the exercise of this right.

content.text: Any person shall be entitled to apply to the courts for the defence of his/her legitimate rights, freedoms and interests.
No law may restrict the exercise of this right.

1991:22.titleEn: Right to Life, and to Physical and Mental Integrity
1991:22.contentEn:
1. The right to life, as well as the right to physical and mental integrity of the person are guaranteed.
2. No one may be subjected to torture or to any inhuman or degrading punishment or treatment.
3. The death penalty is prohibited.

content.text: The right to life, as well as the right to physical and mental integrity of the person are guaranteed.
No one may be subjected to torture or to any inhuman or degrading punishment or treatment.
The death penalty is prohibited.

1991:23.contentEn:
1. Individual freedom and safety of person are inviolable.
2. Search, detention or arrest of a person shall be permitted only in the cases and under the procedure provided by law.
3. Detention may not exceed 24 hours.
4. Arrest shall be made under a warrant issued by a magistrate, for a maximum term of 30 days. The person arrested may lodge a complaint to the judge as to the legality of the warrant, and the judge is bound to give a motivated ruling. The extension of the arrest shall be approved only by the court.
5. The person detained or arrested shall be promptly informed, in a language he/she understands, of the grounds for his/her detention or arrest, and notified of the charges against him/her, in the shortest possible time; the notification of the charges shall be made only in the presence of a lawyer, either chosen or appointed ex officio.
6. Release of the person detained or arrested shall be mandatory, if the grounds for these measures have ceased to exist.
7. A person under preventive arrest shall be entitled to apply for provisional release, under judicial control or on bail.
8. Until a final conviction ruling is delivered, a person shall be presumed innocent.
9. No penalty may be established or applied, save under the law.

content.text: Individual freedom and safety of person are inviolable.
Search, detention or arrest of a person shall be permitted only in the cases and under the procedure provided by law.
Detention may not exceed 24 hours.

Arrest shall be made under a warrant issued by a magistrate, for a maximum term of 30 days. The person arrested may lodge a complaint to the judge as to the legality of the warrant, and the judge is bound to give a motivated ruling. The extension of the arrest shall be approved only by the court.

The person detained or arrested shall be promptly informed, in a language he/she understands, of the grounds for his/her detention or arrest, and notified of the charges against him/her, in the shortest possible time; the notification of the charges shall be made only in the presence of a lawyer, either chosen or appointed ex officio.
Release of the person detained or arrested shall be mandatory, if the grounds for these measures have ceased to exist.
A person under preventive arrest shall be entitled to apply for provisional release, under judicial control or on bail.
Until a final conviction ruling is delivered, a person shall be presumed innocent.
No penalty may be established or applied, save under the law.

1991:24.contentEn:
1. The right to defence is guaranteed.
2. Throughout the trial, the parties shall have the right to be assisted by a lawyer, either chosen or appointed ex officio.

content.text: The right to defence is guaranteed.
Throughout the trial, the parties shall have the right to be assisted by a lawyer, either chosen or appointed ex officio.

1991:25.contentEn:
1. The right to free movement within the national territory and abroad is guaranteed. The law shall lay down the conditions for the exercise of this right.
2. Every citizen shall be guaranteed the right to establish his domicile or residence anywhere in the country, to emigrate, and to return to the country.

content.text: The right to free movement within the national territory and abroad is guaranteed. The law shall lay down the conditions for the exercise of this right.
Every citizen shall be guaranteed the right to establish his domicile or residence anywhere in the country, to emigrate, and to return to the country.

1991:26.contentEn:
1. The public authorities shall respect and protect intimate, family and private life.
2. Any natural person shall have the right to freely dispose of himself/herself, provided he/she does not infringe on the rights and freedoms of others, on public order or on good morals.

content.text: The public authorities shall respect and protect intimate, family and private life.
Any natural person shall have the right to freely dispose of himself/herself, provided he/she does not infringe on the rights and freedoms of others, on public order or on good morals.
The domicile and residence are inviolable. No one may enter or remain in a person's domicile or residence without their consent.
Derogation from the provisions of paragraph (1) is permitted by law in the following situations:
a) for the execution of an arrest warrant or a court decision;
b) for the removal of a danger concerning a person's life, physical integrity, or property;
c) for the defence of national security or public order;
d) for the prevention of the spread of an epidemic.
Searches may be ordered exclusively by a magistrate and may be carried out only in the forms prescribed by law.
Night searches are forbidden, except in cases of flagrant offences.

1991:28.contentEn: The secrecy of letters, telegrams, other postal dispatches, telephone conversations, and other legal means of communication is inviolable.

content.text: The secrecy of letters, telegrams, other postal dispatches, telephone conversations, and other legal means of communication is inviolable.

1991:29.contentEn:
1. Freedom of thought and opinion, as well as freedom of religious beliefs, may not be restricted in any form. No one may be compelled to adopt an opinion or adhere to a religious belief contrary to their convictions.
2. Freedom of conscience is guaranteed; it must be manifested in a spirit of tolerance and mutual respect.
3. Religious denominations are free and shall organise themselves according to their own statutes, under the terms provided by law.
4. In the relations between religious denominations, any forms, means, acts, or actions of religious animosity are prohibited.
5. Religious denominations are autonomous from the State and shall enjoy its support, including by facilitating religious assistance in the army, in hospitals, in penitentiaries, in asylums, and in orphanages.
6. Parents or guardians have the right to ensure, according to their own convictions, the education of minor children for whom they are responsible.

content.text: Freedom of thought and opinion, as well as freedom of religious beliefs, may not be restricted in any form. No one may be compelled to adopt an opinion or adhere to a religious belief contrary to their convictions.
Freedom of conscience is guaranteed; it must be manifested in a spirit of tolerance and mutual respect.
Religious denominations are free and shall organise themselves according to their own statutes, under the terms provided by law.
In the relations between religious denominations, any forms, means, acts, or actions of religious animosity are prohibited.

Religious denominations are autonomous from the State and shall enjoy its support, including by facilitating religious assistance in the army, in hospitals, in penitentiaries, in asylums, and in orphanages.
Parents or guardians have the right to ensure, according to their own convictions, the education of minor children for whom they are responsible.

1991:30.contentEn:
1. Freedom of expression of thoughts, opinions, or beliefs, and freedom of creations of any kind, by word of mouth, in writing, in images, in sounds, or by other means of public communication, are inviolable.
2. Censorship of any kind is prohibited.
3. Freedom of the press also implies the freedom to establish publications.
4. No publication may be suppressed.
5. The law may impose on the mass media the obligation to make public the source of financing.
6. Freedom of expression may not prejudice the dignity, honour, private life of the person, or the right to their own image.
7. Defamation of the country and the nation, incitement to aggressive war, to national, racial, class, or religious hatred, incitement to discrimination, to territorial separatism or to public violence, as well as obscene manifestations contrary to good morals, are prohibited by law.
8. Civil liability for information or for the creation brought to public knowledge lies with the publisher or producer, the author, the organiser of the artistic event, the owner of the means of reproduction, of the radio or television station, under the terms provided by law. Press offences shall be established by law.

content.text: Freedom of expression of thoughts, opinions, or beliefs, and freedom of creations of any kind, by word of mouth, in writing, in images, in sounds, or by other means of public communication, are inviolable.
Censorship of any kind is prohibited.
Freedom of the press also implies the freedom to establish publications.
No publication may be suppressed.
The law may impose on the mass media the obligation to make public the source of financing.
Freedom of expression may not prejudice the dignity, honour, private life of the person, or the right to their own image.

Defamation of the country and the nation, incitement to aggressive war, to national, racial, class, or religious hatred, incitement to discrimination, to territorial separatism or to public violence, as well as obscene manifestations contrary to good morals, are prohibited by law.

Civil liability for information or for the creation brought to public knowledge lies with the publisher or producer, the author, the organiser of the artistic event, the owner of the means of reproduction, of the radio or television station, under the terms provided by law. Press offences shall be established by law.

1991:31.contentEn:
1. The right of a person to have access to any information of public interest may not be restricted.
2. Public authorities, according to their competences, are obliged to ensure the correct information of citizens on public affairs and on issues of personal interest.
3. The right to information must not prejudice measures for the protection of young people or national security.
4. Public and private mass media are obliged to ensure the correct information of public opinion.
5. Public radio and television services are autonomous. They must guarantee to important social and political groups the exercise of the right to airtime. The organisation of these services and parliamentary control over their activity shall be regulated by organic law.

content.text: The right of a person to have access to any information of public interest may not be restricted.
Public authorities, according to their competences, are obliged to ensure the correct information of citizens on public affairs and on issues of personal interest.
The right to information must not prejudice measures for the protection of young people or national security.
Public and private mass media are obliged to ensure the correct information of public opinion.

Public radio and television services are autonomous. They must guarantee to important social and political groups the exercise of the right to airtime. The organisation of these services and parliamentary control over their activity shall be regulated by organic law.

1991:32.contentEn:
1. The right to education is ensured through general compulsory education, through high school and vocational education, through higher education, as well as through other forms of instruction and advanced training.
2. Education of all levels shall be conducted in Romanian. Under the conditions provided by law, education may also be conducted in a language of international circulation.
3. The right of persons belonging to national minorities to learn their mother tongue, and the right to be educated in this language are guaranteed; the manner of exercising these rights shall be established by law.
4. State education is free of charge, according to the law.
5. Educational institutions, including private ones, shall be established and shall operate under the conditions provided by law.
6. University autonomy is guaranteed.
7. The State shall ensure freedom of religious education, according to the specific requirements of each religious denomination. In state schools, religious education shall be organised and guaranteed by law.

content.text: The right to education is ensured through general compulsory education, through high school and vocational education, through higher education, as well as through other forms of instruction and advanced training.
Education of all levels shall be conducted in Romanian. Under the conditions provided by law, education may also be conducted in a language of international circulation.

The right of persons belonging to national minorities to learn their mother tongue, and the right to be educated in this language are guaranteed; the manner of exercising these rights shall be established by law.
State education is free of charge, according to the law.
Educational institutions, including private ones, shall be established and shall operate under the conditions provided by law.
University autonomy is guaranteed.

The State shall ensure freedom of religious education, according to the specific requirements of each religious denomination. In state schools, religious education shall be organised and guaranteed by law.

1991:33.contentEn:
1. The right to health protection is guaranteed.
2. The State is bound to take measures to ensure hygiene and public health.
3. The organisation of medical care and of the social insurance system for sickness, accidents, maternity and recovery, the control over the exercise of medical professions and of paramedical activities, as well as other measures for the protection of physical and mental health of persons shall be established according to the law.

content.text: The right to health protection is guaranteed.
The State is bound to take measures to ensure hygiene and public health.

The organisation of medical care and of the social insurance system for sickness, accidents, maternity and recovery, the control over the exercise of medical professions and of paramedical activities, as well as other measures for the protection of physical and mental health of persons shall be established according to the law.

1991:34.contentEn:
1. Citizens have the right to vote from the age of eighteen years, completed until the day of the elections, inclusively.
2. The mentally deficient or alienated, placed under interdiction, and persons sentenced, by a final court decision, to the loss of electoral rights do not have the right to vote.

content.text: Citizens have the right to vote from the age of eighteen years, completed until the day of the elections, inclusively.
The mentally deficient or alienated, placed under interdiction, and persons sentenced, by a final court decision, to the loss of electoral rights do not have the right to vote.

1991:35.contentEn:
1. Citizens having the right to vote who fulfil the conditions provided for in [Articolul 16 paragraph (3)](#articolul-16-egalitatea-în-drepturi) have the right to be elected, if they are not prohibited from associating in political parties, according to [Articolul 37 paragraph (3)](#articolul-37-dreptul-de-asociere).
2. Candidates must have reached, until the day of the elections, inclusively, the age of at least twenty-three years, in order to be elected to the Chamber of Deputies or to the local bodies, and the age of at least thirty-five years, in order to be elected to the Senate or to the office of President of Romania.

content.text: Citizens having the right to vote who fulfil the conditions provided for in [Articolul 16 paragraph (3)](#articolul-16-egalitatea-în-drepturi) have the right to be elected, if they are not prohibited from associating in political parties, according to [Articolul 37 paragraph (3)](#articolul-37-dreptul-de-asociere).

Candidates must have reached, until the day of the elections, inclusively, the age of at least twenty-three years, in order to be elected to the Chamber of Deputies or to the local bodies, and the age of at least thirty-five years, in order to be elected to the Senate or to the office of President of Romania.

1991:36.contentEn: Meetings, demonstrations, processions or any other assembly shall be free and may be organised and held only peacefully, without any kind of weapons.

content.text: Meetings, demonstrations, processions or any other assembly shall be free and may be organised and held only peacefully, without any kind of weapons.

1991:37.contentEn:
1. Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions and other forms of association.
2. Parties or organisations which, by their aims or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or against the sovereignty, integrity or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.
3. Judges of the Constitutional Court, the People’s Advocates, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
4. Secret associations are prohibited.

content.text: Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions and other forms of association.

Parties or organisations which, by their aims or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or against the sovereignty, integrity or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.

Judges of the Constitutional Court, the People’s Advocates, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
Secret associations are prohibited.

1991:38.titleEn: Labour and Social Protection of Labour
1991:38.contentEn:
1. The right to work may not be restricted. The free choice of profession and workplace is guaranteed.
2. Employees have the right to social protection of labour. The protection measures concern safety and hygiene at work, the working conditions for women and young people, the establishment of a minimum wage per economy, weekly rest, paid holidays, work performed under arduous conditions, as well as other specific situations.
3. The normal duration of a working day is, on average, of maximum 8 hours.
4. For equal work, women shall receive equal pay with men.
5. The right to collective bargaining in matters of labour and the binding force of collective agreements are guaranteed.

content.text: The right to work may not be restricted. The free choice of profession and workplace is guaranteed.

Employees have the right to social protection of labour. The protection measures concern safety and hygiene at work, the working conditions for women and young people, the establishment of a minimum wage per economy, weekly rest, paid holidays, work performed under arduous conditions, as well as other specific situations.
The normal duration of a working day is, on average, of maximum 8 hours.
For equal work, women shall receive equal pay with men.
The right to collective bargaining in matters of labour and the binding force of collective agreements are guaranteed.
Forced labour is prohibited.
The following shall not be considered forced labour:
a) military service or activities performed in lieu thereof by those who, according to the law, do not perform compulsory military service for religious reasons;
b) the work of a convicted person, performed under normal conditions, during detention or conditional release;
c) services required in the event of disasters or other dangers, as well as those that are part of normal civil obligations established by law.

1991:40.contentEn:
1. Employees have the right to strike for the defence of their professional, economic and social interests.
2. The law shall establish the conditions and limits for the exercise of this right, as well as the guarantees necessary to ensure essential services for society.

content.text: Employees have the right to strike for the defence of their professional, economic and social interests.
The law shall establish the conditions and limits for the exercise of this right, as well as the guarantees necessary to ensure essential services for society.

1991:41.contentEn:
1. The right of ownership, as well as claims against the State, are guaranteed. The content and limitations of these rights are established by law.
2. Private property is equally protected by law, regardless of the holder. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may not acquire ownership of land.
3. No one may be expropriated except for a cause of public utility, established according to the law, with just and prior compensation.
4. For works of general interest, the public authority may use the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate the owner for damages caused to the soil, plantations or constructions, as well as for other damages attributable to the authority.
5. The compensations provided for in paragraphs (3) and (4) shall be established by mutual agreement with the owner or, in case of disagreement, by the courts.
6. The right of ownership obliges compliance with the burdens regarding environmental protection and ensuring good neighbourliness, as well as compliance with the other burdens that, according to the law or custom, fall upon the owner.
7. Assets acquired lawfully may not be confiscated. The lawful nature of the acquisition is presumed.
8. Goods intended for, used in or resulting from offences or contraventions may be confiscated only under the conditions provided by law.

content.text: The right of ownership, as well as claims against the State, are guaranteed. The content and limitations of these rights are established by law.
Private property is equally protected by law, regardless of the holder. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may not acquire ownership of land.
No one may be expropriated except for a cause of public utility, established according to the law, with just and prior compensation.

For works of general interest, the public authority may use the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate the owner for damages caused to the soil, plantations or constructions, as well as for other damages attributable to the authority.
The compensations provided for in paragraphs (3) and (4) shall be established by mutual agreement with the owner or, in case of disagreement, by the courts.

The right of ownership obliges compliance with the burdens regarding environmental protection and ensuring good neighbourliness, as well as compliance with the other burdens that, according to the law or custom, fall upon the owner.
Assets acquired lawfully may not be confiscated. The lawful nature of the acquisition is presumed.
Goods intended for, used in or resulting from offences or contraventions may be confiscated only under the conditions provided by law.

1991:42.contentEn: The right to inheritance is guaranteed.

content.text: The right to inheritance is guaranteed.

1991:43.contentEn:
1. The State is obliged to take measures of economic development and social protection, of such a nature as to ensure citizens a decent standard of living.
2. Citizens have the right to a pension, paid maternity leave, medical assistance in state sanitary units, unemployment benefits and other forms of social assistance provided by law.

content.text: The State is obliged to take measures of economic development and social protection, of such a nature as to ensure citizens a decent standard of living.
Citizens have the right to a pension, paid maternity leave, medical assistance in state sanitary units, unemployment benefits and other forms of social assistance provided by law.

1991:44.contentEn:
1. The family is founded on the freely consented marriage between spouses, on their equality and on the right and duty of parents to ensure the upbringing, education and instruction of children.
2. The conditions for entering into, dissolution and annulment of marriage shall be established by law. Religious marriage may be celebrated only after civil marriage.
3. Children born out of wedlock are equal before the law with those born in wedlock.

content.text: The family is founded on the freely consented marriage between spouses, on their equality and on the right and duty of parents to ensure the upbringing, education and instruction of children.
The conditions for entering into, dissolution and annulment of marriage shall be established by law. Religious marriage may be celebrated only after civil marriage.
Children born out of wedlock are equal before the law with those born in wedlock.

1991:45.titleEn: Protection of Children and Young People
1991:45.contentEn:
1. Children and young people enjoy a special protection and assistance regime in the realisation of their rights.
2. The State grants state allowances for children and aid for the care of sick or disabled children. Other forms of social protection for children and young people shall be established by law.
3. The exploitation of minors, their use in activities that would harm their health, morality or endanger their life or normal development are prohibited.
4. Minors under the age of 15 may not be employed as employees.
5. Public authorities have the obligation to contribute to ensuring the conditions for the free participation of young people in the political, social, economic, cultural and sporting life of the country.

content.text: Children and young people enjoy a special protection and assistance regime in the realisation of their rights.
The State grants state allowances for children and aid for the care of sick or disabled children. Other forms of social protection for children and young people shall be established by law.
The exploitation of minors, their use in activities that would harm their health, morality or endanger their life or normal development are prohibited.
Minors under the age of 15 may not be employed as employees.

Public authorities have the obligation to contribute to ensuring the conditions for the free participation of young people in the political, social, economic, cultural and sporting life of the country.

1991:46.contentEn: Persons with disabilities shall enjoy special protection. The State shall ensure the implementation of a national policy of prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, education, instruction, and social integration of persons with disabilities, while respecting the rights and duties of parents and guardians.

content.text: Persons with disabilities shall enjoy special protection. The State shall ensure the implementation of a national policy of prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, education, instruction, and social integration of persons with disabilities, while respecting the rights and duties of parents and guardians.

1991:47.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall have the right to address public authorities by petitions formulated only in the name of the signatories.
2. Legally established organisations shall have the right to address petitions exclusively in the name of the collectivities they represent.
3. The exercise of the right to petition shall be exempt from tax.
4. Public authorities shall have the obligation to respond to petitions within the terms and under the conditions established by law.

content.text: Citizens shall have the right to address public authorities by petitions formulated only in the name of the signatories.
Legally established organisations shall have the right to address petitions exclusively in the name of the collectivities they represent.
The exercise of the right to petition shall be exempt from tax.
Public authorities shall have the obligation to respond to petitions within the terms and under the conditions established by law.

1991:48.titleEn: Right of a Person Injured by a Public Authority
1991:48.contentEn:
1. A person injured in a right of his/hers by a public authority, through an administrative act or through failure to resolve a request within the legal term, shall be entitled to obtain recognition of the claimed right, annulment of the act, and reparation of the damage.
2. The conditions and limits for the exercise of this right shall be established by organic law.
3. The State shall be liable for pecuniary damages, according to the law, for the prejudices caused by judicial errors committed in criminal proceedings.

content.text: A person injured in a right of his/hers by a public authority, through an administrative act or through failure to resolve a request within the legal term, shall be entitled to obtain recognition of the claimed right, annulment of the act, and reparation of the damage.
The conditions and limits for the exercise of this right shall be established by organic law.
The State shall be liable for pecuniary damages, according to the law, for the prejudices caused by judicial errors committed in criminal proceedings.

1991:49.titleEn: Restriction on the Exercise of Certain Rights or Freedoms
1991:49.contentEn:
1. The exercise of certain rights or freedoms may be restricted only by law and only if necessary, as the case may be, for: the defence of national security, order, public health or morals, the rights and freedoms of citizens; the conduct of criminal investigations; the prevention of the consequences of a natural disaster or a particularly serious catastrophe.
2. The restriction must be proportionate to the situation that determined it and may not affect the existence of the right or freedom.

content.text: The exercise of certain rights or freedoms may be restricted only by law and only if necessary, as the case may be, for: the defence of national security, order, public health or morals, the rights and freedoms of citizens; the conduct of criminal investigations; the prevention of the consequences of a natural disaster or a particularly serious catastrophe.
The restriction must be proportionate to the situation that determined it and may not affect the existence of the right or freedom.

1991:50.contentEn:
1. Fidelity to the country is sacred.
2. Citizens entrusted with public functions, as well as the military, shall be responsible for the faithful fulfilment of their obligations and, for this purpose, shall take the oath required by law.

content.text: Fidelity to the country is sacred.
Citizens entrusted with public functions, as well as the military, shall be responsible for the faithful fulfilment of their obligations and, for this purpose, shall take the oath required by law.

1991:51.titleEn: Observance of the Constitution and the Laws
1991:51.contentEn: Observance of the Constitution, of its supremacy, and of the laws is mandatory.

content.text: Observance of the Constitution, of its supremacy, and of the laws is mandatory.

1991:52.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall have the right and the obligation to defend Romania.
2. Military service shall be compulsory for male Romanian citizens who have reached the age of 20 years, with the exception of cases provided for by law.
3. For training within the active military service, citizens may be incorporated up to the age of 35 years.

content.text: Citizens shall have the right and the obligation to defend Romania.
Military service shall be compulsory for male Romanian citizens who have reached the age of 20 years, with the exception of cases provided for by law.
For training within the active military service, citizens may be incorporated up to the age of 35 years.

1991:53.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall have the obligation to contribute, through taxes and duties, to public expenditure.
2. The legal system of taxation must ensure the fair allocation of fiscal burdens.
3. Any other levies are prohibited, except for those established by law in exceptional situations.

content.text: Citizens shall have the obligation to contribute, through taxes and duties, to public expenditure.
The legal system of taxation must ensure the fair allocation of fiscal burdens.
Any other levies are prohibited, except for those established by law in exceptional situations.

1991:54.contentEn: Romanian citizens, foreign citizens, and stateless persons shall exercise their constitutional rights and freedoms in good faith, without infringing on the rights and freedoms of others.

content.text: Romanian citizens, foreign citizens, and stateless persons shall exercise their constitutional rights and freedoms in good faith, without infringing on the rights and freedoms of others.

1991:55.contentEn:
1. The People's Advocate shall be appointed by the Senate, for a 4-year term, to defend the rights and freedoms of citizens. The organisation and operation of the People's Advocate institution shall be established by organic law.
2. The People's Advocate may not hold any public or private office.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall be appointed by the Senate, for a 4-year term, to defend the rights and freedoms of citizens. The organisation and operation of the People's Advocate institution shall be established by organic law.
The People's Advocate may not hold any public or private office.

1991:56.contentEn:
1. The People's Advocate shall exercise his/her powers ex officio or at the request of persons injured in their rights and freedoms, within the limits established by law.
2. Public authorities shall be obliged to provide the People's Advocate with the necessary support in the exercise of his/her powers.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall exercise his/her powers ex officio or at the request of persons injured in their rights and freedoms, within the limits established by law.
Public authorities shall be obliged to provide the People's Advocate with the necessary support in the exercise of his/her powers.

1991:57.contentEn: The People's Advocate shall present reports to the two Chambers of Parliament, annually or at their request. The reports may contain recommendations regarding legislation or measures of another nature, for the protection of the rights and freedoms of citizens.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall present reports to the two Chambers of Parliament, annually or at their request. The reports may contain recommendations regarding legislation or measures of another nature, for the protection of the rights and freedoms of citizens.

1991:58.contentEn:
1. Parliament is the supreme representative body of the Romanian people and the sole legislative authority of the country.
2. Parliament shall be composed of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate.

content.text: Parliament is the supreme representative body of the Romanian people and the sole legislative authority of the country.
Parliament shall be composed of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate.

1991:59.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret, and freely expressed suffrage, according to the electoral law.
2. Organisations of citizens belonging to national minorities, which do not obtain in the elections the number of votes to be represented in Parliament, shall be entitled to one deputy seat each, under the terms of the electoral law. Citizens of a national minority may be represented by one organisation only.
3. The number of Deputies and Senators shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the country's population.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret, and freely expressed suffrage, according to the electoral law.

Organisations of citizens belonging to national minorities, which do not obtain in the elections the number of votes to be represented in Parliament, shall be entitled to one deputy seat each, under the terms of the electoral law. Citizens of a national minority may be represented by one organisation only.
The number of Deputies and Senators shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the country's population.

1991:60.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected for a 4-year term, which may be extended by organic law in the event of war or disaster.
2. Elections for the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be held no later than 3 months from the expiry of the term of office or from the dissolution of Parliament.
3. The newly elected Parliament shall convene, at the summons of the President of Romania, no later than 20 days from the elections.
4. The term of office of the Chambers shall be extended until the new Parliament legally convenes. During this period, the Constitution may not be revised, and organic laws may not be adopted, amended, or repealed.
5. Draft laws or legislative proposals on the agenda of the previous Parliament shall continue their procedure in the new Parliament.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected for a 4-year term, which may be extended by organic law in the event of war or disaster.
Elections for the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be held no later than 3 months from the expiry of the term of office or from the dissolution of Parliament.
The newly elected Parliament shall convene, at the summons of the President of Romania, no later than 20 days from the elections.

The term of office of the Chambers shall be extended until the new Parliament legally convenes. During this period, the Constitution may not be revised, and organic laws may not be adopted, amended, or repealed.
Draft laws or legislative proposals on the agenda of the previous Parliament shall continue their procedure in the new Parliament.

1991:61.contentEn:
1. The organisation and operation of each Chamber shall be established by its own regulations. The financial resources of the Chambers shall be provided for in the budgets approved by them.
2. Each Chamber shall elect a standing bureau. The Speaker of the Chamber of Deputies and the Speaker of the Senate shall be elected for the duration of the Chambers' term of office. The other members of the standing bureaux shall be elected at the beginning of each session. Members of the standing bureaux may be removed before the expiry of their term of office.
3. Deputies and senators may organise themselves into parliamentary groups, according to the regulations of each Chamber.
4. Each Chamber shall establish standing committees and may establish inquiry committees or other special committees. The Chambers may establish joint committees.
5. The standing bureaux and parliamentary committees shall be composed according to the political configuration of each Chamber.

content.text: The organisation and operation of each Chamber shall be established by its own regulations. The financial resources of the Chambers shall be provided for in the budgets approved by them.

Each Chamber shall elect a standing bureau. The Speaker of the Chamber of Deputies and the Speaker of the Senate shall be elected for the duration of the Chambers' term of office. The other members of the standing bureaux shall be elected at the beginning of each session. Members of the standing bureaux may be removed before the expiry of their term of office.
Deputies and senators may organise themselves into parliamentary groups, according to the regulations of each Chamber.
Each Chamber shall establish standing committees and may establish inquiry committees or other special committees. The Chambers may establish joint committees.
The standing bureaux and parliamentary committees shall be composed according to the political configuration of each Chamber.

The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate work in separate sittings and in joint sittings. In joint sittings, the proceedings shall be conducted according to regulations adopted by a majority vote of the Deputies and Senators.
The Chambers shall meet in a joint sitting for:
a) receiving the message of the President of Romania;
b) approving the State budget and the State social security budget;
c) declaring general or partial mobilization;
d) declaring a state of war;
e) suspending or ending military hostilities;
f) examining the reports of the Supreme Council of National Defence and of the Court of Accounts;
g) appointing, upon the proposal of the President of Romania, the Director of the Romanian Intelligence Service and exercising control over the activity of this service;
h) fulfilling other powers which, according to the Constitution or the regulations, are exercised in a joint sitting.

1991:63.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall meet in two ordinary sessions each year. The first session shall begin in February and may not extend beyond the end of June. The second session shall begin in September and may not extend beyond the end of December.
2. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall also meet in extraordinary sessions, at the request of the President of Romania, of the Standing Bureau of each Chamber, or of at least one third of the number of Deputies or Senators.
3. The Chambers shall be convened by their Speakers.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall meet in two ordinary sessions each year. The first session shall begin in February and may not extend beyond the end of June. The second session shall begin in September and may not extend beyond the end of December.

The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall also meet in extraordinary sessions, at the request of the President of Romania, of the Standing Bureau of each Chamber, or of at least one third of the number of Deputies or Senators.
The Chambers shall be convened by their Speakers.

1991:64.contentEn: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall adopt laws, decisions and motions, in the presence of the majority of their members.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall adopt laws, decisions and motions, in the presence of the majority of their members.

1991:65.contentEn:
1. The sittings of the two Chambers shall be public.
2. The Chambers may decide that certain sittings be secret.

content.text: The sittings of the two Chambers shall be public.
The Chambers may decide that certain sittings be secret.

1991:66.contentEn:
1. In the exercise of their mandate, Deputies and Senators shall be in the service of the people.
2. Any imperative mandate shall be null.

content.text: In the exercise of their mandate, Deputies and Senators shall be in the service of the people.
Any imperative mandate shall be null.

1991:67.contentEn:
1. Deputies and Senators shall enter into the exercise of their mandate on the date of the legal meeting of the Chamber to which they belong, subject to validation.
2. The office of Deputy or Senator shall cease on the date of the legal meeting of the newly elected Chambers, or in case of resignation, loss of electoral rights, incompatibility, or death.

content.text: Deputies and Senators shall enter into the exercise of their mandate on the date of the legal meeting of the Chamber to which they belong, subject to validation.
The office of Deputy or Senator shall cease on the date of the legal meeting of the newly elected Chambers, or in case of resignation, loss of electoral rights, incompatibility, or death.

1991:68.contentEn:
1. No one may be, at the same time, a Deputy and a Senator.
2. The office of Deputy or Senator is incompatible with the exercise of any public office of authority, with the exception of that of member of the Government.
3. Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

content.text: No one may be, at the same time, a Deputy and a Senator.
The office of Deputy or Senator is incompatible with the exercise of any public office of authority, with the exception of that of member of the Government.
Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

1991:69.contentEn:
1. No Deputy or Senator may be detained, arrested, searched or prosecuted, either in criminal or administrative matters, without the consent of the Chamber to which he/she belongs, after being heard. The power to judge shall lie with the Supreme Court of Justice.
2. In the event of flagrant offence, a Deputy or Senator may be detained and subjected to search. The Minister of Justice shall immediately inform the Speaker of the Chamber about the detention and search. In the event that the notified Chamber finds that there is no ground for detention, it shall immediately order the revocation of this measure.

content.text: No Deputy or Senator may be detained, arrested, searched or prosecuted, either in criminal or administrative matters, without the consent of the Chamber to which he/she belongs, after being heard. The power to judge shall lie with the Supreme Court of Justice.

In the event of flagrant offence, a Deputy or Senator may be detained and subjected to search. The Minister of Justice shall immediately inform the Speaker of the Chamber about the detention and search. In the event that the notified Chamber finds that there is no ground for detention, it shall immediately order the revocation of this measure.

1991:70.contentEn: The Deputies and Senators shall not be held legally liable for the votes cast or for the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate.

content.text: The Deputies and Senators shall not be held legally liable for the votes cast or for the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate.

1991:71.contentEn: The Deputies and Senators shall receive a monthly remuneration. The amount of the remuneration and the other rights shall be established by law.

content.text: The Deputies and Senators shall receive a monthly remuneration. The amount of the remuneration and the other rights shall be established by law.
The Parliament shall pass constitutional laws, organic laws, and ordinary laws.
Constitutional laws shall be those revising the Constitution.
Organic laws shall regulate:
a) the electoral system;
b) the organisation and functioning of political parties;
c) the organisation and holding of referendums;
d) the organisation of the Government and of the Supreme Council of National Defence;
e) the regime of the state of siege and the state of emergency;
f) offences, penalties, and the enforcement of such penalties;
g) the granting of amnesty or collective pardon;
h) the organisation and functioning of the Superior Council of Magistracy, of the law courts, of the Public Ministry, and of the Court of Accounts;
i) the status of public servants;
j) administrative litigation;
k) the general legal regime of property and inheritance;
l) the general regime governing labour relations, trade unions, and social protection;
m) the general organisation of education;
n) the general regime of religious denominations;
o) the organisation of local administration, of the territory, as well as the general regime regarding local autonomy;
p) the manner of establishing the exclusive economic zone;
r) the other fields for which the Constitution stipulates the enactment of organic laws.

1991:73.contentEn:
1. The legislative initiative shall lie with the Government, Deputies, Senators, as well as with a number of at least 250,000 citizens with the right to vote. The citizens exercising their right to legislative initiative must come from at least a quarter of the country's counties, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 10,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.
2. The citizens' legislative initiative shall not concern fiscal matters, international affairs, amnesty, and pardon.
3. The Government shall exercise its legislative initiative by forwarding the bill to one of the Chambers.
4. The Deputies, Senators, and citizens exercising the right to legislative initiative may only present legislative proposals in the form required for draft laws.
5. Legislative proposals shall be first submitted for adoption to the Chamber to which they were presented.

content.text: The legislative initiative shall lie with the Government, Deputies, Senators, as well as with a number of at least 250,000 citizens with the right to vote. The citizens exercising their right to legislative initiative must come from at least a quarter of the country's counties, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 10,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.
The citizens' legislative initiative shall not concern fiscal matters, international affairs, amnesty, and pardon.
The Government shall exercise its legislative initiative by forwarding the bill to one of the Chambers.
The Deputies, Senators, and citizens exercising the right to legislative initiative may only present legislative proposals in the form required for draft laws.
Legislative proposals shall be first submitted for adoption to the Chamber to which they were presented.

1991:74.contentEn:
1. Organic laws and resolutions regarding the regulations of the Chambers shall be passed by a majority vote of the members of each Chamber.
2. Ordinary laws and resolutions shall be passed by a majority vote of the members present in each Chamber.
3. At the Government's request or on its own initiative, the Parliament may pass draft laws or legislative proposals under an emergency procedure, established according to the regulations of each Chamber.

content.text: Organic laws and resolutions regarding the regulations of the Chambers shall be passed by a majority vote of the members of each Chamber.
Ordinary laws and resolutions shall be passed by a majority vote of the members present in each Chamber.

At the Government's request or on its own initiative, the Parliament may pass draft laws or legislative proposals under an emergency procedure, established according to the regulations of each Chamber.

1991:75.titleEn: Forwarding Draft Laws and Legislative Proposals from One Chamber to the Other
1991:75.contentEn: Draft laws or legislative proposals passed by one Chamber shall be forwarded to the other Chamber of the Parliament. If the latter rejects the draft law or legislative proposal, it shall be returned to the Chamber that passed it for a new debate. A new rejection shall be final.

content.text: Draft laws or legislative proposals passed by one Chamber shall be forwarded to the other Chamber of the Parliament. If the latter rejects the draft law or legislative proposal, it shall be returned to the Chamber that passed it for a new debate. A new rejection shall be final.

1991:76.contentEn:
1. If one of the Chambers adopts a bill or a legislative proposal in a wording different from that approved by the other Chamber, the Speakers of the Chambers shall initiate, through a joint committee, the mediation procedure.
2. If the committee fails to reach an agreement or if one of the Chambers does not approve the report of the mediation committee, the texts in dispute shall be submitted to the debate of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting, which shall adopt the final text by the majority vote provided for in [Articolul 74 paragraphs (1) or (2)](#articolul-74-adoptarea-legilor-și-a-hotărârilor).

content.text: If one of the Chambers adopts a bill or a legislative proposal in a wording different from that approved by the other Chamber, the Speakers of the Chambers shall initiate, through a joint committee, the mediation procedure.

If the committee fails to reach an agreement or if one of the Chambers does not approve the report of the mediation committee, the texts in dispute shall be submitted to the debate of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting, which shall adopt the final text by the majority vote provided for in [Articolul 74 paragraphs (1) or (2)](#articolul-74-adoptarea-legilor-și-a-hotărârilor).

1991:77.contentEn:
1. The law shall be forwarded to the President of Romania for promulgation. The promulgation of the law shall be done within a term of no more than 20 days from its receipt.
2. Before promulgation, the President may request Parliament, only once, to re-examine the law.
3. If the President has requested the re-examination of the law or if the verification of its constitutionality has been requested, the promulgation of the law shall be done within a term of no more than 10 days from the receipt of the law adopted after re-examination or from the receipt of the decision of the Constitutional Court, by which its constitutionality has been confirmed.

content.text: The law shall be forwarded to the President of Romania for promulgation. The promulgation of the law shall be done within a term of no more than 20 days from its receipt.
Before promulgation, the President may request Parliament, only once, to re-examine the law.

If the President has requested the re-examination of the law or if the verification of its constitutionality has been requested, the promulgation of the law shall be done within a term of no more than 10 days from the receipt of the law adopted after re-examination or from the receipt of the decision of the Constitutional Court, by which its constitutionality has been confirmed.

1991:78.titleEn: Entry into Force of the Law
1991:78.contentEn: The law shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania and shall enter into force on the date of publication or on the date provided for in its text.

content.text: The law shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania and shall enter into force on the date of publication or on the date provided for in its text.

1991:79.contentEn:
1. The Legislative Council is a specialised consultative body of Parliament, which endorses draft normative acts for the purpose of systematising, unifying and coordinating the entire legislation. It keeps the official record of the legislation of Romania.
2. The establishment, organisation and functioning of the Legislative Council shall be established by organic law.

content.text: The Legislative Council is a specialised consultative body of Parliament, which endorses draft normative acts for the purpose of systematising, unifying and coordinating the entire legislation. It keeps the official record of the legislation of Romania.
The establishment, organisation and functioning of the Legislative Council shall be established by organic law.

1991:80.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall represent the Romanian State and shall be the guarantor of national independence, of the unity and territorial integrity of the country.
2. The President of Romania shall ensure the observance of the Constitution and the proper functioning of public authorities. To this end, the President shall exercise a mediation function between the powers of the State, as well as between the State and society.

content.text: The President of Romania shall represent the Romanian State and shall be the guarantor of national independence, of the unity and territorial integrity of the country.

The President of Romania shall ensure the observance of the Constitution and the proper functioning of public authorities. To this end, the President shall exercise a mediation function between the powers of the State, as well as between the State and society.

1991:81.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret and freely expressed suffrage.
2. The candidate who has obtained, in the first round of voting, a majority of the votes of the voters registered on the electoral rolls shall be declared elected.
3. If none of the candidates has obtained this majority, a second round of voting shall be organised between the first two candidates established in the order of the number of votes obtained in the first round. The candidate who has obtained the highest number of votes shall be declared elected.
4. No person may hold the office of President of Romania for more than two terms of office. These may also be successive.

content.text: The President of Romania shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret and freely expressed suffrage.
The candidate who has obtained, in the first round of voting, a majority of the votes of the voters registered on the electoral rolls shall be declared elected.

If none of the candidates has obtained this majority, a second round of voting shall be organised between the first two candidates established in the order of the number of votes obtained in the first round. The candidate who has obtained the highest number of votes shall be declared elected.
No person may hold the office of President of Romania for more than two terms of office. These may also be successive.

1991:82.titleEn: Validation of the Mandate and Taking the Oath
1991:82.contentEn:
1. The result of the elections for the office of President of Romania shall be validated by the Constitutional Court.
2. The candidate whose election has been validated shall take the following oath before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting: "I swear to dedicate all my strength and skill for the spiritual and material prosperity of the Romanian people, to respect the Constitution and the laws of the country, to defend democracy, the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens, the sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Romania. So help me God!".

content.text: The result of the elections for the office of President of Romania shall be validated by the Constitutional Court.

The candidate whose election has been validated shall take the following oath before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting: "I swear to dedicate all my strength and skill for the spiritual and material prosperity of the Romanian people, to respect the Constitution and the laws of the country, to defend democracy, the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens, the sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Romania. So help me God!".

1991:83.contentEn:
1. The term of office of the President of Romania shall be 4 years and shall be exercised from the date of taking the oath.
2. The President of Romania shall exercise his/her mandate until the taking of the oath by the newly elected President.
3. The term of office of the President of Romania may be extended, by organic law, in the event of war or disaster.

content.text: The term of office of the President of Romania shall be 4 years and shall be exercised from the date of taking the oath.
The President of Romania shall exercise his/her mandate until the taking of the oath by the newly elected President.
The term of office of the President of Romania may be extended, by organic law, in the event of war or disaster.

1991:84.contentEn:
1. During the term of office, the President of Romania may not be a member of a party and may not hold any other public or private office.
2. The President of Romania shall enjoy immunity. The provisions of [Articolul 70](#articolul-70-independența-opiniilor) shall apply accordingly.
3. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may decide to impeach the President of Romania for high treason by a vote of at least two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators. The Supreme Court of Justice shall have jurisdiction to try such cases, under the law. The President shall be removed de jure from office on the date the conviction becomes final.

content.text: During the term of office, the President of Romania may not be a member of a party and may not hold any other public or private office.
The President of Romania shall enjoy immunity. The provisions of [Articolul 70](#articolul-70-independența-opiniilor) shall apply accordingly.

The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may decide to impeach the President of Romania for high treason by a vote of at least two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators. The Supreme Court of Justice shall have jurisdiction to try such cases, under the law. The President shall be removed de jure from office on the date the conviction becomes final.

1991:85.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister and shall appoint the Government on the basis of the vote of confidence granted by the Parliament.
2. In the event of a government reshuffle or a vacancy in office, the President shall, upon the proposal of the Prime Minister, dismiss and appoint some members of the Government.

content.text: The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister and shall appoint the Government on the basis of the vote of confidence granted by the Parliament.
In the event of a government reshuffle or a vacancy in office, the President shall, upon the proposal of the Prime Minister, dismiss and appoint some members of the Government.

1991:86.contentEn: The President of Romania may consult the Government on urgent issues of particular importance.

content.text: The President of Romania may consult the Government on urgent issues of particular importance.

1991:87.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania may attend Government meetings in which issues of national interest concerning foreign policy, national defense, ensuring public order and, at the request of the Prime Minister, in other situations are debated.
2. The President of Romania shall preside over the Government meetings he/she attends.

content.text: The President of Romania may attend Government meetings in which issues of national interest concerning foreign policy, national defense, ensuring public order and, at the request of the Prime Minister, in other situations are debated.
The President of Romania shall preside over the Government meetings he/she attends.

1991:88.contentEn: The President of Romania shall address messages to the Parliament regarding the main political issues of the nation.

content.text: The President of Romania shall address messages to the Parliament regarding the main political issues of the nation.

1991:89.contentEn:
1. After consulting the Speakers of both Chambers and the leaders of the parliamentary groups, the President of Romania may dissolve the Parliament if it has not granted a vote of confidence for the formation of the Government within 60 days of the first request and only after rejecting at least two requests for investiture.
2. During one year, the Parliament may be dissolved only once.
3. The Parliament may not be dissolved during the last 6 months of the term of office of the President of Romania, nor during a state of siege or a state of emergency.

content.text: After consulting the Speakers of both Chambers and the leaders of the parliamentary groups, the President of Romania may dissolve the Parliament if it has not granted a vote of confidence for the formation of the Government within 60 days of the first request and only after rejecting at least two requests for investiture.
During one year, the Parliament may be dissolved only once.
The Parliament may not be dissolved during the last 6 months of the term of office of the President of Romania, nor during a state of siege or a state of emergency.

1991:90.contentEn: The President of Romania, after consulting the Parliament, may ask the people to express, by referendum, their will on issues of national interest.

content.text: The President of Romania, after consulting the Parliament, may ask the people to express, by referendum, their will on issues of national interest.

1991:91.titleEn: Powers in the Field of Foreign Policy
1991:91.contentEn:
1. The President shall conclude international treaties on behalf of Romania, negotiated by the Government, and shall submit them to Parliament for ratification within 60 days.
2. The President shall, upon the proposal of the Government, accredit and recall the diplomatic representatives of Romania and approve the establishment, termination or change of rank of diplomatic missions.
3. The diplomatic representatives of other states shall be accredited to the President of Romania.

content.text: The President shall conclude international treaties on behalf of Romania, negotiated by the Government, and shall submit them to Parliament for ratification within 60 days.

The President shall, upon the proposal of the Government, accredit and recall the diplomatic representatives of Romania and approve the establishment, termination or change of rank of diplomatic missions.
The diplomatic representatives of other states shall be accredited to the President of Romania.

1991:92.titleEn: Powers in the Field of Defence
1991:92.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall be the commander of the armed forces and shall serve as Chairman of the Supreme Council of National Defence.
2. With the prior approval of Parliament, he may declare partial or general mobilization of the armed forces. Only in exceptional cases shall the President's decision be subsequently submitted for approval to Parliament, within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.
3. In the event of armed aggression against the country, the President of Romania shall take measures to repel the aggression and shall immediately bring them to the attention of Parliament by a message. If Parliament is not in session, it shall convene de jure within 24 hours of the outbreak of the aggression.

content.text: The President of Romania shall be the commander of the armed forces and shall serve as Chairman of the Supreme Council of National Defence.

With the prior approval of Parliament, he may declare partial or general mobilization of the armed forces. Only in exceptional cases shall the President's decision be subsequently submitted for approval to Parliament, within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.

In the event of armed aggression against the country, the President of Romania shall take measures to repel the aggression and shall immediately bring them to the attention of Parliament by a message. If Parliament is not in session, it shall convene de jure within 24 hours of the outbreak of the aggression.

1991:93.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall institute, according to the law, a state of siege or a state of emergency throughout the country or in some localities, and shall request Parliament to approve the measure adopted, within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.
2. If Parliament is not in session, it shall convene de jure within a maximum of 48 hours from the institution of the state of siege or the state of emergency and shall function throughout their duration.

content.text: The President of Romania shall institute, according to the law, a state of siege or a state of emergency throughout the country or in some localities, and shall request Parliament to approve the measure adopted, within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.

If Parliament is not in session, it shall convene de jure within a maximum of 48 hours from the institution of the state of siege or the state of emergency and shall function throughout their duration.
The President of Romania shall also have the following powers:
a) to confer decorations and honorary titles;
b) to grant the ranks of marshal, general and admiral;
c) to appoint to public offices, under the conditions provided by law;
d) to grant individual pardon.

1991:95.contentEn:
1. In the event of committing grave acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, the President of Romania may be suspended from office by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators, after consulting the Constitutional Court. The President may provide Parliament with explanations regarding the acts imputed to him.
2. The proposal for suspension from office may be initiated by at least one third of the number of deputies and senators and shall be immediately brought to the attention of the President.
3. If the proposal for suspension from office is approved, a referendum for the dismissal of the President shall be organized within a maximum of 30 days.

content.text: In the event of committing grave acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, the President of Romania may be suspended from office by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators, after consulting the Constitutional Court. The President may provide Parliament with explanations regarding the acts imputed to him.
The proposal for suspension from office may be initiated by at least one third of the number of deputies and senators and shall be immediately brought to the attention of the President.
If the proposal for suspension from office is approved, a referendum for the dismissal of the President shall be organized within a maximum of 30 days.

1991:96.contentEn:
1. The office of President of Romania shall become vacant in the event of resignation, dismissal from office, permanent impossibility to exercise the powers, or death.
2. Within 3 months from the date on which the office of President of Romania became vacant, the Government shall organize elections for a new President.

content.text: The office of President of Romania shall become vacant in the event of resignation, dismissal from office, permanent impossibility to exercise the powers, or death.
Within 3 months from the date on which the office of President of Romania became vacant, the Government shall organize elections for a new President.

1991:97.contentEn:
1. If the office of President becomes vacant or if the President is suspended from office or if he is temporarily unable to exercise his powers, the interim shall be ensured, in order, by the President of the Senate or by the President of the Chamber of Deputies.
2. The powers provided for in [Articolul 88-90](#articolul-88-mesaje) may not be exercised during the interim of the presidential office.

content.text: If the office of President becomes vacant or if the President is suspended from office or if he is temporarily unable to exercise his powers, the interim shall be ensured, in order, by the President of the Senate or by the President of the Chamber of Deputies.
The powers provided for in [Articolul 88-90](#articolul-88-mesaje) may not be exercised during the interim of the presidential office.

1991:98.contentEn: If the person ensuring the interim of the office of President of Romania commits grave acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, [Articolul 95](#articolul-95-suspendarea-din-funcție) and [Articolul 97](#articolul-97-interimatul-funcției) shall apply.

content.text: If the person ensuring the interim of the office of President of Romania commits grave acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, [Articolul 95](#articolul-95-suspendarea-din-funcție) and [Articolul 97](#articolul-97-interimatul-funcției) shall apply.

1991:99.contentEn:
1. In the exercise of his powers, the President of Romania shall issue decrees which shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decree non-existent.
2. The decrees issued by the President of Romania in the exercise of his powers provided for in [Articolul 91 paragraphs (1) and (2)](#articolul-91-atribuții-în-domeniul-politicii-externe), [Articolul 92 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-92-atribuții-în-domeniul-apărării), [Articolul 93 paragraph (1)](#articolul-93-măsuri-excepționale) and [Articolul 94 letters a), b) and d)](#articolul-94-alte-atribuții) shall be countersigned by the prime minister.

content.text: In the exercise of his powers, the President of Romania shall issue decrees which shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decree non-existent.

The decrees issued by the President of Romania in the exercise of his powers provided for in [Articolul 91 paragraphs (1) and (2)](#articolul-91-atribuții-în-domeniul-politicii-externe), [Articolul 92 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-92-atribuții-în-domeniul-apărării), [Articolul 93 paragraph (1)](#articolul-93-măsuri-excepționale) and [Articolul 94 letters a), b) and d)](#articolul-94-alte-atribuții) shall be countersigned by the prime minister.

1991:100.contentEn: The remuneration and other rights of the President of Romania shall be established by law.

content.text: The remuneration and other rights of the President of Romania shall be established by law.

1991:101.contentEn:
1. The Government, according to its governing program accepted by Parliament, shall ensure the implementation of the domestic and foreign policy of the country and exercise the general management of public administration.
2. In fulfilling its attributions, the Government shall cooperate with the interested social bodies.
3. The Government shall be composed of the Prime Minister, ministers, and other members established by organic law.

content.text: The Government, according to its governing program accepted by Parliament, shall ensure the implementation of the domestic and foreign policy of the country and exercise the general management of public administration.
In fulfilling its attributions, the Government shall cooperate with the interested social bodies.
The Government shall be composed of the Prime Minister, ministers, and other members established by organic law.

1991:102.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister, following the consultation of the party that holds the absolute majority in Parliament or, if such a majority does not exist, of the parties represented in Parliament.
2. The candidate for the office of Prime Minister shall request, within 10 days from the designation, the vote of confidence of Parliament on the program and the entire list of the Government.
3. The program and the list of the Government shall be debated by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting. Parliament shall grant confidence to the Government with the vote of the majority of deputies and senators.

content.text: The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister, following the consultation of the party that holds the absolute majority in Parliament or, if such a majority does not exist, of the parties represented in Parliament.
The candidate for the office of Prime Minister shall request, within 10 days from the designation, the vote of confidence of Parliament on the program and the entire list of the Government.

The program and the list of the Government shall be debated by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in a joint sitting. Parliament shall grant confidence to the Government with the vote of the majority of deputies and senators.

1991:103.contentEn:
1. The Prime Minister, ministers, and other members of the Government shall individually take, before the President of Romania, the oath provided for in [articolul 82](#articolul-82-validarea-mandatului-și-depunerea-jurământului).
2. The Government as a whole and each member individually shall exercise their mandate, starting from the date of taking the oath.

content.text: The Prime Minister, ministers, and other members of the Government shall individually take, before the President of Romania, the oath provided for in [articolul 82](#articolul-82-validarea-mandatului-și-depunerea-jurământului).
The Government as a whole and each member individually shall exercise their mandate, starting from the date of taking the oath.

1991:104.contentEn:
1. The office of member of the Government is incompatible with the exercise of any other public office of authority, with the exception of that of deputy or senator. It is also incompatible with the exercise of a salaried professional representation function within organizations with a commercial purpose.
2. Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

content.text: The office of member of the Government is incompatible with the exercise of any other public office of authority, with the exception of that of deputy or senator. It is also incompatible with the exercise of a salaried professional representation function within organizations with a commercial purpose.
Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

1991:105.titleEn: Cessation of the Office of Member of the Government
1991:105.contentEn: The office of member of the Government shall cease following resignation, revocation, loss of electoral rights, state of incompatibility, death, as well as in other cases provided for by law.

content.text: The office of member of the Government shall cease following resignation, revocation, loss of electoral rights, state of incompatibility, death, as well as in other cases provided for by law.

1991:106.contentEn:
1. The Prime Minister shall lead the Government and coordinate the activity of its members, respecting the attributions incumbent upon them. He/She shall also present to the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate reports and statements regarding the Government's policy, which shall be debated with priority.
2. If the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 105](#articolul-105-încetarea-funcției-de-membru-al-guvernului) or is unable to exercise his/her attributions, the President of Romania shall designate another member of the Government as interim Prime Minister, to fulfill the attributions of the Prime Minister, until the formation of the new Government. The interim period, during the impossibility of exercising the attributions, shall cease if the Prime Minister resumes his/her activity in the Government.
3. The provisions of paragraph (2) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the other members of the Government, at the proposal of the Prime Minister, for a period of no more than 45 days.

content.text: The Prime Minister shall lead the Government and coordinate the activity of its members, respecting the attributions incumbent upon them.
