He/She shall also present to the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate reports and statements regarding the Government's policy, which shall be debated with priority.

If the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 105](#articolul-105-încetarea-funcției-de-membru-al-guvernului) or is unable to exercise his/her attributions, the President of Romania shall designate another member of the Government as interim Prime Minister, to fulfill the attributions of the Prime Minister, until the formation of the new Government. The interim period, during the impossibility of exercising the attributions, shall cease if the Prime Minister resumes his/her activity in the Government.
The provisions of paragraph (2) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the other members of the Government, at the proposal of the Prime Minister, for a period of no more than 45 days.

1991:107.contentEn:
1. The Government shall adopt decisions and ordinances.
2. Decisions shall be issued for the organization of the execution of laws.
3. Ordinances shall be issued on the basis of a special enabling law, within the limits and under the conditions provided for therein.
4. The decisions and ordinances adopted by the Government shall be signed by the Prime Minister, countersigned by the ministers who have the obligation to put them into execution, and published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decision or ordinance non-existent. Decisions of a military nature shall be communicated only to the interested institutions.

content.text: The Government shall adopt decisions and ordinances.
Decisions shall be issued for the organization of the execution of laws.
Ordinances shall be issued on the basis of a special enabling law, within the limits and under the conditions provided for therein.

The decisions and ordinances adopted by the Government shall be signed by the Prime Minister, countersigned by the ministers who have the obligation to put them into execution, and published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decision or ordinance non-existent. Decisions of a military nature shall be communicated only to the interested institutions.

1991:108.titleEn: Liability of the Members of the Government
1991:108.contentEn:
1. The Government is politically accountable only to the Parliament for its entire activity. Each member of the Government is politically accountable jointly with the other members for the activity of the Government and for its acts.
2. Only the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, and the President of Romania are entitled to request the criminal prosecution of the members of the Government for acts committed in the exercise of their office. If criminal prosecution has been requested, the President of Romania may order their suspension from office. The referral to court of a member of the Government entails his/her suspension from office. The Supreme Court of Justice has jurisdiction to judge.
3. The cases of liability and the applicable penalties for the members of the Government are regulated by a law on ministerial responsibility.

content.text: The Government is politically accountable only to the Parliament for its entire activity. Each member of the Government is politically accountable jointly with the other members for the activity of the Government and for its acts.

Only the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, and the President of Romania are entitled to request the criminal prosecution of the members of the Government for acts committed in the exercise of their office. If criminal prosecution has been requested, the President of Romania may order their suspension from office. The referral to court of a member of the Government entails his/her suspension from office. The Supreme Court of Justice has jurisdiction to judge.
The cases of liability and the applicable penalties for the members of the Government are regulated by a law on ministerial responsibility.

1991:109.contentEn:
1. The Government exercises its mandate until the date of validation of the general parliamentary elections.
2. The Government is dismissed on the date of withdrawal by the Parliament of the confidence granted or if the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [Articolul 105](#articolul-105-încetarea-funcției-de-membru-al-guvernului) or is unable to exercise his/her duties for more than 45 days.
3. In the situations provided for in paragraph (2), the provisions of [Articolul 102](#articolul-102-investitura) are applicable.
4. The Government whose mandate has ceased according to paragraphs (1) and (2) performs only the acts necessary for the administration of public affairs, until the swearing in of the members of the new Government.

content.text: The Government exercises its mandate until the date of validation of the general parliamentary elections.

The Government is dismissed on the date of withdrawal by the Parliament of the confidence granted or if the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [Articolul 105](#articolul-105-încetarea-funcției-de-membru-al-guvernului) or is unable to exercise his/her duties for more than 45 days.
In the situations provided for in paragraph (2), the provisions of [Articolul 102](#articolul-102-investitura) are applicable.

The Government whose mandate has ceased according to paragraphs (1) and (2) performs only the acts necessary for the administration of public affairs, until the swearing in of the members of the new Government.

1991:110.contentEn:
1. The Government and the other bodies of the public administration, within the parliamentary scrutiny of their activity, are obliged to present the information and documents requested by the Chamber of Deputies, the the Senate, or the parliamentary committees, through their presidents. If a legislative initiative implies the modification of the provisions of the state budget or of the state social insurance budget, the request for information is mandatory.
2. The members of the Government have access to the proceedings of the Parliament. If their presence is requested, their participation is mandatory.

content.text: The Government and the other bodies of the public administration, within the parliamentary scrutiny of their activity, are obliged to present the information and documents requested by the Chamber of Deputies, the the Senate, or the parliamentary committees, through their presidents. If a legislative initiative implies the modification of the provisions of the state budget or of the state social insurance budget, the request for information is mandatory.
The members of the Government have access to the proceedings of the Parliament. If their presence is requested, their participation is mandatory.

1991:111.contentEn:
1. The Government and each of its members are obliged to answer the questions or interpellations formulated by the deputies or senators.
2. The Chamber of Deputies or the Senate may adopt a motion by which to express its position regarding the issue that was the subject of the interpellation.

content.text: The Government and each of its members are obliged to answer the questions or interpellations formulated by the deputies or senators.
The Chamber of Deputies or the Senate may adopt a motion by which to express its position regarding the issue that was the subject of the interpellation.

1991:112.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may withdraw the confidence granted to the Government by adopting a motion of no confidence, with the vote of the majority of the deputies and senators.
2. The motion of no confidence may be initiated by at least one quarter of the total number of deputies and senators and is communicated to the Government on the date of submission.
3. The motion of no confidence is debated after 3 days from the date when it was presented in joint sitting of the two Chambers.
4. If the motion of no confidence has been rejected, the deputies and senators who signed it may no longer initiate, in the same session, a new motion of no confidence, except in the case where the Government assumes responsibility according to [Articolul 113](#articolul-113-angajarea-răspunderii-guvernului).

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may withdraw the confidence granted to the Government by adopting a motion of no confidence, with the vote of the majority of the deputies and senators.
The motion of no confidence may be initiated by at least one quarter of the total number of deputies and senators and is communicated to the Government on the date of submission.
The motion of no confidence is debated after 3 days from the date when it was presented in joint sitting of the two Chambers.

If the motion of no confidence has been rejected, the deputies and senators who signed it may no longer initiate, in the same session, a new motion of no confidence, except in the case where the Government assumes responsibility according to [Articolul 113](#articolul-113-angajarea-răspunderii-guvernului).

1991:113.contentEn:
1. The Government may assume liability before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, on a programme, a declaration of general policy, or a bill.
2. The Government shall be dismissed if a motion of no confidence, filed within 3 days from the presentation of the programme, the declaration of general policy, or the bill, has been passed under the conditions of [Articolul 112](#articolul-112-moțiunea-de-cenzură).
3. If the Government has not been dismissed according to paragraph (2), the bill presented shall be deemed adopted, and the programme or the declaration of general policy becomes binding on the Government.
4. Should the President of Romania request the reconsideration of the law adopted according to paragraph (3), its debate shall be held in a joint sitting of the two Chambers.

content.text: The Government may assume liability before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, on a programme, a declaration of general policy, or a bill.

The Government shall be dismissed if a motion of no confidence, filed within 3 days from the presentation of the programme, the declaration of general policy, or the bill, has been passed under the conditions of [Articolul 112](#articolul-112-moțiunea-de-cenzură).

If the Government has not been dismissed according to paragraph (2), the bill presented shall be deemed adopted, and the programme or the declaration of general policy becomes binding on the Government.
Should the President of Romania request the reconsideration of the law adopted according to paragraph (3), its debate shall be held in a joint sitting of the two Chambers.

1991:114.contentEn:
1. Parliament may pass a special law enabling the Government to issue ordinances in fields that are not subject to organic laws.
2. The enabling law shall establish, in a mandatory manner, the field and the date until which ordinances may be issued.
3. If the enabling law so requires, the ordinances shall be submitted to Parliament for approval, according to the legislative procedure, until the expiry of the enabling term. Failure to comply with the term entails the cessation of the effects of the ordinance.
4. In exceptional cases, the Government may adopt emergency ordinances. These shall enter into force only after their submission for approval to Parliament. If Parliament is not in session, it shall be convened as a matter of course.
5. The approval or rejection of ordinances shall be made by a law which shall also include the ordinances whose effects have ceased according to paragraph (3).

content.text: Parliament may pass a special law enabling the Government to issue ordinances in fields that are not subject to organic laws.
The enabling law shall establish, in a mandatory manner, the field and the date until which ordinances may be issued.

If the enabling law so requires, the ordinances shall be submitted to Parliament for approval, according to the legislative procedure, until the expiry of the enabling term. Failure to comply with the term entails the cessation of the effects of the ordinance.

In exceptional cases, the Government may adopt emergency ordinances. These shall enter into force only after their submission for approval to Parliament. If Parliament is not in session, it shall be convened as a matter of course.
The approval or rejection of ordinances shall be made by a law which shall also include the ordinances whose effects have ceased according to paragraph (3).

1991:115.contentEn:
1. Ministries shall be organised only under the subordination of the Government.
2. Other specialised bodies may be organised under the subordination of the Government or of the ministries, or as autonomous administrative authorities.

content.text: Ministries shall be organised only under the subordination of the Government.
Other specialised bodies may be organised under the subordination of the Government or of the ministries, or as autonomous administrative authorities.

1991:116.contentEn:
1. Ministries shall be established, organised, and operate according to the law.
2. The Government and the ministries, with the advisory opinion of the Court of Accounts, may establish specialised bodies, under their subordination, only if the law recognises this competence to them.
3. Autonomous administrative authorities may be established by organic law.

content.text: Ministries shall be established, organised, and operate according to the law.
The Government and the ministries, with the advisory opinion of the Court of Accounts, may establish specialised bodies, under their subordination, only if the law recognises this competence to them.
Autonomous administrative authorities may be established by organic law.

1991:117.contentEn:
1. The Army is subordinated exclusively to the will of the people for the guarantee of the sovereignty, independence, and unity of the State, the territorial integrity of the country, and constitutional democracy.
2. The structure of the national defence system, the organisation of the army, the preparation of the population, the economy, and the territory for defence, as well as the status of military personnel, shall be established by organic law.
3. The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the police and the state intelligence services, as well as to the other components of the armed forces.
4. The organisation of military or paramilitary activities outside a state authority is prohibited.
5. Foreign troops may not enter or transit the territory of Romania except under the conditions established by law.

content.text: The Army is subordinated exclusively to the will of the people for the guarantee of the sovereignty, independence, and unity of the State, the territorial integrity of the country, and constitutional democracy.

The structure of the national defence system, the organisation of the army, the preparation of the population, the economy, and the territory for defence, as well as the status of military personnel, shall be established by organic law.
The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the police and the state intelligence services, as well as to the other components of the armed forces.
The organisation of military or paramilitary activities outside a state authority is prohibited.
Foreign troops may not enter or transit the territory of Romania except under the conditions established by law.

1991:118.titleEn: The Supreme Council of National Defence
1991:118.contentEn: The Supreme Council of National Defence shall organise and coordinate in a unitary manner the activities concerning the defence of the country and national security.

content.text: The Supreme Council of National Defence shall organise and coordinate in a unitary manner the activities concerning the defence of the country and national security.

1991:119.contentEn: Public administration in the administrative-territorial units is based on the principle of local autonomy and on that of the decentralisation of public services.

content.text: Public administration in the administrative-territorial units is based on the principle of local autonomy and on that of the decentralisation of public services.

1991:120.contentEn:
1. The public administration authorities, through which local autonomy is realised in communes and towns, are the elected local councils and the elected mayors, under the conditions of the law.
2. The local councils and the mayors shall function, under the conditions of the law, as autonomous administrative authorities and shall resolve public affairs in communes and towns.
3. The authorities provided for in paragraph (1) may also be constituted in the administrative-territorial subdivisions of municipalities.

content.text: The public administration authorities, through which local autonomy is realised in communes and towns, are the elected local councils and the elected mayors, under the conditions of the law.
The local councils and the mayors shall function, under the conditions of the law, as autonomous administrative authorities and shall resolve public affairs in communes and towns.
The authorities provided for in paragraph (1) may also be constituted in the administrative-territorial subdivisions of municipalities.

1991:121.contentEn:
1. The county council is the public administration authority for coordinating the activity of the communal and town councils, with a view to providing public services of county interest.
2. The county council is elected and functions under the terms provided by law.

content.text: The county council is the public administration authority for coordinating the activity of the communal and town councils, with a view to providing public services of county interest.
The county council is elected and functions under the terms provided by law.

1991:122.contentEn:
1. The Government appoints a prefect in each county and in the Municipality of Bucharest.
2. The Prefect is the representative of the Government at local level and directs the decentralized public services of the ministries and of the other central bodies in the administrative-territorial units.
3. The powers of the prefect shall be established according to the law.
4. The Prefect may challenge an act of the county council, of the local council or of the mayor before the administrative court, if he/she considers the act to be illegal. The act is suspended de jure.

content.text: The Government appoints a prefect in each county and in the Municipality of Bucharest.

The Prefect is the representative of the Government at local level and directs the decentralized public services of the ministries and of the other central bodies in the administrative-territorial units.
The powers of the prefect shall be established according to the law.
The Prefect may challenge an act of the county council, of the local council or of the mayor before the administrative court, if he/she considers the act to be illegal. The act is suspended de jure.

1991:123.contentEn:
1. Justice shall be administered in the name of the law.
2. Judges shall be independent and subject only to the law.

content.text: Justice shall be administered in the name of the law.
Judges shall be independent and subject only to the law.

1991:124.contentEn:
1. Judges appointed by the President of Romania shall be irremovable, according to the law. The President and the other judges of the Supreme Court of Justice shall be appointed for a term of 6 years. They can be reappointed in office. The promotion, transfer and sanctioning of judges may be decided only by the Superior Council of Magistracy, under the terms provided by law.
2. The office of judge shall be incompatible with any other public or private office, except for teaching positions in higher education.

content.text: Judges appointed by the President of Romania shall be irremovable, according to the law. The President and the other judges of the Supreme Court of Justice shall be appointed for a term of 6 years. They can be reappointed in office. The promotion, transfer and sanctioning of judges may be decided only by the Superior Council of Magistracy, under the terms provided by law.
The office of judge shall be incompatible with any other public or private office, except for teaching positions in higher education.

1991:125.contentEn:
1. Justice shall be rendered by the Supreme Court of Justice and by the other judiciary instances established by law.
2. The establishment of extraordinary courts is forbidden.
3. Competence and procedure of judgment shall be established by law.

content.text: Justice shall be rendered by the Supreme Court of Justice and by the other judiciary instances established by law.
The establishment of extraordinary courts is forbidden.
Competence and procedure of judgment shall be established by law.

1991:126.contentEn: Hearings shall be public, except for the cases provided by law.

content.text: Hearings shall be public, except for the cases provided by law.

1991:127.contentEn:
1. The judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian.
2. Citizens belonging to national minorities, as well as persons who do not understand or speak Romanian, shall have the right to take cognizance of all the acts and files of the case, to speak in court and to make submissions, through an interpreter; in criminal trials this right shall be ensured free of charge.

content.text: The judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian.

Citizens belonging to national minorities, as well as persons who do not understand or speak Romanian, shall have the right to take cognizance of all the acts and files of the case, to speak in court and to make submissions, through an interpreter; in criminal trials this right shall be ensured free of charge.

1991:128.titleEn: Using of Remedies Against Court Rulings
1991:128.contentEn: Interested parties and the Public Ministry may exercise remedies against court rulings, under the terms provided by law.

content.text: Interested parties and the Public Ministry may exercise remedies against court rulings, under the terms provided by law.

1991:129.contentEn: The courts of law have at their disposal the police placed in their service.

content.text: The courts of law have at their disposal the police placed in their service.

1991:130.titleEn: The Role of the Public Ministry
1991:130.contentEn:
1. In judicial activity, the Public Ministry represents the general interests of society and defends the rule of law, as well as the rights and freedoms of citizens.
2. The Public Ministry exercises its powers through prosecutors constituted in prosecutor's offices, under the terms of the law.

content.text: In judicial activity, the Public Ministry represents the general interests of society and defends the rule of law, as well as the rights and freedoms of citizens.
The Public Ministry exercises its powers through prosecutors constituted in prosecutor's offices, under the terms of the law.

1991:131.contentEn:
1. Prosecutors carry out their activity according to the principle of legality, impartiality and hierarchical control, under the authority of the Minister of Justice.
2. The office of prosecutor is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

content.text: Prosecutors carry out their activity according to the principle of legality, impartiality and hierarchical control, under the authority of the Minister of Justice.
The office of prosecutor is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

1991:132.contentEn: The Superior Council of Magistracy is composed of magistrates elected, for a term of 4 years, by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint session.

content.text: The Superior Council of Magistracy is composed of magistrates elected, for a term of 4 years, by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint session.

1991:133.contentEn:
1. The Superior Council of Magistracy proposes to the President of Romania the appointment of judges and prosecutors, with the exception of trainee judges and prosecutors, under the terms of the law. In this case, the proceedings are chaired, without the right to vote, by the Minister of Justice.
2. The Superior Council of Magistracy fulfills the role of disciplinary council of judges. In this case, the proceedings are chaired by the President of the Supreme Court of Justice.

content.text: The Superior Council of Magistracy proposes to the President of Romania the appointment of judges and prosecutors, with the exception of trainee judges and prosecutors, under the terms of the law. In this case, the proceedings are chaired, without the right to vote, by the Minister of Justice.
The Superior Council of Magistracy fulfills the role of disciplinary council of judges. In this case, the proceedings are chaired by the President of the Supreme Court of Justice.
The economy of Romania is a market economy.
The State must ensure:
a) freedom of commerce, protection of fair competition, creation of a favorable framework for the utilization of all factors of production;
b) protection of national interests in economic, financial and currency activity;
c) stimulation of national scientific research;
d) exploitation of natural resources, in accordance with the national interest;
e) restoration and protection of the environment, as well as maintaining ecological balance;
f) creation of the necessary conditions for increasing the quality of life.

1991:135.contentEn:
1. The State protects property.
2. Property is public or private.
3. Public property belongs to the State or to the administrative-territorial units.
4. Riches of any nature of the subsoil, the communication routes, the air space, waters with exploitable energy potential and those that can be used in the public interest, beaches, the territorial sea, the natural resources of the economic zone and of the continental shelf, as well as other goods established by law, are the exclusive object of public property.
5. Goods in the public property are inalienable. Under the terms of the law, they may be given in administration to autonomous régies or public institutions or may be granted in concession or leased.
6. Private property is, under the terms of the law, inviolable.

content.text: The State protects property.
Property is public or private.
Public property belongs to the State or to the administrative-territorial units.

Riches of any nature of the subsoil, the communication routes, the air space, waters with exploitable energy potential and those that can be used in the public interest, beaches, the territorial sea, the natural resources of the economic zone and of the continental shelf, as well as other goods established by law, are the exclusive object of public property.
Goods in the public property are inalienable. Under the terms of the law, they may be given in administration to autonomous régies or public institutions or may be granted in concession or leased.
Private property is, under the terms of the law, inviolable.

1991:136.contentEn:
1. The formation, administration, use and control of the financial resources of the State, of the administrative-territorial units and of the public institutions are regulated by law.
2. The national currency is the leu, and its subdivision is the ban.

content.text: The formation, administration, use and control of the financial resources of the State, of the administrative-territorial units and of the public institutions are regulated by law.
The national currency is the leu, and its subdivision is the ban.

1991:137.contentEn:
1. The national public budget comprises the state budget, the state social security budget, and the local budgets of communes, cities, and counties.
2. The Government shall annually prepare the draft state budget and the draft state social security budget, which it shall submit separately to Parliament for approval.
3. If the state budget law and the state social security budget law have not been adopted at least 3 days before the expiration of the budget year, the state budget and the state social security budget of the previous year shall continue to apply until the adoption of the new budgets.
4. Local budgets shall be prepared, approved, and executed under the conditions provided by law.
5. No budgetary expenditure may be approved without establishing the source of financing.

content.text: The national public budget comprises the state budget, the state social security budget, and the local budgets of communes, cities, and counties.
The Government shall annually prepare the draft state budget and the draft state social security budget, which it shall submit separately to Parliament for approval.

If the state budget law and the state social security budget law have not been adopted at least 3 days before the expiration of the budget year, the state budget and the state social security budget of the previous year shall continue to apply until the adoption of the new budgets.
Local budgets shall be prepared, approved, and executed under the conditions provided by law.
No budgetary expenditure may be approved without establishing the source of financing.

1991:138.contentEn:
1. Taxes, fees, and any other revenues of the state budget and the state social security budget shall be established only by law.
2. Local taxes and fees shall be established by the local or county councils, within the limits and under the conditions provided by law.

content.text: Taxes, fees, and any other revenues of the state budget and the state social security budget shall be established only by law.
Local taxes and fees shall be established by the local or county councils, within the limits and under the conditions provided by law.

1991:139.contentEn:
1. The Court of Accounts shall exercise control over the formation, administration, and use of the financial resources of the state and the public sector. Under the conditions provided by law, the Court shall also exercise jurisdictional powers.
2. The Court of Accounts shall annually submit to Parliament a report on the management accounts of the national public budget for the expired budget year, including the irregularities found.
3. At the request of the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate, the Court of Accounts shall control the management of public resources and report on the findings.
4. The members of the Court of Accounts, appointed by Parliament, shall be independent and irremovable, according to the law. They are subject to the incompatibilities provided by law for judges.

content.text: The Court of Accounts shall exercise control over the formation, administration, and use of the financial resources of the state and the public sector. Under the conditions provided by law, the Court shall also exercise jurisdictional powers.
The Court of Accounts shall annually submit to Parliament a report on the management accounts of the national public budget for the expired budget year, including the irregularities found.
At the request of the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate, the Court of Accounts shall control the management of public resources and report on the findings.
The members of the Court of Accounts, appointed by Parliament, shall be independent and irremovable, according to the law. They are subject to the incompatibilities provided by law for judges.

1991:140.contentEn:
1. The Constitutional Court shall be composed of nine judges, appointed for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed.
2. Three judges shall be appointed by the Chamber of Deputies, three by the Senate, and three by the President of Romania.
3. The judges of the Constitutional Court shall elect, by secret ballot, the President thereof, for a period of 3 years.
4. The Constitutional Court shall be renewed by one third of its judges, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.

content.text: The Constitutional Court shall be composed of nine judges, appointed for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed.
Three judges shall be appointed by the Chamber of Deputies, three by the Senate, and three by the President of Romania.
The judges of the Constitutional Court shall elect, by secret ballot, the President thereof, for a period of 3 years.
The Constitutional Court shall be renewed by one third of its judges, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.

1991:141.contentEn: The judges of the Constitutional Court must have higher legal education, high professional competence, and at least 18 years of experience in legal practice or higher legal education.

content.text: The judges of the Constitutional Court must have higher legal education, high professional competence, and at least 18 years of experience in legal practice or higher legal education.

1991:142.contentEn: The office of judge of the Constitutional Court is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

content.text: The office of judge of the Constitutional Court is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

1991:143.contentEn: The judges of the Constitutional Court shall be independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable for the duration thereof.

content.text: The judges of the Constitutional Court shall be independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable for the duration thereof.
The Constitutional Court has the following powers:

a) to rule on the constitutionality of laws prior to their promulgation, upon notification by the President of Romania, by one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, by the Government, by the High Court of Cassation and Justice, by a number of at least 50 Deputies or at least 25 Senators, as well as ex officio, on the initiatives for the revision of the Constitution;

b) to rule on the constitutionality of the Parliament's regulations, upon notification by one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, by a parliamentary group or by a number of at least 50 Deputies or at least 25 Senators;
c) to rule on the exceptions raised before the courts of law regarding the unconstitutionality of laws and ordinances;
d) to ensure the observance of the procedure for the election of the President of Romania and to confirm the results of the suffrage;

e) to ascertain the existence of circumstances justifying the interim in the exercise of the office of President of Romania and to communicate the ascertained facts to the Parliament and the Government;
f) to give a consultative opinion for the proposal to suspend the President of Romania from office;
g) to ensure the observance of the procedure for the organization and conduct of the referendum and to confirm its results;
h) to verify the fulfillment of the conditions for the exercise of the legislative initiative by citizens;
i) to rule on disputes having as object the constitutionality of a political party.

1991:145.contentEn:
1. In the cases of unconstitutionality ascertained according to [Articolul 144 letters a) and b)](#articolul-144-atribuții), the law or regulation shall be sent for re-examination. If the law is adopted in the same form, with a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber, the objection of unconstitutionality is overruled, and the promulgation becomes mandatory.
2. The decisions of the Constitutional Court are binding and have power only for the future. They shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania.

content.text: In the cases of unconstitutionality ascertained according to [Articolul 144 letters a) and b)](#articolul-144-atribuții), the law or regulation shall be sent for re-examination. If the law is adopted in the same form, with a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber, the objection of unconstitutionality is overruled, and the promulgation becomes mandatory.
The decisions of the Constitutional Court are binding and have power only for the future. They shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania.

1991:146.contentEn:
1. The revision of the Constitution may be initiated by the President of Romania at the proposal of the Government, by at least one quarter of the number of Deputies or Senators, as well as by at least 500,000 citizens with the right to vote.
2. The citizens who initiate the revision of the Constitution must come from at least half of the counties of the country, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 20,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.

content.text: The revision of the Constitution may be initiated by the President of Romania at the proposal of the Government, by at least one quarter of the number of Deputies or Senators, as well as by at least 500,000 citizens with the right to vote.

The citizens who initiate the revision of the Constitution must come from at least half of the counties of the country, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 20,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.

1991:147.contentEn:
1. The draft or proposal for revision must be adopted by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, with a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber.
2. If an agreement is not reached through the mediation procedure, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, shall decide with the vote of at least three-quarters of the number of Deputies and Senators.
3. The revision is final after its approval by referendum, organized within a maximum of 30 days from the date of adoption of the draft or proposal for revision.

content.text: The draft or proposal for revision must be adopted by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, with a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber.

If an agreement is not reached through the mediation procedure, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, shall decide with the vote of at least three-quarters of the number of Deputies and Senators.
The revision is final after its approval by referendum, organized within a maximum of 30 days from the date of adoption of the draft or proposal for revision.

1991:148.contentEn:
1. The provisions of the present Constitution regarding the national, independent, unitary and indivisible character of the Romanian State , the republican form of Government, the integrity of the territory, the independence of the judiciary, the political pluralism and the official language may not form the object of revision.
2. Likewise, no revision may be made if it results in the suppression of the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens or their guarantees.
3. The Constitution may not be revised during a state of siege or a state of emergency, nor in time of war.

content.text: The provisions of the present Constitution regarding the national, independent, unitary and indivisible character of the Romanian State , the republican form of Government, the integrity of the territory, the independence of the judiciary, the political pluralism and the official language may not form the object of revision.
Likewise, no revision may be made if it results in the suppression of the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens or their guarantees.
The Constitution may not be revised during a state of siege or a state of emergency, nor in time of war.

1991:149.contentEn: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its approval by referendum. On the same date, the Constitution of August 21, 1965 is and remains entirely repealed.

content.text: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its approval by referendum. On the same date, the Constitution of August 21, 1965 is and remains entirely repealed.

1991:150.contentEn:
1. Laws and all other normative acts shall remain in force to the extent that they do not contravene the present Constitution.
2. The Legislative Council shall, within 12 months from the date of entry into force of its organisation law, examine the compliance of the legislation with the present Constitution and shall make appropriate proposals to the Parliament or, as the case may be, to the Government.

content.text: Laws and all other normative acts shall remain in force to the extent that they do not contravene the present Constitution.

The Legislative Council shall, within 12 months from the date of entry into force of its organisation law, examine the compliance of the legislation with the present Constitution and shall make appropriate proposals to the Parliament or, as the case may be, to the Government.

1991:151.contentEn:
1. The institutions of the republic, existing on the date of entry into force of the present Constitution, shall remain in operation until the new ones are constituted.
2. The new Supreme Court of Justice shall be appointed, under the terms of the law, by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, upon the proposal of the President of Romania, within 6 months from the entry into force of the present Constitution.

content.text: The institutions of the republic, existing on the date of entry into force of the present Constitution, shall remain in operation until the new ones are constituted.

The new Supreme Court of Justice shall be appointed, under the terms of the law, by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, upon the proposal of the President of Romania, within 6 months from the entry into force of the present Constitution.

1991:152.contentEn:
1. Within 6 months from the date of entry into force of the Constitution, the Constitutional Court and the Court of Accounts shall be established.
2. The judges of the first Constitutional Court shall be appointed for a period of 3, 6 and 9 years respectively. The President of Romania, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall each designate one judge for each period.

content.text: Within 6 months from the date of entry into force of the Constitution, the Constitutional Court and the Court of Accounts shall be established.

The judges of the first Constitutional Court shall be appointed for a period of 3, 6 and 9 years respectively. The President of Romania, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall each designate one judge for each period.

2003:1.contentEn:
1. Romania is a national, sovereign and independent, unitary and indivisible State.
2. The form of government of the Romanian State is the republic.
3. Romania is a democratic and social State governed by the rule of law, in which human dignity, the rights and freedoms of citizens, the free development of human personality, justice and political pluralism represent supreme values, in the spirit of democratic traditions of the Romanian people and the ideals of the December 1989 Revolution, and are guaranteed.
4. The State shall be organised according to the principle of the separation and balance of powers – the legislative, executive and judicial – within the framework of constitutional democracy.
5. In Romania, the observance of the Constitution, of its supremacy and of the laws is mandatory.

content.text: Romania is a national, sovereign and independent, unitary and indivisible State.
The form of government of the Romanian State is the republic.

Romania is a democratic and social State governed by the rule of law, in which human dignity, the rights and freedoms of citizens, the free development of human personality, justice and political pluralism represent supreme values, in the spirit of democratic traditions of the Romanian people and the ideals of the December 1989 Revolution, and are guaranteed.
The State shall be organised according to the principle of the separation and balance of powers – the legislative, executive and judicial – within the framework of constitutional democracy.
In Romania, the observance of the Constitution, of its supremacy and of the laws is mandatory.

2003:2.contentEn:
1. National sovereignty shall vest in the Romanian people, who shall exercise it through its representative bodies, constituted by means of free, periodical and fair elections, as well as by referendum.
2. No group or person may exercise sovereignty in one’s own name.

content.text: National sovereignty shall vest in the Romanian people, who shall exercise it through its representative bodies, constituted by means of free, periodical and fair elections, as well as by referendum.
No group or person may exercise sovereignty in one’s own name.

2003:3.contentEn:
1. The territory of Romania is inalienable.
2. The borders of the country are enshrined in organic law, with the observance of the principles and other generally recognised rules of international law.
3. The territory is organised, from an administrative point of view, into communes, towns and counties. Under the terms of the law, some towns are declared municipalities.
4. Foreign populations may not be displaced or colonised on the territory of the Romanian State.

content.text: The territory of Romania is inalienable.
The borders of the country are enshrined in organic law, with the observance of the principles and other generally recognised rules of international law.
The territory is organised, from an administrative point of view, into communes, towns and counties. Under the terms of the law, some towns are declared municipalities.
Foreign populations may not be displaced or colonised on the territory of the Romanian State.

2003:4.titleEn: The Unity of the People and Equality among Citizens
2003:4.contentEn:
1. The State has as its foundation the unity of the Romanian people and the solidarity of its citizens.
2. Romania is the common and indivisible homeland of all its citizens, without distinction as to race, nationality, ethnic origin, language, religion, sex, opinion, political affiliation, wealth or social origin.

content.text: The State has as its foundation the unity of the Romanian people and the solidarity of its citizens.

Romania is the common and indivisible homeland of all its citizens, without distinction as to race, nationality, ethnic origin, language, religion, sex, opinion, political affiliation, wealth or social origin.

2003:5.contentEn:
1. Romanian citizenship is acquired, retained or lost under the terms provided for by the organic law.
2. Romanian citizenship may not be withdrawn from a person who acquired it by birth.

content.text: Romanian citizenship is acquired, retained or lost under the terms provided for by the organic law.
Romanian citizenship may not be withdrawn from a person who acquired it by birth.

2003:6.contentEn:
1. The State recognises and guarantees to persons belonging to national minorities the right to the preservation, development and expression of their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity.
2. The protection measures taken by the State for the preservation, development and expression of the identity of persons belonging to national minorities shall conform to the principles of equality and non-discrimination in relation to the other Romanian citizens.

content.text: The State recognises and guarantees to persons belonging to national minorities the right to the preservation, development and expression of their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity.

The protection measures taken by the State for the preservation, development and expression of the identity of persons belonging to national minorities shall conform to the principles of equality and non-discrimination in relation to the other Romanian citizens.

2003:7.contentEn: The State shall support the strengthening of links with Romanians living abroad and shall act for the preservation, development and expression of their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity, in compliance with the legislation of the State of which they are citizens.

content.text: The State shall support the strengthening of links with Romanians living abroad and shall act for the preservation, development and expression of their ethnic, cultural, linguistic and religious identity, in compliance with the legislation of the State of which they are citizens.

2003:8.contentEn:
1. Pluralism in Romanian society is a condition and a guarantee of constitutional democracy.
2. Political parties shall be constituted and carry on their activities under the law. They shall contribute to the definition and expression of the political will of the citizens, in compliance with national sovereignty, territorial integrity, the rule of law and the principles of democracy.

content.text: Pluralism in Romanian society is a condition and a guarantee of constitutional democracy.

Political parties shall be constituted and carry on their activities under the law. They shall contribute to the definition and expression of the political will of the citizens, in compliance with national sovereignty, territorial integrity, the rule of law and the principles of democracy.

2003:9.titleEn: Trade Unions, Employers' Associations, and Professional Associations
2003:9.contentEn: Trade unions, employers' associations, and professional associations shall be constituted and carry on their activities according to their own statutes, under the law. They shall contribute to the defence of the rights and to the promotion of the professional, economic, and social interests of their members.

content.text: Trade unions, employers' associations, and professional associations shall be constituted and carry on their activities according to their own statutes, under the law. They shall contribute to the defence of the rights and to the promotion of the professional, economic, and social interests of their members.

2003:10.contentEn: Romania fosters and develops peaceful relations with all States, and, to this end, relations of good neighbourliness, based on the principles and on the other generally recognised rules of international law.

content.text: Romania fosters and develops peaceful relations with all States, and, to this end, relations of good neighbourliness, based on the principles and on the other generally recognised rules of international law.

2003:11.contentEn:
1. The Romanian State pledges to fulfil punctually and in good faith its obligations as deriving from the treaties to which it is a party.
2. Treaties ratified by Parliament, according to the law, are part of the domestic law.
3. Should a treaty to which Romania is to become a party contain provisions contrary to the Constitution, its ratification may only take place after the revision of the Constitution.

content.text: The Romanian State pledges to fulfil punctually and in good faith its obligations as deriving from the treaties to which it is a party.
Treaties ratified by Parliament, according to the law, are part of the domestic law.
Should a treaty to which Romania is to become a party contain provisions contrary to the Constitution, its ratification may only take place after the revision of the Constitution.

2003:12.contentEn:
1. The flag of Romania is tricolour; the colours are arranged vertically in the following order from the flagpole: blue, yellow, red.
2. Romania's National Day is December 1.
3. Romania's national anthem is "Deșteaptă-te române" ["Awaken thee, Romanian"].
4. The State Coat of Arms and the State Seal shall be established by organic laws.

content.text: The flag of Romania is tricolour; the colours are arranged vertically in the following order from the flagpole: blue, yellow, red.
Romania's National Day is December 1.
Romania's national anthem is "Deșteaptă-te române" ["Awaken thee, Romanian"].
The State Coat of Arms and the State Seal shall be established by organic laws.

2003:13.contentEn: In Romania, the official language is Romanian.

content.text: In Romania, the official language is Romanian.

2003:14.contentEn: The capital of Romania is the Municipality of Bucharest.

content.text: The capital of Romania is the Municipality of Bucharest.

2003:15.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall benefit from the rights and freedoms enshrined in the Constitution and other laws, and shall have the obligations laid down therein.
2. The law shall only have provisions for the future, with the exception of the more favourable criminal or administrative offence law.

content.text: Citizens shall benefit from the rights and freedoms enshrined in the Constitution and other laws, and shall have the obligations laid down therein.
The law shall only have provisions for the future, with the exception of the more favourable criminal or administrative offence law.

2003:16.contentEn:
1. Citizens are equal before the law and public authorities, without any privilege or discrimination.
2. No one is above the law.
3. Public offices and dignities, civil or military, may be occupied, under the law, by persons who have Romanian citizenship and domicile in the country. The Romanian State guarantees equal opportunities for women and men to hold such offices and dignities.
4. Under the conditions of Romania's accession to the European Union, Union citizens who meet the requirements of the organic law have the right to elect and to be elected to the authorities of local public administration.

content.text: Citizens are equal before the law and public authorities, without any privilege or discrimination.
No one is above the law.

Public offices and dignities, civil or military, may be occupied, under the law, by persons who have Romanian citizenship and domicile in the country. The Romanian State guarantees equal opportunities for women and men to hold such offices and dignities.

Under the conditions of Romania's accession to the European Union, Union citizens who meet the requirements of the organic law have the right to elect and to be elected to the authorities of local public administration.

2003:17.contentEn: Romanian citizens shall enjoy the protection of the Romanian State while abroad, and shall fulfil their obligations, except for those incompatible with their absence from the country.

content.text: Romanian citizens shall enjoy the protection of the Romanian State while abroad, and shall fulfil their obligations, except for those incompatible with their absence from the country.

2003:18.contentEn:
1. Foreign citizens and stateless persons residing in Romania shall enjoy the general protection of persons and property, as guaranteed by the Constitution and other laws.
2. The right of asylum shall be granted and withdrawn under the law, in compliance with international treaties and conventions to which Romania is a party.

content.text: Foreign citizens and stateless persons residing in Romania shall enjoy the general protection of persons and property, as guaranteed by the Constitution and other laws.
The right of asylum shall be granted and withdrawn under the law, in compliance with international treaties and conventions to which Romania is a party.

2003:19.contentEn:
1. Romanian citizens shall not be extradited or expelled from Romania.
2. By way of derogation from the provisions of paragraph (1), Romanian citizens may be extradited on the basis of international conventions to which Romania is a party, under the law and on a reciprocal basis.
3. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may be extradited only on the basis of an international convention or on a reciprocal basis.
4. Expulsion or extradition shall be decided by the courts of law.

content.text: Romanian citizens shall not be extradited or expelled from Romania.

By way of derogation from the provisions of paragraph (1), Romanian citizens may be extradited on the basis of international conventions to which Romania is a party, under the law and on a reciprocal basis.
Foreign citizens and stateless persons may be extradited only on the basis of an international convention or on a reciprocal basis.
Expulsion or extradition shall be decided by the courts of law.

2003:20.contentEn:
1. The constitutional provisions concerning the rights and freedoms of citizens shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, with the covenants and with the other treaties to which Romania is a party.
2. Where there is inconsistency between the covenants and the treaties on fundamental human rights to which Romania is a party and domestic laws, international regulations shall take precedence, unless the Constitution or domestic laws contain more favourable provisions.

content.text: The constitutional provisions concerning the rights and freedoms of citizens shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, with the covenants and with the other treaties to which Romania is a party.

Where there is inconsistency between the covenants and the treaties on fundamental human rights to which Romania is a party and domestic laws, international regulations shall take precedence, unless the Constitution or domestic laws contain more favourable provisions.

2003:21.contentEn:
1. Any person may apply to the courts of law for the defence of his/her legitimate rights, freedoms and interests.
2. No law may restrict the exercise of this right.
3. The parties shall be entitled to a fair trial and a settlement of their cases within a reasonable time.
4. Special administrative jurisdictions are optional and free of charge.

content.text: Any person may apply to the courts of law for the defence of his/her legitimate rights, freedoms and interests.
No law may restrict the exercise of this right.
The parties shall be entitled to a fair trial and a settlement of their cases within a reasonable time.
Special administrative jurisdictions are optional and free of charge.

2003:22.titleEn: Right to Life, Physical and Mental Integrity
2003:22.contentEn:
1. The right to life, as well as the right to physical and mental integrity of the person are guaranteed.
2. No one shall be subjected to torture or to any inhuman or degrading punishment or treatment.
3. The death penalty is prohibited.

content.text: The right to life, as well as the right to physical and mental integrity of the person are guaranteed.
No one shall be subjected to torture or to any inhuman or degrading punishment or treatment.
The death penalty is prohibited.

2003:23.contentEn:
1. Individual freedom and safety of person are inviolable.
2. Search, detention or arrest of a person shall be permitted only in such cases and according to such procedure as provided by law.
3. Detention may not exceed 24 hours.
4. Preventive arrest shall be ordered by a judge and only in the course of criminal proceedings.
5. During criminal investigation, preventive arrest may be ordered for a maximum of 30 days and may be extended by a maximum of 30 days each time, without the total duration exceeding a reasonable term, and no more than 180 days.
6. During the trial phase, the court is obliged, under the conditions provided by law, to periodically verify, and no later than 60 days, the legality and grounds of the preventive arrest and to order, immediately, the release of the defendant, if the grounds that determined the preventive arrest have ceased or if the court finds that there are no new grounds to justify the maintenance of deprivation of liberty.
7. The court's rulings regarding the preventive arrest measure are subject to the legal remedies provided by law.
8. The detained or arrested person shall be promptly informed, in a language he understands, of the reasons for the detention or arrest, and of the charge, as soon as possible; the charge shall be brought to his knowledge only in the presence of a lawyer, chosen or appointed ex officio.
9. The release of the detained or arrested person shall be mandatory if the reasons for these measures have disappeared, as well as in other situations provided by law.
10. The person under preventive arrest has the right to request provisional release, under judicial control or on bail.
11. Until a final conviction ruling is rendered, a person shall be presumed innocent.
12. No penalty may be established or applied except under the conditions and on the basis of the law.
13. Deprivation of liberty may only be of a criminal nature.

content.text: Individual freedom and safety of person are inviolable.
Search, detention or arrest of a person shall be permitted only in such cases and according to such procedure as provided by law.
Detention may not exceed 24 hours.
Preventive arrest shall be ordered by a judge and only in the course of criminal proceedings.

During criminal investigation, preventive arrest may be ordered for a maximum of 30 days and may be extended by a maximum of 30 days each time, without the total duration exceeding a reasonable term, and no more than 180 days.

During the trial phase, the court is obliged, under the conditions provided by law, to periodically verify, and no later than 60 days, the legality and grounds of the preventive arrest and to order, immediately, the release of the defendant, if the grounds that determined the preventive arrest have ceased or if the court finds that there are no new grounds to justify the maintenance of deprivation of liberty.
The court's rulings regarding the preventive arrest measure are subject to the legal remedies provided by law.

The detained or arrested person shall be promptly informed, in a language he understands, of the reasons for the detention or arrest, and of the charge, as soon as possible; the charge shall be brought to his knowledge only in the presence of a lawyer, chosen or appointed ex officio.
The release of the detained or arrested person shall be mandatory if the reasons for these measures have disappeared, as well as in other situations provided by law.
The person under preventive arrest has the right to request provisional release, under judicial control or on bail.
Until a final conviction ruling is rendered, a person shall be presumed innocent.
No penalty may be established or applied except under the conditions and on the basis of the law.
Deprivation of liberty may only be of a criminal nature.

2003:24.contentEn:
1. The right to defence is guaranteed.
2. Throughout the trial, the parties shall have the right to be assisted by a lawyer, chosen or appointed ex officio.

content.text: The right to defence is guaranteed.
Throughout the trial, the parties shall have the right to be assisted by a lawyer, chosen or appointed ex officio.

2003:25.contentEn:
1. The right to free movement within the national territory and abroad is guaranteed. The law shall lay down the conditions for the exercise of this right.
2. Every citizen shall be guaranteed the right to establish his domicile or residence in any locality in the country, to emigrate, and to return to his country.

content.text: The right to free movement within the national territory and abroad is guaranteed. The law shall lay down the conditions for the exercise of this right.
Every citizen shall be guaranteed the right to establish his domicile or residence in any locality in the country, to emigrate, and to return to his country.

2003:26.contentEn:
1. Public authorities shall respect and protect intimate, family and private life.
2. An individual has the right to freely dispose of himself, provided that he does not infringe upon the rights and freedoms of others, public order or morals.

content.text: Public authorities shall respect and protect intimate, family and private life.
An individual has the right to freely dispose of himself, provided that he does not infringe upon the rights and freedoms of others, public order or morals.
The domicile and the residence are inviolable. No one may enter or remain in the domicile or residence of a person without his consent.
Derogation from the provisions of paragraph (1) shall be permissible by law in the following situations:
a) execution of an arrest warrant or of a court sentence;
b) removal of any danger to a person's life, physical integrity, or property;
c) defence of national security or public order;
d) prevention of the spread of an epidemic.
Search shall be ordered by a judge and carried out under the conditions and in the forms provided by law.
Searches during the night shall be forbidden, save for flagrant offences.

2003:28.contentEn: Secrecy of letters, telegrams, other postal items, telephone conversations and other legal means of communication is inviolable.

content.text: Secrecy of letters, telegrams, other postal items, telephone conversations and other legal means of communication is inviolable.

2003:29.contentEn:
1. Freedom of thought and opinion, as well as freedom of religious beliefs, may not be restricted in any form whatsoever. No one may be compelled to adopt an opinion or adhere to a religious belief contrary to their convictions.

2. Freedom of conscience is guaranteed; it must be manifested in a spirit of tolerance and mutual respect.

3. Religious denominations shall be free and organise themselves according to their own statutes, under the terms provided by law.

4. In the relationships between religious denominations, any forms, means, acts or actions of religious animosity are prohibited.

5. Religious denominations shall be autonomous from the State and shall enjoy its support, including by facilitating religious assistance in the army, in hospitals, in penitentiaries, in asylums and in orphanages.

6. Parents or guardians have the right to ensure, according to their own convictions, the education of minor children for whom they are responsible.

content.text: Freedom of thought and opinion, as well as freedom of religious beliefs, may not be restricted in any form whatsoever. No one may be compelled to adopt an opinion or adhere to a religious belief contrary to their convictions.
Freedom of conscience is guaranteed; it must be manifested in a spirit of tolerance and mutual respect.
Religious denominations shall be free and organise themselves according to their own statutes, under the terms provided by law.
In the relationships between religious denominations, any forms, means, acts or actions of religious animosity are prohibited.

Religious denominations shall be autonomous from the State and shall enjoy its support, including by facilitating religious assistance in the army, in hospitals, in penitentiaries, in asylums and in orphanages.
Parents or guardians have the right to ensure, according to their own convictions, the education of minor children for whom they are responsible.

2003:30.contentEn:
1. Freedom of expression of thoughts, opinions, or beliefs, and freedom of artistic creation of any kind, by means of words, in writing, in images, by sounds or any other means of public communication, are inviolable.

2. Any form of censorship is prohibited.

3. Freedom of the press also implies the freedom to set up publications.

4. No publication may be suppressed.

5. The law may impose on the mass media the obligation to make public the source of their financing.

6. Freedom of expression may not prejudice the dignity, honour, private life of a person, or the right to their own image.

7. Defamation of the country and the nation, incitement to aggressive war, to national, racial, class or religious hatred, incitement to discrimination, territorial separatism or public violence, as well as obscene conduct contrary to morality, shall be prohibited by law.

8. Civil liability for information or creation brought to public knowledge shall lie with the publisher or producer, the author, the organiser of the artistic event, the owner of the means of duplication, of the radio or television station, under the terms provided by law. Offences of the press shall be established by law.

content.text: Freedom of expression of thoughts, opinions, or beliefs, and freedom of artistic creation of any kind, by means of words, in writing, in images, by sounds or any other means of public communication, are inviolable.
Any form of censorship is prohibited.
Freedom of the press also implies the freedom to set up publications.
No publication may be suppressed.
The law may impose on the mass media the obligation to make public the source of their financing.
Freedom of expression may not prejudice the dignity, honour, private life of a person, or the right to their own image.

Defamation of the country and the nation, incitement to aggressive war, to national, racial, class or religious hatred, incitement to discrimination, territorial separatism or public violence, as well as obscene conduct contrary to morality, shall be prohibited by law.

Civil liability for information or creation brought to public knowledge shall lie with the publisher or producer, the author, the organiser of the artistic event, the owner of the means of duplication, of the radio or television station, under the terms provided by law. Offences of the press shall be established by law.

2003:31.contentEn:
1. The right of a person to have access to any information of public interest may not be restricted.

2. Public authorities, according to their competence, shall be bound to ensure that citizens are correctly informed on matters of public interest and on issues of personal interest.

3. The right to information must not prejudice the measures for the protection of young people or national security.

4. Public and private mass media shall be bound to ensure that the public is correctly informed.

5. Public radio and television services shall be autonomous. They must guarantee to important social and political groups the exercise of the right to airtime. The organisation of these services and parliamentary control over their activity shall be regulated by organic law.

content.text: The right of a person to have access to any information of public interest may not be restricted.
Public authorities, according to their competence, shall be bound to ensure that citizens are correctly informed on matters of public interest and on issues of personal interest.
The right to information must not prejudice the measures for the protection of young people or national security.
Public and private mass media shall be bound to ensure that the public is correctly informed.

Public radio and television services shall be autonomous. They must guarantee to important social and political groups the exercise of the right to airtime. The organisation of these services and parliamentary control over their activity shall be regulated by organic law.

2003:32.contentEn:
1. The right to education is ensured by general compulsory education, by high school and vocational education, by higher education, as well as by other forms of instruction and professional development.
2. Education of all levels shall be carried out in Romanian. Under the conditions provided by law, education may also be carried out in a language of international circulation.
3. The right of persons belonging to national minorities to learn their mother tongue, and the right to be educated in this language are guaranteed; the ways to exercise these rights shall be established by law.
4. State education is free of charge, according to the law. The State shall grant social study grants to children and young people from disadvantaged families and to those who are institutionalized, under the conditions provided by law.
5. Education of all levels shall be carried out in state, private and denominational units, under the conditions provided by law.
6. University autonomy is guaranteed.
7. The State shall ensure freedom of religious education, according to the specific requirements of each religious denomination. In state schools, religious education is organized and guaranteed by law.

content.text: The right to education is ensured by general compulsory education, by high school and vocational education, by higher education, as well as by other forms of instruction and professional development.
Education of all levels shall be carried out in Romanian. Under the conditions provided by law, education may also be carried out in a language of international circulation.

The right of persons belonging to national minorities to learn their mother tongue, and the right to be educated in this language are guaranteed; the ways to exercise these rights shall be established by law.

State education is free of charge, according to the law. The State shall grant social study grants to children and young people from disadvantaged families and to those who are institutionalized, under the conditions provided by law.
Education of all levels shall be carried out in state, private and denominational units, under the conditions provided by law.
University autonomy is guaranteed.
The State shall ensure freedom of religious education, according to the specific requirements of each religious denomination. In state schools, religious education is organized and guaranteed by law.

2003:33.contentEn:
1. Access to culture is guaranteed, under the conditions provided by law.
2. The freedom of the person to develop his/her spirituality and to have access to the values of national and universal culture may not be restricted.
3. The State must ensure the preservation of spiritual identity, the support of national culture, the stimulation of the arts, the protection and conservation of cultural heritage, the development of contemporary creativity, the promotion of Romania's cultural and artistic values in the world.

content.text: Access to culture is guaranteed, under the conditions provided by law.
The freedom of the person to develop his/her spirituality and to have access to the values of national and universal culture may not be restricted.

The State must ensure the preservation of spiritual identity, the support of national culture, the stimulation of the arts, the protection and conservation of cultural heritage, the development of contemporary creativity, the promotion of Romania's cultural and artistic values in the world.

2003:34.contentEn:
1. The right to health protection is guaranteed.
2. The State is obliged to take measures to ensure hygiene and public health.
3. The organization of medical assistance and of the social insurance system for illness, accidents, maternity and recovery, the control of the exercise of medical professions and paramedical activities, as well as other measures for the protection of the physical and mental health of the person shall be established according to the law.

content.text: The right to health protection is guaranteed.
The State is obliged to take measures to ensure hygiene and public health.

The organization of medical assistance and of the social insurance system for illness, accidents, maternity and recovery, the control of the exercise of medical professions and paramedical activities, as well as other measures for the protection of the physical and mental health of the person shall be established according to the law.

2003:35.contentEn:
1. The State recognizes the right of every person to a healthy environment and to an ecologically balanced environment.
2. The State shall ensure the legislative framework for the exercise of this right.
3. Natural and legal persons have a duty to protect and improve the environment.

content.text: The State recognizes the right of every person to a healthy environment and to an ecologically balanced environment.
The State shall ensure the legislative framework for the exercise of this right.
Natural and legal persons have a duty to protect and improve the environment.

2003:36.contentEn:
1. Citizens have the right to vote from the age of 18, completed up to and including the day of the elections.
2. Persons who are mentally deficient or alienated, placed under interdiction, and persons convicted, by a final court decision, to the loss of electoral rights do not have the right to vote.

content.text: Citizens have the right to vote from the age of 18, completed up to and including the day of the elections.
Persons who are mentally deficient or alienated, placed under interdiction, and persons convicted, by a final court decision, to the loss of electoral rights do not have the right to vote.

2003:37.contentEn:
1. Citizens with the right to vote who meet the conditions provided for in [Articolul 16 paragraph (3)](#articolul-16-egalitatea-în-drepturi) have the right to be elected, if they are not prohibited from associating in political parties, according to [Articolul 40 paragraph (3)](#articolul-40-dreptul-de-asociere).
2. Candidates must have reached, up to and including the day of the elections, the age of at least 23 years to be elected to the Chamber of Deputies or to the bodies of local public administration, the age of at least 33 years to be elected to the Senate and the age of at least 35 years to be elected to the office of President of Romania.

content.text: Citizens with the right to vote who meet the conditions provided for in [Articolul 16 paragraph (3)](#articolul-16-egalitatea-în-drepturi) have the right to be elected, if they are not prohibited from associating in political parties, according to [Articolul 40 paragraph (3)](#articolul-40-dreptul-de-asociere).

Candidates must have reached, up to and including the day of the elections, the age of at least 23 years to be elected to the Chamber of Deputies or to the bodies of local public administration, the age of at least 33 years to be elected to the Senate and the age of at least 35 years to be elected to the office of President of Romania.

2003:38.titleEn: Right to be Elected to the European Parliament
2003:38.contentEn: Under the conditions of Romania's accession to the European Union, Romanian citizens have the right to elect and to be elected to the European Parliament.

content.text: Under the conditions of Romania's accession to the European Union, Romanian citizens have the right to elect and to be elected to the European Parliament.

2003:39.contentEn: Meetings, demonstrations, processions or any other assemblies are free and may be organized and conducted only peacefully, without any kind of weapons.

content.text: Meetings, demonstrations, processions or any other assemblies are free and may be organized and conducted only peacefully, without any kind of weapons.

2003:40.contentEn:
1. Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions, employers' associations, and other forms of association.
2. Parties or organizations which, by their purposes or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or the sovereignty, integrity, or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.
3. Judges of the Constitutional Court, the ombudsmen, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen, and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
4. Associations of a secret nature are prohibited.

content.text: Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions, employers' associations, and other forms of association.

Parties or organizations which, by their purposes or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or the sovereignty, integrity, or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.

Judges of the Constitutional Court, the ombudsmen, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen, and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
Associations of a secret nature are prohibited.

2003:41.titleEn: Labour and Social Protection of Labour
2003:41.contentEn:
