The Chambers, if not in session, shall be compulsorily convened within 5 days from the submission or, as the case may be, from the forwarding. If, within a maximum of 30 days from the submission, the notified Chamber does not pronounce on the ordinance, it is deemed adopted and is forwarded to the other Chamber, which also decides under an emergency procedure. The emergency ordinance including norms of the nature of an organic law shall be approved by the majority provided for in [Articolul 76 paragraph (1)](#articolul-76-adoptarea-legilor-și-a-hotărârilor).
6. Emergency ordinances may not be adopted in the field of constitutional laws, may not affect the regime of the fundamental institutions of the State, the rights, freedoms and duties provided for by the Constitution, the electoral rights, and may not concern measures for the forced transfer of goods into public property.
7. The ordinances with which the Parliament has been notified shall be approved or rejected by a law in which the ordinances whose effects have ceased according to paragraph (3) shall also be included.
8. The law of approval or rejection shall regulate, if necessary, the measures required with regard to the legal effects produced during the period of application of the ordinance.

content.text: The Parliament may adopt a special enabling law empowering the Government to issue ordinances in fields that are not subject to organic laws.
The enabling law shall compulsorily establish the field and the date until which ordinances may be issued.

If the enabling law so requires, the ordinances shall be submitted to the Parliament for approval, according to the legislative procedure, until the expiry of the enabling term. Failure to comply with the term entails the cessation of the effects of the ordinance.
The Government may adopt emergency ordinances only in extraordinary situations whose regulation cannot be postponed, having the obligation to state the reasons for the emergency within them.

The emergency ordinance shall enter into force only after its submission for debate under an emergency procedure to the Chamber having the competence to be notified and after its publication in the Official Gazette of Romania. The Chambers, if not in session, shall be compulsorily convened within 5 days from the submission or, as the case may be, from the forwarding. If, within a maximum of 30 days from the submission, the notified Chamber does not pronounce on the ordinance, it is deemed adopted and is forwarded to the other Chamber, which also decides under an emergency procedure. The emergency ordinance including norms of the nature of an organic law shall be approved by the majority provided for in [Articolul 76 paragraph (1)](#articolul-76-adoptarea-legilor-și-a-hotărârilor).

Emergency ordinances may not be adopted in the field of constitutional laws, may not affect the regime of the fundamental institutions of the State, the rights, freedoms and duties provided for by the Constitution, the electoral rights, and may not concern measures for the forced transfer of goods into public property.
The ordinances with which the Parliament has been notified shall be approved or rejected by a law in which the ordinances whose effects have ceased according to paragraph (3) shall also be included.
The law of approval or rejection shall regulate, if necessary, the measures required with regard to the legal effects produced during the period of application of the ordinance.

2003:116.contentEn:
1. The Ministries shall be organised only under the subordination of the Government.
2. Other specialised bodies may be organised under the subordination of the Government or of the Ministries, or as autonomous administrative authorities.

content.text: The Ministries shall be organised only under the subordination of the Government.
Other specialised bodies may be organised under the subordination of the Government or of the Ministries, or as autonomous administrative authorities.

2003:117.contentEn:
1. The Ministries shall be established, organised and function according to the law.
2. The Government and the Ministries, with the advisory opinion of the Court of Accounts, may establish specialised bodies, under their subordination, only if the law recognises this competence to them.
3. Autonomous administrative authorities may be established by organic law.

content.text: The Ministries shall be established, organised and function according to the law.
The Government and the Ministries, with the advisory opinion of the Court of Accounts, may establish specialised bodies, under their subordination, only if the law recognises this competence to them.
Autonomous administrative authorities may be established by organic law.

2003:118.contentEn:
1. The Army is exclusively subordinated to the will of the people for the guarantee of the sovereignty, independence and unity of the State, the territorial integrity of the country and the constitutional democracy. Under the conditions of the law and of the international treaties to which Romania is a party, the Army contributes to the collective defence in the military alliance systems and participates in actions regarding the maintenance or restoration of peace.
2. The structure of the national defence system, the preparation of the population, of the economy and of the territory for defence, as well as the status of the military personnel, shall be established by organic law.
3. The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply, accordingly, to the other components of the armed forces established according to the law.
4. The organisation of military or paramilitary activities outside a state authority is prohibited.
5. Foreign troops may enter, station, carry out operations or pass through the territory of Romania only under the conditions of the law or of the international treaties to which Romania is a party.

content.text: The Army is exclusively subordinated to the will of the people for the guarantee of the sovereignty, independence and unity of the State, the territorial integrity of the country and the constitutional democracy. Under the conditions of the law and of the international treaties to which Romania is a party, the Army contributes to the collective defence in the military alliance systems and participates in actions regarding the maintenance or restoration of peace.

The structure of the national defence system, the preparation of the population, of the economy and of the territory for defence, as well as the status of the military personnel, shall be established by organic law.
The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply, accordingly, to the other components of the armed forces established according to the law.
The organisation of military or paramilitary activities outside a state authority is prohibited.
Foreign troops may enter, station, carry out operations or pass through the territory of Romania only under the conditions of the law or of the international treaties to which Romania is a party.

2003:119.titleEn: The Supreme Council of National Defence
2003:119.contentEn: The Supreme Council of National Defence shall organise and coordinate in a unitary manner the activities regarding the defence of the country and the national security, the participation in maintaining international security and in the collective defence in the military alliance systems, as well as in actions of maintaining or restoring peace.

content.text: The Supreme Council of National Defence shall organise and coordinate in a unitary manner the activities regarding the defence of the country and the national security, the participation in maintaining international security and in the collective defence in the military alliance systems, as well as in actions of maintaining or restoring peace.

2003:120.contentEn:
1. Public administration in administrative-territorial units is based on the principles of decentralisation, local autonomy and deconcentration of public services.
2. In the administrative-territorial units where citizens belonging to a national minority have a significant share, the use of the language of the respective national minority is ensured in writing and orally in relations with the authorities of the local public administration and with the deconcentrated public services, under the conditions provided for by the organic law.

content.text: Public administration in administrative-territorial units is based on the principles of decentralisation, local autonomy and deconcentration of public services.

In the administrative-territorial units where citizens belonging to a national minority have a significant share, the use of the language of the respective national minority is ensured in writing and orally in relations with the authorities of the local public administration and with the deconcentrated public services, under the conditions provided for by the organic law.

2003:121.contentEn:
1. The public administration authorities, through which local autonomy is achieved in communes and cities, are the elected local councils and the elected mayors, under the conditions provided for by law.
2. The local councils and mayors operate, under the conditions provided for by law, as autonomous administrative authorities and resolve public affairs in communes and cities.
3. The authorities provided for in paragraph (1) may also be established in the administrative-territorial subdivisions of municipalities.

content.text: The public administration authorities, through which local autonomy is achieved in communes and cities, are the elected local councils and the elected mayors, under the conditions provided for by law.
The local councils and mayors operate, under the conditions provided for by law, as autonomous administrative authorities and resolve public affairs in communes and cities.
The authorities provided for in paragraph (1) may also be established in the administrative-territorial subdivisions of municipalities.

2003:122.contentEn:
1. The county council is the public administration authority for coordinating the activity of the communal and city councils, in order to provide public services of county interest.
2. The county council is elected and operates under the conditions provided for by law.

content.text: The county council is the public administration authority for coordinating the activity of the communal and city councils, in order to provide public services of county interest.
The county council is elected and operates under the conditions provided for by law.

2003:123.contentEn:
1. The Government appoints a prefect in each county and in the Municipality of Bucharest.
2. The prefect is the representative of the Government at the local level and manages the deconcentrated public services of the ministries and of the other bodies of the central public administration in the administrative-territorial units.
3. The attributions of the prefect shall be established by organic law.
4. There are no subordination relationships between the prefects, on the one hand, and the local councils and mayors, as well as the county councils and their presidents, on the other hand.
5. The prefect may challenge, before the administrative court, an act of the county council, the local council or the mayor, if he/she considers the act illegal. The challenged act is suspended by law.

content.text: The Government appoints a prefect in each county and in the Municipality of Bucharest.

The prefect is the representative of the Government at the local level and manages the deconcentrated public services of the ministries and of the other bodies of the central public administration in the administrative-territorial units.
The attributions of the prefect shall be established by organic law.
There are no subordination relationships between the prefects, on the one hand, and the local councils and mayors, as well as the county councils and their presidents, on the other hand.
The prefect may challenge, before the administrative court, an act of the county council, the local council or the mayor, if he/she considers the act illegal. The challenged act is suspended by law.

2003:124.contentEn:
1. Justice is administered in the name of the law.
2. Justice is unique, impartial and equal for all.
3. Judges are independent and subject only to the law.

content.text: Justice is administered in the name of the law.
Justice is unique, impartial and equal for all.
Judges are independent and subject only to the law.

2003:125.contentEn:
1. Judges appointed by the President of Romania are irremovable, under the conditions provided for by law.
2. Proposals for appointment, as well as promotion, transfer and sanctioning of judges are the competence of the Superior Council of Magistracy, under the conditions of its organic law.
3. The office of judge is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

content.text: Judges appointed by the President of Romania are irremovable, under the conditions provided for by law.
Proposals for appointment, as well as promotion, transfer and sanctioning of judges are the competence of the Superior Council of Magistracy, under the conditions of its organic law.
The office of judge is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

2003:126.contentEn:
1. Justice is administered through the High Court of Cassation and Justice and through the other courts of law established by law.
2. The competence of the courts of law and the procedure of judgment are provided for only by law.
3. The High Court of Cassation and Justice ensures the unitary interpretation and application of the law by the other courts of law, according to its competence.
4. The composition of the High Court of Cassation and Justice and the rules of its operation shall be established by organic law.
5. The establishment of extraordinary courts is prohibited. Specialised courts in certain matters may be established by organic law, with the possibility of participation, as the case may be, of persons from outside the magistracy.
6. The judicial review of the administrative acts of the public authorities, by way of administrative litigation, is guaranteed, with the exception of those concerning relations with the Parliament, as well as command acts of a military nature. The administrative litigation courts are competent to settle the claims of persons injured by ordinances or, as the case may be, by provisions of ordinances declared unconstitutional.

content.text: Justice is administered through the High Court of Cassation and Justice and through the other courts of law established by law.
The competence of the courts of law and the procedure of judgment are provided for only by law.
The High Court of Cassation and Justice ensures the unitary interpretation and application of the law by the other courts of law, according to its competence.
The composition of the High Court of Cassation and Justice and the rules of its operation shall be established by organic law.

The establishment of extraordinary courts is prohibited. Specialised courts in certain matters may be established by organic law, with the possibility of participation, as the case may be, of persons from outside the magistracy.

The judicial review of the administrative acts of the public authorities, by way of administrative litigation, is guaranteed, with the exception of those concerning relations with the Parliament, as well as command acts of a military nature. The administrative litigation courts are competent to settle the claims of persons injured by ordinances or, as the case may be, by provisions of ordinances declared unconstitutional.

2003:127.contentEn: Court hearings are public, except in the cases provided for by law.

content.text: Court hearings are public, except in the cases provided for by law.

2003:128.titleEn: The Use of the Mother Tongue and of Interpreters in Justice
2003:128.contentEn:
1. The judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian.
2. Romanian citizens belonging to national minorities have the right to express themselves in their mother tongue before the courts of law, under the terms laid down in the organic law.
3. The ways of exercising the right stipulated in paragraph (2), including the use of interpreters or translations, shall be established so as not to hinder the proper administration of justice and not to involve additional expenses for those concerned.
4. Foreign citizens and stateless persons who do not understand or speak Romanian have the right to take cognizance of all the documents and works in the file, to speak in court and to submit conclusions, through an interpreter; in criminal trials, this right is ensured free of charge.

content.text: The judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian.
Romanian citizens belonging to national minorities have the right to express themselves in their mother tongue before the courts of law, under the terms laid down in the organic law.

The ways of exercising the right stipulated in paragraph (2), including the use of interpreters or translations, shall be established so as not to hinder the proper administration of justice and not to involve additional expenses for those concerned.

Foreign citizens and stateless persons who do not understand or speak Romanian have the right to take cognizance of all the documents and works in the file, to speak in court and to submit conclusions, through an interpreter; in criminal trials, this right is ensured free of charge.

2003:129.contentEn: Against court rulings, the interested parties and the Public Ministry may exercise remedies, under the terms laid down by the law.

content.text: Against court rulings, the interested parties and the Public Ministry may exercise remedies, under the terms laid down by the law.

2003:130.contentEn: The courts of law have police forces placed in their service.

content.text: The courts of law have police forces placed in their service.

2003:131.titleEn: The Role of the Public Ministry
2003:131.contentEn:
1. In judicial activity, the Public Ministry represents the general interests of society and defends the rule of law, as well as the rights and freedoms of citizens.
2. The Public Ministry exercises its powers through prosecutors constituted in prosecutor's offices, under the terms laid down by the law.
3. The prosecutor's offices operate within the courts of law, manage and supervise the criminal investigation activity of the judicial police, under the terms laid down by the law.

content.text: In judicial activity, the Public Ministry represents the general interests of society and defends the rule of law, as well as the rights and freedoms of citizens.
The Public Ministry exercises its powers through prosecutors constituted in prosecutor's offices, under the terms laid down by the law.
The prosecutor's offices operate within the courts of law, manage and supervise the criminal investigation activity of the judicial police, under the terms laid down by the law.

2003:132.contentEn:
1. Prosecutors carry out their activity according to the principle of legality, impartiality and hierarchical control, under the authority of the Minister of Justice.
2. The office of prosecutor is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

content.text: Prosecutors carry out their activity according to the principle of legality, impartiality and hierarchical control, under the authority of the Minister of Justice.
The office of prosecutor is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.

2003:133.contentEn:
1. The Superior Council of Magistracy is the guarantor of the independence of justice.
2. The Superior Council of Magistracy is composed of 19 members, of which:
   - a) 14 are elected in the general meetings of magistrates and validated by the Senate; they are part of two sections, one for judges and one for prosecutors; the first section is composed of 9 judges, and the second of 5 prosecutors;
   - b) 2 representatives of civil society, specialists in the field of law, who enjoy a high professional and moral reputation, elected by the Senate; they participate only in the plenary sessions;
   - c) the Minister of Justice, the President of the High Court of Cassation and Justice and the Prosecutor General of the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
3. The President of the Superior Council of Magistracy is elected for a one-year term, which cannot be renewed, from among the magistrates provided for in paragraph (2) letter a).
4. The term of office of the members of the Superior Council of Magistracy is 6 years.
5. The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy are taken by secret ballot.
6. The President of Romania presides over the works of the Superior Council of Magistracy in which he participates.
7. The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy are final and irrevocable, with the exception of those provided for in [Articolul 134 paragraph (2)](#articolul-134-atribuții).

content.text: The Superior Council of Magistracy is the guarantor of the independence of justice.
The Superior Council of Magistracy is composed of 19 members, of which:

a) 14 are elected in the general meetings of magistrates and validated by the Senate; they are part of two sections, one for judges and one for prosecutors; the first section is composed of 9 judges, and the second of 5 prosecutors;
b) 2 representatives of civil society, specialists in the field of law, who enjoy a high professional and moral reputation, elected by the Senate; they participate only in the plenary sessions;
c) the Minister of Justice, the President of the High Court of Cassation and Justice and the Prosecutor General of the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
The President of the Superior Council of Magistracy is elected for a one-year term, which cannot be renewed, from among the magistrates provided for in paragraph (2) letter a).
The term of office of the members of the Superior Council of Magistracy is 6 years.
The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy are taken by secret ballot.
The President of Romania presides over the works of the Superior Council of Magistracy in which he participates.
The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy are final and irrevocable, with the exception of those provided for in [Articolul 134 paragraph (2)](#articolul-134-atribuții).

2003:134.contentEn:
1. The Superior Council of Magistracy shall propose to the President of Romania the appointment of judges and prosecutors, with the exception of trainee judges and prosecutors, under the terms provided by the law.
2. The Superior Council of Magistracy shall act as a court of law, through its sections, in the field of disciplinary liability of judges and prosecutors, according to the procedure established by its organic law. In these situations, the Minister of Justice, the President of the High Court of Cassation and Justice, and the Prosecutor General of the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have no right to vote.
3. The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy in disciplinary matters may be challenged before the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
4. The Superior Council of Magistracy shall also perform other powers established by its organic law, in fulfilling its role as guarantor of the independence of justice.

content.text: The Superior Council of Magistracy shall propose to the President of Romania the appointment of judges and prosecutors, with the exception of trainee judges and prosecutors, under the terms provided by the law.

The Superior Council of Magistracy shall act as a court of law, through its sections, in the field of disciplinary liability of judges and prosecutors, according to the procedure established by its organic law. In these situations, the Minister of Justice, the President of the High Court of Cassation and Justice, and the Prosecutor General of the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have no right to vote.
The decisions of the Superior Council of Magistracy in disciplinary matters may be challenged before the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
The Superior Council of Magistracy shall also perform other powers established by its organic law, in fulfilling its role as guarantor of the independence of justice.
The economy of Romania is a market economy, based on free initiative and competition.
The State must ensure:
a) the freedom of commerce, the protection of fair competition, the creation of a favorable framework for harnessing all factors of production;
b) the protection of national interests in economic, financial and currency activity;
c) the stimulation of national scientific and technological research, of art, and the protection of copyright;
d) the exploitation of natural resources, in accordance with the national interest;
e) the reconstruction and protection of the environment, as well as the maintenance of ecological balance;
f) the creation of the necessary conditions for raising the quality of life;
g) the application of regional development policies in accordance with the objectives of the European Union.

2003:136.contentEn:
1. Property shall be public or private.
2. Public property shall be guaranteed and protected by law and shall belong to the State or to the administrative-territorial units.
3. The riches of public interest in the subsoil, the airspace, waters with exploitable energy potential, of national interest, beaches, the territorial sea, natural resources of the economic zone and of the continental shelf, as well as other assets established by the organic law, shall be the exclusive object of public property.
4. Assets in public property shall be inalienable. Under the terms provided by the organic law, they may be given for administration to the self-managed public utilities or to public institutions, or they may be granted by concession or lease; likewise, they may be given for free use to public utility institutions.
5. Private property shall be inviolable, under the terms provided by the organic law.

content.text: Property shall be public or private.
Public property shall be guaranteed and protected by law and shall belong to the State or to the administrative-territorial units.

The riches of public interest in the subsoil, the airspace, waters with exploitable energy potential, of national interest, beaches, the territorial sea, natural resources of the economic zone and of the continental shelf, as well as other assets established by the organic law, shall be the exclusive object of public property.

Assets in public property shall be inalienable. Under the terms provided by the organic law, they may be given for administration to the self-managed public utilities or to public institutions, or they may be granted by concession or lease; likewise, they may be given for free use to public utility institutions.
Private property shall be inviolable, under the terms provided by the organic law.

2003:137.contentEn:
1. The formation, administration, utilization and control of the financial resources of the State, of the administrative-territorial units and of the public institutions shall be regulated by law.
2. The national currency shall be the Leu, and its subdivision, the Ban. Under the conditions of accession to the European Union, the circulation and replacement of the national currency with that of the European Union may be recognized by organic law.

content.text: The formation, administration, utilization and control of the financial resources of the State, of the administrative-territorial units and of the public institutions shall be regulated by law.

The national currency shall be the Leu, and its subdivision, the Ban. Under the conditions of accession to the European Union, the circulation and replacement of the national currency with that of the European Union may be recognized by organic law.

2003:138.contentEn:
1. The national public budget shall include the state budget, the state social insurance budget, and the local budgets of the communes, cities and counties.
2. The Government shall annually elaborate the draft state budget and the draft state social insurance budget, which it shall submit separately for approval to Parliament.
3. If the law on the state budget and the law on the state social insurance budget have not been adopted at least 3 days before the expiration of the budgetary exercise, the state budget and the state social insurance budget of the previous year shall continue to be applied until the adoption of the new budgets.
4. The local budgets shall be elaborated, approved and executed under the terms provided by the law.
5. No budgetary expenditure may be approved without establishing the source of financing.

content.text: The national public budget shall include the state budget, the state social insurance budget, and the local budgets of the communes, cities and counties.
The Government shall annually elaborate the draft state budget and the draft state social insurance budget, which it shall submit separately for approval to Parliament.

If the law on the state budget and the law on the state social insurance budget have not been adopted at least 3 days before the expiration of the budgetary exercise, the state budget and the state social insurance budget of the previous year shall continue to be applied until the adoption of the new budgets.
The local budgets shall be elaborated, approved and executed under the terms provided by the law.
No budgetary expenditure may be approved without establishing the source of financing.

2003:139.contentEn:
1. Taxes, fees and any other revenues of the state budget and of the state social security budget shall be established only by law.
2. Local taxes and fees shall be established by the local or county councils, within the limits and under the conditions provided by law.
3. The amounts representing contributions to the establishment of certain funds shall be used, under the conditions provided by law, only according to their intended purpose.

content.text: Taxes, fees and any other revenues of the state budget and of the state social security budget shall be established only by law.
Local taxes and fees shall be established by the local or county councils, within the limits and under the conditions provided by law.
The amounts representing contributions to the establishment of certain funds shall be used, under the conditions provided by law, only according to their intended purpose.

2003:140.contentEn:
1. The Court of Accounts shall exercise control over the manner of formation, administration and use of the financial resources of the state and of the public sector. Under the conditions provided by the organic law, the litigations resulting from the activity of the Court of Accounts shall be settled by the specialized courts of law.
2. The Court of Accounts shall annually submit to Parliament a report on the management accounts of the national public budget for the expired budget year, including the irregularities found.
3. At the request of the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate, the Court of Accounts shall control the manner of management of public resources and report on the findings.
4. The councillors of accounts shall be appointed by Parliament for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed. The members of the Court of Accounts shall be independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable throughout its duration. They are subject to the incompatibilities provided by law for judges.
5. The Court of Accounts shall be renewed by one third of the councillors of accounts appointed by Parliament, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.
6. The revocation of the members of the Court of Accounts shall be made by Parliament, in the cases and under the conditions provided by law.

content.text: The Court of Accounts shall exercise control over the manner of formation, administration and use of the financial resources of the state and of the public sector. Under the conditions provided by the organic law, the litigations resulting from the activity of the Court of Accounts shall be settled by the specialized courts of law.
The Court of Accounts shall annually submit to Parliament a report on the management accounts of the national public budget for the expired budget year, including the irregularities found.
At the request of the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate, the Court of Accounts shall control the manner of management of public resources and report on the findings.

The councillors of accounts shall be appointed by Parliament for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed. The members of the Court of Accounts shall be independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable throughout its duration. They are subject to the incompatibilities provided by law for judges.
The Court of Accounts shall be renewed by one third of the councillors of accounts appointed by Parliament, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.
The revocation of the members of the Court of Accounts shall be made by Parliament, in the cases and under the conditions provided by law.

2003:141.contentEn: The Economic and Social Council is a consultative body of Parliament and the Government in the fields of expertise established by its organic law of establishment, organization and operation.

content.text: The Economic and Social Council is a consultative body of Parliament and the Government in the fields of expertise established by its organic law of establishment, organization and operation.

2003:142.contentEn:
1. The Constitutional Court is the guarantor of the supremacy of the Constitution.
2. The Constitutional Court shall be composed of nine judges, appointed for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed.
3. Three judges shall be appointed by the Chamber of Deputies, three by the Senate and three by the President of Romania.
4. The judges of the Constitutional Court shall elect, by secret ballot, its president, for a period of 3 years.

(5) The Constitutional Court shall be renewed by one third of its judges, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.

content.text: The Constitutional Court is the guarantor of the supremacy of the Constitution.
The Constitutional Court shall be composed of nine judges, appointed for a term of 9 years, which may not be extended or renewed.
Three judges shall be appointed by the Chamber of Deputies, three by the Senate and three by the President of Romania.
The judges of the Constitutional Court shall elect, by secret ballot, its president, for a period of 3 years.
(5) The Constitutional Court shall be renewed by one third of its judges, every 3 years, under the conditions provided by the organic law of the Court.

2003:143.contentEn: The judges of the Constitutional Court must have higher legal education, high professional competence and a seniority of at least 18 years in legal activity or in higher legal education.

content.text: The judges of the Constitutional Court must have higher legal education, high professional competence and a seniority of at least 18 years in legal activity or in higher legal education.

2003:144.contentEn: The office of judge of the Constitutional Court is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher legal education.

content.text: The office of judge of the Constitutional Court is incompatible with any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher legal education.

2003:145.contentEn: The judges of the Constitutional Court are independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable throughout its duration.

content.text: The judges of the Constitutional Court are independent in the exercise of their mandate and irremovable throughout its duration.
The Constitutional Court has the following powers:

a) to rule on the constitutionality of laws, prior to their promulgation, upon notification by the President of Romania, by one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, by the Government, by the High Court of Cassation and Justice, by the People's Advocate, by a number of at least 50 deputies or at least 25 senators, as well as ex officio, on the initiatives for the revision of the Constitution;

b) to rule on the constitutionality of treaties or other international agreements, upon notification by one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, by a number of at least 50 deputies or at least 25 senators;

c) to rule on the constitutionality of the Parliament's regulations, upon notification by one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, by a parliamentary group or by a number of at least 50 deputies or at least 25 senators;

d) to rule on objections of unconstitutionality regarding laws and ordinances, raised before the courts of law or commercial arbitration; the objection of unconstitutionality may also be raised directly by the People's Advocate;

e) to settle legal disputes of a constitutional nature between public authorities, at the request of the President of Romania, of one of the Speakers of the two Chambers, of the Prime Minister or of the President of the Superior Council of Magistracy;
f) to ensure the observance of the procedure for the election of the President of Romania and to confirm the results of the suffrage;
g) to ascertain the existence of circumstances justifying the interim in the exercise of the office of President of Romania and to communicate the findings to the Parliament and the Government;
h) to give advisory opinion for the proposal to suspend the President of Romania from office;
i) to ensure the observance of the procedure for the organisation and conduct of the referendum and to confirm its results;
j) to verify the fulfilment of the conditions for the exercise of the legislative initiative by citizens;
k) to rule on appeals having as object the constitutionality of a political party;
l) to perform other duties provided by the organic law of the Court.

2003:147.contentEn:
1. The provisions of the laws and ordinances in force, as well as those of the regulations, found to be unconstitutional, shall cease their legal effects within 45 days of the publication of the decision of the Constitutional Court if, within this interval, the Parliament or the Government, as the case may be, do not bring the unconstitutional provisions into line with the provisions of the Constitution. During this term, the provisions found to be unconstitutional are suspended de jure.
2. In cases of unconstitutionality concerning laws, prior to their promulgation, the Parliament is obliged to re-examine the respective provisions in order to bring them into line with the decision of the Constitutional Court.
3. If the constitutionality of the treaty or international agreement has been ascertained according to [Articolul 146 litera b)](#articolul-146-atribuții), it may not be the subject of an objection of unconstitutionality. The treaty or international agreement found to be unconstitutional may not be ratified.
4. The decisions of the Constitutional Court shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. From the date of publication, the decisions are generally binding and have power only for the future.

content.text: The provisions of the laws and ordinances in force, as well as those of the regulations, found to be unconstitutional, shall cease their legal effects within 45 days of the publication of the decision of the Constitutional Court if, within this interval, the Parliament or the Government, as the case may be, do not bring the unconstitutional provisions into line with the provisions of the Constitution. During this term, the provisions found to be unconstitutional are suspended de jure.

In cases of unconstitutionality concerning laws, prior to their promulgation, the Parliament is obliged to re-examine the respective provisions in order to bring them into line with the decision of the Constitutional Court.

If the constitutionality of the treaty or international agreement has been ascertained according to [Articolul 146 litera b)](#articolul-146-atribuții), it may not be the subject of an objection of unconstitutionality. The treaty or international agreement found to be unconstitutional may not be ratified.
The decisions of the Constitutional Court shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. From the date of publication, the decisions are generally binding and have power only for the future.

2003:148.contentEn:
1. The accession of Romania to the constitutive treaties of the European Union, for the purpose of transferring powers to the Community institutions, as well as for exercising jointly with the other Member States the competences provided for in these treaties, shall be done by law adopted in the joint sitting of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.
2. As a result of the accession, the provisions of the constitutive treaties of the European Union, as well as the other binding Community regulations, shall take precedence over the contrary provisions of the domestic laws, in compliance with the provisions of the act of accession.
3. The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the accession to the acts revising the constitutive treaties of the European Union.
4. The Parliament, the President of Romania, the Government and the judicial authority shall guarantee the fulfilment of the obligations resulting from the act of accession and from the provisions of paragraph (2).
5. The Government shall transmit to the two Chambers of the Parliament the drafts of the binding acts before they are submitted for approval to the institutions of the European Union.

content.text: The accession of Romania to the constitutive treaties of the European Union, for the purpose of transferring powers to the Community institutions, as well as for exercising jointly with the other Member States the competences provided for in these treaties, shall be done by law adopted in the joint sitting of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.

As a result of the accession, the provisions of the constitutive treaties of the European Union, as well as the other binding Community regulations, shall take precedence over the contrary provisions of the domestic laws, in compliance with the provisions of the act of accession.
The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the accession to the acts revising the constitutive treaties of the European Union.

The Parliament, the President of Romania, the Government and the judicial authority shall guarantee the fulfilment of the obligations resulting from the act of accession and from the provisions of paragraph (2).
The Government shall transmit to the two Chambers of the Parliament the drafts of the binding acts before they are submitted for approval to the institutions of the European Union.

2003:149.titleEn: Accession to the North Atlantic Treaty
2003:149.contentEn: The accession of Romania to the North Atlantic Treaty shall be done by law adopted in the joint sitting of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.

content.text: The accession of Romania to the North Atlantic Treaty shall be done by law adopted in the joint sitting of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.

2003:150.contentEn:
1. The revision of the Constitution may be initiated by the President of Romania upon the proposal of the Government, by at least one quarter of the number of deputies or senators, as well as by at least 500,000 citizens with the right to vote.
2. The citizens who initiate the revision of the Constitution must come from at least half of the counties of the country, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 20,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.

content.text: The revision of the Constitution may be initiated by the President of Romania upon the proposal of the Government, by at least one quarter of the number of deputies or senators, as well as by at least 500,000 citizens with the right to vote.

The citizens who initiate the revision of the Constitution must come from at least half of the counties of the country, and in each of these counties or in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 20,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.

2003:151.contentEn:
1. The draft or proposal for revision must be adopted by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber.
2. If an agreement is not reached through the mediation procedure, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, shall decide by a vote of at least three-quarters of the number of deputies and senators.
3. The revision is final after its approval by referendum, organised within a maximum of 30 days from the date of adoption of the draft or proposal for revision.

content.text: The draft or proposal for revision must be adopted by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, by a majority of at least two-thirds of the number of members of each Chamber.

If an agreement is not reached through the mediation procedure, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, shall decide by a vote of at least three-quarters of the number of deputies and senators.
The revision is final after its approval by referendum, organised within a maximum of 30 days from the date of adoption of the draft or proposal for revision.

2003:152.contentEn:
1. The provisions of the present Constitution regarding the national, independent, unitary and indivisible character of the Romanian State, the republican form of government, the integrity of the territory, the independence of the judiciary, political pluralism and the official language may not be the subject of revision.
2. Likewise, no revision may be made if it results in the suppression of the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens or of their guarantees.
3. The Constitution may not be revised during a state of siege or a state of emergency, nor in time of war.

content.text: The provisions of the present Constitution regarding the national, independent, unitary and indivisible character of the Romanian State, the republican form of government, the integrity of the territory, the independence of the judiciary, political pluralism and the official language may not be the subject of revision.
Likewise, no revision may be made if it results in the suppression of the fundamental rights and freedoms of citizens or of their guarantees.
The Constitution may not be revised during a state of siege or a state of emergency, nor in time of war.

2003:153.contentEn: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its approval by referendum. On the same date, the Constitution of August 21, 1965 is and remains entirely abrogated.

content.text: The present Constitution shall enter into force on the date of its approval by referendum. On the same date, the Constitution of August 21, 1965 is and remains entirely abrogated.

2003:154.contentEn:
1. The laws and all other normative acts shall remain in force to the extent that they do not contravene the present Constitution.
2. The Legislative Council, within 12 months from the date of entry into force of its organisation law, shall examine the conformity of the legislation with the present Constitution and shall make corresponding proposals to the Parliament or, as the case may be, to the Government.

content.text: The laws and all other normative acts shall remain in force to the extent that they do not contravene the present Constitution.

The Legislative Council, within 12 months from the date of entry into force of its organisation law, shall examine the conformity of the legislation with the present Constitution and shall make corresponding proposals to the Parliament or, as the case may be, to the Government.

2003:155.contentEn:
1. Draft laws and legislative proposals undergoing legislative procedure shall be debated and adopted according to the constitutional provisions prior to the entry into force of the revision law.
2. The institutions provided for by the Constitution, existing on the date of entry into force of the revision law, shall remain in office until the establishment of the new ones.
3. The provisions of paragraph (1) of [articolul 83](#articolul-83-durata-mandatului) shall apply starting with the next presidential term.
4. The provisions regarding the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall be implemented within no more than 2 years from the date of entry into force of the revision law.
5. The sitting judges of the Supreme Court of Justice and the Court of Accounts counselors appointed by the Parliament shall continue their activity until the expiration date of the mandate for which they were appointed. In order to ensure the renewal of the Court of Accounts every 3 years, upon the expiration of the mandate of the current Court of Accounts counselors, they may be appointed for another mandate of 3 years or 6 years.
6. Until the establishment of specialized courts, the disputes resulting from the activity of the Court of Accounts shall be settled by the ordinary courts.

content.text: Draft laws and legislative proposals undergoing legislative procedure shall be debated and adopted according to the constitutional provisions prior to the entry into force of the revision law.
The institutions provided for by the Constitution, existing on the date of entry into force of the revision law, shall remain in office until the establishment of the new ones.
The provisions of paragraph (1) of [articolul 83](#articolul-83-durata-mandatului) shall apply starting with the next presidential term.
The provisions regarding the High Court of Cassation and Justice shall be implemented within no more than 2 years from the date of entry into force of the revision law.

The sitting judges of the Supreme Court of Justice and the Court of Accounts counselors appointed by the Parliament shall continue their activity until the expiration date of the mandate for which they were appointed. In order to ensure the renewal of the Court of Accounts every 3 years, upon the expiration of the mandate of the current Court of Accounts counselors, they may be appointed for another mandate of 3 years or 6 years.
Until the establishment of specialized courts, the disputes resulting from the activity of the Court of Accounts shall be settled by the ordinary courts.

2003:156.contentEn: The law on the revision of the Constitution shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania within 5 days from the date of its adoption. The Constitution, as amended and supplemented, after approval by referendum, shall be republished by the Legislative Council, with the updating of the names, giving the texts a new numbering.

content.text: The law on the revision of the Constitution shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania within 5 days from the date of its adoption. The Constitution, as amended and supplemented, after approval by referendum, shall be republished by the Legislative Council, with the updating of the names, giving the texts a new numbering.
